description:
From the publisher's website:
                                                                 

     THE BOARD GAME FOR ONE PERSON ONLY....ANYONE, ANYWHERE, ANYTIME.
     EVERY GAME AS EASY OR AS DIFFICULT AS YOU CHOOSE TO MAKE IT.
     107 LETTERS...CAN YOU USE EVERY ONE OF THEM ?
     EVERY GAME YOU PLAY.........ITLMAKEUTHINK.


OBJECT OF THE GAME 
                                                                    Successfully place all 107 letters on the board, by forming words both horizontally and vertically.  
                                                                     With 7 different levels of play to choose from, the smaller that you make your playing area, the more difficult your game then becomes.

It's a game like scrabble and crossword puzzles. 
The player who creates the words with the most rare letters scores the most points.

Italy needs a champion!
Are you a skilled and focused pizza maker …?

Look at the ingredients and sort your cards out quickly.  You must prepare your customers' orders as quickly as possible.  Put 4 cards together and make a pizza with cheese, peppers, mushrooms and olives!

As the game progresses the orders will get more and more complicated and the challenge will be harder and harder to meet!

A game requiring alertness and observation for novice pizza makers and head chefs.

boardgame: Zombicide: Black Plague Special Guest Box – John Howe
description:
A box set of four Zombicide: Black Plague survivors that were designed by John Howe. The survivors include:
Dravog the Hewer
Cyrine
Gorvin
Hildir the Wise

Also included are a couple of unique spell equipment cards made specifically for this box.

Play as one of the 12 gods of the Greek pantheon as you inspire your followers to build new temples and collect piety from the faithful.

Each turn players roll 5 dice to gather piety, build temples, move priests, or gather power to use special abilities. The player with the most piety collected when the end is triggered is the winner!

The players have to go around the game track three times with their trucks, while picking up and delivering cargo. Movement is determined by a die. At the end of the game, the players get points awarded for the cargo on their truck (more if it is in their own Color) and for the order of their arrival at the finish.

The game theme is about the USS Los Angeles rigid airship, designated ZR-3, which was built in 1923–1924 by the Zeppelin company in Friedrichshafen, Germany as war reparation. It was delivered to the United States Navy in October 1924 and after being used mainly for experimental work, particularly in the development of the American parasite fighter program, was decommissioned in 1932.
Players are rolling dice and travels along the routes (two different routes in red and blue colors), which crosses Atlantic Ocean (from Germany to US). The first player, who cross the finish line, wins the game.

The Battlefield 2-player card game from NewVenture Games has been popular for some time. The American Civil War edition commemorates the 150th anniversary of that great conflict, the cards being completely redesigned to depict army units, officers, and civilians from the 1860s. One player assumes the role of the Union, the other plays Confederate units, meeting on the field of battle. In skirmish after skirmish, players deploy their forces card after card, fighting gallantly for their nation's survival.

Each of the two decks contain 72 cards, from the volunteer infantry with aged muskets, to the advanced firepower of artillery and cavalry. Famous generals take to the field, supported by the Corps of Engineers, civilian support groups, and even the President may make an appearance. Hope to avoid disease and inclement weather, and hope for a prisoner exchange or timely reinforcements.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Q Continuum Card Pack
description: This card pack is the grand prize for the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, the Q Continuum. It consists of 3 Upgrades, 1 Captain and 1 Admiral.

Players are suburbanite homeowners trying to grow attractive lawns in front of their houses, starting with basic grass cards and adding ornaments of various kinds. Players draw cards and either play or store them in a public 'shed' for later. One card may be held secretly in hand. Some cards are played offensively on other players to steal or destroy their items, or add negative scoring to their lawns. Players have the option to trade cards on their turn and score bonus points by finishing the game with specific configurations of grass cards.

From the front cover and opening chapter:

Rules for a tabletop game of jungle exploration and monster hunting for kids age 7 and older.

Includes Bonus Game Cowboys vs. Dinos

The crazy wild man stumbled out of the jungle into the village on the Nile. He babbled about lost treasures, a remote valley, and a giant ape. Nobody believed him at first, but then, as he gasped his last breath, his hand fell to his side and a golden medallion clattered to the ground. Rumors quickly spread to all parts of Africa: somewhere in the deepest, darkest jungle is a valley filled with treasure...and one giant ape guarding it all.

Into the Jungle
Valley of the Ape offers some simple rules for kids to play a tabletop game of exploration and monster hunting. Adults might offer some advice and help collecting all the terrain and pieces; they could also provide guidance, answer questions, or referee disputes at the game table during play.

User description
Valley Of The Ape is a light, kid friendly wargame. Players take turns moving then attacking. Resolution is done with d6. The PDF includes rule summary sheets and marker cards that can be cutout and use during play.

This is a fan-made expansion available for download from the Hans im Glück website.

It contains one sheet of 24 printable tiles that each contain one or more city parts with one or more city gates (in German: city gates = Stadttore).

The new rule is that roads are allowed to end at an edge of a tile that shows a city gate.
family: Series: Carcassonne Official Cut & Play Expansions

description:
Many Parsecs of Space (working title but yes - pun intended!) is an upcoming space 4X game that reimplements Martin Wallace's innovative deck building mechanic from A Few Acres of Snow. Anyone familiar with AFAoS or Mythotopia will be playing MPoS in minutes, though it's far from a simple reskinning. MPoS takes the core mechanic in an entirely new direction by introducing exploration and trade in a multi-player science fiction setting. Location (world) cards are variable and hidden at setup and revealed only when explored. This is not only thematic, but creates infinite replayability and solves the dominant strategy issues (ex. Halifax's Hammer) that can plague low luck games with a static setup. Trading with your neighbor is essential because nearby star systems may lack the resources you need to expand your empire.

GAME FEATURES:

     Unique sentient races for asymmetrical play.
     Secretly customize your race beforehand so other players won't know what they're up against until first contact!
     Choose a government and economy that allows you to discard, reserve, and remove cards according to your playing style. 
     A world suitable for your race to colonize may not be for your neighbor. One race's garbage is another race's treasure!
     Technology cards function like empire cards from AFAoS but with the added wrinkle of a tech tree.
     Steal resources and technology from your neighbor or trade partner with piracy and espionage.
     5 scarce resources (energy, metals, military, organics, and science) must be acquired so trading is a MUST to efficiently grow your empire.
     Discover ancient artifacts on dead planets and worm holes that connect distant star systems.
     Terraform tide locked worlds to make them suitable for colonization.
     Invade an outpost or colony to make it your own or NUKE it so nobody can colonize it!

A lightweight, 2-player game of Time Travel and Technology.

Players draft technology cards each round to keep them to use as influence, or to bid on later in the round.

Players then travel back in time, bidding their influence on establishing technologies to buildup the timeline.

Players return to the present day to draft new techs, and build off the old ones, carefully selecting technologies that benefit them the most.

At the end of the third round players gain victory points for techs they influenced, and dependency bonuses are paid out to the owner of supporting technologies. The player with the highest victory point total is the winner.

Drapacze Chmur ("Skyscrapers") is a fast game suitable for children. Players, simultaneously, search through a pile of square building elements. Whoever builds higher skyscraper is the winner. 
The only rule is, that each next element needs to have less windows than the previous.

There is also an game mode, where the specific color of an element gives a player 1 additional point.

boardgame: Darkest Night: In Tales of Old
description:
This newcomer fancies himself a historian; always telling stories about how our people used to live, or how some great hero from the past would deal with the crisis of the hour.  It seems silly to say, but it makes the horrors around us seem more manageable; like we've beaten them before.  The victory that recently seemed hopeless has become conceivable.

I wonder how much truth there is in these fables.  Could anyone really fabricate wondrous items like we hear about in legends?  Command wild animals to do their bidding?  Craft servants out of light and shadow?  I've never seen such things, but I've heard of them, in tales of old...

In Tales of Old, the fourth expansion to Jeremy Lennert's Darkest Night, sends your heroes racing across the kingdom to unravel the mysteries of the holy relics' resting places.

Instead of searching for keys, you now draw Mystery cards that reveal how to obtain clues to the relic's location, and perhaps some other rewards. These mysteries may lead you to explore the depths of ancient catacombs teeming with traps, or encounter an antique trader willing to part with old scrolls for the right price.

Gather your knowledge, courage, and strength as you uncover the kingdom's secrets in the hopes of vanquishing darkness once and for all...

The Mazin family is traveling in an exciting city to see and do marvellous things. Your job is to help family members find attractions around town to win trophies.

In The Mazins, place at least one city tile in between the family members and the actions. This way, you create a labyrinth. Every family member is connection with one sight-seeing object – but with which one? Role a die to see which family member wants to travel. The first one who takes the right sight-seeing object marker gets a coin, and the first player with five coins wins.

Flying saucers game has an interesting introductory story. Somewhere in space four civilizations fight for resources.

Figurines represent different categories of flying saucers, which differ in speed, size and power. Flying saucers destroy enemy saucers by stopping next to it. Flying saucer with more power, or joined multiple saucers can destroy enemy flying saucer. The goal of the game is to destroy the main enemy flying saucer.

The game can be played with dice or without it.

See, there are these things called giggities. They sort of flutter around touching peeps and absorbing their traits. If one touched you, for example, it might nab itself your mighty hairstyle, or maybe your Grandma’s taco recipe, or your ability to blast fireballs out of your nose. That sort of stuff…

Giggity giggers are zazz wagglers (magical sorts) who have hipped themselves to the gist of how to capture giggities, yoink the traits the giggities nabbed from others, and g’zoink them onto themselves. So, like, if you’re a giggity gigger and you capture a giggity who once touched me and absorbed my buns of steel, you can waggle your zazz and get yourself some sweet, sweet buns of your own.

That’s G’Zoink in a nutshell. Simple, yet hideously complex. Players are giggity giggers who catch giggities and g’zoink their traits in an attempt to become the most awesome zazz waggler since zazz waggling first became a thing that’s a thing. It’s not that easy, though, because giggities don’t only absorb the good stuff. If you don’t play your cards right you might end up with horrible backne or a blue hairy tongue or something even worse.

G’Zoink takes place on Mutha Oith, the setting for the Low Life RPG and the Dementalism card game, but familiarity with such things is not necessary for players to enjoy the game.

Each round, players select a card from one of the locales.  The highest card wins the round and takes all the cards.  You may also acquire The Fix cards which will give you additional positive or negative points at the end of the game.  The game ends when all cards have been taken.  Players total the points on all their Giggities and add in any bonus or penalty points from The Fix cards.  The highest score wins.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Apeldoorn, a city of the Netherlands.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from the theme park Clearwater Marine Aquarium

Hope City is a cooperative board game for 3-6 players. The Citizens work together to achieve objectives, construct City Sites, and build Hope City. But Punks attack, corrupt, and make objectives harder to achieve. If Citizens become corrupt, they can break bad. If they fail objectives, Urban Blights can emerge! The Citizens have 7 years to build Hope City otherwise chaos could bring the city to ruin!

Citizens collect resources which they use to attempt an objective card. There are conditions too which must be met to achieve the objective. Sometimes this requires a vote, rolling the dice, moving to a different city space, removing a punk, or pulling the right resource from the bag. Every objective has rewards for success or consequences for failure. The major objectives always reward success with "Construct 1 City Site." But they are expensive and take collaboration. There are some cheaper objectives that offer this reward but more luck is needed.

Citizens have talents & temptations as actions. Temptations corrupt citizens as do successful Punk attacks. If the citizen takes too much corruption, they break bad and can harm the team. Citizens can become good again, though.
This is an entertaining party game like Taboo where you have to describe a word to your teammates without using specific words as fast as you can!

Trivial game with questions about Greece and Cyprus

It contains 6 fields of questions: 
1.civilization and arts
2.cinema
3.history and mythology
4.geography
5.sports
6.language and tradition

Excellent trivial game in which winners and losers gain some knowledge!

Creepy Treat Combat is a collectable trading trading card game based on the cards found in the video game Costume Quest by Double Fine

The game uses a simple push your luck mechanic.  Each card has a number, and may have text describing a special effect.  Players take turns drawing cards or holding, attempting to reach the highest total without going over 13.

Long after the collapse of our civilization, would-be cult leaders vie for influence over the masses. Discover mysterious relics from our time and use them to lead your cult to power.

Scrapture is a resource management game for 2-4 players. During the game players attract initiates, fill up their shrines, send their followers on missions, and discover relics. Players must adapt to their opponents’ maneuvers if they wish to claim victory.

To win the game, players must acquire and use relics to earn influence. At the end of a round if at least one player has 10 or more Influence, the game ends and player with the most Influence wins.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Rotterdam, the largest port of Europe.

Objective: gain as many victory points as possible; use the bible as a guide for all vital questions.

Biblisches Frage- & Antwort-Spiel is a trivia game. Players ask each other questions extracted from better-known Bible stories.
For each correct answer the player gains a card/victory point.
The game includes 2x 110 cards with 110 questions and answers each.

Later edition (after WW2) are containing modifications, such as difficulty levels (with more victory points for more difficult questions).

All The cheese of 80's B-movies converges into one alien zombie infested town. And you must survive!

SO what makes this so 'Deluxe'?

Well its because it also includes the first expansion IT'S A TRAP!!! before its released separately. 40 more cards plus the New Trapping Dynamic AND ALIENS!

It’s time for a survival game that doesn't take it self so seriously! You are a mercenary dropped into the overrun town of Wellsville. Your only ticket out is by retrieving survivors, searching for the antidote and murdering the undead (or your fellow players).

Travel through the overrun town, gain access to buildings by drawing door cards, and collect Loot cards to find rewards. Use looted weapons to increase your attack rolls, defend against zombies, Alien Bio-weapons, Idiot Strippers and your fellow players.

Beware the Event cards as they change the dynamics of the whole board, and either help or screw everyone. Amass 10 survivor points by any means necessary and get to the choppa to win.

Gameplay involves rolling dice for movement and battle, and card pulls for reward, door opening and mass events. So much variety you'll never get bored.


    Easy battle mechanics that allow you to collect zombies, or pick off other players,



    Play as a zombie when you die and wipe out the other players to win alternately.
A childrens game based on the film with the same name. The game mechanic is dice-rolling with very few choices.

boardgame: Torpedo Junction: Beneath the Southern Cross, Part 1
description:
Revised July 2024

Torpedo Junction: Beneath the Southern Cross, Part I is the first installment of simulations of the Guadalcanal Campaign and other naval/air battles in 1942. Torpedo Junction includes the Battle of the Eastern Solomons, Santa Cruz, and several other scenarios occuring in the eastern portion of the Solomons -- around the area known as "Torpedo Junction".

I've been calling BTSC "Flat Top on steroids".  The scale is 16.8 nautical miles per hex; the map covers from Shortland Island to Esperitu Santo, and Off Board Movement Mat allow players to move between the map and Rabaul, Truk, Noumea, Fiji, and Townsville.

The game revolves around three goals:  "Fuel 'em, Find 'em, Strike 'em".  You will need to track fuel status of your task forces, and might have to find an oiler at an inconvenient moment.  Your carriers and long-range scout planes (PBYs, H6Ks, and H8Ks) search for the other side, and when you find them, launch strikes to take out the enemy carriers.

The game includes 1.3" x .65" counters for every warship and most auxiliaries. Plane counters are .625" square, and most game markers are either that size or 1/2" square.

Each turn is one hour of time. Each hex is 16.8 nautical miles; ground units are mainly regiments and each step is roughly a company.  Plane types are rated for movement and endurance, and all planes can add extra bursts of speed--at the risk of losing endurance.  Planes also have various combat modifiers.

The game includes numerous player aid cards as well as a 16-page Charts & Tables booklet.

The files you see here are near-final production graphics. Bruce Yearian is the Graphic Designer.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
KENDO TAIKAI, meaning Kendo Championship, is an entertaining strategy cardgame for 2-6 players, based on the physical and mental aspects of kendo, the traditional japanese fencing. It comes with three rule versions: an easier one with less and open cards for beginner players and for competition purposes, a similar easy one with more cards and hidden cards designed for more players, and a more complex one for advanced players.

There are three gameplay-versions of the game. All of them can by played with the same deck or with its parts.



Version 1: Kendo Shiai - Kendo CompetitionThe Kendo Competition is a more complex variant rule designed to more advanced players and for those who practice kendo in reality. It takes more time to learn and needs bigger attention while playing, however the gameplay is very much like a real kendo bout.

Difficulty: 4/5
Players: 2
Gameplay: 10-20 mins
Cards needed: 110


    at the beginning players draw 8 cards
    during the game players must swith their cards and pick a new one befor every attack or counterattack
    a player is awarded an IPPON or hitpoint if he or she make the opponent not be able to react to an attack. The player wins who gets the 2nd IPPON first.
    it is also possible to get HANSOKU or penalty points during the game for stepping out of court, or losing the bamboo sword (SHINAI)which is exactly the same in real kendo comeptitions.
    The players can gain adventage by altering their cards. The use of the cards is more complex than it is in the 2. version: Musashi's Challenge.




Version 2: Musashi no chōsen - Musashi's ChallengeMusashi'c Challenge is much easier to learn and play the kendo playing cards.It also makes possible a simulatnous gameplay of 2-6 players. It needs less attention as it offers entertainment for children and non kendo practicioners as well. The players can have a glimpse into the wonderful World of Kendo, the Way of the Sword.

Difficulty: 2/5
Players: 2-6
Gameplay: 3-10 mins
Cards needed: 105


     at the beginning the players draw 12 cards
     it is allowed to switch only once and only the the beginning. the players don't keep picking new cards during the game
     players can choose whom they attack (when >2 players)
     there is no IPPON or HANSOKU nor marking sign during the game. 
     the player who is the last one to lose all of the cards form the hand wins.
     attacks can be parried or turned back to the attacker or even to other player, and can also be strenghtened with additional cards.
     the players can take other player's cards and use them if they can't react to an attack.




Version 3: Shobu - Bushi no ketto - Samurai's DuelThe Shobu - Samurai's Duel is a variant of the Musashi's challenge version, but with only the half of the cards.

It is the easiest and the suggested way to start to get acquainted with the Kendo Playing Cards, however it needs different strategy than Musasi's Challenge and Kendo Shiai since it is played with open cards.
Therefore it is also the best choice for a competition since the spectators can easily follow the game and may think together with the players.

Difficulty: 1.5 / 5
Players: 2
Gameplay: 3-10 mins
Cards needed: 55
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Planet Zongo' is based on the proliferation of small arms. This game is about an epidemic, but instead of being threatened by diseases or zombies, the players must escape their own weapons. In this game, players may even want to give weapons to  their opponents in order to get rid of them.  
 Like in other games, players need armies to protect themselves or attack their opponents. They also have populations to sustain their armies. But the problem is: 
 1. Weapons never leave the game. When an army is killed, its weapons stay on the board. Those weapons are given to another population (even belonging to another player). That population thus becomes an army. 
2. But armies need populations. When an army is not sustained by a population, it starves and is removed from the game. However, as explained in rule 1, its weapons stay on the board and will be given to another population. 
 As more and more weapons are added in the course of the game, this mechanic threatens the players with a snowball effect: The more armies get destroyed, the more populations become armies by receiving weapons.The  more populations become armies, the less armies can be sustained… And so it gets only worse: as those unsustained armies are eliminated, the remaining populations receive even more weapons ...  
 The winner will be the player that best survives this process.
Typical spin and move game about the Russo-Japanese War (8 February 1904 – 5 September 1905). The first player, who cross the route first, wins the game.

This is a card game where up to six players take turns to evolve fish from the Cambrian era up to the Carboniferous era. The title comes from a geologists mnemonic for the Earth's geological eras. 
Players have a hand of cards which depict many aspects of evolution including species of fish; events such as major catastrophes and changes to the plankton species; breeding and feeding; genetic mutations and advantages. Each game lasts 6 rounds and players try to evolve the most advanced fish they can before inevitable extinctions reduce their populations to dust.

The object Santa's Sleigh Ride is to race other "Santas" throughout the world, stopping at highly populated cities along the way to deliver gifts. The first player to arrive back at the North Pole wins the game. But, watch out! Your journey along the Candy Cane Path will be filled with a few twists and turns.

This fun, fast-paced and educational game will provide a great way for your family to come together at the holidays or any time of year.

The Goddess Izanami has fallen, allying the spirits of Yomi with the demons of Jigoku to invade the mortal realm through Yomi's Gate! Will you enlist with the shogunate to protect the celestial order? Or will you join Izanami and seek to shatter it?

Yomi's Gate is a tactical game of map control and unit management. Lead samurai and oni units into battle and use each of their special abilities to conquer a map that's different every time you play.

Capture the enemy stronghold, defeat their leaders, or eliminate their army to win!
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Breaker Blocks is a 1v1 tabletop game where you place tiles to power up circuits, input commands, and overwhelm your opponent. You win if, at the end of the game, you control more circuits than the other player. Use power tiles to charge your circuits and command modules to perform unique actions that can trip up your opponent.

Designed for quick, portable play, Breaker Blocks fits in a small bag, features durable acrylic components, and sets up in seconds. New players can learn the basics of gameplay in 5-10 minutes, with actual games typically playing in 10-20. Essential gameplay can be learned in just a turn or two, but strategic mastery will take much longer.

Dead Sails is a 3-4 player tabletop game where you use coins to attack ships and out-plunder your pirate friends. The player with the most treasure at the end of the game wins. Plays in 30-60 minutes.

Played with laser-cut coins, Dead Sails is a bidding game about choosing your battles wisely and outwitting your friends. Use attack coins to plunder ships from the Merchant Fleet and the Royal Navy, treasure coins to bid on weapons from the Black Market and to hire mercenaries, and special coins like the Spyglass to gather intelligence on what your opponents are plotting.

This is compilation of 3 games + puzzle.

Star trails is a simple game for younger players. While playing children can learn about the solar system. The task to orbit the solar system with space shuttle and make photos of planets and asteroids.
Points are collected in two ways. The first way by reaching finish line (first gets 12 points, second 10, third 8 ...). Another way is to collect counters. Each counter brings 1 point. The winner is the player who ends up with the most points.

Flying saucers:
Figurines represent different categories of flying saucers, which differ in speed, size and power. Flying saucers destroy enemy saucers by stopping next to it. Flying saucer with more power, or joined multiple saucers can destroy enemy flying saucer. The goal of the game is to destroy the main enemy flying saucer.

Svemirska patrola:
Rude Martians come to the Moon, but they don't respect traffic rules. Space patrol from the Earth comes to charge fines.
Players move figurines and collect counters by stopping on the field next to counter. The winner is the player who collects the most counters.

This is the First in the Pages of War Series of Card Games: M.O.D.E (Men, Orcs, Dragkins, Elves)

Pages of War: M.O.D.E is a richly strategic game with simple mechanics and a unique flavour.

You take on the role of a bard of 1 of the 4 races in the western lands of the continent of Murghingale: MEN of the Blue Mountain, ORCS of the Black Hills, DRAGKINS of the Cloudy Peaks, and ELVES of the Verdant-Wood.

As a bard of one of these races your goal is to tell the greatest tale of its glory and strength by reciting the rhymes and songs of history.

BASICS:
Each player gets a deck (Historia) of 30 cards containing 22 heroes of 3 of the 5 suits (Axe, Bow, Sword, Lance, and Winged), 3 Jacks (generals of their 3 suits), 3 Aces (special tactics) and 2 monarchs (King and Queen).

The Goal is to create chapters (groups of heroes) that are strong enough to find the other players' Kings and Queens and destroy them - defeating other players' chapters and collecting their cards as spoils of war as you battle to achieve victory.

Once a player's King and Queen are destroyed they are eliminated from the battle to win but can turn the tide of combat with their remaining cards by adding their heroes to the chapters of other players.

This pack of promo cards contains one card in each player color. Each player starts the game with a Gambler's Risk. During the inspection phase, before you complete a deal, you may remove this card from play to double the penalty paid on that merchant bag, if it is inspected.

boardgame: Mage Wars: Dice Tower 2015 Funding Campaign Promo Card Set
description:
8 Mage Wars Arena promo cards made available through the 2015 Dice Tower Season 11 funding campaign on Kickstarter (part of Promo Pack A).

The cards contain creatures (Messenger of Bim-Shalla, Unstable Fire Imp, Dwarf Kriegshammer, Magma Golem), enchantments (Reinforce, Bind Spirit, Life Link) and a conjuration (Hidden Tunnels).

From the box:

The hilarious and insightful game where... "Everyone has an opinion and every opinion counts!" - if you match!!!

Gameplay:
The object of Elephonkey is for teams of 2+ players to score points by matching their opinions with those of their teammates by listing and ranking the top 5 answers in each opinion poll.  During each round, each team draws an opinion poll card and a category on the card is chosen by rolling the provided dice (one die decides the category while the other determines the point values).  When the timer begins, each player begins listing their opinions (e.g. Top 5 Bruce Willis movies) on one of the provided pads. After 90 seconds, players compare their answers with their teammates and are awarded points for direct and indirect answers, unless otherwise stated. The game ends once one team reaches the pre-determined number of points.

From the publisher:

"It's the fast-find song frenzy that pits your playlist against your friends' in a simultaneous music challenge for up to four players!  Connect up to four MP3 players (not included) and let your fingers do the flyin' as you show what you know about your music collection.  When you're done playing, use the console to play your favorite music!  Plus, the console stores everything, making it easy for you to take this tune-tappin' song-matching game anywhere you go!  Game console comes with cables, 60 cards, and instructions."

This unique game is played by everyone connecting their MP3 players or smart phones to the game console via the included audio cables.  Players take turns drawing cards and reading aloud the categories.  All players must then search through their playlists in a race to be the first one to play an appropriate song.  If the song does indeed fit the category, that player earns a point; otherwise, they lose a point. Once all cards have been played, the game ends and the player with the most points is declared the winner.

An alternate take on the Connect 4 style of gameplay, 5-Across is a vertical game played with checkers.  The board is placed on the stand to hold it vertically and players drop checkers into one of the 7 slots until one player succeeds in getting 5 in a row either vertically, horizontally, or diagonally.

From the box:

Everyone loves a great party...with the Murder Mystery Video Party Game, you'll throw the party your friends won't stop talking about  It's fun!  To play, you and your friends assume the roles of the characters in the story. You act out your parts as the game is played.

When a popular restauranteur is suddenly murdered, no one is allowed to leave and everyone is suspect! Why is Miss Svenson so emotional about the murder? Who is Oscar Himmel and what does he know about Miss Swift? At THREE POINTS in the movie, the detective will tell you to stop the video, question your suspects and pick up clues to track down the killer.  Is Fred Simms really an aluminum siding salesman?  Who is the floosie hanging around Mr. Engels and where did the waiter disappear to?

A thrilling whodunnit!  YOU FIND THE KILLER and YOU SOLVE THE MURDERS.  But watch out, your character may be the next victim!

Pig Out, Pete! is an electronic game in which Pete moves around and makes funny sounds as he throws up plastic food. Every 15 seconds, Pete will throw up a piece of food and players must dig through a tray of slime/gunk (much like Gak) for a plastic food piece of the same color. Once a player finds the correct piece, they set it aside along with the matching piece that Pete threw up. After Pete has fully purged himself and all of the pieces have been collected, the player with the most wins.

This is a deduction through conversation game. Exercise your vivid imagination and infer the thing that comes into your opponent’s mind by using the sense of touch. 
Due to the nature of the game, the visually impaired people can enjoy and play, too!

//story

In ancient times, when the mammoths and dinosaurs walked the land, or perhaps even before that, there was a civilization called “Dattakamo”. Its people excelled at making things out of clay, of which they made many: from what they found at the bottom of the ocean, to what they saw in the beyond the galaxy; from flowers and animals around them, to things from the imaginary future.  They treasured what they had made, and lived happily. One day, however, a volcano suddenly erupted and the civilization perished.

Time flew by, and now in the present, the works of the “Dattakamo” people have been excavated and brought into the Academy. What are they? Some of the artifacts are broken; others, with strange and bizarre shapes. All of them were treasures of the “Dattakamo” people.

In the Academy, a renowned professor and his students sit around these artifacts. The professor wonders what the pieces are, while his students discuss his ideas earnestly.

// Game Progress

The purpose of this game is that the students guess right what the professor imagines. The students ask the professor three questions, one at a time, during their turns. This is called the “discussion”, and a discussion has 3 rounds.  The professor has to answer correctly these questions. During this phase, students can freely touch the piece as many times as they want.
If a student manages to make a correct guess, he gets the piece from the professor.
When all the players have finished playing the role of the professor, the game ends and the player who acquires the most artifacts is the greatest professor ? and wins!
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
One random player chooses to secretly place either treasure or a bomb into the McGuffin. The players then take turns to move the McGuffin from player to player with their cards. At the end of the round the player who has the McGuffin may or may not open it. If they choose to keep it closed they gain 1 victory point. If they open it and it is treasure they get 2 victory points. If there is a bomb inside they lose 1 victory point. After 3 rounds whoever has the highest victory points wins.

For now this is a playtesting copy of the new McGuffin that will be KickStartered soon.

boardgame: Thunder Hamsters and the Temple of Cheese
description:
In Thunder Hamsters and the Temple of Cheese, players take on the role of one of the legendary Thunder Hamsters while they delve into the fabled Temple of Cheese. Players have several paths to choose from and will encounter large wheels of cheese, traps, guards, items of all sorts, and many other wonders.

This game has story-telling at the heart of it. As each encountered item is revealed, the player must make up a story to indicate how to pick up/ward off/handle the situation. Once the story is told, a judge is picked from the other players at the table and the story is ranked on a scale of likelihood. The player then must make a die role to determine the attempt's success or failure for that encounter. Other players may also toss in a coin to challenge the original story and a roll-off ensues.

Rules guide the players in basic mechanics such as movement, but the story-telling aspect opens up the game to nearly limitless possibilities.

The Aurloks are largely organized into nomad tribes, following the rhythm of the seasons and the migrations of the animals they hunt. They are generally a peaceful people, but do not underestimate them for this appearance! Their war-songs can echo as quickly as a thunderstorm in the summer. The Aurloks then become savage and merciless warriors whose wrath is guided by their totems: the Manitous.

User summary:
古代建築 (Ancient Construct) is a 3-dimensional puzzle game in which 24 pieces comprised of a total of 99 blocks are piled on top of one another in a chimney-like stack. The player who stacked the greatest number of blocks through their pieces wins!

There are six 3-block pieces, ten 4-block pieces, seven 5-block pieces, and one 6-block pieces, for a total of 24 pieces. This allows 2-4 people to play. If anyone makes the tower collapse during the game, they lose. The blocks are slightly uneven, so the game demands 3-dimensional spatial awareness, a good sense of balance, and dexterity in stacking.

Publisher's summary:
99個のブロックからなる24個のピースを煙突状に積み上げる、立体パズルゲームです。
積み上げたピースの総ブロック数が多い人が勝ちとなります。
3ブロックピースが6個、4ブロックピースが10個、5ブロックピースが7個、
6ブロックピースが1個の合計24ピースなので、2～4人で公平にプレー出来ます。途中で倒したら負けです。
ブロックは微妙に歪なので、立体空間認識とバランス感覚と積み上げる手先の器用さが必要とされます。

It is the final stage of the 9th five-year plan and the oblast Odusad is still behind the expectations of the State Committee for Agriculture. After poor harvests in the prior years things finally look better and the comrades are looking forward to a bountiful harvest. The pumpkins are tall as tractor wheels and the flowers smell sweeter than ever. If only those pesky weeds will not spoil everything. The head of the Kolkhoz “Druzhba” has proclaimed the “Operation: Dandelion” in order to get rid of the weeds before they completely take over the farm.
All the comrades will have to work together if they want to save the harvest and fulfill the five-year plan. It will be challenging, but it can be done!

Operation: Dandelion is a light-weight cooperative family board game. 
It is played over 5 rounds. During each round additional weed tokens will be placed onto the Farm Tiles while the players will move around and clear the fields from these weeds. They have only a limited number of actions available and as the game progresses it becomes more and more challenging to keep the weeds under control. Farm Tiles can be overgrown, the oblast government might send additional support and the players can work overtime – but will it be enough?
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
You and your friends are tired of having all your great stuff go missing. You know it's that creepy house on the corner, and you have had enough. You are each going to gather a group of friends from the neighborhood and make a mad dash into the house to try to get your items back.

To start The Kids vs. The House, players determine how many items — as few as one and up to twenty — they need to recover from the house. This allows them to customize the length of their game.

You then flip the fist item card over and see how many kid team points you need to have to try to get the item back.

Each player starts with two cards from the Action/Kid deck. This deck contains kids from the neighborhood worth 1-5 points each that you add to your hand to try to build up a team with enough points to retrieve the item. There are also four other types of cards that cause things, do things, and help protect you from the creepy things that can happen in the house:


     Chaos cards always go off when drawn. Chaos she is a harsh mistress.
     Protection cards are used to prevent or deflect many of the negative events and actions in the game.
     Incident cards can be played on other players during your action phase to try to disrupt their chances of acquiring the item.
     Trap cards always go off when drawn, with the house apparently trying to defend its ill-gotten gains. Beware the house!


During your turn, you draw a card from the Action/Kid deck. If it doesn't instruct you to play it immediately (traps and chaos cards), then you can put it in your hand. You then have the option to perform an action. If you have a card that is playable (Incident and some Chaos), you can play this on the other players to help yourself or hurt their chances to win.

Turns proceed around the play area until someone has at least as many points in their hand as shown as the target number on the item. Once that minimum is met, they can choose to fold their hand and place it face down. They now cannot be targeted by any incidents, but can be affected by Chaos and Trap cards.

Once a person folds, everyone yet to play in that round draws one card, then everyone reveals their cards to see who has the most points and clams the item.

If this wasn't the final item, shuffle the cards and repeat with the next item. When the final item is claimed, the person with the most points at the end wins.

---
We currently are planning a Kickstarter launch in August 2016

Keyflower: Pig Shelter, a new village tile for the Keyflower, parallels the Sheep Shelter included in Keyflower: The Farmers. Pigs placed in the field formed by the tile may be allocated to the pig shelter. At the end of the game, each allocated pig scores the indicated number of points.
Nie Tej! is a traditional card game, very popular in Poznań, based on memory and bluff. It consists of 55 colorful cards and instruction with a mini-dictionary of Poznań dialect.

The Magic Shop is a game of magical, mercantile mayhem! Players each assume the role of a wizard or witch running their own magic shop, using ingredient cards to to concoct potions, talismans, scrolls, and magical weapons and armor with the help of your apprentices. Apprentices are sent into the market to gather magical ingredients and advertise to potential customers, or you may have some stay in the lab to help with the work of enchanting.

Ingredient cards are placed into your "cauldron" to produce a dice pool. This dice pool is then rolled in an attempt to complete enchantment lines (sets of specific dice facings). Players may opt to try and add more dice to their cauldrons to complete additional lines to make their items more valuable, but risk drawing magical accident cards that may make their apprentices vanish, curse the item, or turn some of your hard-earned gold into lead.

Cast spells to help you in your endeavors by having an apprentice visit the caster's tower, or hex your opponents to give you an edge in your quest to be the greatest magic shop owner in the city.

Try to anticipate your customers' needs when they arrive and sell them as many of your finished items as possible and pave the way to your victory! Victory Points are earned primarily by making the most gold selling your enchantments, but victory points may also be earned from completing all enchantment lines on a magic item. Points may be lost if your items end up cursed. The wizard or witch with the highest number of victory points at the end of eight turns is considered the winner.

Elemental Conflux is a game of dueling elements.

Each player summons elemental creatures to the battlefield to fight for dominance over five elemental nodes. Players create five stacks of creatures that will do battle, with the highest strength totals winning the battle.

Spells and certain powerful entities will make combat interesting and exciting. Summoners conjure additional creatures to their stack, Elemental Lords reduce the strength of the opposing elements' stacks, mighty dragons win no matter how many creatures oppose it (but can be thwarted by a wish from a Djinn) and cunning Wizards will very literally mix things up!

Cast spells to turn the tide of battle. Fires of destruction might rain down, eliminating cards from your opponent's stack, a divination will allow you to peak at your opponents stack before battle is resolved, or a mighty whirlwind might scatter creatures into each others stacks.

Battles commence, and the player with at least three out of five winning stacks takes control of one of five Elemental nodes. The player who takes control of at least three out of five elemental nodes wins the game.

Ladrones ("Thieves") is a party-game, created by David Vaquero, easy to learn and very funny to play. In this game, 2 to 5 players fight each other for being the best white collar thief.

The players will try to form a band of specialist in some criminal skills, like strong-box opening or server hacking, to perform the most risky robberys. They could draw cards from the deck or place the highest bid to form their crew.

When a player is ready to carry out a robbery, he will announce it and will replace its specialist cards by the respective color specialist dices. The player will have only 30 seconds to get the perfect combination in any dice by throwing them as many times as necessary.

After the assault, it doesn't matter if the player has had success or not, it's time to go out and run. The player will have another 30 seconds to throw the Driver's dice to left behind the police and scape.

The game will finish when all the plans of robbery run out or all the available diamonds have gone. The player who has the biggest diamond amount is the winner.

You’re tougher. Meaner. Better equipped. You’ve taken on a drekload of challenges the Sixth World has thrown at you, and you’ve survived. Barely, sometimes, but that’s enough. You’re walking with a certain confidence, maybe even a little strut. You’re ready for the next thing the streets are going to throw at you.
At least, you think you are. Now you’re going to find out.

High Caliber Ops is a massive expansion for the award-winning Shadowrun: Crossfire deck-building game. How massive? How does two new character roles, more than a dozen new Black Market cards, fifty new obstacles, and a large bundle of new Karma upgrades sound? On top of that, we’ve got five brand-new missions, and a set of basic cards with new art. Taken together, you have the tools for high-powered, high-risk, high-reward shadowruns. Add High Caliber Ops to your Crossfire game to see just how much danger you can take on!

Pani-High! is a card game in which players scramble for twelve Place cards. When the deck runs out, the player who obtained the most Place cards will be the winner.

Game play
Sum up the points of the Combat cards played by 2 players. Player with higher points will be the winner. 
The point of this game is that after the match with the Combat cards, the losing player's Combat cards will be discarded, and the other Combat cards return to the hand of the player who won the battle.

To win the battle, you must also place the large number against other player.

However, in such a case, the winner's deck of cards will be exposed and remembered by other players, which will be disadvantage for next rounds of the game.

boardgame: Monopoly: The View from the Shard
description:
A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from London's highest viewing platform.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Servcorp,  a multinational organisation that sells serviced office space, virtual office products and IT services.

Defeat your friends to win honor become Master of the DOJO! Choose a fighting style, build your character, and crush the competition!

Dojo is a fast paced, martial arts, card-drafting, character building contest of smashing your friends and trying to earn HONOR from the MASTERS of the DOJO. Choose a Fighting Style with unique abilities and advance down the Fire or Water path. 
The MASTERS of the DOJO watch over training, and if you please them, they may award you Honor. MASTERS may want you to build up your strength, and others prefer tricks!

Training Phase: Do Drills to improve your basic stats; Strength, Dexterity, and Chi. Study Techniques, which can be used multiple times during combat but expend your energy. You may also plan Tricks which are one time use and will likely catch your opponents off-guard.

Sparring Phase: Use the tricks, techniques, and drills that you've learned to take on your opponents in head-to-head or group combat. You can additionally use the abilities granted by your Fighting Style.

When Sparring begins, the combatants "Size-Up" each other, betting energy to see who will get the first strike.

Every opponent you knock out will earn you Honor and bring you one step closer to becoming MASTER OF THE DOJO!

description:
Nation is a great game of tactics and military strategies where the diplomacy and the ruse of each player take precedence over the chance and the stroke of luck.

The object of the game is to build 1 capital and 10 headquarters.
Each player starts with a Capital, represented by a hexagon with a star.

The game is played in turns:
1- Raising army
2- Moving armies
3- Fighting
4- Building headquarters

The Capital and headquarters are providing movement and force points (1 each to a limit of 6).

Movement points are used to move pieces (tanks, ships and fighters).

Force points are the force of a unit (including Capital and HQ) have to attack or defend. Units can be combined from adjacent hex, the force with the higher points total win, the defeated force must retreat according to specific rules. If a unit cannot retreat it is destroyed (Capital and HQ cannot retreat)

HQ are constructed if a player has at least one tank on a city hex.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from, an US company, known for favorite hand tools, power tools, tool storage products, specialty tools...

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from the organisation Gromit Unleashed, which raises funds for Wallace & Gromit’s Grand Appeal, the Bristol Children’s Hospital Charity. Bristol Children’s Hospita.l

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Nördlingen, a city in Germany.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Donauwörth, in Germany.

A children's game in which you have just finished school, and you have to go back home with the least possible distractions. The game teaches children some basic principles about life. The game play is just roll and move.

Crusher is a fantasy trading card game that can be played with various deck sizes:  A Skirmish game involves no deck, just a starting hand of 7 cards.  A Clash game uses 20-card decks.  A Battle game uses 40-card decks.  A War game uses 100-card decks.

Two players exchange attacks using character cards and booster cards played out of hand. The object is to eliminate all of your opponent's characters in hand while that opponent lacks the ability to draw more characters from the deck.

boardgame: Super Dungeon Explore: Claws of the Wyrm Warband
description: An expansion for Super Dungeon Explore: Forgotten King. This warband introduces the elven Dragon Blade Hero, who is pitted against the kobold Ser Sharpclaw and his band of kobolds and drakes.

boardgame: Super Dungeon Explore: Emerald Valley Warband
description: An expansion for Super Dungeon Explore: Forgotten King. This warband introduces the Silver Chevalier Hero, and the Glimmerwing dragon mini-boss who rules with its pig and mushroom minions.

boardgame: Super Dungeon Explore: Dungeons of Crystalia Tile Pack
description: An expansion for Super Dungeon Explore: Forgotten King. This tile pack offers 6 crystal-themed dungeon tiles and 12 slime models.

boardgame: Super Dungeon Explore: Von Drakk Ghost House Tile Pack
description:
Beware the things that go bump in the night! The Glauerdoom Moor is a haunted land filled with restless spirits, walking dead, and cackling witches. Von Drakk Ghost house lets you battle through the graveyards, spooky mansions, and villages of this cursed realm.

Von Drakk Ghost House expands your games of Game: Super Dungeon Explore with 12 unique dungeon layouts and new creeps that are compatible with your existing dungeon tiles, ensuring no game is ever the same.

Cards of Cthulhu a deck building game is centered around the Lovecraft genre which Cthulhu is famously known by.

In the game you will compete to build a better deck than the other investigators, defeat Ancient Ones, all the while not going insane.

Cards of Cthulhu is a deckbuilding game where you and up to 4 other players each strive to build the best deck, called your library and score as many victory points in order to win the game. Each player starts the game with the same library, 7 Strengths and 3 Fears.  Players shuffle the library and deal themselves 5 cards.  Each Strength is worth 1 power.  Fears are worth nothing.  The opening hand can have from 2 to 5 points of power.  The main deck of cards is then dealt 5 cards out to the Storyboard.  If any events happen, do what the card says, otherwise, check for card's with surprise effects (which usually add to your insanity).  Now the first player takes their turn. They can use their hand of cards to buy items from the storyboard based on their cost (and, in the case of Horrors, the insanity level to banish them).  Non-horror cards are placed in that player's graveyard, Horror cards are banished to the banished stack.  At the end of the turn, the player draws their five cards again, if the library lacks sufficient cards, shuffle the graveyard back in to the library and complete the rest of the five card draw.  The play is passed on to the next player, who then refills the storyboard from the main deck of any cards that were purchased or banished by the prior player.  Resolves and event and surprise effects and then their turn starts.

Insanity rules - In the game, it is very important to track just how insane you are!  Everyone starts out totally normal - with zero insanity - event, surprise effects and using certain arcane or artifacts will increase or decrease your insanity.  If you reach 13 insanity, you have crossed over and succumbed to insanity.  At this point, the only way to reduce your insanity is playing cards that reduce your insanity or discarding cards to reduce your insanity by the Victory Point value of the card.  You'll need to do this as soon as possible because if your reach 20, you've completely lost your mind and are a vassal for the Ancient Ones (or, in game terms, you just lost and are out of the game).

boardgame: Ground Pounders:  The STAR CORPS Card Game
description:
From the publisher's website:

Ground Pounders is a card game of STAR CORPS combat.  It simulates the battles of the Corporate-Colonial Wars of the late 22nd Century.  It simulates those battles on a scale much larger than the STAR CORPS miniatures rules normally allow, with most Force Cards representing a Company (which is 4-10 squads).

User description
The game is played with large Force Cards face up in front of each player. Each Force Card has both a Victory Point and Hit Point value. Once enough damage has been done to a card to equal or exceed it's Hit Points, the unit is eliminated and the player who eliminated it gains it's Victory Point value.

The players are also dealt a hand of smaller Action Cards which are not revealed when dealt. Action Cards are generally played to inflict damage to enemy units, although some activate other effects such as healing friendly units or allowing a player to draw additional cards.

Games may be played either until a player has reached a set Victory Point total, or until only one player has units remaining.
The PinBox 3000 is a cardboard, assembly-required, customizable, modular arcade "art-cade" system of tabletop pinball, and gravity-powered games.

Cards are placed center of table, stacked, face down.
Each card has four of these numbers on it (1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9)

First player calls a number, turns over top card, reads number.
If number called is one of four digits on card, player keeps it.
Otherwise it is placed faced down on bottom of main pile.
Next player does the same procedure, and so on.

When all cards are taken, he who has the most wins.

The Trials of Idavare is a card-based party game for 9–27 players. Join a fierce battle of truth and deception, a battle of conviction that threatens your very life. As your faction struggles to claim Idavare, well-chosen words are more precious than any card dealt, and a single misstep can mean certain death. Victory depends on your ability to debate, lie, reason, and – should the night call you – move silently...

In The Trials of Idavare, every player secretly receives a faction card and a skill card. At night, the evil, sneaky werewolves transform into beast form and take a villager's life. During the day, the villagers must determine who is a creature of the night and hang them before the last one of the innocent is inside a werewolf's belly. Besides their senses and wits, some villagers have useful skills at their disposal that might help in battle – just pray the skill is not used by a werewolf...
You say a phrase and the others have to guess what you are saying. Too simple? No, because you have to poke your tongue out or bite on your tongue  or ... during talking.

The goal of the game is to filled all the values on a pyramide using dice compbinations.

By throwing 3 die, choose 2 and add them up or multiply them up. Find the result on the pyramid and put a token on it. You must start by the bottom of the pyramid and climb up.

You get 3 wild values that you can use during a game to place a additional token on a vlaue of your choice (it must be a valid choice from the bottom to the top).

You get 3 chances to pick an unused dice from an opponent during the game.

boardgame: Saban's Adventures of Oliver Twist: Das große Spiel
description:
You roll a dice, select a direction and move this number of fields. You try to reach one of the adventure fields, which allows you to challenge one of your opponents. The winner gets one chip. If you go on a field, on which an other player is standing, you can steel a  chip from them.
The first, who has 7 chips wins.

boardgame: Gute Zeiten Schlechte Zeiten: Das Spiel
description: You roll a  dice and move your character as many fields on the board. The field, you reach tells you which kind of challange eg drawing, acting, you have to fulfill. The first, who has 15 points wins.

This is a game for 2 players.
Each Player has pawns in 4 Colors (1 Player large pawns the other small).
Each player has start fields for his pawns on one of the sides of the board. The players start regions are on the opponent sides of the board.
All fields are connected with horizontal and vertical lines.
The fields are colored (1 of the 4 colors of the pawns) or  black.
On Players turn he can move 1 pawn in one direction as far as he wants and no other pawn is on the way.
The pawn can be moved on the position of an opponent pawn. This opponent pawn is out of the game.
Independent of this if a pawn is now on a colored field the opponent is only allowed to move the pawn with this Color in his next turn. If the player has choosen a black field the opponent may choose any of his pawns.
The player who cannot move the allowed pawn in his turn looses.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Mansons, a company in New Zealand.

Each player gets a secrect card with 4 characters, which he has to move to his target fields in his corner of the board. The first player fulfilling this wins.
At the beginning the 16 pawns are placed in the corners and the 9 caps, which can hide the pawns,  are placed in the middle of the board. Each turn you move one of the pawns to an adjacent field or jump if it is occupied and additional move one of the caps.

The game is based on the German TV series "Gute Zeiten Schlechte Zeiten".
The whole game board has square fields like a chess board.
There are additional drawn walls which separate parts of the boards from each other (rooms). The lines have spaces where the rooms are linked (doors).
There are 20 differnt pawns representing each a different character of the TV series.
At the begin of the game, each player gets 2 cards of the character stock, showing each 1 character/pawn and a room Card showing 1 room.
The player who is first to get his 2 character in the room of his room card wins.
On players turn he rolls 2 dice and moves 1 character for each of the die the number shown. Movement is allowed horizontal, vertical and diagonal also with changing while moving. Moving through walls is not allowed.
One of the oppenents (the fasted) may say which charcter is owned by the actual user. If this is right the Player has to show this character card and has only 1 die and 1 move in his next turns.
If the opponent is wrong, he has to show one of his character cards with the same effect. If a player has to Show his second card, he looses and is out of the game.
The game can also end if only 1 player is in the game. This player has won the game.

The players have time to memory the items on the panels, then the cards are turned facedown.
The topmost card of the stock is taken and shown to all players. The first player, who says which hidden item is laying on the panel of the same color as the card drawn, gets the memorized card and the new card is put on the panel facedown.

boardgame: Sherlock Holmes Consulting Detective: Murder Behind Closed Doors
description:
It is a cold winter day when Sherlock Holmes receives Inspector Gregory into his office in Baker Street. The inspector shudders a few seconds from the change in temperature, rubs his gloves, then finally takes off his coat and accepts an invitation to sit down.

"A frosty morning, eh, Gregory? I think it takes a rather unusual murder to force you to come see me with this cold."

"Your deduction is sadly apt, dear Holmes. They killed Dr. William Young this morning, at eight, as he opened the clinic where he worked."

So begins "Murder Behind Closed Door," a single extra case expansion originally written by a fan in Italian, and later published by Asterion Press as an Asterion Gaming Days 2015 event promo for Sherlock Holmes Consulting Detective.

Available for download on the Asmodee Italia website.

Translated into English by BGG Forum members and freely available in the Files section.

description:
ファイアエムブレム０（サイファー） ("Fire Emblem 0 (Cipher)") is a collectible card game based on the Fire Emblem video games. Currently, it is being released in Japan.

Both players choose one main character at the start of the game. All the cards in the game are character cards. Each character has a base attack strenght, as well as support strength (in addition to certain other powers and abilities). Each player has five 'orbs' ('life' represented by cards'), which the other player tries to get down to zero by attacking the main character.

There's five phases in a game.
1) Starting Phase
Untapping tapped characters, and drawing 1 card.
2) The Bond Phase
Playing 1 card into the ´bond´ area. Each card has a value (top left). The value of cards in the 'bond' area determines the value of the characters you can put into play.
3) The Sortie Phase
Play characters, or level up certain characters. The characters you can play depends on their card value and the value of cards in your ´bond´ area.
4) The Action Phase
Move your characters into the opponent's area; attack your opponent's characters; or use a skll.
5) The Final Phase
Skills that say 'during this turn' end, and the turn goes to the opposing player.

The goal of the game is to defeat your opponent's main character by destroying his 'orbs.'

The three Legendary cities modules each include 6 new heroes and wonders (some with a figurine and counters), 3 objectives and 15 events.
Troy emphasizes the military aspect of the game and Syracuse the economic one. Jerusalem introduces a divine aspect.

These first 4 modules can be combined in any way.

Finally, the Punic wars extends also the player count with a scenario for 2 players. It adds a Barbarian faction (a special set with 3 triremes, 3 fortresses, and 8 Barbarians hordes) which will be a neutral faction controlled by both players in Rome-Carthage war.

description:
A pick-up and delivery game based on Atlantic Trade in the 17th Century. The object of the game is to ship cargo from port to port in exchange for victory points; the first to 25 victory points wins.

During your turn you draw 2 cards from the deck, move each of your ships a maximum of 5 spaces, and play any number of cards. You can also load or unload cargo at port. When the turn ends battles are resolved with a roll of the dice.

There are many different types of cards in the deck to keep the game fun and exciting. Wind cards allow you to increase ship movement. Battle cards increase your odds in battle, or can be used to escape battle altogether. There are also wild cards which allow you to adjust the value of the cargo, or summon pirates to attack an opponent.

boardgame: Putin's War: Reclaiming the Soviet Empire in Eastern Europe
description:
The main idea is, NATO devolves toward operational ineffectiveness and, on that basis, old Vladimir decides to try for the big solution to get back all the former Soviet Union's western borderlands.

In designing the game (already done despite the long wait for its publication), I came up with a new system that emphasizes the following perspective: today's First World militaries are part way through a systemic transformation broadly comparable to the one they went through during the 1930s.

The issue in the 30s was how to integrate mechanization into what otherwise still remained foot-slogging, horse-drawn armies. The debate centered around how much horse cavalry to keep mixed in with the tanks, trucks and other combat vehicles. The idea was the large mass of unmotorized units would form a "base of maneuver" from which the relatively more scarce -- but elite -- moto-mech units would operate.

Of course, by the war's midpoint all that had been settled. The question had come to center on determining the optimal mix of tracked and wheeled vehicles, etc.

Today, we're witnessing a similar transformation in that the former moto-mech elite has, in effect, taken on the role of the previous era's foot-sloggers and horse cavalry: they're now the new "base of maneuver" element, holding the line while, evolving atop and past them, is the new elite: the special forces, which are themselves increasingly robot-equipped.

Given that the cost and social advantages involved in deploying small elites rather than the mass mechanized armies of the previous era (the draft isn't going to come back), I see us now at about the 1938-equivalent in terms of the new transition.

Aside from that, another characteristic is more and more of the First World's armies combat experience is increasingly contained within the personnel of the SoF. That, of course, further enhances their relative combat power.

To smoothly model all that, I've come up a system I've titled the "Big And Dumb Armies Super System" (BADASS). I'll be using it again.

This design will be appearing in Modern War issue number 29 (May-June 2017).

Central City: Heroes is a cooperative board game for 1-4 players. Each player takes on the role of a unique Superhero with their own unique powers. Players work together to battle minions, complete missions, recruit other heroes, and unlock their upgrade cards. This will all lead up to the final showdown with the Superhero's Nemesis. The Players must complete five missions and defeat the Nemesis in order to win the game. Heroes will accomplish these things by moving around and exploring Central City.

The board consists of 16 interchangeable location hex tiles that make up Central City. Every time you play the city itself will change.

Players will begin by creating their characters. To do so they will get a Superhero Player Board, a Super Power Card and a Secret Identity Card. Combining the Hero Board with the Super Power Card will create the Player's Hero Persona. With 6 Player Characters and 12 Super Power Cards, there are 72 different Hero Personas in the Central City: Heroes Core Set. The Players will also receive their own Secret Identity Card which the game will be trying to force players to reveal.

Are you ready to become a guardian of Central City?

Each round is filled with surprising and outrageous comparisons from a wide range of people, places, things and events. Add to your Apples to Apples Party Box or play as a standalone game. Greatest of All Mountains is a competitive Mountain building card game for 2-5 players that can be played in 45-90 minutes. The game currently includes nearly 160 cards.

Each player represents a mountain which is vying for the title of Greatest of All Mountains. To achieve this, players must acquire the most victory points in the three phases of the game, using various abilities and events to gain an advantage over the other players.

In hopes of achieving the high honor of being named Greatest of All Mountains, each player must first form the terrain of their mountain. By adding slopes, lakes, cliffs and more to their mountain they craft the foundation of their strategy. Players may also compete to gain the all or nothing "Tallest Mountain" award for additional VP.

Next, players must build facilities on their terrain. This is where strategy becomes very important as the facilities built greatly effect how the next phase will play out. Build the best facilities on the faces of your mountain or choose to build sub-par facilities on your opponent's mountains.

Finally, each player must attract tourists to their mountain. The goal is to get tourists to your mountain's "Safe Zone" where they will be scored at the end of the game. Tourists frequently change hands, so players must employ a variety of strategies and abilities to make sure their tourists are safe from the other players.

Turnpike is the game of highway mayhem. Life on the road means relying on quick wits, mad skills, and a bit of luck to get you there in one piece. Turnpike captures the rolling shark tank that is the modern highway system where complete strangers shamelessly screw each other over in an infuriating race that no one really wins — until now.

1. Players roll dice to move. The play actions are inter-weavable and open. Players may do the following in any order: 

     move their car
     play cards
     move a non-player car 
     add or subtract a car
     generally wreak ruin upon the road 


3. Every turn, players move non-player cars out of their way and into the path of opponents.

4. The cards give players movement bonuses, allow control over road traffic, and let them mess with other players.

5. The card types are: DRIVER, TRAFFIC, AGGRESSIVE, INSTANT.

6. The winner is the player to push through the traffic jam and get to the EXIT first.

description:
The latest trick up the Necromancers' sleeves is a manipulation of the zombie infection that allowed skilled archers to retain their deadly accuracy in zombie form. The experiment rotted their flesh away, but as long they can still hold a bow and arrow, they will serve their sinister purpose to the bitter end.

Deadeye Walkers function like standard Walkers for most game purposes. Same place in the targeting priority order, 1 action per activation, 1 Damage to kill, 1 XP per kill. Of course, the huge difference comes when they attack with their fearsome bows!

Deadeye Walkers Attack whenever they activate while having a Line Of Sight on a Survivor (or more) at Range 0-3. Then, they perform a standard Zombie Attack on the designated Zone. If several Zones are eligible, they choose their target according to these priorities:

1- The Zone with the most Survivors.
    2- The noisiest Zone.
    3- If all eligible Zones have the same amount of Survivors and Noise, the players choose.

Walking the streets will never be safe while Deadeye Walkers roam about. Get inside, or get away, or an arrow to your knee might put an end to your adventuring days.

The Deadeye Walkers box comes with 21 Deadeye Walker miniatures and 6 spawn cards to add these skeletal zombie archers to your game! The Deadeye Walkers come in three different sculpts.
