From 2017 to 2023 there was an annual design contest, but due to Bezzy life challenges this has been temporarily suspended.

Additional rules are on the website.

The Dread began three years ago with the rise of the bone legions in the south. Since then it has spread across the known world, a waking nightmare that takes hundreds of horrible forms. Ghosts howl in the night. Skinchangers lurk in the wilds. Fallen soldiers rise to slaughter the living. Entire cities fall to a Chant that turns all who hear it into mindless killers. We don’t know why this is happening. We don’t know how to stop it. All that we know with certainty is that we are fighting a war and we are losing. Over a third of the Empire has been lost to the Dread, and each day brings new horrors. In this dark time we have one hope: Phoenixes are returning. Every citizen knows tales of the Phoenixes, champions who can face death and return stronger than before. In the centuries following our brutal civil war the Phoenixes have become legends… and now you are one of them.

Phoenix: Dawn Command is a card-based roleplaying game with a unique approach to character death. In Phoenix, players assume the role of supernaturally empowered champions bound together to defend a fantasy world under attack by threats that no mortal could withstand. These heroes face harsh odds every mission, and many won’t survive. But death isn’t the end of your character’s story; it’s the start of the next chapter.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Pixel Tactics: Game Caster / Eric Summerer & Army of One / Sam Healey
description: A set of two promo cards featuring podcasters Eric Summerer and Sam Healey. These were given out in the 2015 Dice Tower Kickstarter Campaign.

Skirmish game set now during Second World War, with a very specific 1/48 scale collection of miniatures, fast and fluid game mechanics and excellent tactical level.

Rules, condensed in only two pages, allow to start playing with only three miniatures per side in a very limited battle table.

In the Battleboxes (Starter Set) there are three metal miniatures of a faction to be painted,  cardboard tokens and three scenic elements to be cut out, a 5 inch ruler to be able to measure during the game, and finally cards to manage characters.

In addition, there are already new characters with their own specific card available in order to expand the factions of the battleboxes.

It’s a game that combines both wargame as well as boardgame elements and that can be appreciated by a wide audience thanks to its simple dynamics but demanding skills.

Oblik is an abstract strategy game of pattern creation and set collection. Each turn, you place a tile next to one already on the playing surface, forming clusters of your player color in an attempt to create specific scoring patterns, while at the same time collecting sets of matched or mismatched shapes and colors.

BuildaBeast is a card game where you get to create cool creatures. Your goal is to craft powerful beasts by buying head, body, and tail cards. But beware, if you play too many body cards without a tail, you’ll bust! Players buy cards and build beasts until the supply of new cards is exhausted. Then the players tally the cards they have gained to see who has the most victory points.

Players team together to survive the perils of the "Sands of Infinity" arena. In this epic struggle of real-time action, players use magic, mechanical weapons, and sheer brute force to beat the opposing team and win the day.

Sands of Infinity is modular and scenario-based, allowing for infinite replays. Varying classes, items and treasure to be discovered, and mysteries to be solved are all part of any game. Games are quick, and brutal — with a maximum game time of twenty minutes. The goal of each scenario varies, but is similar to a League of Legends style MOBA; thus Capture the Flag, Base Control, Elimination, and many other goals are possible.

Woof is a danish board game for the whole family... and their dog.

Woof contains 200 different questions and exercises in different degrees of difficulty, so everybody will be able to play, even the dog. An exercise could be having to stand still on all fours for 20 seconds or sit with a treat on the nose for 2 seconds.

If you answer questions correctly or complete an exercise you gain a (cardboard) treat, but if you fail you gain a (cardboard) dog poo. If you collect 4 dog poo tokens you are out of the game.

The Genius: Horror Race is essentially a racing game. There are five "racers": Vampire, Frozen Corpse, Nine-Tailed Fox, Mummy, and Zombie. Each racer will compete on a track of twelve spaces.

At the beginning of the game, each player is dealt with five "racer" tokens, one for each kind of racer. Among them, the player secretly chooses two and discards the rest. All the discarded tokens form a central pool which will be used for moving the racer.

Each player, turn by turn, takes three tokens out of the pool and place those tokens on one of the three spots on the board: "Move 1," "Move 2," and "Move 4." For instance, a player took two Mummy tokens and one Vampire token from the pool, and places a Mummy token on the "Move 2" spot and the remainder on the "Move 4" spot.

After all the players have taken three tokens out of the pool and placed them on the board, the racer whose tokens wins the untied majority in each spot will move accordingly. For example, if there are 2 Vampire tokens, 1 Nine-Tailed Fox token, 3 Mummy tokens, and 3 Zombie tokens on the "Move 1" spot, only the Vampire will move 1 space.

After a round, all used tokens are taken back to the pool and the next round proceeds in the same manner. The game ends when a racer passes the finish line. Whoever bet for the first racer is awarded 3 points, second, 2 points, and the third, 1 point. The player with the most points wins the game.

KONTER ("Counter") is a dice-handball game with tactics, fast pace and team spirit – for 2 dice-handball game players, 10 years and up.

The sport of (European) handball is characterized by quick moves and a lot of goals. KONTER has been inspired by this; with its specific game course, it creates a similar feel of play.


     On his turn, the active player rolls two dice at the same time – for himself and for his opponent (passive player). Now the active player chooses one die for the passive player and puts the die on its tray. If he uses his Tactics cards, the active player may change the number rolled on his die.
     After that, he immediately carries out an action; e.g., he can substitute a playing figure with a "3" and strengthen his own defense, or he can try to start an offense to get a chance of throwing a goal. Then, with his die, the passive player has a consequent reaction, e.g., guarding opposing offense spaces with a "1," or fine-tuning his own tactics.
     Both players try to use their "all-rounder" (a playing figure with special abilities) tactically and cleverly; there are different kinds of throws, fouls with 2-minute penalties, yellow cards and just one "Super Glove"; for reinforcement, the "Team Spirit" comes into play…


The player who has "thrown" the most goals by the end of the game wins. And who else does? Of course, the player with the best tactics, the fastest pace, and with the Team Spirit as the eighth player on his side.

boardgame: Salamis ad Actium: Naval Wargames Rules for Ancient Times 2000 BC to 900 AD
description:
From the back of the rulebook:

With these rules you can take command of the fleets of antiquity with oared galleys roaming the Mediterranean, maintaining control of your Empire and keeping the Pirates in check. In those days warfare was up close and personal. The wars between Greece and Persia, Rome and Carthage, The Roman Civilk War, all are covered. You can ram, carry out oar rakes, board the enemy, fire archers and ballistae, hurl rocks from stone throwers.
An extensive list of ships is provided with their advantages and disadvantages. We have incorporated our usual command system which allows for both good and bad commanders, and which has an effect on the flow of the action. The turn is of variable length and certain actions will only be permitted during an Intermediate Phase which happens at random.
A simple campaign system is provided as a basis for those with Imperial ambitions
40 pages
Recommended scale 1/1200
Complexity 6/10
Level of detail 6/10
Avg game length 2-3 hours
Dice required D10, D6
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
There are 7 different fashions for 2, 3 or 4 players to play the game. Each player has up to 24 pawns, which can be moved in every direction or jump over other pawns. In some games opponent pawns can be taken by jumping over them, as long as they are not in neutral territory (starting positions and center spaces). The goal is to get ones pawns to the starting positions of the opponent or on the center spaces of the board.

The game was published before 1908.

The Emperor watches over his land, using his elemental magic to ensure the safety and prosperity of his subjects. With the aid of his most loyal companion, a Dragon, he has kept his borders secure for years. In return, the spirit of respect and gratitude from his subjects lends him power and helps him focus on improving his magical skills. One day, however, the Emperor senses an ancient Evil bubbling up from beneath a long-forgotten ruin far to the north. The Evil begins to advance toward the Palace, its insatiable appetite for destruction drawing it to the center of the Emperor’s power. As it approaches, it blights the land with Curses, sapping the Emperor’s resources.

In this asymmetric cooperative card game for two players, one player is the Dragon, who collects resources from a hex map of the empire, while the second player is the Emperor, who uses the gathered resources to power spells and create Magic Items to combat the advancing enemy. The object of the game is to acquire the three Magic Items before Evil reaches the Palace or Curses cover too much of the land.

Boon is a redesign of the German card game, Sheepshead (which is the Americanized version of Schafkopf). Its game mechanics are the same as many of the house rules in Sheepshead. But rather than being played with a standard deck of cards, Boon uses a custom deck and renames elements of the game to make it easier to learn.

The objective is earn the most victory cards possible, by winning tricks and rounds. A player may choose to take the "Boon" pile of cards to help his hand. However in doing so, all other players are allied against this player. A test of strategy ensues as players decide the best card to play on each turn.

For full instructions, please see the game's official website, http://boonthegame.com/.

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: Battleground Set – Terrain Pack
description:
Extend the battlefields of Normandy with these 6 new two-sided terrain tiles.

With:

     Rivers
     Corn fields that obscure line of sight
     Swamps that slow troops down
     Forest
     ...
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

description:
Kamasutra: The Game of Love was originally published as 8 pages of rules insert between pages 92 and 93 of Lee Siegel's book Love in a Dead Language. It is a competitive two-player game played on a board easily customized from a standard Pachisi board. In addition to the board and the text of the Kamasutra itself, the game requires the following components:


     6 pawns of three sizes and two colors
     64 unique cards divided into eight suits (each card representing a sexual technique) 
     1 pair of dice


One player has three male pawns, the other has three female ones. The goal of the game is to move these from their home circle to the central Kamaloka ("World of Eternal Pleasure"). To make the final approach to the Kamaloka requires that each pawn first collect one card in each of the 8 suits of the Kamakala deck. Cards may usually be acquired when a pawn is moved into a space containing a pawn of the opposite sex. Special spaces provide other conditions for acquisition, loss, or trading of cards.

The board design includes some double-sized spaces that provide a choice of paths, allowing players to adjust the effects of dice rolls for movement. Also, a pawn may move clockwise or counter-clockwise, but only one of the two directions on any given turn.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Overview and goal:
OGO is a game for two players played on a hexagonal arrangement of hexagonal cells. You will need 5 clear rings and 31 black and 31 white pieces.

The goal is to make a four in a row in your color before placing your third ring.

Play:
One player plays black the other white. The board starts out empty. Black moves first, turns alternate.

Place a piece onto an empty cell on the board. When a straight line of three pieces is made; place a ring over one of the three pieces. Pieces with rings are neutral and are not considered when making a four in a row.

Expansion for X-Bugs/Micro Mutants.

Contains:

     Foam sheet with locations for bases (for 2-player games).
     20 terrain tokens with special powers.
     Rulebook

The Party is a verbal-social game for two or more players. One player describes the plot of either a book, movie, or TV show (telling the group which of those it is) but converts everything into fantasy roleplaying scenarios and characters. For example:

"The Party consists of two Dragonriders, the best Dragonriders in their clan, who must attend a school for elite Dragonriders where the competition is fierce, and one of them ends up dying in a dragon flying accident." (Top Gun)

Play passes to the first correct guesser.

Crave is a social psychology game, with only verbal components.

Crave is played by creating the need of some tangible object in another Crave-player's mind, enough so that they physically leave the current room and travel to some destination to obtain said object. They cannot be tasked with retrieval and cannot be forced to go. The idea is that you can talk so glowingly, so descriptively, so persuasively about some item (food, beverage, clothing, knick-knack, etc) that it would induce someone else to go get it.

Get someone to do this, and you won "a Crave".

Crime Lords of Knight City is a cooperative card game in which players are detectives trying to put away a notorious crime lord. You'll do this by solving enough crimes to build a solid case against the crime lord. If too many cases go cold, the crime lord wins and takes over the city.

The basic steps in the game include drawing Badge Cards, using Assets and Solving Crimes.

The Badge cards feature icons that match icons on the crimes. Each turn, you are able to play one of your Badge Cards onto one, or more, of the crimes in your docket. If you are able to match all the icons on a crime, it is solved.

As the game progresses, more and more cases will be opened. If you ever have more than 3 crimes in your docket, you must allow one of those crimes to go cold. If too many crimes go cold, the crime lord wins and the players lose the game.

To win the game, players must solve and close enough cases to build a case against the Crime Lord. Each Crime Lord has its own special ability and a varying number of points required to win and/or lose the game. So, each time you play, the difficulty and challenges will change.

Troquons! is a game of bartering. What the name doesn't tell you is that it allows you create mean, stupid, brilliant or dumb maxims and proverbs. Thanks to Troquons! you'll get morally subtle gifts, capable of summarizing life in several strong images. But watch yourself, you risk every moment looking like an absolute idiot. Deeply cerebral or a medical mystery, such is the unpredictable journey to the land of clowning around!

Each player receives 3 cards: subject + verb + moral. From the beginning, you have a proverb in hand.

The barter phase begins: you can swap one or more cards as many times as you want between players and/or only once with the pile! Thus, your proverb may become "the artist in you + is a slobbering Mmnster + speaks him gently in his ear"

Each player reads their proverb and must reveal their viewpoint  if another player asks "but what you meant by that?". Luck and your barter strategy, combined with your imagination, will make you defend or justify the indefensible "maxims" to make them... brilliant!

The voting phase closes the round: every player submits  a secret ballot of 1 card of "you're awesome" to the author of the best proverb and 1 card of "let's barter!" to the one who deserves it! If you receive the Awesome card, it's worth 1 point (i.e. 1 blue cube), while a "let's barter" card is just a friendly message that expresses condolences and to try again.

Troquons! is a very simple and addictive game that will make you appear sometimes as a great and gifted philosopher gifted of profound reflexion and decisive for the future of mankind, and sometimes as a schemer trying desperately to justify a proverb that is worth not a cent.

Discover 312 new cards where it's best to let other players answer!

Expand your horizons thanks to 3 new categories: Career and Ambitions, Family/Friends/Love and Psychoanalysis!

Also, deepen the 3 current theme:
Sex, Daily Life, Philosophy & Progress

A true parody of classic quizzes, Questions de merde 2  invites new players find their best answers

Second degrees, anecdotes, relevations... the options are endless options to think of and win!

Méditations foireuses is the game that ends well when it ends badly.

The principle is accessible to everyone: you must end sentences, which you are given at the start of the game, in the most borked way possible!
All of this is in a spirit of pure parody of proverbs, sayings and other more or less deep thoughts...
 An example dedicated to lovers of le droit de perdre (the publisher): "nothing is used to accept defeat... except when we win for nothing."
 4 rules are proposed with the game: 

     respect nothing 
     play with a single card in hand 
     takes time to guess which player wrote each maxim 
     invent phrases by starting with the end!



In short, you find a original, crazy game, ideal for  raucous evenings and/or a surprising gift. It fits into your pocket and adapts to all opportunities! Méditations Foireuses contains 55 cards and comes in its cardboard case.

This promo package contains:

2 Chog Frappe Cards
5 Green Variant Chogs

The Chog Frappe card makes all players discard all chogs. The green Chogs are simply more Chogs to add to the game.

Great drawing!

Who will find the word that fits?

5 drawings (all 5 at the same time) appear on the coloured backs of the cards. On the front sides, a number of squares and a number are present.

A card is turned over: the first to say something directly related to the picture takes the round. This word must consist of a certain number of syllables (number of squares on the card) or to a number of letters (numbers on the card). The fastest wins as many cards as squares.

At the end of the game, the player who has the most cards wins!

An intense, multiplayer electronic puzzle game based on visual perception and pattern recognition. The objective of the game is to be the first player to play all of the pieces in your hand correctly according to the rules of the puzzle. The game provides instant feedback so there is no debate as to whether a move is correct or incorrect.

Zebulon is completely different from the 1997 game from Abacuspiele.

Cards have words written in a circle. The card is flipped up in the middle of the players... the first who reads the word aloud wins!

Each player attempts to obtain the plutonium crystal and bring it back to their own planet.

This is a simple roll-and-move game with the ability to draw cards and play them in order to move further, be protected from attacks, take wormholes, etc.

Animals parade before your eyes! Who can remember them the best?

Memory game involving animals. Each player is given an Answer card. The game will ask you questions and this card will allow you to answer.

Welcome to the world of Artificer! Artificer is a deck-building board game with a twist. You must buy (and forge) new cards to put into your deck, but you must also buy ingredients to make artifacts from recipes. Once you have made an artifact, it remains in play and you may use its special ability for the rest of the game! You earn prestige for making artifacts and for buying titles once you have met their requirements.

Imagine that you are players in a democratic country.

Each player takes a voting card. The oldest player is the first President. They take the President card and chooses their Minister by placing the Minister card in front of the player of their choice. The same player may be Minister more than once during the game. The other players act as the People.

The deck of cards is placed in the middle of the player, 4 are revealed and the cards closest to the deck is the most valuable, descending in value to the 4th card.

At the top of the deck, there's a symbol at the corners of the card, corresponding with one of the phrases on the President card. The President reads the cards to all players, for example: "what would I like to bring to the desert with me?". The other players place their Voting cards (facedown) on each one of the 4 faceup objects.

The Voting Cards are then turned over to reveal the votes. Players score by correctly guessing which card the President chose. The Minister wins a point if they also chose the same card as the President. Each player who is not the Minister, can score an additional point if they guessed the Minister's card as well (if they both chose the same card and you voted for it, you get 2 points! If the Minister and President each chose a separate card, you only win a point if you guessed one or the other). The President scores up to 3 points, 1 point per non-Minister player that guesses his card.

At the end of the round, the 4 faceup cards are placed at the bottom of the deck. The new President is the player sitting to the left of the current President. The game is played for as many rounds as each player is President twice (in a 3 or 4 player game) or each player acts as the President once (5 or 6 player game). The person with the most points wins! In case of a tie, both (or several) players share the victory!

Stay linked and on track as you twist and criss-cross hands and feet in this fun and physical game for all ages.

Jungoeira comprises eighteen foot-pads and eighteen hand-pads, and a deck of ‘track’ cards all packed in a handy rucksack. Players lay out a track of hands and feet as shown on one of the cards (two tracks if you are playing in two teams). Then players race along the track placing a foot on a foot-pad and a hand on a hand-pad, all the while staying linked to the person in front and behind – often with hilarious results.

Jungoeira, published early this year and already a hit in Scandinavia, was devised by Oxford Games’ very own Frederica Scott Vollrath. So we are delighted to be able to introduce this fun-filled party game to the UK as an Oxford Games exclusive.

Imagine a high quality old-school style wooden table game that creates a 4 sided hockey-soccer field with pinball style paddles for each player. This 4 player family/party game is fast. Not just in the speed of play, but in the rapid fluctuations between triumph and anguish that each player endures as they quickly lose or gain Marbles. Be the first player to get rid of your marbles to win the game!
Our “Secret Sauce” is the spontaneous laughter that always results from Blowing on the Marble. For example, while the main action in the game is whacking the marble with the pinball paddles towards another opponent’s goal, the rules allow players to assist or prevent the marble from scoring by blowing on it. So, when players are blowing at the marble against each other, it ALWAYS creates laughter.

A 32 card pack is used, consisting of A, K, Q, J, 10, 9, 8, 7 in each of the four suits, but when playing the game the four jacks are regarded as forming a separate trump suit. So there are effectively five suits: jacks (trumps), clubs, spades, hearts, diamonds.

The ranking of the trumps from high to low is: jack of clubs, jack of spades, jack of Hearts, jack of Diamonds.

In the four non-trump suits the cards rank from high to low A, 10, K, Q, 9, 8, 7.

Certain cards carry penalty points, and the object of the game is to avoid taking tricks containing those cards. First, all the cards carry their normal penalty point values as in Ramsch:

Jacks    2
Aces    11
Tens    10
Kings    4
Queens    3

Taking queens in tricks has an additional effect. The club, spade and heart queens carry extra special penalties which swamp the normal card points above, but these can be neutralised by taking the diamond queen:

Queen of clubs    300 points
Queen of spades    200 points
Queen of hearts    100 points
Queen of diamonds cancels all special queen penalties for the player who wins it in a trick

Nines, eights and sevens are neutral - the carry no penalty and have no special effect.

The player to dealer's left leads. Players must follow suit if they can. A player who has no card of the suit led can play any card.

Jacks count as belonging to the trump suit, not as members of the suits printed on them. This means that if a jack is led the other players must follow with a jack if they can.

[Rules paraphrased from Pagat.com entry.]

Cover the city in graffiti and earn respect of your rivals by creating the most original words. But be careful! Only the graffiti left at the end of the game will allow you to earn points. Don't hesitate to cover the creations of your opponents, while always thinking to protect your own!

Each player has a character card and corresponding tokens (by colour) that are used to tag which graffiti was done by them. The game is played in 6 rounds, with 2 phases per round. The Night phased is when you do the actual graffiti and the Day phase is when players explain their creations and voting takes place. Each player starts with a hand of 7 cards. There are 2 types of cards in play, brick walls and Subway cars (BW and SC for the rest of this explanation). Each player then secretly constructs a word (no, this is not scrabble, you can have fictional words. Acronyms may be constructed, but with a limit of 3 vowels or 3 consonants in a row. You must explain what each of the letters means), as long as the words are made up entirely or BW or SC cards.  No mixing is permitted! The words must be pronounceable (no smattering of all consonants or all vowels). Add your artist tag (worth the corresponding number of points hidden on the underside) to your creation and the Night phase is complete.

During the day phase, you reveal your word to other players and then explain why your creation is the best of all (in a counter-clockwise manner). Once you've explained your reasoning as to why your creation is the best, voting takes places. Simultaneously, each player points to the creation they find the best and places their tag on the appropriate creation. If no one votes for your creation, you do not turn your tag over and receive no points for this round. Each draws up to their 7 card hand limit and the Night phase recommences.

Aside from SC and BW cards, there specialty cards within the game:

Police Officer cards (that you may have drawn from the deck) protect your creation from being painted over by only one person. The remaining players are free to attack your design.

Bag cards allow you to draw an additional card at the end of the Day phase.

Eraser cards steals the opponent's card of your choice, that is part of a creation on the table.

Ladder cards permit you to cover a second creation of someone (instead of the typical creation that was just played).

Covering others' creations (possible as of the second Night phase) requires that you use the same card type + 1 (4 letter BW creation must be covered by 5 BW cards).

The aforementioned Special cards must be used BEFORE placing the current Day rounds creation in your personal player area.

The rounds continue until the sixth round has past.

Endgame scoring is calculated by counting the cards of each word that has not been painted over by other players multiplied by the number indicated on the tag placed on he word. Example: 7 letter word X 3 point tag = 21 points for that word alone.

The player with the most points wins!

The 122 letters and the logo of the game were designed by hand by 5 'Street Artists' internationally recognized for their great talent: Cope2, Indie184, Nasty, bead, RosyOne. Their characters and sets were beautifully illustrated by Regis Torres.

boardgame: A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic – Smuggler's Den Game Supplement
description:
Monsters aren’t the only thing to lurk in the dimly-lit alleys and dark places of the world. Smugglers, cutthroats, and thieves also skulk in the shadows of the night, moving crates of stolen goods and selling secrets…for a price!

This 15 card Game Supplement for the Dark Gothic Deck Building Game adds new Gear, Allies, Minions, and more, some utilizing the new Roaming mechanic first introduced in the Colonial Horror Expansion. It also includes a new set of Dusty Crate cards, collected for cheap, with a random pull to see what’s inside! You never know what you might find when cracking open a Dusty Crate found in a Smuggler’s Den! One thing is for certain, it’s a lot of fun finding out!
family: Game: A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic

description: This is a Boss expansion for Super Dungeon Explore. It contains one Goro dungeon boss, 6 Giri creeps with additional treasure and explore cards.

boardgame: Super Dungeon Explore: Deeproot Wolf Rider
description: This is an additional, large size hero for Super Dungeon Explore. It includes a miniature and the hero card needed to add it to the game.

boardgame: Super Dungeon Explore: Stilt Town Zombies Warband
description: This is an expansion for Super Dungeon Explore that adds the Survivor Marie-Claude hero and Mr. Bitey Zombie Bunny pet along with Gruesome George mini-boss, 2 spawning points, and 11 other enemies with associated cards.

Tabbybrook Mages harness water in all its forms, using it to wash away their wounds, and drive their enemies back. When pressed they can even unleash frozen blasts of ice or scalding streams that burn the flesh with all the intensity of fire.

Contents

     Tabbybrook Mage Assembled Model
     Tabbybrook Mage Hero Card
This expansion for Super Dungeon Explore adds the hero miniature Twilight Knight and the associated hero card.

Great Battles is a miniatures wargame that is played on a hex grid,  it combines elements of board gaming and miniature wargaming in its rule mechanics. Movement and firing ranges as well as arcs of fire, front, flank and rear zones are all regulated by a hex grid.

Each turn represents 30 minutes of real time and each hex is 250 yards across.  Each hex can only contain one combat unit though commanders can stack with other commanders and/or friendly units. Units of deployed artillery, infantry or cavalry are faced towards a hex spine and have 2 front, 2 flank and 2 rear hexes. Command stands, infantry in square and limbered artillery do not have facings and can be positioned anywhere within a hex.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Westfront 1944 is a stand alone expansion for History of War which adds new German and Allied cards to the game system.

The game consists of two sets - one with the Axis forces and one with the Allied forces. You need at least one of each decks and the basic game rules to play.

Included are special scenario rules and Land Cards for the recreation of the Battles after D-Day towards Germany.

It uses a special way point system, in which you follow certain routes (Lands). The Axis player tries to retreat, while the Allies player tries to cut of the retreat of the German forces.

Seven Men's Morris is played in the same way as Nine Men's Morris, with two changes, the first is that each player has seven men and the second is that it is played on a modified board (which is similar to that used by Six Men's Morris, except it a cross in the center).

Hunting Season is a family friendly card game for 3 to 6 players.  If you like bluffing games, hidden identity games, or deduction games this is a game you are going to want to own. Unlike some games that allow others to knock you out of a game this game is unique in that you must be tricked into knocking yourself out. But don't worry some mistakes are forgiving. Work with shaky alliance to find out if your hunt is safe to go on or not but beware the wrong hunt could end your season.

Point Cards are what you need to collect before the end of the Season. If you have the most points at the end of the season you will win. This is of course assuming you didn't encounter another Hunter or Bigfoot.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Niobium is a Psionic agent. The mind of a Psionic is his most formidable weapon. Psychic attacks and abilities make this class both extremely powerful and highly versatile on the battlefield. Specifically, Niobium can mimic the abilities of any other agent in play, making him a true “Jack of All Trades.” And, he can use his amazing psycho-kinetic powers to turn his body into a human bullet.

Niobium was created for the second Galaxy Defenders kickstarter.

Tritium is a Support agent – Support agents are specialists in using devices. The presence of these agents in a team may be the key to winning the battle. Instead of using two weapons, Tritium has an almost endless supply of devices that appear randomly in her secondary weapon slot, so her focus in the game is to support the team with a plethora of high-tech gadgets.

She was available as part of the second Galaxy Defenders kickstarter.

boardgame: Skylords: Warriors of The Wind Core Set
description:
SkyLords is a game of mid-air jousting where warriors leap from towers and clash in flight. The combat tests skills of both agility and brute strength. A powerful, heavy fighter may offer up a formidable defense, but a lighter opponent can attack from above. It is crucial to find a balance between power and weight that will beat the strategy of your opponent.

Games of SkyLords are for 2 players, ages 12 and over. Players draw from the same deck, which must contain at least 26 cards.

Before beginning, players set the number of starting Game Points, which corresponds to the amount of Life each player starts with.

At the end of each turn, players deduct Game Points corresponding to the amount of damage received. The first player to reach zero loses.
This master alien was released as part of the second Galaxy Defenders kickstarter. It is useable in custom scenarios and downloadable scenarios.

boardgame: Galaxy Defenders Master Alien: The Experiment
description: The experiment was released as a master alien during the second kickstarter campaign of Galaxy Defenders. It can be added as an additional master alien to battle in the base game.

Each player has a set of 4 pawns that start in his or her corner of the board. The goal is to move the pawns around the board to the "home" section. Movement is determined by a die. The Players have to pay an entry of game coins into the bank. The winner is the first player to move all pawns "home". pawns can be brought into play by rolling the number indicated at the "home" sections.

In game 1 up to 3 players share one game track (red or black). If a player gets on a "W"-space, the pawn either is returned to the players corner and has to start anew, or the player has to pay a prior fixed amount of game coins into the bank. If a pawn would get on a space, that is alredy occupied by another pawn, it jumps over and gets one space ahead.

In game 2 all players move around the same game track and may capture each others pawns. Captured pawns are returned to their player's corner and must start their journey over. If a pawn gets on a "W"-space, the player has to pay a prior fixed amount of game coins into the bank. If the "W"-space is already oppupied, the first pawn gets captured and it's player has to pay the same amount of game coins to the owner of the second pawn.

The clover on the cover may indicate that this game was published by Klee

The elite alien legion was released as part of the second Galaxy Defenders kickstarter. It can be fully integrated with the base game and the expansions for Galaxy Defenders in order to provide a more difficult challenge.

These weapons were released as a part of the second Galaxy Defenders Kickstarter. They can be used and implemented into the core game or its expansions in order to change out the original weapon layout of the agents.
This expansion was released during the second Galaxy Defenders campaign on Kickstarter. It includes 2 agents and 2 new power armors to use with the base game or expansions.

The kingator is a master alien for use with the expansions of Galaxy Defenders which was relesed during the second Galaxy Defenders kickstarter. It can be used to increase the difficulty of the game and add variety.

AFTERSHOCK: A Humanitarian Crisis Game is a boardgame that explores the interagency cooperation needed to address the emergency and early recovery phase of a complex humanitarian crisis. It is intended both for hobby play and for use in professional education about humanitarian assistance and disaster relief.

The game is set in the fictional country of Carana, but is loosely modeled on disasters such as the 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami and the 2010 Haiti earthquake. It explores approximately three months of humanitarian operations, including both the initial emergency and a later period of early recovery. Because Carana is a fragile, conflict- affected country, relief and reconstruction efforts may also involve issues of social unrest and political instability, especially during the early recovery stage once the initial shock of the crisis has worn off.

The primary objective of all players is to address the urgent humanitarian needs of the local population (thereby scoring "relief points"). However, players also need to maintain public and political support for their organizations ("operations points"), whether to govern (Carana), sustain the relief mission (HADR-TF), or secure financial support (UN and NGOs).

"At-Risk" cards indicate the current emergency needs for food, water/sanitation, medical, or shelter supplies in each district of the capital. Players must bring the necessary supplies into the country, and allocate aid teams to deliver them in time. Aid teams might also be engaged in rescue operations, aid coordination, needs assessment, infrastructure repair, public relations or other key tasks. Event cards inject many of the numerous challenges associated with relief operations into game play, but also indicate the self-help efforts of the local population as they struggle to survive and recover.

Game play is cooperative, but it is possible for some, all, or no player to win.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Flagship: A Game of Naval Action
description:
A WWII era naval-themed game game of strategy for two players. The object is to attack and capture the opponents flagship with one of your submarines. The game setup and play is very similar to the later and more well known Stratego. Each player sets a "fleet" of 20 ships on the first three rows (24 spaces)of the 8x8 gridded board nearest its home base. Four spaces are left vacant. As in Stratego the placement/order of battle is hidden from the opponent. One side is chosen to go first and movement is one space in any unoccupied direction with alternating turns. Attacks are made when one piece moves to a vacant space directly in front of an opponent's piece and the rank/class of the ship is announced. The ship with the highest assigned value is the winner and the lesser piece is removed from the board. If both have the same value, they're both removed. One important difference is that each side is given 4 Submarines with the lowest assigned value of 1, but they take ALL pieces except the 4 Subchasers (value 2). Attacks need not be made when a piece moves into attack position. If no value is announced with the move, the piece acts a block to the opposing piece. The player whose Submarine attacks the opponents flagship first wins the game. If both flagships are removed through a draw attack, the game continues until one fleet is removed entirely from the board or the game may be called a draw.

Fleet pieces/order of battle

1 Flagship - value 10
1 Battle Cruiser - value 8 
2 Battleships - value 6
2 Cruisers  - value 5
3 Destroyers - value 4
3 Torpedo Boats - value 3
4 Subchasers - value 2
4 Submarines - value 1

20 pieces in each fleet (red and blue). Pieces are made of wood.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Wrath of the Righteous – "Goblin Skull Bomb" Promo Card

"Can't put it back together, but you can get a new skull."

description:
Use one of the reels included to build the pyramid. Start at the base of the pyramid and reach the top to pick up a key to the underground labyrinth. Each player uses a reel to find their starting space and the path they have to take to get to the top. They use the dice to move to each space shown on the reels. The sphinxes can be placed to delay the players - needing to roll the number on the back of the sphinx to pass.

The winner is the one who has completed the route in the correct way, and if there is more than one who has done this, the one who arrived first.

9 promo cards.

5 Law Cards - Unification Policy, Peace Treaty, Trade Agreement, Labor Reform, and Secret Combinations
4 Character Cards (all with the same ability)

Note: These are the same Viceroy promo cards that were offered by the Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign
