boardgame: Ca$h 'n Guns: Second Edition – More Ca$h 'n More Guns
description:
Just when you were starting to relax around your fellow thieves, crooks and criminals comes More Cash 'n More Guns, an expansion for Ca$h 'n Guns that'll have you looking over your shoulder long after the police sirens have faded.

In this expansion, you can get your hands on four new pistols, new loot cards and new special powers. The simple act of robbing a bank has never been more fun!

The Darkness looms and your master is the only one who can banish it. It will take him 4 days. You, his apprentices, must defend him till he has finished casting his spell or be forever plunged into darkness. Your only hope is to build the powerful arcane defenses handed down to you by your master; Powerful towers. Time is of the essence.

Real Time Tower Defense is a real time co-operative boardgame for 2-4 players that plays in under 60 minutes. In what is a true tower defense game, players (the Apprentices) build towers to defend their base from the hordes of mindless monsters besieging them and survive 4 “Days” (Having at least 1 Team Health left without going below 1 at any point during the game). The game is played in real time, increasing the tension, giving players just enough time to do what they need to without overwhelming them. The game is run to a sound track which prompts players through the game (much like Space Alert or Escape but somewhere in between). There is a break between each “Day” giving players time to plan for the next while the difficulty of the game increases. Players also gather resources and build their towers in real time using a sand timer, meaning there are two aspects of the game competing for attention. Forget about one of these aspects and you will lose the game.

Rationality Cardinality consists of two types of cards, Question cards and Answer cards. Each round, one player is the judge; they draw a Question card and read it to the other players, who pick one of the ten Answer cards from their hand and play it face down. One random card is added to serve as the control player, and the cards are shuffled together. The judge reads the cards and picks their favorite, and the player who submitted it gets a point.

The cards themselves are part nerd humor and part teaching material: they take concepts from rationality, philosophy, economics, and other fields, make them into jokes, and provide captions to explain the meaning.
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG

boardgame: Der Hobbit: Smaugs Einöde – Smaug: Die Mini-Erweiterung
description:
The Fight against Smaug gets harder with this new rule. The power of the dragon's shield is rolled by dice.
1-6 = The rolled power must be exactly achieved by the player's cards.

An entertaining game on how to behave like a "typical Norwegian".


     When you are abroad do you boast of Norwegian pop groups that you really do not like?
     Do you think a stranger must be crazy for asking to sit beside you when there are other vacant seats on the bus?
     Have you ever turned over to Swedish television when Norway beats Sweden in cross country skiing, just to hear the Swedish television commentators? 


If you answer "yes" to any of these questions, you have very good chances succeeding in this game.

Brunost, Langrenn and Lusekofte (Brown cheese, cross country and "cardigan") is a wonderful entertaining game, where you get or lose points depending on how typically Norwegian you behave (for better or worse...) The game is a perfect gift and a festive way to get to know both yourself and your friends. Perfect for a party, a dinner party or a barbecue evening. Enjoy!

boardgame: ASSEGAIS AND BAYONETS: A Solitaire Game of the Zulu War of 1879.
description:
BACKGROUND: ASSEGAIS AND BAYONETS is semi-historical simulation of the Zulu War of 1879. The Game begins shortly after the Great Britain determined that the Zulu Leader, Cetswayo, is in violation for not disbanding his Impis (Battalions). Natal is feeling threatened and has organized a Military Force to cross into Zululand and force Cetswayo to disband his Army. As the Commander of British Forces in Natal, South Africa, you must protect the British Settlements and squash the Forces of Cetswayo by capturing their Capital of Ulundi and 8 additional Zulu Kraals.

GAME OBJECTIVE: Player takes command of the British Forces during the invasion of Zululand. The Player’s Objective is to attempt to capture the Zulu Capital of Ulundi and 8 other Zulu Kraals before the Campaign Deck is exhausted or the Zulus have Looted 5 of the starting British controlled Towns. If the Zulu Army Loots 6 or more of the originally British controlled Towns or Settlements, the Zulus win. Also, if the British do not capture Ulundi and 8 other Zulu Kraals before the Campaign Deck is exhausted, the Zulus Win.

Introduction:
Each player assumes the role of a Christian within the church. Each Christian will collect harmful sins through the game. Knowing that your fellow Christians may be in error, you will still need to band together and help everyone overcome their sins.

REBUKE Junior is a family game that uses teamwork and hand management to remove Sin cards. This game is not trivia based and because of this it allows everyone to pick up and play easily.

REBUKE Junior educates players in three ways. 
1. It shows the sins that break The 10 Commandments. 
2. It demonstrates the Christ-like traits that keep us from temptation. 
3. It finally shows that by following God and asking for forgiveness our sins, we are covered in Christ's righteousness.

Great to play with:
• Ministries
• Bible Studies
• Families
• Friends
• Churches
• Academy Classrooms
• Anti-Bullying School Programs
• Road Tripping
• Game Nights
• Camping

Do you find the original "REBUKE" too easy? Want a challenge or more strategy? Then shuffle "Heavy Burdens" expansion into your game! Heavy Burdens is created for adult audiences. This expansion covers Sins that are considered taboo. Do you think you can handle this rebuke?

In a world where iniquity abounds we mustn't give up. Even during the most trying times when our own burdens are heavy. Keep up the "good fight" and follow Christ because his yoke is easy and his burden is light.

Heavy Burden cards can be easily stored in the original REBUKE box.

Great to play with:

     Bible Studies
     Friends
     Young Adult Vespers
     Road Tripping
     Game Nights
     Camping

boardgame: Adventure Time Card Wars: Sundae School
description:
Adventure Time Card Wars: Sundae School is a single card that can be added to any of the Adventure Time Card Wars games. Sundae School is a "Rainbow Building", the text of which reads "While you have an even amount of Hit Points, your Creature on this Landscape has +2 ATK."

This card was released in the July 2015 comic Adventure Time with Fionna & Cake: Card Wars #01.

Once upon a time, when Shadow Lord the evil one swept across the land, the legendary Dragon Lord gathered the heroes and eventually defeated the Shadow Lord. The Dragon Lord, however, was seriously injured and eventually died, transformed into Runes, which are sealed with the shadows deep inside the temple. Now, the temple is long wasted, and the power of the enchantment has become weak, so weak that the power of shadows are leaking. The descendants of the heroes gather, march into the ruin to find the runes and seal the shadows again — but the shadow's followers have infiltrated the group.

Runes & Ruin is a fantasy partnership game designed for 4 to 6 players, with various characters of different abilities, and numerous artifacts that are the key to success. Players advance their team toward victory by exploring the ruin and attacking others. The heroes must find all the runes before the seal fails; the shadow follower must sabotage the heroes' plan. Depending on the number of players and the stage of the game, one to three shadow followers are hidden among the players, just as truths are always hidden underneath lies...

description:
Description from the publisher:

The ancient Egyptians worshipped cats, which clearly explains what happened to them. Cats may be cute, but they've got teeth, claws and siege weaponry? Munchkin Kittens, an expansion for any standalone Munchkin game, has thirty new cards that some of you may be allergic to. Don't be fooled ... they're adorable, but deadly!

Will the Three Bears race home before Goldilocks?

A promotional card given out during the 2015 Watch It Played Indiegogo campaign.

Part of the Dead of Winter series.

Ciapanò (also known by the names Rovescino, Traversone, Busca, Vinciperde and Mattazza) is a trick-taking game where the goal is not to get points.

A 40 card pack is used. In each suit the cards rank as follows: 3 (highest), 2, Asso (ace), Re (king), Cavallo (horse, or queen), Fante (jack), 7, 6, 5, 4 (lowest).

The cards have point values and the object is to avoid taking tricks containing valuable cards. There is an extra penalty for winning the last trick. The values of the cards are as follows:

Each ace                           1 point
Each 3, 2, re, cavallo or fante    1/3 of a point

Deal (anticlockwise) 8 cards to each player for 5 players, 10 each for 4 players and 13 each for 3 players (in this case the dealer receives 14 cards and discards one card, which will be given to the winner of the last trick). Turn to deal passes to the right after each hand.

There are no trumps. The player to dealer's right leads first. Any card may be led, and the other players must play a card of the suit led if they have one. A player with no card of the suit led may play anything. The highest card of the suit led wins the trick, and the winner leads to the next trick.

When all tricks have been played, the values of cards taken in tricks are counted. Each player except the winner of the last trick scores as penalty points the value of the cards they have taken, disregarding any fractions - for example a player who has taken three and one thirds point scores 3; a player who has taken just two thirds of a point scores nothing. The scores of all the players for one hand should add up to 11, so the player who won the last trick scores the difference between 11 and the total of the amounts scored by the other players.

If one player wins all the points, this is called Cappotto. The player with all the points scores zero for the hand and the opponents score 11 each.

Any player whose cumulative score is 31 or more points drops out of the game and does not play any more. The last people left in play (1, 2 or 3 as players as agreed in advance) win the match. Alternately, some play that when a player reaches or exceeds 31, the player with the lowest score wins.

Players fulfill orders, and get victory points.
They need resources fulfill an order, but their hand-size is limited.
If a player cannot (or doesn't want to) fulfill an order, he must hand it off to the next player with resources.

A division to corps level operational game from D-Day to/across the Rhine.  Half-month turns, 10 mis/16kms per hex.  51 Allied and 42 German division to corps level units.

Relatively fast simple game mechanics expanding on my Ardennes Petite and Patton's Lorraine Minigame game system.

Allied Strategic Bombing, Close Air Support, Strafing, and Air Drops.  Waffen SS Ruthlessness (and Consequential GI Rage), German Strategic Reserve, etc.


    NOTE:  Allied army headquarters units have been added and replace the "truck convoys" for supply.  And 3rd Army -supplied units get a +1 shift in attacking combat odds.  :-)

boardgame: Fleets: The Pleiad Conflict – Corporate Lords
description:
In Fleets: The Pleiad Conflict – Corporate Lords, an expansion for Fleets: The Pleiad Conflict, each corporation gains a special ability as well as its own unique escort ship.

Two neutral fleets move between the planets; the Pirates fire their guns at anyone entering the system, while the Trade Convoy allows the players to get extra resources from other systems. The players may also build operation bases where they can recharge and repair (hopefully without too much interruption). The conflict is escalating...

The game is based on the 1934 movie "Gold" with Hans Albers. At the beginning of the game, the players move around in the laboratory to find out, who plays "Ingeneur Holt". The roles are previously determined by card drawing. If a pawn gets on a space, that is occupied by another pawn, this pawn has to reveal it's identity. When "Holt" is exposed, he sabotages the gold-making machine and the sliding doors begin to close. Now all players have to leave the laboratory before the doors are closed. The pawns that don't make it, die in the explosion and their players are expelled from the game. The rest have to move over the turntable and reach one of three escape tunnels. The first one to accomplish this wins the game.

The players move their 15 pawns along the black lines or by jumping over other pawns. The goal of the game is, to get all pawns on the spaces of ones color at the opposite side of the game board. But before moving to the destination spaces, the players have to gather al their pawns in one of the blue areas.

A promo card given out at Cool Mini or Not's booth for buyers of the base game during Gencon 2015.
Also given as a "Thank You" for play testers of the Xenoshyft: Dreadmire stand alone expansion.

Jin Khan is a Heroic Storm Trooper that replaces surviving units upon promotion.

Become a God... but without powers!

In Just Dust you are a god without powers in search of Redemption. The only chance you have to redeem yourself is to prosper the people assigned you. You can influence some key-characters that give you instant benefits.

Your goal on the game is to arrive at the end with more followers than your rivals.

Just Dust was inspired to my book: Solo Polvere.

The game is composed 108 cards divided into:

     1 card "FAVOUR OF ANCIENT GOD"
     4 cards "HEART OF TOWN"
     5 cards characters
     98 building cards: 40 cards production places (various colours) and 58 cards "RED” places.



The cards are all: characters, structures, resources and money.
The game hasn’t a set number of rounds. The player with the "FAVOUR OF ANCIENT GOD" card starts the game. He choose a character card placing it in front of himself and performs the action with the respective profit.
Then the other players, starting from the left, perform the action without the profit.
When all the players have selected the character and played the actions, the round ends.

Build production places or "RED" places. Get the necessary resources and acquire followers.

The winner is the one who has at the end of the game the highest number of followers.

"Squires, welcome to Medieval Academy, the place where you'll be trained to become a knight. You'll have to master the arts of Jousts and Tournaments, complete dangerous Quests, and polish your Education. You'll also have to serve the king, and show that you have a sense of Charity and Gallantry. Only one of you will be knighted by King Arthur, so it's time to show what you’re capable of." —Father Advevan Nicolus Emilius, chairman of the Medieval Academy.

In Die Holde Isolde, a "family+" game, each player takes the role of a squire who wants to outdo the others in the different training categories to score Chivalry Points. To achieve this goal, during the six turns of the game, the players must wisely draft the cards that are the most useful to them and play them at the right time to move their discs up the training tracks.

At the end of turn VI, the squire who has the most Chivalry Points wins the game and is knighted by King Arthur!

Die Holde Isolde consists of both the Medieval Academy base game and the Galantry and Magic mini-expansions.

The little sheep ran away, encountering the wild boar Bruno along the way! They're now scared and want to return quickly to their pasture.

In Eddy, das schwarze Schaf, you use the black sheep Eddy to return these sheep to their pasture before Bruno can make it in.

In Lauf, Pinguin!, four little penguins are on their way to the penguin playground, jumping from ice floe to ice floe while also trying to collect anchovies and sardines.

But suddenly dangerous orcas appear between the ice floes. Who will reach the penguin playground first? And who will have caught the most fish?

boardgame: Wer holt die Kuh vom Eis?

In Zugepackt, weggeschnappt!, the two birds Anton and Adele have a fun game to present to you. They want to know who can first recognize the two colors showing on the dice on the image cards laid out before you. As soon as you've found it, be fast and snatch the right card!

Machi Koro: Großstadt-Erweiterung includes both the Millionaire's Row and Harbor expansions for Machi Koro.

Millionaire's Row consists of thirteen new establishment cards, including a winery, a moving company and a general store, while Harbor has ten new establishments, one new starting establishment and two new landmarks.

“Quilt-opoly” board game is from The DMC Corporation and debuted at the International Quilt Market in 2008.  It features quilts and quilting, and lets quilters share their passion for quilting with friends and family.

The properties are all quilt blocks with escalating degrees of complexity. Players pick their favorite quilting game pieces and move around the “blocks” collecting Quilting Techniques and Block Designs to create their very own quilting empire! However, they must be careful that they don’t pick the “You sewed the seams uneven and now the points don’t match” game card or they will have to go to “Rip Out Seam” without passing “Start Quilting” and collecting $200.

boardgame: We Didn't Playtest This Village at All!
description:
Your village once had bananas. Now it has none. Clearly, werewolves...er...banana thieves are to blame! And there's no way you're a banana thief. You're an honest, hardworking villager.

In We Didn't Playtest This Village at All!, each player is dealt one secret We Didn't Playtest style card, which has both an action and a note telling you whether you are a Nice Villager or a Banana Thief. The village gets one action to deal with the banana crisis, and they must vote on who gets to play their one card! That lone card will determine the fate of the villagers and banana thieves, possibly sending some of them to Banana Jail.

The game lasts 1-10 minutes, depending on how long people talk before the vote.

boardgame: Remember Limerick! The War of the Two Kings: Ireland, 1689-1691 – Pro Religione et Libertate
description:
Pro Religione et Libertate (PRL) is an operational level wargame expansion for Red Sash Games' 2010 title Remember Limerick! The latter covers the war between King James II and King William III in Ireland, from 1689-1691. PRL expands the game to cover the Dutch invasion of England in 1688 (a.k.a. the Glorious Revolution) and the fighting in Scotland that took place in parallel with the war in Ireland, 1689-1692.

PRL belongs to Red Sash Games' Lace Wars series of 17th and 18th Century operational wargames and uses the same scales (units are battalions and regiments, map distances are 4 leagues per hex, turns are 3 weeks each).

PRL has scenarios for 2 players. These allow the players to fight the invasion of 1688 and the Scottish campaign. When combined with Remember Limerick! up to 4 people can play. There are 2 campaign scenarios, one starting in 1688 and one in 1689.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Urugal Expansion – Cabal of the Onyx Cliffs
description:
Craftet from the bones of monstrosities of ages past, Urugal Golems prowl the battlefield to claim the skeletons of their enemies.

Three new Golems for your Golem Arcana Army, the Urugal.

New recruits have arrived to Takoashi University. Increase the mayhem in your games of Tentacle Bento with this 19 card, holofoil expansion pack. Including events, characters, all-stars, and students, every holofoil card in this expansion pack features unique art to make your games more colorful than ever!

1863 is a two player wargame of a pivotal year in the American Civil War. 1863 saw the Union victories at Gettysburg in the east and Vicksburg in the west, and the Chattanooga-Chickamauga campaigns when the Confederates had the opportunity to win a decisive battle and potentially gain foreign recognition.

Each player commands one of the two Theater of Operations; East and West. What makes this game unique is that one player commands the Union in the Eastern Theater, and the Confederates in the Western Theater; the second commands the Union in the Western Theater, and the Confederates in the Eastern Theater. Both players are competing to gain the most Victory Points in each Theater while defeating their opponent on the other.

The objective of each player is to score the most Victory Points in terms of seizing critical cities and winning battles. The idea is that you are positioning yourself for a major promotion for total command of all your side's armies for the rest of the war. Units represent corps, divisions and individual leaders with their staffs. Each turn is one month.

Published in S&T 297, Mar/Apr 2016

A mighty family and a righteous name. It is but a stepping stone to your true calling. Your rightful dominion… over all the world!  Reign of Man is an empire management and territory control war game, for 2-5 players, that takes around an hour from start to finish.

Summary: 
Reign of Man is a fast paced new take on the strategy war game that tips its hat to classics of the past while presenting a fresh and approachable combination of territory control, engine building and rapid dice driven battle resolution. Reign of Man moves along at a clip, as each player can only choose one action to take each turn, and it doesn’t take all afternoon to play to resolution, unlike many of its predecessors. Players are all equally effected by global events, which everyone sees coming several turns in advance. This helps guide you to knew strategies and lend replayability without being punishingly random. Winning comes down to engine development and not just having all the land. Exactly how you win depends on how you rule your kingdom. The Legendfolk and March of the Damned expansion introduce a host of high fantasy themes and, like the core game, are brought to life by the incredible artwork of Erwin Arozza.

Reign of Man - Legendfolk (included expansion)

Legendfolk introduces a set of fantastical ancient races that exist in the world but aren’t widespread enough for a grab at controlling it. They fear for their people and will offer their skills in return for protection. Players may contact them and dedicate armies from their available army pools to bolster their defenses, through a bidding process, in order to take advantage of their special abilities and units. This expansion can be played with 2 - 5 players in conjunction with the Reign of Man rules and components.

Reign of Man – March of the Damned (included expansion)

The world shudders as your people watch the dead rise under the command of great and powerful Lich Queens. Wicked beasts, they wield otherworldly powers and lay waste to our lands. Who will save us? Who will command our armies? The people await your mercy and your might. This variant can be played by itself or along with the Legendfolk. The Reign of Man rules apply to March of the Damned.

Gameplay Overview

Players begin the game with an empire mat that is somewhat developed and get right into the action of taking a single action each turn, in clockwise order.

Basic actions are to:

Develop your empire, building your engine by making improvements to your empire mat  
Mobilize armies by moving them to adjacent territories, and in some cases trigger battles, resolved by the rolling of dice  
Raise more armies by placing them on territories you control.  
Starting in the third turn players will be effected by a World Event card each turn. You can always see these cards two turns in advance of their taking place, so they will drive dynamic strategic decisions. Each development category on your mat has a high rank ability that grants you victory points. As soon as one emperor reaches three victory points, the game is over and they win! The rightful ruler of all the world!

Attributes

Reign of Man rounds move lightning-quick because you can only "do one thing" on your turn. Even battle resolution moves quickly. There is no money. While player elimination is possible, it is very much not common.

Designer Notes

This game is a territory control war game and definitely tips its hat to some such classics of the past, but it doesn't play like most of those games. It's a much shorter overall play time and is more approachable. Turns are fast-paced and battles are resolved in no time. You won't suffer a slow grind to attrition. You won't have to sit at the table and finish a long grind to the inevitable, wherein you feel like you've already lost and there's no coming back. Victories are rarely won by player elimination, but are a race to prominence. We wanted to preserve the best parts of those classic games in the genre and fix the problems we saw germane to their most common mechanics. We love this game and sincerely hope you will too!

description: Wertvolle Schätze (Precious Treasures) is a tile laying game, presenting 20 treasures named in the Bible. Numbered tiles are drawn from a bag. On your turn, you place one tile or change one for a new one from the bag. To make a discovery, place the L-shaped tiles around a treasure, matching the numbers and placing black and white numbers alternating. The player who places the fourth, last tile around a treasure gets it. Whoever gets five treasures first, wins.

400 entertaining questions and answers about beer.

A player throw a die that show the different categories and try to answer correct and score points.

The legendary Hero, Candy, has battled her way across the length and breadth of Crystalia. Skill is nothing without courage. Fortunately, Brave-Mode Candy possesses courage aplenty, and her bravery is a bright light to all who join her.

Brave-Mode Candy also replaces Super Dungeon Explore: Candy and Cola from the first edition of Super Dungeon Explore and contains a hero card for Soda Master Candy, which can be used with either model.

Contents

     Brave-Mode Candy Assembled Model
     Never-Lost Cola Assembled Model
     Brave-Mode Candy Hero Card
     Soda Master Candy Hero Card
     Never-Lost Cola Pet Card
     Never-Lost Cola Treasure Card

In El Diablo, players have to bet on the number of tricks they expect to achieve and then do it!

The cards are numbered from 1 to 15, the fifteen being the strongest, but we must take into account the special cards:

    El Diablo can take the value of zero or sixteen up to you.
    The Chameleon allows you to take the value of a card played or play 
    The Wizard allows you to vary the value of a card from another player!


In El Diablo tension mounts, mounts for fast and animated parts, it is the devil who wants that!

Pondemonium is a free print'n'play fantasy wargame for two players, where frogs and turtles fight over the control of a Pond.
The game Pondemonium is based on the Eureka Miniatures "Pond Wars" range.
This ruleset adds various components (all free to download to print'n'play) that make the game similiar to classic boardgames, the player can either use the fantastic miniatures from Eureka or the print'n'play version of them.
The gameplay revolves around the turtles and frogs armies: the first must defend the pond with its better defensive ability, while the second must surround the enemies and conquer the pond with its major number of troops and its mobility.
The main mechanics of this game are: action points represented as cards, movement based on how much terrain is
crossed, and d6 rolling for battling.

BACKGROUND
Since the beginning of time, the Dark Frond Swamp has been a theater for battles between Frogs and Turtles. While Turtles live in an empire based on manpower and industrial development, Frogs are ruled by an Evil King that assures the wellness of his reign through war and conquests. For ages Turtles were forced to rise the level of their military tactics to defend the borders from the brute force of the Frog army. Battle after battle, Turtle defenses weakened, giving Frogs a great opportunity. Now the Frog King is coming, with new technological warfare machines. The next assault could create a direct passage to the Turtle land. The time is right, and unexpected: Turtles are not warned about the Frogs arrival. Will the heroic captain Cracked Shell prevail even in this desperate battle?

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Zikia – Heralds of the Great Weald
description:
The outriders for the Zikia tribes, these Golems herald the uprising of The Zikia Great Weald. Harnessing the fury of a thousand tribes they bring vengeance to the Zikia's enemy.

Three new Golems for your Golem Arcana Army.

The players move around the board, while buying and selling shares of public corporations. Movement is determined by a die. Three special dies determine the share prices and dividends. If a the stock rises, dividends are payed to the share holders. If a player has at least 6 shares of a company, he has to buy this company. As a result he, instead of the bank, has to pay the dividends. But he also gets 10 times the dividend from players who land on one of the spaces of his company. The game Ends when one player gets bankrupt or if the bank runs out of money. The Player with the most capital (money, shares, companies) wins the game.

Coup: Rebellion G54 – Anarchy, an expansion for La Mame Games' Coup: Rebellion G54, adds seven new roles and actions to that game for more vicious fun: Anarchists, Paramilitary, Arms Dealers, Financier, World Bank, Plantation owners, Socialists, and Social Media.

description: The perfect blend of luck,skill and strategy. Players roll cowrie shells in their attempt to get the shells to land smile side up. You are allowed up to 3 rolls per turn and you can roll any or all of your shells each turn. The strategy increases as scoring points becomes more difficult and penalties and bonuses apply.

Pyrkon: Atak Koziołaków is a modified version of the Mafia, enriching the game with a lot of interesting characters that have a genuine skills. 
The action takes place at Multigenre Fan Convention Pyrkon.

The Vampire Lord has entered the Castle and is slowly infecting everyone to take control. The Lady and her Hero are pure of heart and can resist him for a while, but they have only one night to find him and one chance to kill him when the sun rises.

In Vampire Panic, (which is possibly not the game's final name), all players are dealt a character card, with one player starting as the Vampire Lord. Players trade cards in two rounds of play and may secretly become infected.  The vampires will attempt to infect as many humans as they can while concealing their identity, while the surviving humans are trying to identify the location of the Vampire Lord by the end of the night.

Vampire Panic is a light social deduction game that attempts to combine the hidden roles and fast play of Akihisa Okui's One Night Werewolf with the tense infection mechanism of David Ausloos's Panic Station.

boardgame: Shadows of Malice: BGG Fan Card Set
description:
This is a special set of 8 playable cards available to BGG users that become fans of Shadows of Malice.  The cards are:

1. Treasure card: Cache of Shards.  Avatars find 3d3 randomly colored and colorless soulshards
2. Treasure card: Cache of Potions.  Avatars find 2d3 random potions (better be banded for this one!)
3. Fate card: Caller of the Wild.  (Invoked) Avatar may choose creature type for encounter from those available for terrain
4. Fate card: Stormy weather. (Instant) Avatar's band suffer 50% movement reduction
5. Ability card: Firekin. Creature delivers +1d* fire wounds, gets +1d* fire shield, suffers +1 wound received from cold
6. Ability card: Frostkin. Creature delivers +1d* cold wounds, gets +1d* cold shield, suffers +1 wound received from fire
7. Ability card: Levikin. Creature delivers +1d* electricity wounds, gets +1d* electricity shield, suffers +1 wound received from acid
8. Ability card: Acrikin. Creature delivers +1d* acid wounds, gets +1d* acidshield, suffers +1 wound received from electricity

A game published about the German City of Hamm.
By playing the players get to know a little bit about the city and its shops.
During each player's turn they roll a six-sided dice to move along a game-board depicting a map of Hamm. At certain spaces players can acquire cards of the shops or they can go sightseeing by following the instructions on the back of the card. Sometimes there is only information written, sometimes you have to pay an amount of money or at other times you may earn some cash.

The game ends when the last player reaches the finish space with his playing piece. The winner is the person who has acquired the most sightseeing and/or shop cards.

Evocation refines and streamlines 3-5 player game play and adds a 1-2 player challenge mode!
Evocation comes with 11 new game-tiles and downloadable original soundtrack.

A trivia game where a team of men play against a team of women. A spinner shows whether the current team will answer a "his", "hers", or "his & hers" question, or do a "his & hers" challenge. A team correctly answering a question about the opposite sex, get more points than for questions about their own. Challenges have both teams competing to see who can list more items for the category given.

The King of Joseon reenacts the tragic story of the boy king Danjong, who ascended the throne at the age of twelve but was shortly dethroned by his uncle, Grand Prince Sooyang. The game is set on the year 1452, when some remained loyal to the king while others took the side of the ambitious usurper. Which side are you on? And who will prevail?

There are 32 cards that represent various characters. Most of the cards have numeric values on them: 1 King; 1 Grand Prince [0]; 4 Bandits; 2 Queens [9]; 2 Ministers [8]; 2 Councilors [7]; 2 Generals [6]; 2 Eunuchs [5]; 2 Ambassadors [4]; 6 Prefects [3]; 6 Policemen [2]; and 2 Clowns [1].

At the beginning of the game, all cards are dealt to the players. (In a 3-player game, the dealer receives one less.) The player with the Grand Prince reveals it. On his/her turn, the player can either (1) play a set of two identical cards and use its special ability or (2) attempt to steal a card from another player.

If a player revealed the Grand Prince, his/her winning condition is to have the King and three cards out of the Queens, Ministers, and Councilors. For other players, the winning condition is to have the King card only (and nothing else) in his/her hand.

The overall flow is simple enough, but the special powers of the cards make the game more tactical than it appears.

In Bugz, there are ants, beetles and mosquitoes; there are red bugs, green bugs and blue bugs; there are hollow bugs, spotty bugs and solid bugs; and there are small bugs, medium bugs and large bugs. In the game, players take it in turns to try and collect as many bugs as they can – but in order to score points, all the bugs they pick up in a single turn must have at least one feature in common.

To play the game, 20 cards are laid out face-down in a grid. Player 1 then begins the game by turning up 2 cards in place. If these cards share a feature, he can either end his turn and score these, or keep going by turning up another card. If all 3 cards share a feature, once again, he can either end his turn and score these, or keep going, and so on.

The more bugs a player collects in a single turn, the more points he will get. But if at any point the cards he has turned up don’t all share a feature, he must turn them back over and won’t score any points at all.

Players continue taking turns until the grid can no longer be refilled to 20 cards, whereupon the game is over, and the player with the most points is the winner.

boardgame: Long Island (fan expansion for Age of Steam)
description:
With a census-estimated population of 7.8M in 2014, Long Island is the most populated island in any U.S. state or territory. It is also the longest and largest island in the contiguous United States extending 118 miles from New York Harbor to Montauk Point. However, it's maximum north-south distance is only 23 miles.

The Long Island Rail Road (LIRR) is the busiest commuter railroad system in North America, carrying an average of 338K passengers each weekday. Chartered in 1834, it is also the oldest railroad still operating under its original name. It is also the only railroad that operates 24/7 with significant off peak, weekend and holiday service.

In Age of Steam: Long Island, players will be climbing all over each other as they struggle to be the first to get passengers to their destinations.

Significant differences:


    Designed for 2-3 players, w/ few cubes and a lot of opportunities for blocking.
    Each round, each player can deliver at most one passenger East and one passenger West.
    Once-around auction for player order.  
    Locomotive/Urbanization alternate being available with only 1 (two-player) or 2 (three-player) other actions being available at all.
    The Early Riser action (three-player only) bundles First Build, First Move, and Locomotive into one but restricts the player to one delivery.

Players build a new version of the world in the Chronicles Series, starting with the first game: Chronicles 1: Origins. First, players take the role of leaders of a small and emerging tribe in the early Stone Age, collaborating to avoid threats, exploit opportunities, and build the tribe to a position of growth and stability. Then, once a variety of tribes have been created, players take leadership of a city-state that evolved from an original tribe and contest the region with their opponents who are the leaders of city-states of their own. Using Legacy mechanisms, the world evolves as the city-states use exploration, conquest, culture, and religion to out-compete their opponents and try to establish the most enduring enterprise. Not every tribe created in this game will survive to the next...

Each game in the Chronicles Series uses Rob Daviau's Legacy System within the game itself, but will use the new Echo System to allow the world you create in Chronicles 1: Origins to be continued in the second game of the Chronicles Series due out in 2017.

This Kickstarter-exclusive mini-expansion for Captain’s Wager introduces a scrappy twist to your high-seas scheming. Inside are two Thrifty Engineer crew cards—cunning salvagers who can snatch Scrap Metal from rival treasure stacks or, failing that, pocket gold straight from the supply. The expansion also includes five Scrap Metal treasure cards, one in each player color. If taken into your hand as an item, a Scrap Metal card is immediately placed into your claimed treasure stack. Its gold value becomes X, where X equals the number of Scrap Metal cards you've claimed.

A clone of Trivial Pursuit.

Players start in the center of the hex shaped board, the "Erogenous Center". In turn, they roll a die and move that number of spaces down one of the six radial tracks where they will land on either "Subject Position" or "Capital Position" spaces. The "Dealer" (an opponent, or partner in team games) draws a card and, based on the symbol on the space, asks a sexual trivia question. If answered correctly the player rolls and moves again. If incorrect play passes to next player. If players correctly answer a question while on one of the six outside Capital Zone spaces they get a plastic ring ("Stage Pieces") they can place on their spindle shaped game pawn. If they land on an "Erogenous Zone" space they are asked a question from a card but instead of being right or wrong the answer has to satisfy their Dealer. Once they have all six rings from the outside Capital Zones they move towards the center Erogenous Center space and try to answer an Erogenous Zone question there. If their answer satisfies the Dealer, they win. If not, they must leave the Erogenous Center on the next turn and return again.

Game covers biggest land battle of XVII century, fought between  the Ukrainian Cossacks, led by Hetman Bohdan Khmelnytsky, aided by their Crimean Tatar allies, and a Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth army under King John II Casimir. It was a battle of a Cossack rebellion in Ukraine that took place in the years 1648–1657.
Game uses Husaria system, designed for XVII century battles.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Templarmech Inquisition takes place in a future where Templarmechs, Cybersamurai, TechnoVikings, and Gamma/Meta/Star Romans go to war.

The game is played with units (cards) arranged in ranks. Player can command units to mobilize (move) or activate to perform various actions. Players decide on their commands in secret and preform them simultaneously. Players can use honor to bring more units into play.

"DVD Bonus Extra DVD 500 New Action Film AFL Trivia Questions For The 2007 Season 2 DVD Extra Bonus DVD"

AFL DVD Challenge is the Trivia Game for the whole family.
Prove what you have known all along. That when it comes to matters AFL you've always been right.

Official AFL Trivia Challenge has over 1000 questions and answers presented on DVD with exciting action shots from the greatest game in the world.

Come sail away to the magical island offered in Krosmaster: Arena – Piwate Island, an expansion for Krosmaster: Arena that includes a new double-sided board, a single figure, and punch board. Rules for the new island are also included.

This game is an exciting family war-game. 
Atmospheric, beautifully executed game.

Very simple rules. 
The aim of the game is to capture (to destroy) towers of opponents and to save own. You have 4 generals, which you can develop. 
The peculiarity is that to win you have to capture at least one tower of each opponent.

The objective of the game is as follows: the value of the cube + the overall level of a General (1....4) + 1 (if you are near the tower) + the value of the coin of reinforcements.

As in real war, the victory happens with a genius commander + luck!!
Push your luck game for kids by Melissa and Doug.  Players take turns drawing jewels from a bag to put in their treasure chests. If they draw a frown they have to put their treasure back in the bag.

boardgame: Euphoria: Participate In The Game or Watch It Played
description:
A promotional card given out during the 2015 Watch It Played Indiegogo campaign.

This is a specially made ethical dilemma card for the game, to be added to the base game.  This card does not replace any of the ethical dilemma that come with the game.

The card is compatible with first and second printing versions of Euphoria.

A promotional card given out during the 2015 Watch It Played Indiegogo campaign.

This promo introduces the new Pitcher card 'Pep Budgie' with the card number #P9.

INTRO:
Beekeeper is a family friendly game for children and adults to enjoy. The game consists of 37 Hexagonal Playing Pieces, 1 set of instructions and a nice Black Cloth Pouch w/ drawstrings to hold all the pieces! Set-up time is approx. 2-3 minutes with playtime amounting to 5-10 minutes, and this game can be played with up to 6 players.
 GAMEPLAY:
1st) dump out the hexagonal pieces onto your playing surface and find the queen chit (she has an image on each side of the chit so it should be easy to find her).
2nd)Place the queen in the middle of the playing surface.
3rd)Arrange the rest of the pieces (beekeeper logo side facing up) around the queen piece until you have 3 rows around the queen. (The playing surface should resemble a giant hexagonal shape, or honeycomb)
4th) Youngest player gets to go first by pulling 1 chit from the game (*NOTE* only pieces with 3 open sides can be removed from the arranged pieces).  He/She will then flip over the piece and find the following;
BEE POINTS:  These give you points to win the game
ANIMALS:  These guys make you lose turns
HONEY TOKENS: You need at least 6 honey tokens to pull the queen from her throne when she has at least 3 open sides and end the game and with her you get 10 bee points.
BEE NET or HONEY EXTRACTOR:  Use these cards once during gameplay to steal a bee chit or honey chit from an opposing player (once used they must be discarded to the side)
  Once the first player's turn is complete, then the person to the left is next to pull a piece from the hive.

ENDING AND WINNING THE GAME:
  The game is ended when the Queen chit is removed from the playing surface, which can happen two ways;
  One way is if she has at least 3 open sides and someone has 6 or more HONEY TOKENS.
  The other way is if there are no more chits to pull from the game, then the queen is rewarded to the next player's turn.
  The game is won by the person with the most Bee Points, so count up all of your drones', larvas', workers' and queen bee's chit values and that will be your Bee Point count.

Norshanks Game Cause:
 As with most of the games that Norshanks Games will be marketing, this game is tied to Beekeeping Associations in various countries, and every time a game is sold, part of the profits will be donated to the Purchaser's Current Country of Residence National Beekeeping Association.
 The donated funds will go to the Beekeeping Associations to help save the bees by; researching colony collapse disorder, increasing bee populations, educating the public about the importance of bees, and many other great projects!
 With your help we can help reverse the collapse of bee populations everywhere!

Game is expected to be ready for shipment in Winter.

A simple Star Wars themed Card game.
Each turn a villain card is revealed and players use their 8 hero cards to bid on them. Different villains give different points; Darth Vader, however, gives negative points.

This expansion for Cards Against Humanity has got 30 cards fully-illustrated by some of the best graphic designers in the world, including Milton Glaser (I ♥ NY), Susan Kare (the Apple Macintosh), Jessica Hische (Daily Drop Cap), and legendary printmakers including Jay Ryan, Yann Legendre, Olly Moss, Mike Mitchell, Paul Octavious, and Sonnenzimmer.

"Shake and toss the dice! Race against the clock to create the most words using the letters rolled. Now you have words on your mind!"

Words on my Mind is a word dice game in which players roll lettered dice and then all use the same dice to form as many words as possible in the time allowed, in almost a simplified Boggle-style game.

Five-Minute Delivery is a game where players take on the role of bundle deliverers in the goblin town of Goobliopolis. On a randomly constructed board, players listen to directions from the narrator on an MP3 as they try to deliver bundles to various locations. The locations are determined by Address Cards each player gets, and the routes are different based on the layout of the tiles that make up the town. Because the length of the game is determined by the MP3's that work with the game, it takes... five minutes to play. (The title seems obvious now, doesn’t it?) The game is played in real time (meaning everybody plays at the same time with no turns). A variety of MP3’s give players different directions and (along with the random town layout) different play experiences every time.

A party game with cards of three categories: Description of words, Pantomime, Lists

Split into two teams! The more the better! Make sounds, pantomime or anyhting you can to describe the most words to your teammates!

boardgame: Clockwork Wars: Academy & Volcano Bonus Tiles
description:
The first of two bonus tilesets created for the Clockwork Wars Kickstarter. This expansion consists of four new tiles:


     Two Academy tiles, which are used to generate additional workers.
     Two Volcano tiles, which erupt and cause chaos and destruction.


Also included is a double-sided reference card (thick cardboard stock) with all necessary rules.

boardgame: Clockwork Wars: Gulag & Dynamo Bonus Tiles
description:
The second of two bonus tilesets created for the Clockwork Wars Kickstarter.  This expansion consists of four new tiles:


     Two Gulag tiles, which are used to capture and remove enemy soldiers.
     Two Dynamo tiles, which provide potent scoring opportunities.


Also included is a double-sided reference card (thick cardboard stock) with all necessary rules.

Mech Arena is a tactical card-wargame where you pilot giant Mechs in gladiatorial combat. Where your goal is to defeat your opponents by shooting, hacking and kicking their Mechs to bits. An easy task, save for the fact they are trying to do the same to you. Equal parts MMA and demolition derby it is no wonder Mech Arena is the sport of choice for all citizens of the twenty third Century.

To get started build your Mech Deck from a variety of System, Chassis and Pilot microdecks. Then combat each other in a turn based battle on the arena game board. The battles are quick pace and every draw and play of the card counts because when your deck is depleted you are out of the game.

Litterateur is a card-drafting and word-building game for up to five players.

For five rounds, players will draft cards into their hands and use these cards, along with three common cards, to produce a word. the player with the best word will get to choose any three cards in play to put into their score pile. The player in second place will get two cards and players in third and fourth place will get one card each.

In the sixth round, players will use these previously scored cards to produce a final word. The player with the best word in this final round wins the game.

To win, players must draft wisely at the beginning and end of each round.

Part of Bandai's Party Joy series of lighter boardgames. (#27)

This game about home delivery in Japan.

The players draw luggage card, load them on their trucks and gain victory points when the deliver them at their destination. However, the destinations are not easily found. Moreover, there is a deadline, depending on the type of luggage, and players receive no points when they are to late.
In addition, when a player catches up with a truck of another player, he make take some of his luggage.

It became stereotypical pick-and-deliver game, published in Japan about three years earlier than Auf Achse.

Already out of print.

2 different cards from the Abacus "Katalog-Edition 16" from their catalogue 2015.

The "Signalfeuer" (signal fire) cards can be used as alternative starting cards and start with a meeple (= scout) added. Game play runs as usual and ends until 4x4 cards have been played.
 For scoring a scout on a "Signalfeuer" creates a straight viewing line from tower to tower to tower and so on. Each Tower scores one point.

into the woods is a forest walk simulator.

Find your way through the forest, enjoy the scenery, or -  dare to explore the forest in the rising mist.. will you be able to find your way home?

The game uses unique semi transparent tiles to create a three dimensional landscape drawing for the player to explore - and get lost in.

To win the game you have to reach the other end of the forest,
fulfill a task on your way (like climbing the highest mountain),
and finally return home safely.

But beware of the fog:  The longer your expediton takes, the foggier it gets -  and getting stuck in the mist means losing the game...

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – The Road to Winterfell
description: Ancient evil awakens in the lands beyond the Wall. The last Targaryens bind their future to the Dothraki lord, Khal Drogo. From the heights of the Eyrie in the east to the heart of Casterly Rock in the west, the Great Houses of Westeros plot to increase their own power. Now, you can continue to follow the events of A Game of Thrones in The Road to Winterfell, the second Chapter Pack in the Westeros cycle! Like the other packs in this cycle, The Road to Winterfell is filled with iconic characters and locations from the first book in A Song of Ice and Fire. Here, you’ll meet iconic characters like Rakharo and Syrio Forel, and you’ll find a Targaryen attachment so powerful, it’s limited to one copy per deck! The Road to Winterfell also quickly expands the number of available plots with two new neutral plots and one plot loyal to House Lannister.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
User summary:
This is an arithmetic puzzle game in which addition is the key to victory! Ten pieces inscribed with the numbers 1 to 10 are placed on the board and the person with the higher number left over at the end is the winner. If one player runs out of legal moves and cannot place a piece during the game, he loses.

Pieces are placed next to one another on the board. To place a piece next to a single other piece on the board, a player must place a piece with a higher number (whether even or odd). To place a piece next to two or more other pieces on the board, a player must place a piece a piece with the same number as the first digit of the sum of the pieces already on the board.

The game demands that players do a lot of addition to find the pieces that can be placed and to understand the strategy behind it.

Publisher's summary:
足し算ガ決め手の算数パズル的ゲームです。
数字1～10が書かれたの10個の駒をボードに配置して最後に残った駒の数
が大きい方が勝ちとなります。ただし途中で合法手が無く置けなければ負け
となります。
ボード上の駒に接するように配置して、接した駒が1個ならそれより
も大きな数字（偶数奇数の区別有り）、2個以上なら接した駒の合計数の一の
位と同じ数字の駒が置けます。
配置出来る駒を見つけゲームの戦略を考えるには一度に沢山の足し算が必要なゲームです。

Not to be confused with Beehive

Rome: from Republic to Empire is a solo game in which you take control of the Roman Empire in the period from 200BC to 100AD. You will need to conquer the provinces of Europe, the Middle East and North Africa using a card based conflict system. Gather resources, using a dice rolling mechanism, to expand the glory of Rome in each of six key areas: Military, Spiritual, Production, Commerce, Political and Wonders.  However, beware! This is the time of the slave uprising and so if you fail to keep your citizens happy they will revolt and ultimately overthrow your rule.

Objective: Be the first leading your riding stable to biggest success.
As a owner of a riding stable each player moves along a circle according rolled number; the space you are landing on requires certain activities like giving correct answers, buying horses, participating in tournaments, 'event cards' (scandals)...
depending on result you may move your meeple forward on a ranking list. First player entering target zone (250 victory points) is the winner.

Family game in which you throw the dice and move pawns.
Number of Players 2 - no limit but above the 8 players played not very well.
The game refers to the fairy tales.

The players have to move their three pawns on the track of their color from space 1 to space 100. Movement is determined by the "Verolette" in the center of the game board. The "Verolette" gives results from 1 to 9.
Some spaces have a Symbol with Special meaning.
Dot: Move 3 spaces forward and move an opponent 3 spaces backward. 
Cross:  Move 6 spaces forward and move an opponent 6 spaces backward.
Crossed dot:  Move 9 spaces forward and move an opponent 9 spaces backward.

The players have to move 4 or 6 pawns into the center of the game board. Movement is determined by a die. Under certain conditions, the mpawns can change from an outer into an inner circle. On the green circle there are 4 meeting places. The first two opponents to meet there combine their pawns (stack one on the other) and are allies for the rest of the game. They win or lose as one. The combined pawns can be separated, if an opponent lands on a space that is already occupied by two combined pawns.

boardgame: Trivial Pursuit: Pop Culture – Quiz Night
description:
It's popular culture- in a box!
Trivial Pursuit Popular Culture Quiz Night edition is all about entertainment, gossip and trends of modern times. The all-new question cards include NZ popular culture and cover everything from sporting heroes to fashion icons and mainstream media.

It’s like charades if charades made a lot of questionable choices, got hooked on meth, and became sexually insatiable. One player draws a card and silently acts out the dirty word or phrase. Every one else tries to guess while trying not to shit themselves laughing. There are also wild cards with special missions and side games that will embarrass and delight.

There are games that cross the line, this game screws the line and give it the herp.

In the beginning, the oldest player in the group is named The Warden of Filth. The Warden draws a card and silently acts out the dirty word or phrase. The first player to correctly guess wins the round and holds onto the card. After each round, a new player becomes Warden following a clockwise or counter-clockwise direction.

Some cards have special missions and side games that will have you sharing your darkest secrets, arm wrestling on someone's back, yelling "penis," and learning more about your friends than you ever wanted to know.

Wŭ Xíng is a family friendly hand management game where players attempt to gain 15 points in each of the five elements of the Tao.

Players use the creation power of the elements during the creation phase in order to eliminate all cards from their hand. Once a player is able to discard all of their cards the destruction phase begins.

During the destruction phase all players that still have cards work together to destroy as many of the remaining cards as they can.

Once all possible destructions are made, the winner of the hand is given the points for the remaining cards, and a new hand is played.

boardgame: Nothing Personal Expansion Pack #1: Game Designers
description: 9 new gangsters based on game designers

boardgame: Nothing Personal Expansion Pack #2: The Dice Tower Crew
description: 9 new gangsters based on The Dice Tower Crew

boardgame: Nothing Personal Expansion Pack #3: Movies and TV
description: 9 new gangsters based on Movie & TV personalities

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Fokker Dr.I
description:
From the publisher:

The Fokker Dr.I triplane saw widespread service in the spring of 1918. It became renowned as the aircraft in which Manfred von Richthofen, the "Red Baron" gained his last 19 victories. The Dr.I offered exceptional maneuverability, due to the rudder and elevator controls that were light and powerful. Thanks to the exceptional skill of the Red Baron and his "Flying Circus", the Dr.I triplane has entered the popular imagination as the most famous and iconic airplane of WW1. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Aviatik D.I
description:
From the publisher:

The Aviatik D.I was a single-engine, single-seat fighter biplane. It was also known as Berg D.I or the Berg Fighter because it was designed by Dipl. Ing. Julius von Berg. The D.I was the first fighter aircraft designed by the Austro-Hungarian Air Service (Luftfahrtruppen). It was flown by a number of the famous aces, such as Frank Linke-Crawford, Karl Sabeditsch and Karl Turek. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Siemens-Schuckert D.III
description:
From the publisher:

The Siemens-Schuckert D.III was a German single-seat fighter built by Siemens-Schuckert Werke. The D.III was a development of the earlier Siemens-Schuckert D.IIc prototype, and it was appreciated by enthusiastic pilots for its handling and its rate of climb. It was flown by a number of famous aces, such as Helmut Lange, Josef Veltjens and Oliver Von Beaulieu-Marconnay.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Sopwith Triplane
description:
The Sopwith Triplane was a British single seat fighter aircraft designed and manufactured by the Sopwith Aviation Company. Pilots nicknamed it the Tripehound or simply the Tripe. The Triplane became operational with the Royal Naval Air Service in early 1917 and was immediately successful. It was flown by a number of famous aces, such as Raymond Collishaw, Roderic Stanley Dallas and Robert Alexander Little.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

–Description from the publisher

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – SPAD XIII
description:
From the publisher:

The SPAD XIII was a French biplane development of the earlier highly successful SPAD S.VII. It was one of the most capable fighters of the war, and one of the most widely employed. The single-engine Spad XIII was faster than its main contemporaries, and it was renowned for its speed and strength in a dive. This aircraft was flown by famous French and Italian pilots and also by American pilots including the ace Eddie Rickenbacker. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Hanriot HD.1
description:
From the publisher:

The Hanriot HD.1 was a World War I single-seat fighter aircraft, combining clean lines with a light wing loading. Manufactured in France, the HD.1 was supplied to the Belgian and the Italian air forces, and it proved highly successful. It was flown by a number of the famous aces, such as Belgian Willy Coppens and Italians Mario Fucini and Silvio Scaroni.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – SPAD S.VII
description:
From the publisher:

The SPAD S.VII was the first of a series of highly successful biplane fighter aircraft produced by Société Pour L'Aviation et ses Dérivés (SPAD) during the First World War. The S.VII was renowned as a sturdy and rugged aircraft with good climbing and diving characteristics. It was flown by a number of famous pilots, such as the French ace Georges Guynemer and the American pilot Robert Soubiran.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Fokker D.VII
description:
From the publisher:

The Fokker D.VII was a German World War I fighter that quickly proved itself to be a formidable aircraft. The D.VII was noted for its high maneuverability and ability to climb at high angles of attack, its remarkably docile stall, and its reluctance to spin. It was flown by a number of famous German aces such as Hermann Göring, Gotthard Sachsenberg and Rudolf Stark.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

This is not a complete game.

From the publisher:

Two bands of nomadic survivors are on a high-speed trek down the rubble of Interstate 9 to what is fabled to be the last source of water. Will you be able to quench your thirst or will die trying? In an odd mix inspired by Wings of War, Mille Borne, and Mad Max, we have a head to head card game with dice rolling, location smashing, and card shooting fun!

A deck of Distance Cards is shuffled and placed between both players. A deck of Item Cards is also shuffled and place between both players. Finally, a set of six vehicle cards (representative of each player's car layout) and a gear box card is given to each player.

Players take turns drawing cards from the Distance Deck and rolling 1d6 to try to beat the difficulty value listed on each card. Different locations have different difficulty values, sometimes causing damage to vehicles or rewarding players with items like armor or weapons. As players successfully maneuver through locations, they add these cards to their Distance Traveled deck.

Players may also choose to attack each other to cause damage to their vehicles, use items to boost their position, or press their luck and avoid disastrous terrain (no one likes minefields).

The player with the most Distance Traveled cards at the end of the deck or have gained 10+ cards than their opponent wins.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Albatros D.Va
description:
From the publisher:

The Albatros D.V was a fighter aircraft used by the Luftstreitkräfte (Imperial German Air Service) during World War I. The D.V was the final development of the Albatros D.I family, and the last Albatros fighter to see operational service. The improved D.Va version was widely used by the Luftstreitkräfte, and piloted by German aces as Udet and Jacobs, until it was replaced by the Fokker D.VII in the last months of the war. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Albatros D.II
description:
From the publisher:

The Albatros D.II was a German fighter produced in response to pilot complaints about poor upward vision in the Albatros D.I. The forward view was improved with changes on wings and cabane struts, and this aircraft was appreciated for its impressive climb rate and for its frontal twin machine gun. It was flown by a number of famous German aces such as Oswald Boelcke and Manfred von Richthofen and the Hungarian Rudolf Von Szepessy.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

This is not a complete game.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Sopwith Snipe
description:
From the publisher:

The Sopwith Snipe was a British single-seat biplane fighter of the Royal Air Force (RAF). The Snipe was not a fast aircraft, even by the standards of its time, but its excellent climb and maneuverability made it a good match for contemporary German fighters and it was selected as the standard post-war single seat RAF fighter. It was flown by a number of famous aces such as William George Barker, Alexander Kazakov and Grigoriy Sapozhnikov. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Fokker E.III
description:
From the publisher:

The Fokker E.III was a German single-seat monoplane fighter, the first aircraft armed with a synchronized, forward firing machine gun. This airplane has been also used by the Austro-Hungary Empire and Turkey during World War One. The Fokker E.III was the definitive version of the Eindecker, with larger, newly designed wings than the original Fokker and was piloted by Aces as Max Immelmann, Hans-Joachim Buddecke and Oswald Boelcke. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards. This is not a complete game.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Morane-Saulnier Type N
description:
The French fighter-scout Morane-Saulnier Type N was the first French aircraft to be developed specifically as a fighter. It had an advanced and aerodynamic design, but was not easy to fly due to its stiff controls and high landing speed. Introduced in 1915, the Morane-Saulnier N was adopted by French Aéronautique Militaire, GB Royal Flying Corps and Imperial Russian Air Force. This aircraft was seen on the western front in April 1915, and was piloted by Aces such as Sergent Eugene Gilbert, Jean Chaput, and Jean Navarre.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards. This is not a complete game. You must own a WW1 Wings of Glory - Starter Set to play or WW1 Wings of Glory - Rules and Accessories Pack to play.

– Description from the publisher.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Bristol F.2B Fighter
description:
From the publisher:

The Bristol F.2B Fighter was a British two-seat biplane fighter and reconnaissance aircraft of the First World War flown by the Royal Flying Corps. Despite being a two-seater, the F.2B proved to be an agile aircraft that was able to hold its own against opposing single-seat fighters. It was flown by a number of famous aces such as the Australian Eustace Headlam and the British W.F.J. Harvey. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Roland C.II
description:
From the publisher:

The LFG Roland C.II, usually known as the Walfisch (Whale), was an advanced German reconnaissance aircraft of World War I. Because of its great speed, when it was first introduced, it could be intercepted only from above. It was also used in a fighter escort role and had a crew of two, pilot and observer/gunner. It was flown by a number of famous German aces, including The Red Baron, Manfred Von Richthofen. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Airco DH.4
description:
From the publisher:

The Airco DH.4 was a British two-seater biplane of the First World War. It was the first British two seat light day-bomber to have an effective defensive armament. The DH.4 proved a huge success and was often considered the best single-engine bomber of the War. It was flown by a number of famous British and American aces, such as Frederick Cotton and Charles Bartlett. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW1 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW1 Wings of Glory game product. boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Gotha G. V
description:
From the publisher:

The Gotha G. V was a long-range heavy bomber used by the Luftstreitkräfte (Imperial German Air Service). This bomber was agile for its size and difficult to shoot down, but because of its large size, and its unexceptional engine power, it demanded high skill from pilots during takeoffs and landings. The Gotha G. Vs were principally used as night bombers, and dropped 83 tons of bombs over England.

Subtitle: Being the truthful account of a tabletop wargame for wee ships in the Age of Discovery, put down in writing by Nicholas Wright Esq. and accurately rendered through illustrations by Orestis ‘Orestix’ Ermeides Esq.

Galleys and Galleons is a standalone set of naval wargaming rules based on the Song of Blades engine. G&G allows players to fight out fast-paced naval battles without cluttering the table with charts or unnecessary record keeping.  Note that no miniatures are included: players can use any sailing ship miniatures of any scale from any source they want.

Easy to learn, fast to play, games concluded in around one hour.

Flexible: designed for each player to command up to 6-7 ships, the rules are equally enjoyable playing one-on-one duels or small fleet actions with multiple players per side.

Although the core rules focus on early modern voyages of discovery, mercantile exploitation and piracy, additional rules cater to players wishing to sail for stranger shores with the inclusion of mythical beasts, submersibles and more.

Expansive - includes rosters for 36 historic vessels plus numerous shore forts, monsters and more.

description:
Exciting and educational boardgame for learning about the Kanza language and culture. The traditional language of the Kanza people is Kadnzele, or simply Kanza. ALthugh it is a separate language, it is closely related to the languages of the Osage, Ponca, Omaha and Quapaw tribes. Kanza is a Siouan language, and so it also has similarities in grammar and vocabulary with loway, Otoe-Missouria, the languages of the Sioux peoples, as well as more distant ties to Crow, Mandan, Hidatsa, Biloxi and others.

Wajiphanyin is played as a roll and move game. Points are awarded for translations from English to Kanza, Identifying animals, other objects and words  with the Kanza words, and translating Kanza to English. An emphasis is placed on worlds from the natural environment. Score sheets are used to keep track of the points and task. The player with the highest score at the end of the game is the winner. The duration of the game (usually time) should be decided prior to playing.

During a long and very devastating period in the history of the tribe, usage of the language began to taper off dramatically. This trend continued into the twentieth century until only a handful of the fullbloods in the 1970's could speak fluently. Today all of these elders are gone. The Kanza people today speak English as a first language, but many can still understand and use the Kanza words and phrases.

The Kaw Nation is interested in both preserving the language and more importantly reviving it. The Kanza Language Prodject is a special department of the tribal operation devoted to this task. This game is an important part of the education and preservation of this language and its importance in the culture.

From the publisher's website:

In the spring of 1943, Benito Mussolini ordered the Italian Fascist Milizia Volontaria per la Sicurezza Nazionale (MVSN, better known as the Blackshirts) to form an armored division. The “M” Armored Division would draw its personnel from battle-hardened Blackshirt veterans of the Eastern Front, and be equipped with the very latest German-supplied weapons and vehicles.

The M Division received its weapons and began training, but never actually entered combat despite Mussolini’s desire to show his German allies what motivated Italians could do when armed to the same standard as the German Army. Intended as a politically-reliable palace guard for Mussolini, Il Duce hoped the division could be made ready for combat in Tunisia, and then later pressed for its deployment in Sicily. When Mussolini fell from power in July 1943, the division was still not ready for action.

Blackshirt Division is a supplement for Panzer Grenadier: An Army at Dawn, allowing Mussolini’s boys to fight the Americans and British in North Africa. It is not a complete game: ownership of An Army at Dawn (and only An Army at Dawn) is necessary to play all of the 10 scenarios included. Blackshirt Division also includes 88 laser-cut and mounted playing pieces.
