description:
The expansion Power Grid: The Stock Companies offers players a completely new game experience, specifically three new ways to play either Power Grid or Power Grid Deluxe.

The main game is The Stock Companies: As shareholders, the players want to get control over different stock companies so they can acquire the greatest wealth.

The two variants are a little bit closer to the well-known Power Grid experience.

• The Biggest Electricity Distributors: In this first variant the players are shareholders who try to get shares of the most successful companies: the companies that supply electricity to the most cities.

• The Competition of the Private Companies: In this second variant the players control their private stock companies and try to establish them successfully on the electricity market. They sell shares of their own companies to generate more cash and they are able to buy shares of other players’ companies. Thus, they can also earn a portion of the other players’ successes.

If you own all Power Grid maps and the expansion New Power Plants, this expansion offers 222 new possibilities to play Power Grid and Power Grid deluxe.

The rules for this expansion are based on module 9 "Shares" of the game 504. As 504 is a modular game system, we are able to take that module and mix it with Power Grid. The results are three different options to play this expansion. For the main game, we focused on the first option of the shares module, so you will discover a lot of surprises. Both variants are a little bit closer to the well-known Power Grid experience; they are for gamers who want to experiment with the game system.

Medspil is an entertaining quizgame, where you will use your knowledge and strategic skills to win over your opponents. You either play alone or in teams.

There are always 3 possible answers to each question, and your or your team chooses the question category each turn. There are five different categories; Nature, Science and Health; Entertainment; Useless Knowledge; Sport and Games; History, Geography and Tourism.

First team pick a category, and answers a question. If the opponents disagree, they can supply their own answer - but if it's incorrect there will be consequences. The team that answers correctly will move the category line towards themselves.

First team to get three category lines on their side of the board wins the game.

There are no dice or luck in the game, only you and your teams knowledge and will to take a chance.

boardgame: Mars Attacks: The Miniatures Game – Extermination
description:
Just when it seemed like things couldn’t get any weirder! The final push for Greenville is underway, and both sides call on heavy reinforcements – and one or two oddities, in order to get the job done!

The Science Division arrive to bolster the Martian Invasion effort. Under the direction of the insane genius of Chief Surgeon Gorl, they are here to finish the job that Tor started, whether he likes it or not. And as if that wasn’t enough, they bring giant bugs with them as well.

To meet this threat, Novas Vira have called in their most elite commando unit – the infamous Tiger Corps. Led by General Jaret, this mixed bunch of desperadoes have been fighting the Martians for years, and pack a punch that Tor and Gorl will not soon forget. Emerging from the ruins to help them comes the biggest terrestrial non-human defender yet, as Tunga the gorilla takes to the fight along with her keeper Craig.

boardgame: Mars Attacks: The Miniatures Game – Attack from Space
description: The heroes of IDW’s Mars Attacks comic arrive on your tabletop to make your games even more epic! General Zar, still bitter from his disastrous first mission to earth in 1962 is hell bent on revenge. Buck Spencer, ex-astronaut, veteran, US Senator and all-American hero, desperate to serve his country and his planet one more time. Xiuhcoatl, an Aztec warrior out of his own time who’s found his own way of dispensing justice on the invaders. And Sidney Rose, sitting atop his beloved pet flea Henry, now the size of a tank thanks to Martian interference.

boardgame: Mars Attacks: The Miniatures Game – Martian Dredd
description:
Result of a genetic experiment that even the Science Division doesn’t want to be associated with, Martian Dredd is a menace to the whole invasion effort! Lethally efficient, and armed and garbed as a representative of justice, this insane Martian recognizes no loyalty to anyone, and wishes only to punish the guilty and uphold the law – whatever that means!

This set contains 4 pre-assembled plastic figures including:

Martian Dredd
    2 Dead US Troopers
    1 Dead Martian
    Clear plastic helmet

A family game of skill, excitement and fun played in familiar surroundings.

You build your companies in the centre of the city where space is at a premium and most valuable!  Can you beat the competition to become the most successful entrepreneur in town?

Complete with a beautiful playing board (which is a map of the city), 80 company cards (4 per company, 5 companies per player), 8 marker tokens, 3 dice and full easy to understand instructions.  Includes fascinating information on the companies taking part.

On your turn you roll dice (once or twice) and choose how to use them to adjust your horizontal and vertical markers.  These determine the position where you may (a) Add a tile, (b) Remove a tile, (c) rotate a tile or (d) challenge the owner of the tile in that space. The four tile for a company when placed in a square and correctly-oriented form the company logo, which cannot be removed/challenged.  Locations nearer the middle of the city score more points than those on the edge (score is the sum of the distances from the nearest edges.

Any Number can play.
1344 questions on the British Royal Family in 6 categories:-
Queen & Duke
Charles & Diana
Mixed Monarchs
Queens Family
Royal Riddles
Miscellaneous

language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Yokai is a simple card game, the players task is to beat the other demons using a trio of colorful cards during the 9 turns of game. A turn ends when a player close a monocolor or a three colors trio, monocolor trio count all the points on the cards, otherwise a three colors trio don't count the card with the average points value. The player with the most points at the end of the ninht turn will become the new owner of the temple of Murochi, while the other players will have to suffer the wrath of the homeless Monk.
 STORY
Crazy umbrellas and catfishes that wear kimono?! There is a strange air in Murochi Temple tonight.. It Seems that some spirits are living here... Sounds Interesting, who knows which of these demons will win the game? Unless they do not wake up the old monk.....

The Society of Eccentric Inventors is a board game for two to five players. Each player takes on the role of an inventor and member of the eponymous Society of Eccentric Inventors. In their boundless wisdom, the Society has decided to put together a friendly competition to determine the greatest inventor among their ranks. To win that fine honor, inventors must manage their resources and eccentricities and watch out for spies, as each inventor creates remarkable inventions to impress and outdo his or her fellow Society members.

boardgame: No Lo Testeamos Ni Un Poquito
description:
No Lo Testeamos Ni Un Poquito is an expansion for We Didn't Playtest this At All fully compatible with We Didn't Playtest This At All Legacies mechanics.

The game brings 42 new card 100% “meid in Argentina”, they are more Argentinian than ever!

This expansion mixes the best (and the worst) of argentinian culture and lots of winks to geek and pop culture with cards like "Mangazo", "Jodete", "Macanudo", "Mazinger Beta", "Heisenberg" and "Boda Roja" among others.
family: Game: We Didn't Playtest This At All

description:
Renards et Poulets (Foxes and chickens) is a travel game dated 1980.

It is a 2 player game for ages 10 and up.

Goal of the game: 
The chickens have to occupy 9 blue positions (the foxes lair).
The foxes have to eat the chickens en stop them conquering their lair.
The winner is the one who fulfills their goal first.

How to play:
The chickens may move one place following the black lines on the board. They can't go backwards. The Foxes can go in all directions ; they can jump any number of chickens, in condition that the end of that straight line there is an open place on the board, and eat the chickens he has jumped. The eaten chickens are then removed from the game. If a fox forgets a possible jump he must wait for the other to complete a jump before moving.
Chickens can't eat foxes, but they can block them.
The game starts with 20 chickens so if the foxes eat 12 or more they have won because the chickens can't occupy the foxes' lair which contains of 9 places on the board.

A star is dying.  Harvest resources, hire crew, modify your ship, and manage to get out with the highest profit before it supernovas.

Novanauts is a card and dice game for 2 to 4 people that will take around 1 to 2 hours to complete. Players in the game act as the sponsor of a space salvage mission. The star in this solar system is close to going supernova, so time is limited.

Each player starts with a ship capable of harvesting resources from available planets in the solar system for profit. Each planet has a unique mixture and abundance of each of the possible resources: Noble Gas, Organics, Mineral Ore, and Research Samples.  Resources are claimed by rolling dice to see if a particular harvest was successful. The monetary value of each resource is set by a market, which shifts up and down as demand grows or shrinks for the resource.

As the game progresses, players will hire crew and buy modifications to enhance their ship and gain new abilities to help them reach this goal. Crew usually grants small buffs, and modifications often grant new abilities.  While a handful of weapons exist to punish a player who has put all his victory points in one basket, it is often not an efficient use of time or resources to pursue hostilities.

The game progresses through events called solar instabilities.  Each die roll has a chance to produce one or more of these events.  As more and more of these events occur, the player's home base, Stardock, slowly moves further and further away from the unstable star.  When the Stardock reaches the last planet in the system, it prepares to leave and does so immediately before the star explodes.

The game ends when either the star goes supernova (harming any players still left in the solar system) or every player safely leaves before that point.  The winner is the player who has accumulated the most victory points through collecting money, crew, modifications, and research samples.

Conan: Rise of Monsters (CROM) is a new skirmish miniatures game based on the Conan stories by Robert E. Howard as well as the amazing Marvel and Dark Horse Comics. The game features exquisite 28mm plastic pre-painted miniatures in Starter Boxes supplemented with separate warbands, leaders, and monsters. It is a completely scaleable tabletop miniatures game with rules, special dice, and markers!

Thoth-Amon has assembled the blasphemous Legion of Set to bring the Serpent God into the world of Hyboria. His sorcerers, swordsmen, and serpents scour the lands gathering ancient artifacts and destroying the icons of those gods who might oppose his apocalyptic plans. King Conan of Aquilonia has forged an alliance to stop them--the Circle of Iron. His knights, archers, pikemen, war mammoths, and other beasts are all that stand in the way of the dread sorcerer and his unholy minions.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Game Over: Remix is the sequel to last year's successfully funded Game Over. It's a card game that mixes fast paced battles with dozens of video game inspired characters and items. Accommodating 2 - 6 players, rounds last 30 minutes each with gameplay specifically designed around replay value. The 198 card game is a mix between random chance and thought provoking strategy.

Game protagonists are simple creatures. They're programmed to either kill, collect points, or find treasure. So when the TV goes OFF and they're left to their own devices, they continue trying to do just that. The characters refer to these achievements simply as "Wins", and their thirst for them is insatiable. In Game Over, each player will take control of three characters to compete for a win in 1 of 3 ways.


     Last Man Standing: Player's are knocked out of the game once all 3 of their characters have died. Be the last to survive for this win scenario.
     High Score: Gems can be collected throughout the game for bonus points. Be the first to accumulate 25 or more points to win this way.
     Quest Complete: There are 3 enchanted golden items hiding within the deck. The first player to find all of them has completed their quest and won the game.

Election is a poker style card game where the absurd events of the campaign trail dictate the action. Each player's hand consists of cold hard cash and political strategy.

There are 3 decks of cards in Election, the States, the War Chest and the Campaign Trail decks. The goal of the game is to win the most votes. Votes are won by winning Elections, in which a set # of states are up for grabs.

Players are dealt 7 cards War Chest cards at the beginning of the game. War Chest cards consist of money, boosts and strategic, "take that" cards that allow you to affect the "Elections" and the other players winnings.

On their turn players draw from the humorous Campaign Trail deck to find out if their Campaign is going well, in which they can improve their hand, or if it's not and they receive a disadvantage.

When a player draws an Election Card, the appropriate # of state cards are drawn and an Election takes place, in which each player plays their highest valued hand. However, "Take That" cards, such as steals, and recounts can affect the results. The winner of the Election wins the States and the votes. After an Election, players turn in their cards (unless they drew the option to retain a card) and everyone is dealt a new hand and continues gameplay until all the States and votes are won.

From the MMP website:

The 29th Infantry Division was a National Guard division filled out by draftees, mostly from Maryland and Virginia. Its three infantry regiments traced their roots back to before the American War of Independence and through the American Civil War, leading to the 29th being called the"Blue and Gray Division."

The 29th participated in the campaign in Northwest Europe from D-Day to VE-Day, and suffered over 20,000 battle casualties (the second-most of any U.S. division in the war) as it fought from Omaha Beach through the bocage to St. Lô and Vire, then taking Brest, onto the Siegfried Line, crossing the Roer, through the Ruhr, and to the Elbe—commanded the entire time by Major General Charles H. Gerhardt, one of the ETO’s more controversial generals.

This Action Pack showcases ten of the varied, intense actions that the 29th Infantry Division engaged in from D-Day to October 1944. The two boards and the included overlays should have lots of use for actions throughout Northwest Europe.

Action Pack #11: 29 Let's Go! contains:

two 8"×22" geomorphic mapboards (71, 72)
one sheet of overlays
ten ASL scenarios featuring the new maps:


    AP101 When I Call Roll – 6½ turns, 6 June 1944, Dog Green Beach, Vierville-sur-Mer, France
    AP102 Better Late Than Never – 7 turns, 8 June 1944, Pointe-du-Hoc, France
    AP103 That Damn Bridge – 6½ turns, 8 June 1944, Grandcamp-les-Bains, France
    AP104 Linkup – 6½ turns, 9 June 1944, Auville-sur-la-Vey, France
    AP105 Cota’s Last Stand? – 6 turns, 12 June 1944, Montmartin-sur-Graignes, France
    AP106 Helluva Patrol Leader – 6 turns, 12 June 1944, Montmartin-sur-Graignes, France
    AP107 Better Fields of Fire – 6 turns, 8 September 1944, Fort Penfield, France
    AP108 Yes Sir! – 5½ turns, 8 September 1944, Kerrognant, France
    AP109 Not Bad For A Lone Croc – 7½ turns, 14 September 1944, Fort Montbarey, France
    AP110 Display Of Enthusiasm – 6½ turns, 4 October 1944, Breberen, Germany

Three new wonders cards for the game Historia given as stretch goals for backers of either the Historia: Capitals Expansion or the Historia: Path of Destiny Expansion.

The cards:
Atlantis (Era I): Lose 2VP to recover 4 Power Cubes from the Used Pool
Bomarzo (Era II): Permanently remove 1 wonder to take an Art action
La Civetta (Era III): Move 2 available Power Cubes to the Used Pool to gain 2VP

Special Ops #6 includes 8 articles, 2 ASL scenarios, 2 ASLSK scenarios, and Storm Over Normandy, a complete game.

Featured articles cover ASL Action Pack #11: 29 Let's Go!, The Mighty Endeavor (SCS), The Devil's Cauldron (GTS), and previews of both the upcoming OCS game Hungarian Rhapsody and the first game in the Battalion Combat Series (BCS), Last Blitzkrieg.

ASL/ASLSK scenarios:

     ASL O11 - Short-lived offensive: Slovakia 1939, Slovakian vs Hungarian
     ASL O12 - Loss of Hope: Philippines 1942, Americans vs Japanese
     ASLSK S58 - Exit no1: France 1944, Americans vs Germans
     ASLSK S59 - Mopping up: France 1944, Americans vs Germans
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Series: Advanced Squad Leader Starter Kit

description:
Bay Area / China map for Trains

This was given out to attendees of the 2015 AEG Big Game night as part of the swag box

The last battle against the forces of darkness will be one that you won’t come back from.  Being one of the mightiest warriors in the land it is time to prove your worth the only way you know how, in brutal bloody combat.  In the frenzy of battle you will receive mortal blows but death can wait until after the battle.  It is time to prove that you are the mightiest warrior that the world has ever seen; punch and cleave your way through the enemy forces and earn your mark in history.

Eternal Glory is a game for 2 to 4 players, in which players compete to be the first to reach one-hundred kills in battle.  Along the way the warriors will break their weapons, face formidable foes and even receive mortal injuries.  Through the use of the cards in their hand they can punch, cleave and even come back from death.

Players draw a card to their hand, play or discard.  They must lay cards in order to increase their kill count and play axes to be able to perform weapon attacks.  Players may place Formidable Foes, Break Weapon and Mortal Wound cards on rival players to hinder their progress and then use your Warp Spasm to come back from death or a Furious Assault to defeat larger foes in one all out attack.

The first player to reach or surpass the kill count goal ends the battle and will be written into the history books, while others succumb to the reaper knowing that their names will be forgotten in time.

Catch My Drift is a hilarious and interactive card game of hip hop charades. Think Taboo meets urban dictionary. Outguess your opponents in the most lingo to win! Teammates race against the clock to guess as much urban lingo as possible in 2 minutes. Each lingo card is worth 1 or 2 points.

The person giving the clues is called the shot caller. If the shot caller mentions a violation word while helping the team guess the lingo, the opponent a.k.a. security will clap the hand clapper and deduct 1 or 2 points if a violation word is mentioned.
The shot caller can mention the clue, use gestures, be creative and have fun. The team with the most points after 3 rounds wins!
Game play is 25 minutes 4 or more players required

What's more fun than flying a spaceship made of sewer pipes? Flying a spaceship made of sewer pipes, radioactive isotopes, and high explosives!

This expansion takes features from the Galaxy Trucker digital game and puts them on your table. Get ready to haul superheavy cargo, dangerous artifacts, and tasty beverages conveniently packaged in bottles of glass. Does this sound too easy? The new adventure supercards will double the danger!

This expansion requires only the base Galaxy Trucker game. Each flight becomes a special mission with a unique way of defining and rewarding success.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: The Visby: Extra Player Pack contains components allowing up to two additional players to join a game of Visby, the base game of which includes the other materials required for play. Up to two Visby: Extra Player Packs can be added to the base game, increasing the maximum player count to six.

Visby: Der Abt is a small expansion for Visby. The Monk now gains seals (victory points) instead of wares for the number of already played cards. The end of the game requires more seals and therefore the expansion comes with an additional seal card to indicate the amount of seals you have.

El Presidente is a game for 2 - 6 players all trying to become the next leader of the small banana republic of Savatori

This is achieved by taking on the role of one of the islands 6 people of note, campaigning around the island, rallying supporters at a variety of locations, employing agents to ensure you've the best entourage, delivering speeches and backstabbing the competition while all the time ensuring you have the longest and shiniest limo!

At the end of the game, the candidate with the highest accumulated popularity is made the next El Presidente!

Will you sink into political obscurity, or will you lead the tiny banana republic into it's glorious future?

Blindes Huhn: Maus & Habicht adds mice and hawk cards to the Blindes Huhn base game, along with job cards that — if completed — provide as many points at the end of the game as the number of players who contributed.

The expansion brings two options which can be implemented to the base game independent from each other, but can be added to the game both together too right from the beginning.
Mice and Hawks: Take a mouse for every chicken card you acquire during the auction phase showing a mouse. Take a hawk for every hawk on a chicken card you get during an auction (not in case of payment by another player). At the end of the game, every hawk eats a mouse (and a blind hen, if there is no mouse in your display) and every surviving mouse eats a corn.
Mission/Job cards: draw randomly two or three of these cards and keep them secret from the other players. When scoring, prove that you have met the requirements of your mission cards and earn additional points. Missions are different, for example collect a minimum of specific cards (value 1, 2, 3 or 4) or a sequence in a common color (green 1-2-3-4).

Blindes Huhn: Doppelkorn adds double grain cards to the Blindes Huhn base game. The four "Doppelkorn" cards replace 6 of the regular Golden Corn cards in the deck. During the end of game scoring, a "Doppelkorn" without Blind Hen does not have any effect. Accompanied by one or two Blind Hens the "Doppelkorn" scores +2 or even +4 points. The according Blind Hens score positive points as usual.

Be the first player to move all your four pieces around the board and into the middle, where the Ferris wheel is. This game uses the basic ludo rules but with some graphical modifications. For example, instead of a die this game uses a spinner.

You are allowed to move out one of your pieces onto the board if you get a sun when you spin the spinner. If you land on the same square as an opponent, that player's piece is moved back to its starting position.
Blindes Huhn: Farb-Mehrheiten is a mini-expansion for Blindes Huhn that consists of five colored chickens used to mark majorities for additional points at the end of the game.

Blindes Huhn: Viehdoktor adds two veterinarian cards to the Blindes Huhn base game. You can use a veterinarian to heal a Blind Hen. Discard the Blind Hen and add the veterinarian card to your display as a chicken card with value 2 of any color. If you do not use the veterinarian until the game ends you gain no benefit. You may only use the veterinarian for a Blind Hen immediately after the auction where you got this Blind Hen.
Essen 2015 add-on.

Blindes Huhn: Früher Vogel adds "first blind hen" and "first grain" pieces to the Blindes Huhn base game that can be used as an additional blind hen or grain in the final scoring for more victory points.

From the box:

A fast & fun family card game where each player races to build a fleet of Schwinn bikes.

Players collect cards by drawing, picking blind from other players' hands and by looking through the discard pile after playing a "Go To Bike shop card".
Wheels + Frame + Seat + Handlebars + a Helmet = 1 bike = 16 points.
Bonus cards can add to the score.

Most points wins the game.

This game is similar to Scrabble, but in this game the tiles don't have different values, and the squares on the board don't either.

Each player gets 8 tiles. Every round starts with the player spinning a spinner that will say whether he or she should create a crossing word in either the beginning, middle or end of an already existing word on the board. If you can't make any words you have to take extra tiles.

The first player to get rid or all his or her tiles wins the game!

The title Pinsamhetsfaktor translates to "Embarrassment Factor".

A Swedish party game consisting of a deck of cards with questions nobody should be proud of knowing the answer of. For example "What color does the drunk cell mattress have?" and "What body part is the most delicious according to cannibals?"

Each and every one of the questions are worth different points, and if you're the quickest to know the correct answer you get those points. The more embarrassing it is to know the answer, the more points it's worth.

The player with the highest score wins the game of embarrassment.

boardgame: Tjusarspelet för killar och tjejer: Sune och hans värld
description:
Freely translated from the back of the box:
"Sune and Sophie has taught their friends how to allure somebody and today they have arranged a competition of that art. Everyone wants to be in it. They have written down the names of the students and given them a score based on how easy or difficult it is to allure them. In order to succeed in their competition you have to play your allure cards cleverly, because then you get the chance to allure some of the most advanced girls/boys and than means extra points."

The gameplay is fairly easy and quite creative:
Cards worth different amounts of points are placed on the squares of the board. Your job is to move your piece to the squares that hold the highest points. Because, whatever square you land on, you get the card that is placed there (and its value is added to your score).

The tricky part is the way you move your piece. See, there is no die, so you move by playing your 4 "move cards" in a particular order that you decided before the round started. You don't know the order of the other players' "move cards" until they are actually played, so it's tricky to plan ahead how you should move in order to get the most points.

A game consists of 7 rounds. After that, the player with the most points wins the game.

Theme:
After a zombie apocalypse, several characters try to thrive upon the ashes of a ruined city.

Game-play:
Players select randomly a character at the start of the game. Each player must discard cards in order to make his character perform actions such move across the map, kill zombies or jump across security walls. Each character must complete several objectives before been able to access the safe zone and win the game.

Goal:
Be the first in complete your objectives and get access to the safe zone.

Freaky Becky is a drop dead fashion girl who has fallen apart!  Players must put her back together before time runs out! Freaky Becky rotates on her base while players attempt to place her 13 body parts and accessories.  When all of Becky’s parts have been placed, the player hits the “stop” button on the base to stop the rotation and the player WINS.  If time runs out BEFORE player has finished, Becky will spin quickly and throw off all the pieces that have been placed, and player LOSES.

boardgame: Letter Tycoon: 2015 Coaster Set Expansion Powers
description:
A promo at Gen Con 2015 was a 7 card coaster set consisting of 6 random letter "cards" and 1 bonus dual sided coaster featuring the first preview of Tycoon powers from an upcoming expansion.

If used at the table, the coaster power visible is ACTIVE for that game of Letter Tycoon.

Side A (orange):
IN GAME: Pay $1 to discard and refresh all the community letters at the start of your turn.

Side B (green):
END GAME: Earn $1: Score per patent if you can form a valid word. (minimum 3 patents)

In Business Cards, each player takes on the role of an employee of a struggling company. The branch is going to go through some significant downsizing and can only keep one employee on board. This can be accomplished through hard work, brown nosing the boss, sabotaging co-workers, or a combination of the three.

Each player starts out with three Idea cards which can be played for points or effects, and three Supplies cards which can be used to play Idea cards. The object of the game is to collect more points than your co-workers by the end of the game.

Each Idea card consists of an Employee side with an effect, a cost, and a point value; and a Boss side which contains a Boss effect. During a turn, an employee can take two actions. These actions may include playing an Idea card with Supplies card, discarding an Idea card to gain two more Supplies cards, or drawing a new Ideas card from the deck.

After each round of game play, however, the Boss decides to complicate things with an idea of his own. This is done by drawing an Idea card from the top of the deck. The Boss side action is then used as an effect for the subsequent round of game play. After the Boss action is applied to the new round, the boss moves to the right, and the player who started the last round begins the new round. The game ends when the Boss runs out of ideas (when an Idea card can no longer be drawn during the Boss's turn.)

Beardsmith is a fast-paced tile placement game. Players are dwarven beard stylist apprentices. Watch the mayhem unfold one tile at a time when an important customer comes in and all the apprentices scramble to show their master that they deserve to take his place! Make the best impression by having the least number of open ends in your color at the end of the game, but watch out for the other apprentices who might try to sabotage you with a snip of their shears or some bubble gum! Do you have what it takes to outwit your opponents and claim the title of Beardsmith?

In Beardsmith players take turns playing tiles on a start tile, which is the face of a customer. Most tiles have regular braided sections of beard on them and these must match neighboring tiles  as they are played. The players each have their own draw pile, and color. A player will have three tiles, played open hand, and on their turn will draw a tile and play one of their four tiles. There are some special tiles that can help a player out, or give another player a headache. These are scissors, gum, and the wild tile.

The game ends when all the tiles have been played. Each player adds up their score by gaining one point for each visible tile in their color, and losing a point for each open end (a section of braid that leads to nowhere) and any braided ends that touch a gum tile. The player with the highest score wins!

Beard on!

Alien Pack is fully compatible with Project: ELITE.

Miniatures


    1 Mega-Boss Caristar + Profile card.
    4 Boss Aliens Calarook, Chronovore, Ephialtes, Breedar + Profile cards.
    15 Special Aliens Slug(6), Myriad(6), Creeping(3)+ Profile cards.
    16 Advanced Aliens Shooter(4), Biter(4), Runner(4), Guardian(2), Eruptor(2)


Plastic Tokens

    8 Extermination Tokens
    8 Demolition Tokens
    8 Recon/Recovery Tokens

Mega-Boss Bundle is fully compatible with Project: ELITE.

Includes 2 Mega-Boss Aliens: Therion, Turtalon + Profile cards.

Sandman is fully compatible with Project: ELITE.

Includes Sandman Character + Profile Card.

Rahdox is fully compatible with Project: ELITE.

Includes Rahdox Special Alien + Profile card.

Originally released as a downloadable ruleset, Super Dungeon: Arena is an alternative game mode for players to use with their existing collection of Super Dungeon models.  In Arena, two players each take control of a mighty warband consisting of one Hero, one mini-boss, two spawning points, and the spawning points’ associated monsters. These warbands then do battle against one another in a challenge known as a scenario. Each scenario has its own unique rules and victory conditions, which require a warband to use its abilities and strengths in new and clever ways.

Super Dungeon: Arena includes a beautifully illustrated rulebook, an intricately detailed double-sided game board, an Arena Champion model, and numerous cards, tokens and dice for players to battle it out head-to-head. As players construct their own warbands from any combination of Super Dungeon models or from a single warband box, Arena promises to be an exciting experience each time.

Super Dungeon introduced the world to the whimsy and charm of chibi-miniature board games inspired by classic video games, and it continues to be the leader in providing a fun and lighthearted family board game experience. Strongly supported with dozens of expansions which provide new Heroes, monsters, dungeon tiles, and gameplay options, Super Dungeon: Arena is sure to be fun, new edition for longtime fans and new fans of Super Dungeon!

The expansion Eight Epics Exceed increases the difficulty of Eight Epics for those who can usually clear the base game very easily.

This expansion consists of 16 cards: 12 "situation cards" that make the disaster cards harder to clear and 4 additional disaster cards with extraordinary difficulty.

From the publisher:

Action to stop climate change came too little and too late. Geoengineering, although dangerous and unproven, is now on the table. A world like this has no winners, but can you minimize the hardship your regions face?

In Geoengineering 101, you're one of two to four world leaders, and you have two parallel but sometimes competing goals: One is to minimize the hardship your regions endure from heat, drought, or flood.   The other is to fully decarbonize the world economy.  Only by eliminating all fossil fuel use can climate change be slowed and the game won. However, in the meantime, the air conditioners and pumps running on fossil fuels can shield you from the some of the impacts.

A world where climate change has gotten so bad that geoengineering is on the table is one without winners, only those that lose less.  There's only one way to "win" in Geoengineering 101: have the least amount of Hardship when all Carbon Tokens are finally removed.  There's plenty of ways to lose: having all regions destroyed by the climate going off the charts, or when all the players suffer a total of 100 Hardship - which in a typical game, is only 10-15 turns away.

In Geoengineering 101, you'll take turns with other players to carry out geoengineering programs or acts of deep green resistance.  There's a time delay to your actions, though: the program you initiate now doesn't come up until your next turn.  In between, other players' programs happen and if uncoordinated, can trigger side effects that cause hardship, randomize the sequence of future actions, or shift global rainfall patterns.

The game takes inspiration from real climate science and geoengineering ideas, as well as the political implications of different nations unilaterally or cooperatively meddling with the weather. Enjoy the game, but let's hope that carbon emissions can be stopped before we ever have to play it for real.

A horse is to be caught. Each player secretly decides with which character (playing piece) he wants to catch it. All pieces start at the farm with exception of the horse, which starts far away on the grass. the active player chooses a card from his hand and moves the indicates character. A special sign (+, =, -) on the card tells him if he has to move the piece the same number of spaces as the previous player or 1 less or 1 more. As soon as a player moves his secretly written character onto the same space of the horse or the horse to his character, he wins the game.

boardgame: Dino Dude Ranch: Deluxe Velociraptor Expansion
description:
The Deluxe Velociraptor Expansion adds Velociraptors to Dino Dude Ranch, which brings a new scoring mechanic.

The deluxe expansion also upgrades the food resource pieces from punchboard tokens to custom cut wooden tokens.

boardgame: Star Realms: The Ark Promo Card
description:
Promotional card given out at GenCon 2015 to participants in Star Realms tournaments.

The Ark
Machine Cult Ship
Cost: 7
Ability: Scrap up to two cards in your hand and/or discard pile. Draw a card for each card scrapped in this way.
Scrapping Ability: Destroy target base.

A version of this card with alternate art was released in the Star Realms: Promo Pack I.

description:
Black Sands is a Fantasy themed Tactical Skirmish Game. Players each control a class based hero, and the game comes with 4 prearranged and unique class decks though a draft mode is also available.

Gameplay:
The game is played on a modular hex grid map, and players use their deck of cards as their hero's abilities. Cards are used to defend your hero or to attack others, as well as special abilities.

The goal of the Black Sands is to be the last player or team left standing. It can be played as a free-for-all of 2-4 players, or as two teams of 2 players. Two games used together could expand the maximum amount of players up to 8, with teams allowed in any even combination (I.E. pairs, or 4v4).

Using a pool of "energy," each round players take turns maneuvering their characters. The character pieces have hexagonal bases labeled with the characters Front, Left, and Right sides. Cards can have active effects and/or prepared effects. Players can play active effects from their hand on their turn. An example of an active effect would be "The next attack you make this turn gets +2 damage." To use a card's prepared effect, a player places the card face down in one of three piles marked for their character's Front, Left, and Right sides. Prepared cards are used as interrupts for opponents actions, and will take place before the completion of the action they are responding to. A prepared effect might read something like "Block the next attack against the prepared side."
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Ticket to Ride Map Collection 5: United Kingdom & Pennsylvania
description:
Ticket to Ride Map Collection 5: United Kingdom & Pennsylvania contains a double-sided game board with new rules for use with Ticket to Ride or Ticket to Ride: Europe with players now creating train lines in the British Isles and in Pennsylvania.

The Pennsylvania half of this map collection adds a share mechanism to the basic TtR game. Each time that a player completes a route, they claim a share of one of the companies depicted next to that route. At the end of the game, whoever holds more shares of a company than all other players receives points as a reward.

The United Kingdom half of this map collection introduces the concept of technology to TtR. At the start of the game, players can build only one- and two-train routes and only in England. By spending wild cards, players gain the ability to build routes three trains long or longer, in addition to being able to build ferries across water and build train routes in Scotland, Wales and Ireland. Players can also buy bonus cards that allow them to score extra points for taking certain actions during play. The game takes us to the beginning of the railway era, so the title correct for the whole United Kingdom of Great Britan and Ireland. 
Take notice: Designer Alan R. Moon has himself mentioned in his Design Diary of the game that "The Advanced Technology Cards were not fully playtested". He also doubled down on that sentiment in the rules forum with "I've seen several posts where players are playing with the Advanced Technologies the first time they play the game. These cards were included because we had extra cards that could be used for them, and we considered including them as a bonus, but they were never intended to be part of the basic game.".

Although officially an expansion for Ticket to Ride or Ticket to Ride: Europe, this could be used for Ticket to Ride: Nordic Countries.  However use with Nordic Countries would be limited to three players and use of spare trains.  The UK map requires 35 trains, the Pennsylvania map requires 45 trains, and Nordic Countries contains 40 of each colour (with 5 spare).

Part of Ticket to Ride series.

A set of 4 promotional Agent cards for Dominare.

Sophus Thaelin (Artisan/Knave)
Archbishop Salvatore (Clergy)
Marianna Kylene (Noble/Royal)
Susannah (Plebian/Royal)

Included inside AEG's version of Patronize

You have to stay on your toes to win the Mashin’ Max Game! You have to hurry and scurry around the board as fast as you can and gather as many berries as you can while you’re doing it. But you’ve got to protect your critters from Mashin’ Max, because he thinks mashing critters is silly fun – and you lose all your berries if he mashes one of yours! He spins and slams and you can only win by dodging his big fists!

On your turn, you will make sure Max's arm is raised, then pull back a snail lever on side of the board that makes him spin about and then slam his fist down. If it lands on a space with a creature, that player's creature is removed from play and their collected berries are returned to the berry pile. Each space on the board also has a number and pictures of berries. The number is for movement and depending on the number of berries pictured determines how many you take from the berry pile. So, if the fist landed on a 2 when you sent Max spinning, you would move any of your characters that many spaces. After you have finished moving count the number of berries on that space and put them into your stump and play passes to the next player. The game ends when the berry pile is empty or all of a player's critters have been mashed by Max and the player with the most berries wins.

Malifaux had always been a blistering cauldron of cultures, alliances and powerful personalities. Up until Now, the city has maintained a tight balance, with the Guild enforcing an iron peace.  But things are changing.  The old alliances are breaking and the tension is about to snap.  There are whispers of Neverborn who wield the magic of death, music carried on the wind which brings out men's darkest desires, and the ancient creatures from Earthside awakening and making their way to Malifaux.  The city will never be the same...

Shifting Loyalties expands the world of Malifaux, introducing a Campaign system which allows players to track the progress of their Crews over a series of games.  It also adds new models for each Faction, including the fearsome Emissaries of Fate.  Experience Malifaux like never before as characters change and evolve, and question their very loyalties.

Lingering Hues is a solitaire "tower defense" type card game. A player may play as one of the heroes, both heroes, or let a second player control a hero.

Heroes must last the night by warding off the advancing Kimryr, using a specialized dice system to match the enemy K.O. points. By playing certain "candle" cards, starting dice may be swapped for special stock of dice which eliminate higher or lower pips of the dice.

Hero traits, special abilities, and cooperation can push the battle in their favor. Players must have at least 1 Capacity Point,(health) after encountering all the enemies in the game to be victorious.

The zepuin on your shoulder flaps nervously as the giant tusks of a palgus wroth surface in the distance. It doesn't seem pleased with all the exchange of cannon fire. Neptune rises on the iceberg riddled coast. Someone put your loot on that ship, and the time to retaliate is running thin!

Overview: Plundering Pluto is a dexterity, dice and deduction game pitting two seafaring K9 pirates against one another. The first player to destroy the three sectors of their opponents ship with their p.a.w.s. canon fire is the winner.

The game has three levels of play for various types of gamers. The introductory dexterity game simply builds the strength of ships by rolling the die, then taking turns firing cannon cards to subtract their values.

The next levels of gameplay introduce ship customization with weapons and icebergs. Finally, a bluffing, deduction card game may be used to add a strategic twist. The choice method to plunder Pluto is yours!

A strategic card game of epic superpowers and divine kingdom building…

Introduction:

Descending from heaven, divine angels find themselves fashioned in human bodies. Bound to who they truly are, these angels discover themselves called by God to build His magnificent kingdom. Chosen and blessed to be given special powers from heaven, these spiritual beings race amongst themselves to influence the realm of Earth. In an epic battle of extraordinary powers, faith and fellowship, these devout angelic bishops seek God for guidance and authority. Which faithful child will God use to build His holy kingdom? God knows, but only time will tell!

Game Description:

Race for the Kingdom (RFTK) is a strategy card game, where 2-4 players become powerful angels gifted with unique individual abilities. These angels race around the realm of earth building and seeking God’s kingdom. With heavenly favor and a divine influence to fellowship with mankind, your challenge is not with flesh nor blood, but against other principalities and their impressive powers.

In RFTK, the game is about racing to achieve 1 of 3 victory conditions:

     Kingdom Expansion (place all 12 kingdom churches in play)
     Kingdom Development (stack 3 churches on a chosen number of territories)
     Kingdom Rapture (hold in hand 3 out of the 4 God cards)

You are an ambitious magistrate with a decree from the king to build the best hamlet possible. But building a hamlet isn't just about a robust economy and zoning laws. You have to negotiate the political landscape, deal with crazy things like dwarven larceny and ogre attacks, and build a wildly successful military while also bringing culture to your uncouth citizens.

In Super Hardcore Hamlet Builder Pro 5000 Of The Stars; 1599 AD (or just Hamlet Builder Pro for short), each player will design and build their own hamlets. You can start off small with tents and mining camps, and work your way up to smelters, blacksmiths, siege factories and more. But you can't just focus on military, after all, your people need to eat. Many will want a better place to sleep, too, and that is just the beginning of your job as magistrate.

Over 7 rounds of game play, each player will build a couple dozen buildings into their hamlet, while managing their income, storage, military, and culture. Each round presents a new challenge. Will you be a Hamlet Builder Pro?

Naming cartoon characters, super powers and stuff you need to survive a zombie apocalypse sounds like a cinch. But there’s a catch: in Egghead, the roll of the die determines one of five ways to play. So you might have to recall things from the 80s in alphabetical order or name stuff a pirate would say with your answer starting with the last letter of the previous player’s answer. If the egg timer goes off during your turn, one of your golden egg cards turns rotten. The last player with at least one golden egg card wins the game. Everyone else gets egg-straordinary memory and word skills as a parting gift.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Gen Con 2015 Lantern Promos
description:
The Dying Lantern and Vibrant Lantern promo gear cards were created to celebrate Kingdom Death: Monster's first ever public appearance!

The cards were originally given away at Gen Con 2015 to people who participated in a demo of the game vs. the White Lion.  If the group died, each received the Dying Lantern promo. If they survived, they were given the Vibrant Lantern promo.  Both were later made available in the Kingdom Death webstore for purchase.

These gear cards were also included with the Pinup Sci-fi Twilight Knight promo miniature, which is not part of the game.

The creator has stated that these promos are just for fun. They do not have associated recipes or rules on how to incorporate them into a campaign; it is meant to be left up to the players how to use them.

Chess married to Dice. The game consists of 8 dice, 4 in white and 4 in black. The dice are all identical, having the 6 chess pieces on each die.. You need a chess-set and board to play this game.

Four games are described:
Game 1: Kamikaze. Each player rolls 3 dice and may move any chess piece on the board that is shown on the dice. If a pair is rolled you may move that piece and throw again for a second move. If a triple is rolled, any piece can be moved and you get a further throw. If a player is placed in check, the opponent has up to three rolls to escape. If he fails to do so, it is Chuck-A-Mate.

Game 2: Pair-Fect. Same as above but only two dice are rolled. A pair rolled lets you move any piece. After a pair, the same player rolls again, even if his opponent is in check.

Game 3: Pair-Less. Four dice are rolled. Pairs in this game do not count and cannot move.Triples allow any piece to be moved and a second throw. If four of a kind are rolled, you may take any piece from your opponent and roll all the dice again. If four Queens are rolled, that is automatically Chuck-A-Mate.

Game 4: Four-Fun. Same as Game 3 but you may move any piece showing on the four dice that you roll. There are no extra moves. You may use the doubling die that is included in the game which works like in Backgammon. This is only sensible if you are playing for points over a set number of games.

Stabling Rivalry is an exciting, card-driven, streamlined simulation of thoroughbred racing. You and an opponent represent friendly, rival stable owners, who train and race horses for fun and profit. On this racing day you both have a lot of horses at the track, and you’re focusing on bragging rights. Which one of you is the better judge of horseflesh—and which stable will win the most races and side bets? Playable in 30 minutes to an hour, Stabling Rivalry will provide the thundering excitement of the sport of kings—and the ultimate answer!

From his stable of horses, each player selects one for each race. During play, a player draws a card from a small display of action cards and then plays one from his hand. If it matches the color required on his horse’s card for the current furlong, its normal value is used. (If not, it is played "penalty-side" up-- with a lesser value.)

The cards played each furlong are totaled throughout the race. If tied at the start of a furlong, initial post position breaks it. Class markers allow a player to re-draw a card-- to help himself or to hinder his opponent. Additionally, players can "jockey for position" and sometimes "dump" the face-up cards of the display; the leader and other player have separate criteria for this.

After both players have played a card for the current furlong, the top card of a special deck representing the other horses in the race is turned face-up. Some of these cards have various effects in addition to their numerical value, while others have two possible values and one of the players will have to choose which to play.

After all furlongs have been played in a race, the horse with the most total value wins. Class markers and then post position breaks ties between players. The number of races won plus betting chips won are totaled to determine the winner of the game.

I´ts time to make the carousel spin! Check the picture on your secret disc and try to build the picture by turning the three levels of the carousel on your turn. Be careful though, as you might accidentally help someone get closer to victory.

Picture Carousel is the perfect game for anyone who loves exciting twists and turns! The double-sided game board offers players double the fun - just turn it over and keep playing!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: The plushies are lost on the game board and Peanut needs your help to find them. Different colors and shapes make your task exciting. Learning about colors, shapes and numbers is fun with cuddly Ty toys!

boardgame: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Go Time Action Game
description:
Test your balance like a ninja!

It´s time to test your Ninja skills! Balance your way along the path made of sewer grates, and try to get farther than the other players. The winner is crowned the ultimate Ninja master!

Players set up a path of 16 manhole covers (4 different colors) that lead to the green manhole cover, which is the door to the Turtles' sewer home.  Each player spins the spinner to determine in which order he or she will move along that home to the finish line.  Based on each spin, players attempt to negotiate the color sequence of manhole covers that are their path.  Players must follow the sequence, moving foot, hand, foot hand as far as they can while maintaining their balance.  Once each player is done with their turn, they place their foam "sai" or Japanese sword, at the last manhole cover successfully reached.  It's from there that the player will begin their next turn.  Be the first Ninja warrior to reach the green manhole cover 3 times and become the "ultimate Ninja warrior"!
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
From the publisher:

If Cards Against Humanity and Pictionary had a baby it would be Scandalous. This is basically a drawing game where you have a card with a word on it - like Whisker Biscuit.  The defitition for Whisker Biscuit is written underneath, and you need to get your team to say "Whisker Biscuit." That's it- it's very simple, and very funny. Cards are assigned a point value of 1-3 based on how difficult we've judged it to draw, and game ends at 20 points.

boardgame: POOP: The Game Limited Edition Bidet Wild Cards
description:
POOP is an UNO-like game where the first player to run out of cards wins! Players are given a 5-card hand and take turns laying cards (pooping) but trying not to clog the toilet! If the toilet is clogged the player must pick up the entire poop stack and a new toilet is started. If 3 cards of the same color are played in a row then the toilet is Flushed and that toilet is reset with zero poop cards. Some cards make players perform crazy acts on their turns.

The Limited Edition Bidet Wild Cards pack includes four wild cards, one printed for each version of Poop: The Game in print.

The players start with 175,- gamemoney and have to get from their starting point to the roundabout and a crossing on the other side of the board and back to their starting point, while minding the traffic rules. The first to achive this, gets a bonus of 50,-. Movement is determined by a die and the traffic lights at the center of the board. While playing, the players have to pay fees or receive bonuses. The game ends when the first player returns to his starting point and the player with the most money wins the game.

boardgame: Snowdonia: Trans-Australian Railway & The Daffodil Line
description:
In Snowdonia: The Trans-Australian Railway expansion, 1-4 players help to complete one of the iconic transcontinental railways. Matching the route completed in 1937, players build from Sydney (my home town - Matt) on the East to Perth in the West, requiring changes of rail gauge (sort of). The Trans-Australian Railway includes the longest dead-straight stretch of track in the world at 478km. Players have to make use of a new resource: water, which is scarce in this desert country, as well as coping with wild weather and the possibility of drought and flash-flooding.

This advanced scenario, for 1-4 players, adds more variety to the weather, as well as introducing water as a valuable resource in the harsh climate of Australia.

With a little bit of space left in the 33-card deck template, the pack also includes a reprint of Snowdonia: The Daffodil Line, specifically just the bits for the scenario, NOT the miscellaneous extra cards.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
From the box:

Full sail ahead!
The importance of the sea was considerable in 19th century Brittany, form the Royal and Merchant Navies to the fishing fleets. The ports are as diverse as they are numerous, from the Orient to Ponant, Lorient, a port of the Royal India Company, Nantes with its international trade, Saint Malo the port-town of buccaneers, Paimpol with its Terre-Neuve breed of life-saving dogs and last but not least that high-point of fishing and maritime history, Douarnenez. At a time when the wind, the currents and the tides were master, commercial shipping and fishing was assured by 'bisquines', 'chasse-marees', 'lougres', 'goemoniers' laden with sea-weed and other ships.

Hatoup will take you on a series of original regattas with simple rules to follow, allowing you to discover the maritime feeling of the Breton ports.

Overview of the game:

Two teams of two players each compete to be the first to deliver the 4 trade goods (gold, wine, linen and timber) to the right port. There are 5 major ports: Douarnenez, Nantes, Saint-Malo, Lorient and Paimpol. Each player is native to one of the ports and their mission is to deliver all 4 commodities to the port that buys them. Each port accepts only one type of goods (for instance Nantes is the port for wine). But you cannot deliver to your own port, but to the free port of Paimpol instead (thus the Nantes player must take the wine to Paimpol). The movement of each ship is driven by the play of one card from your hand each round. There are 52 movement cards in four suits (sextant, telescope, knife and cosh) numbered 1 to 10 plus M, I, K (this is a customised poker-like deck). After playing a card you follow the instructions of how to move your ship. The goal is to deliver your cargo and bring your ship back to port. The winner is the team who docks all eight ships first.

The Golden Ages: Cults & Culture, an expansion for The Golden Ages, adds the ability to play with a fifth player, while also including new Civilizations, new Wonders, new Future Technology cards, and a new deck of Culture cards.

Players may now progress their civilizations thanks to amazing inventions, or they may spread their own religious beliefs. The challenge to reach for The Golden Ages is more exciting than ever!
This game is based on the War at Sea system and is a what-if WWI era naval simulation on conflict between Mexico and Cuba in the Gulf of Mexico and the Caribbean.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In a mysterious castle atop a lonely mountain, the world’s greatest magicians gather together to decide who is worthy enough to be accepted into their fraternal order.  Acceptance into this powerful alliance is the dream of the entire magical world, but these conjurors do not open their doors easily.  Any young apprentice who wishes to join must pass an arduous test.

One by one, the magicians appear in the great Hall of Mirrors.  As they multiply, each candidate must look past all illusions to discover each sorcerer’s most precious secret:  their magic words of power.  Those who guess correctly earn rewards; those who fail slip further and further away.  In the end, only the player who correctly identifies the most powers may join this mystical cabal.

From Goku and Sailor Moon, to Ash Ketchum and a Colossal Titan, the Anime Deck adds 100 cards inspired by your favorite new and classic shows. It also includes ten Anime-based scenarios and locations so that you can have your fights at a Maid Café or during a beach episode!

This deck can be played on its own but we highly recommend playing it with the Core Deck.

The Challenge Deck adds 100 new victory conditions for your fights. Instead of fighting to the death, now you can argue about who would win at Football, Quidditch, or The Game of Thrones. Never have the same fight twice!

The Challenge Deck is a modifier deck, and thus requires an additional game deck in order to be played.

Gambling and Hookers sees 2-4 players competing to have the wildest night out. Think the night before The Hangover, or one of Barney’s legendary nights.

Players are vying to be the first to buy a set number of vices - vices like alcohol, gambling, drugs and hookers. But each vice bought leaves you open to different downers. Buy a hooker, for example, and risk other players hitting you with a pimp card, or an S.T.D.

So it’s a take-that style card game, but one where you control which attacks you are vulnerable to.

Objective:
Be the first reaching space 75.

Players take turns rolling the die and moving the indicated number. If a player ends on a shaded space, he follows the instructions in the manual, which may cause him to pay or get a coin, skip a turn, or move back or forward.

This game was likely published before the First World War.

This game is about a King that wants to build a new castle and the players are the builders competing for the job.
The two Guilds in the game have a dislike for each other , the one being the Merchants Guild and the other the Thieves Guild.
From the merchants guild you can get the building blocks, to build a castle wall, so the King can inspect your works and see if he likes your work to give you the job of building the his castle. The Thieves guild also wants to take the job with their own builders (so later on can have control over the castle) but the Merchants guild refuses to give any request forms for building blocks. So the players can have special action through the game from the Thieves, and while the Thieves are getting paid, with Blocks from the players, they get to build their part of the Castle.

The game is a Midweight euro game with hand management while the players get to compete my bidding and majority controlling, managing the resources they get.

In Shinobi Arts, players become shinobi (ninja) and scramble for valuables using ninjutsu (shinobi arts).

The valuables have various characteristics, and the value changes depending on the combination of the valuables brought back. In addition, the number of ninjutsu that can be used is limited, and it decreases by getting valuables or by struggling against another player.

Collect information by cleverly using your limited resources. The player who brings back the most valuable items wins.

MOVIE:https://youtu.be/SC10ONrx2Y8

Comrades and Patriots is a combined game from This and That Games. It includes Most Glorious Comrade and the sequel, Most Incorruptible Patriot. The two games are playable separately or together. The players are playing Communist or Democratic leaders, vying for Proletariat or Voters. You have a hand of cards, and draw a card and play a card every turn. Each player's leader also has a power that helps them throughout the game. The first to ten million wins!

In Cadeau caché, players try to find the right gift for each of their six characters. The difficult aspect of this simple game is memory. Even if you find the right gift for a character, you must remember it when the die brings you back on the square that gives you the chance to benefit this information. Taking notes is prohibited!

Skirmish Outbreak is a set of rules for miniature wargaming set in the familiar world of the zombie apocalypse, borrowing in particular from the movie 28 Days Later and its idea of the "fast" zombie.

Turn sequence revolves around Action points that are determined each turn and then spent by the players to perform actions such as movement, combat, etc. The game is scenario based, with victory conditions changing from scenario to scenario. Scenarios can be played as one shot or linked in massive campaigns. The zombies in the game can be controlled by a player or by an AI, which makes the game particularly suitable for solitaire and cooperative play.
