description:
Panda! is a light and fun trick-taking game based on Cucumber (Agurk, Gurka).

The most important object is to not win the last trick, but if you can save the strongest and most interesting card (AMBASSADOR, FINANCIER, TIGER, MONKEY, CROCODILE, DRAGONS and PANDA) to the last trick, then it will be a great surprise for your opponent!

The combinations of these cards changes the end of the game and makes more fun and laugh!

2000 years ago, in the faraway land of China the imperial court got anxious: the beloved pets of the majestic Emperor Han has escaped, and started to play tricks with the servants of the Heavenly Empire, appearing at different spots of the court from time to time. Even though the emperor loved all of his pets, he summoned the Dragons of the Four Cardinal Directions to his court in his desperation, to have his little ones regulated.
However, he did not realize that his beloved animals, using their special powers, can easily outsmart even those enormous beasts… The offiials of the court, i.e. the players have to try collecting the FEWEST possible points after 12 turns of the game. While doing so, they can only rely on their luck and tactical skills.

Dominup is a game for 2 to 4 players that can be played with a Double-seven binary-coding dominoes set (36 tiles) or a standard Dominoes set.

There are two types of placements in Dominup. In both cases the tile must be aligned with an imaginary orthogonal grid:


     ‘Climb’ placement: The tile is placed atop two adjacent tiles of the same level, so that the numbers (symbols) of the placed tile match the symbols underneath (one of each supporting tile).



     ‘Expand’ placement: The tile is placed on the table and orthogonally adjacent to at least one tile already placed.


Randomly distribute the tiles face-down among the players (as in dominoes).

The player with the double-7 starts by placing it in the middle of the playing surface.

Starting with the second player, players take turns in anticlockwise order doing all of the following steps in order until the game end condition is reached:

1. If you can make a climb placement with at least one of your tiles, then you must do so. You can choose which tile to place, as long as the placement is legal. Repeat this action until you can’t make any more climb placements.

2. If you have at least one tile left, then make one expand placement.

If, at the end of your turn, you have no tiles left, then you’ve won and the game ends. However, the other players can continue playing to determine 2nd, 3rd, and 4th place if they wish.

Variant: Misere Dominup

In this variant, the player who runs out of tiles first loses.

Dominimum is a derivative of Mystique Energy (by the same designer) that can be played with a Double-seven binary-coding dominoes set. Dominimum is for 2 to 4 players.

To set up, shuffle the tiles face-down on the table. Each player then draws a tile without showing it to the other players, looks at it, then places it before them so the other players can’t see it. If both symbols are the same (i.e. it is a double tile) then discard the tile to the supply and re-draw until you get a tile with two different symbols; then reshuffle the supply. This is your "mission tile". Each player then draws a second tile; this is your "playing tile".

The first player places her playing tile on the table face-up. Then, starting with the second player, players take turns in anticlockwise order, placing their playing tile on the table adjacent to at least one placed tile so that the tiles fit into an imaginary orthogonal grid, then drawing a new playing tile from the supply (if possible).

The game ends when the last tile has been placed on the table, and each player then calculates her score. For each of the two symbols on your mission tile, find the largest contiguous, orthogonally connected group of that symbol. Count the number of squares in that group. Note: Diagonal connections do not count! Once you have counted the largest group for each of your two symbols, multiply these two values to determine your score.

The player with the lowest score wins. In case of a tie, the tied player who played her final tile first wins.

The Tree is a game for 1 to 4 players that is played with a Double-seven binary-coding dominoes.

Deal 9 tiles to each player (as in Dominoes) and keep the rest face-down (the supply).

The player with the lowest double tile places it on the table horizontally before her (if nobody has a double tile then the lowest ‘blank-any’ tile, or the lowest ‘one-any’ tile and so on). The tree will grow away from her, so leave enough room.
 Definitions: the central pip and the small and big circles that form the symbols of the tiles are called ‘rings’. A tile placed ‘above’ means placed horizontally on the table, adjacent to at least one tile already placed, and in the direction of growth (no stacking).

From now on, and starting with the second player and playing in anticlockwise order, players take turns placing one tile from their hand so that:

a) It must be placed above a previously placed tile so that half of it touches half of the tile already placed (remember the definition of ‘above’).

b) The symbol above has exactly the same rings as the symbol below, plus one more.
        
c) If the other half of the tile also rests above a tile, the corresponding symbols must also obey rule ‘a’. In this case, play an extra turn.
      
d) If the placed tile has been ‘inserted’ inside a hole so that there are tiles above it, the corresponding symbols of the tiles above it must also obey rule ‘a’. In this case, play an extra turn.
        
If, at the beginning of your turn, you can’t make a valid move, draw a tile from the supply (if any) and pass your turn to the next player.

If, at the end of your turn, you’ve run out of tiles, then you win and the game ends; however, the other players can continue playing to determine 2nd, 3rd, and 4th place if they wish. If, at the end, a tree with all tiles has been built, please post a picture on boardgamegeek!

If the supply is empty and all players pass in succession, then nobody wins and you have to play again.

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Treaty of Champions
description:
Treaty of Champions is the seventh Hero and Monster Collection and contains four heroes and three monster types.

Heroes:

     Jonas the Kind
     Grey Ker
     Krutzbeck
     Zyla


Monsters:

     two demon lords
     five skeleton archers
     three crow hags


Quests:

     Sindaea’s Secret
     Treaty of Champions
family: Game: Descent – Journeys in the Dark

description:
Racing games, that allow to use DieCast models (1/62 scale like Matchbox, HotWheels, etc.), or a cardboard models.

Each car has some parameters like Acceleration, Braking, TopSpeed, etc.

Depending on these parameters each driver will note the speed of his car in next turn.

Then depending on the speed each car is moved on track using 4 moving pieces. Length, and angle of moving pieces are different and depend on speed.

Cars may become damaged if collided.

No board is necessary. Race may take place on table, diorama, printed track, carpet, etc.

Smaller scale elements (1/180) are also prepared.

Simulation mode, and RC (less damage mode) mode are available.

This dinosaur card game lets children try out a great range of questions. The 100 questions are divided in two categories of age: One for 4-5 year olds and the other for 6-8 year olds. The game is played with the help of Mr and Miss Haricot, two funny characters from the game. The different categories are numerous: the different kind of dinosaurs, their height, their disappearance, the fossils...

The trivia game that you liste at Rouge FM in the morning when going to your job has transformed itself into a board game to intertain you even more. Answer theses impossible questions that draws a wierd picture of our daily habits and where answers are even more impressives.

Question impossible: The game where you can change your mind!

Each game gives you access to seven guilds with high recognition in the world of Bicolline. This game gives you a choice of 160 different cards in order to build your own army. Take the destiny of Bicolline into your hands and start the conquest of this fantasy world.

When a player has no more reputation, the game ends and the player who has the most of it wins. The game is played in multiple rounds and each players turn have 4 phases each. First you refresh used up cards, then you activate your "at the beggining of turn" cards skills if they are in your production or play area, then its battle time! You have to play a card and activate its power. Finally, you finish your turn by activating your "at the end of turn" card skills. Play continue turn base until the end.

A setting twist to the popular role playing game, Werewolf. Villagers must hunt down the Werewolves before they become the hunted.

The game takes place in a dystopian future when modern civilisation collapsed, giving way to a new world order.

Based on the 80's game "Mafia" by Dimitry Davidoff, Andrew Plotkin used the same platform to create Werewolf. It's a great group role-playing game of 7 players or more. It's easy to learn and so fun to play. With the art from Jenny Donovan. New characters fit this post apolyptical setting like the seer, the doctor or the fighter.

Kalypto is a fast-paced easy to learn quick two player card game. With a second copy of the game, you can make a bigger deck to play with more players, but you start with seven cards instead of five.

In Kalypto, players try to eliminate each other using armies of shadows. In the box you get shadows with a strength from 1 to 10 (4 of each number), action cards like seize, extort, rise and devour (4 of each) and one very powerful candle card. To set up, shuffle the deck and deal out five cards to each player and place the deck in the center. Each player chooses at least one shadow and places it face down in front of him, this is the start of Their army. If you run out of cards in your army or hand, you lose the game. You are now ready to battle!

On your turn, you choose two actions to perform between four possibilities: you can draw a card from the deck and add it to your hand, you can summon a shadow from your hand that you add to your army, you can use a power card from your hand and place it in the discard pile or you can choose one of your shadows and an opponent shadow! Reveal both cards and the lowest number is sent in the discard pile. If they are the same, they are both discarded. Each action can only be done once per turn. When you've done two of these actions, your turn ends and the next player begins his turn. There are four action cards that are self explanatory: seize makes you steal one enemy shadow and add it to your army face up; Extort allows you to steal a random card from an enemy and add it to your hand; Rise allows you to draw two cards or make your opponent draw two cards (but be careful! If a player has more than seven cards in his hand, he loses!); And finally, devour allows you to choose 1 card from an enemy hand or army and discard it (which is called the pit). The candle card always matches the power of the shadow it fight, and if a player steal this card, he loses the game! If a player draws more cards than there is in the deck, he loses! Play goes like that until there is a winner.

Sweet Tooth is the quick and easy game that everyone will enjoy.
Get ready for a sugar rush as you frantically trade sweets with your friends and family.
Pig out on someone else's sweets and avoid going on a diet.
Aim for the highest number of sweets at the end of each round.
Will you have the sweetest tooth when the timer runs out?

Shuffle the 36 knight cards and deal all the deck to all the players. Each player keeps these cards as a face down pile in front of him. At the same time, they reveal the first card of their deck. The one that revealed the highest card takes all of the cards and place them under his deck. If there are ties for the highest card, tied players play a second card face down, then a third card face up to see who wins all the cards in play. If an other tie occurs, you repeat (one face down, then one face up).

If a player loses all of his cards, he is out of the game. The winner is the one that won all the 36 cards.

The drawings and the lenght of the weapons help the children players to understand the cards value : The longest blade wins!

Pocket Adventure is a solitaire adventure game in a compact deck of cards. Gather your things and explore the land, encounter monsters, meet travellers and visit towns. Collect treasure and complete quests. Do it for glory, for fortune or just for the thrill of adventure.

A turn begins by choosing a Location to explore. Each Location has an Explore value (the number of cards you will get to pick from next turn when you explore), and one or more icons. The icons determine what happens to you on your turn. A camp icon means you get to rest, heal and change your equipment. An Event icon means you draw an Event card and follow the instructions (this will be a change in weather or a roadside encounter). A town icon means you generate a town (draw 3 cards) and choose one townsfolk to interact with from the town. A monster icon means you draw a card and battle the monster on it. If you win, you will earn treasure!

Complete three quests before you run out of Resolve or Health and you win. Run out of resolve, and you retire... run out of Health and you will meet an unfortunate end.

The game requires a minimum of 7 pages of cards to be printed and cut out.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This is a set of rules with scenario instructions and background material to create a story set in London during WW2. Players take on the role of characters involved in a net of wartime espionage, and attempt to fulfill specific goals assigned  to their characters by the scenario.

Based on the premises of the situation, players discuss possible ways to advanced the story. Next, players use the Matrix system created by Chris Engle to establish how likely certain proposed developments in the plot are to occur, and roll dice to determine how the story will progress. Discussion after discussion and roll after roll, an overall narration emerges.

Specific victory conditions exist based on the mission that is being played, but as in many storytelling games what matters the most is to work as a team towards the creation of a rich and satisfying narrative.

The year is 1919. America is in its golden age of grift. Clever thievery has moved from shell games and three-card monte to a more mature art form: the heist.

In this head-to-head match, you must compete to hire a team of professional criminals, plan heists on unsuspecting marks, and muscle out your opponent to earn money. It's all about return on investment and knowing your opponent. The player with the most money at the end wins.

The game plays in a series of 5-7 rounds, each round with three phases: The Purchase, The Plan, and The Heist.

THE PURCHASE: Spend Wisely.
Hire crew members to pull your heists. We've got Grifters, Partners, Insiders, Thieves, Muscle, Getaway Cars, and Cops on the Payroll. Spend wisely.

THE PLAN: Choose Carefully.
Plan your heists by by secretly sorting your crew cards in your hand. Place your crew face-down when ready. Choose wisely.

THE HEIST: Plans unfold.
Plans are put in motion that cannot be changed! You attempt your planned heists, but if you run into your opponent you must rely on your best muscle to save you.

-- publisher website --

A press-your-luck card game where players take on the role of a team of miners digging for diamonds.

Players take turns sending a number of their miners down into the mine to dig the diamond vein.  Each strike of the pick could score the player diamonds, but also risks a cave-in that will injure the miners and leave the player with fewer hands for the next dig.  This could even result in the player being carried back to the surface empty handed.  The player with the most diamonds once the vein has been completely mined is the winner.

The vein consists of a mix of 1-3 diamonds, cave-ins that injure 1-3 miners, or empty strikes.  The player draws cards until they choose to return to the surface with however many diamonds they've mined, or until all the miners they sent into the mine have been injured in cave-ins.

boardgame: Pulp Adventures: Journey to the Hollow World
description:
Pulp Adventures: Journey to the Hollow World is the first of a series of modular adventure games where players cooperatively adventure through the pages of a comic book. Specific tiles are placed to create each page and card sets are added to the base game for each different adventure.

Each player has a deck of cards representing their character's skills and abilities and may play cards toward encounters even when it is not their turn.

A party game where players take turns acting as judge by drawing a character card with image of a person and all other players play from their hand a description of that person and then the judge determines which player's card best fits or is most desired.  Similarity in game play to apples to apples and other games

DETAILED GAMEPLAY


     Everyone has 7 red assumption cards in hand. Assumptions cards have red backs, and simple text on the front. Some are ridiculous, some are serious.
     Player A draws a black character card from the pile without looking, revealing it to everyone else. Closing your eyes works. Holding it to your forehead works. So does holding it out in front of you.
     Everyone else plays a red assumption card from their hand about the revealed character by putting it face-down. Players draw a new assumption card from the red pile so everyone's hands contain 7 cards.
     Player A, with the character card still a mystery, flips the played  assumptions cards one at a time, reading them aloud (and inherently drawing their own assumptions about the character they haven't seen yet). Then, FINNNALLLLY, they get to flip their character card. Lol.
     Player A picks the assumption they think best fits the character. The person who played the chosen assumption is the winner, and keeps the character card, collecting it as a point earned.
     That's it! Rotate Player A and repeat. The game ends when X points are reached, or Y time has passed (you make up these numbers), or everyone hates each other. Preferably, before this last one happens. Enjoy!
publishers: You Think This is a Game LLC

description: Another high-carb expansion faction! The Grains faction also includes nine new fighters tiles, three Grains-only power cards, and a sheet of nine thought bubble clingers.

In the GATE, a strategy game for two players, each player takes four faction cards and places the cards in front of them not shuffled, but separated by faction, face up in ascending order.

Once a player purchases a card from a faction's deck, that card becomes the source of various effects (mostly for attack action) and the topmost card of each faction's deck is the source of defense. Each turn, a player can do three kinds of actions with the cards in hand:


    Enhance their factions.
    Place guard cards (face down, one card for a faction).
    Attack (to different spots).


If he succeeds with an attack, the opponent receives damage; if damage reaches a certain amount, that faction's "gate" perishes. When a player's two gates are demolished, the game ends and that player loses.

The core box of the GATE contains eight sets of factions, some of which are connected with worlds of other games, such as the DOME. More faction packs will be released after the core set.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Jane Austens Hochzeitsball: Traumpaare is a Schmiedespiele-exclusive expansion for Jane Austen's Matchmaker, in which you have to match the exact couples from the books.

Set in the near future, Earth 667C gives 2 to 6 players the opportunity to save humanity from our sun going supernova. In twenty solar cycles, the sun will explode and destroy everything in our solar system. Use the remaining resources of our planet to construct your ship in orbit. Build it fast. Build it strong. Build it to save as many humans as you can and launch your spaceship to the nearest exoplanet before the supernova.

Constructing your ship is only half the game. For those that survive the solar event and launch their ships, they must face new challenges on the way to our new exoplanet home, Earth 667C. The Journey to the new planet has boosts and perils along the way, including black holes, quasars, enemy vessels, and asteroid fields. Your ship building skills and the luck of the dice combine to determine your crew and survivors' fates. Will you be the first to Earth 667C?

Earth 667C is a card and turn-based tabletop game for 2-6 players or teams. Perfect for ages 10 and up, the game is fun, encourages strategic thinking, and includes just enough chance and player relationship dynamics to create three game phases with unique challenges.

Phase 1: Ship Building
Purchase Ship Components from Space Dock, and construct the fastest ship carrying the most people. Customize your ship by doubling your navigation for better travel. Double your engineering department for increased speed. Or, add more food and life support to increase your capacity to carry survivors. Pick your strategy for your ship and start building. Optional Ship Components are available for purchase. Components like weapon systems, shuttle bays, and radiation shield provide bonuses or protection during The Journey.

Phase 2: Launch Your Ship
When the Solar Cycle hits 8, you can launch your fully constructed spaceship. Roll the dice and aim for 12 or higher to push your ship into position for The Journey. Get 12 or lower? Then you must wait, and hope that the solar clock does not tick down any further. You must launch your ship before the clock hits zero, and the sun goes supernova.

Phase 3: The Journey
When the clock hits zero and sun engulfs the Earth, the real Journey begins. Play a single turn journey, or an eight turn journey. Roll the dice and discover what boosts and perils await you while traveling to humanity's new exoplanet home. Encounter new enemies that attack and slow your ship, or pass a strong hydrogen cloud that boosts your engines. Utilize your optional Ship Components to ward off enemies or discover wormholes to increase your speed. This phase will put your ship building skills to the test. The events during The Journey can slow the fastest ship, and propel others to victory.

Be the first ship with the most survivors to reach Earth 667C and ensure the survival of the human race on our new exoplanet home!

Dominomega is a game for exactly 3 players for the nestorgames' double-seven binary-coding dominoes set. It is the result of applying OMEGA scoring to this dominoes set.

Each player has an allocated symbol (pip, small circle or large circle). Before the game starts, shuffle all tiles face down. Each player draws one tile (don't show it to the other players).

Starting with the 'pip' player, players take turns (pip - small circle - large circle) placing their tile on the table and orthogonally adjacent to a previously placed tile if any, so that the tiles fit into an imaginary orthogonal grid, then drawing a new playing tile from the supply (if possible).

Once all the 36 tiles have been placed, players calculate their score:

To calculate your score, multiply the sizes of all groups of squares (half tiles) containing your symbol. Notice that some half tiles contain several symbols (example: the number 7 contains all three). Notice also that each symbol is present exactly 36 times.

The player with the highest score wins. The maximum possible score is 531441.

Variant for 2 players: The small circle is not used.

Chainsaw wielding bears, gun toting dogs and monster fish await you as you race across Alaska to be the first to spell 'Tundra'. Based on the award winning comic strip 'Tundra' by Chad Carpenter, you and your opponents will fall into fits of laughter with over 100 jokes that allow you to reek [sic] havoc on others or reap the benefits for yourself. This is the only Alaskan board game you will ever need to own!

The goal of Tundra is to be the first person to trek across the State of Alaska and collect enough letters to spell T-U-N-D-R-A These are obtained by; T - Ride a dog sled in Nome, U - Build an igloo in Barrow, N - Ice fish on the Yukon River, D - Hike the Chilkoot Trail, R - Commercial fish out of Dutch Harbor, A - Hunt on Kodiak Island.

Operation Iron Fist is the introductory scenario of Board Wargame Magazine #11 Iron Guards: Battle of Shanghai.  It is a P&P game with 4 page rule, 1 A4 map, and 26 counters.

Players play the commander of National Revolutionary Army (NRA) 9th Army Group and Imperial Japanese Navy (IJN) Shanghai Special Naval Landing Forces respectively.  Every hex is 3-4km across and every turn is 7-8 days.  Every units represents battalion to division.  Players roll a dice to get operation points to activate ground units to fight each other.  Player occupied the IJN HQ wins the game after 3 turns.

Set up you own sub-particle laboratory! Experiment with the most fundamental particles! Make sure you are one step ahead the other scientists! Publish your research to secure your findings! Perishing is not an option if you aim to write your name alongside the best scientists in history!

Publish or Perish is a deduction game for young scientists in which you want to find out the secret properties of each particle, collect the data, and accumulate your knowledge. But be sure to publish it before any of the other competitors do, or you might just miss the chance of winning.

Thanks to the app (and your mobile device) all secrets stay secret, and handling crucial information is both easy and intuitive. Not to mention the holograms in your hands! The augmented reality enhanced app allows you to see and enjoy experiments to the fullest!

Note: We worked hard to make sure that the science behind the game is accurate. We cooperated with scientists and consulted on all of the content.

Recall of Cthulhu presents the horrors of the mythos in a way they were meant to be, cute and cuddly! This classic matching game can be played by the most deranged patient in the Sanatorium, as well as young cultists aged 4 and up!

Included in the game are 60 tiles representing 15 creatures, items and places of the Cthulhu Mythos, as well as a player aid for playing the advanced game.

The game can be played as a simple matching game, or the advanced game can be played which adds a layer of complexity to the game. In the advanced game, tiles have varying point values, as well as effects on the game itself. The advanced game is suitable for young cultists aged 8 and up.

Description from the publisher:

Big 2 Revolution, the first release of the Revolution series from Smoothie Games, adds dynamic features to the original Big 2 shedding game. The card deck of 52 standalone poker cards features twenty cards — J, Q, K, A & 2 — that have been customized with hero abilities to enrich the original Big Two gameplay.

Level up in this apocalyptic world as the Walkers aim to have their revenge against the survivors. Munchkin Zombies: The Walking Dead is a 56-card expansion for Steve Jackson's Munchkin Zombies stand-alone game.

A simple roll and move race game played on a map of Australia overlaid with a 12x12 grid f squares numbered from 1 (Brisbane) to 136 (Sydney), with 8 unnumbered squares.
The object appears to be to move from Brisbane to Sydney via the numbered squares. 9 of the squares appear to be hazards: 5 at sea (2 wrecks, 2 contrary winds), and 4 on land (3 desert and 1 bush).

The game appears in the "Australian Town and Country Journal" on May 12 1909 and described as a 'New Game' by a Sydney Bookstall company.  An advertisement for the game was later published in the "West Australian" newspaper (Perth) on Wed 6. Oct. 1909, p.6 col. 7:

"This, it seems, is an adaptation to the Commonwealth of a map game, said to have been exceedingly popular in the different countries to which it has seen made applicable. The Australian map is marked of into 136 squares, and with the aid of dice which determine each stage and counters for each of two, three, or four players, a journey is performed throughout Australia, beginning at Brisbane and terminable only at Sydney. In the shape of contrary winds, a wreck, deserts, bush, and other obstacles to easy journeyings, the way of the traveller is made difficult, and at the same time exciting, and by an ingenious contrivance in the rules the question whether he will ever reach Sydney is attended with considerable doubt, which enhances the interest of the game. Both for young and old it should prove thoroughly diverting, and it has the added advantage of possessing considerable educational value. Wigg and Son are selling it at a price intended to bring it within the reach of everyone".

110 cartes. Pétards. Étoiles. Lettres. Cartes d’aide.

40 jeux et 1 variante. Dans tous les sens ça fuse !

« LIBERTÉ, ÉGALITE, PARTAGE ». 
Telle est la devise, dans l’univers de TOUTILIX :
On y joue avec des pétards, des étoiles, et les toutes les lettres de l’alphabet latin.
On y joue aussi avec absolument tout ce qui s’écrit avec ces mêmes lettres, et souvent dans toutes les langues en même temps. 
On navigue parfois très loin dans l’imaginaire et l’expérience. 
On réflérit ! 
Débutants et experts jouent parfois ensemble.
Petit prix et partenariat avec ATD QuartMonde : Les lettres AUSSI pour tous.

D’un jeu à l’autre de Toutilix, tout peut changer :
Les SENSATIONS, le MODE DE PENSÉE sollicité. Le TYPE : jeu de lettres, de mots pas écrits, de mots écrits, de phrases. 
La MÉCANIQUE de jeu : Rapidité, imagination, enchères, bluff, stop ou encore, course, combinaison, coopération, mémoire, stratégie, etc. 
Le DOSAGE D’ALÉATOIRE. La FORME : Cartes en main, en tas, ligne, spirale, en tapis, carré, cercle, projetées, tapées, palpées à tâtons, etc. 
Le RYTHME, l’AMBIANCE…

Alors on joue à quoi ?

À l’un des 12 jeux du nouveau livret illustré de la boîte.
Ensuite, on EXPLORE TOUT L’UNIVERS DE TOUTILIX : Un moteur de recherche simple à utiliser 
vous guidera en quelques clics dans votre choix de jeu, selon le moment et l’envie.
Livret des 40 jeux, livret jeu par jeu, et livret de la boîte à télécharger.

Pour tout ceci on clique sur www.toutilix.fr !

Driller is a hybrid, quick, family card game. Players have to devise a strategy to score the best score with cards given. There is no pile, no drawing, you get all cards at the beginning and have to deal with them!

Driller uses a mobile device to scan the cards, visualise the drilling and control points, player turns and drill temperature. The device is placed on the game box and players place the cards beneath it, so the app can scan them.

The deck consist of 44 cards - 4 different ground types, each with 10 cards with values 1-10 and one EndDrilling card. The number on a card indicates the speed of the drill. Players can score points for speeding up the drill, using EndDrilling card or matching the card ground type with the ground, currently being drilled through. But if the players drill too fast for too long, the drill may overheat and break! Be aware that other players may want to destroy the drill on purpose to stop you from scoring!

Translated from the rule book:

A lot of different pairs of cards lie scattered over the table. When revealing a card good eyes and quick hands are needed. Who keeps seeing everything and reacts fast fetches the most pairs!

The game consists of 54 cards, making 27 pairs of 2 cards each. The cards are put into a tile. Each player turns over the uppermost card on the tile and reveals it. As soon as the new card is revealed all players can put their hand on the other card of this pair, that was revealed before the current one. The player who has the most pairs when the tile is used up is the winner.

description:
Defend Burma!

Can you help defend Burma from the onslaught of the Japanese conquerors?  Or, can you expand the Greater East Asia Co-prosperity Sphere by liberating Burma from the imperial colonials?  You now have the chance to try your skills on the tactical battlefield during the crucial months between December 1941 and May 1942.  Squads of infantry, tanks, artillery, support, and even some airpower! The tools are at your command.  Can you succeed?

- 22"x28" Historical map with 3 scenarios for a campaign game or they can be played as standalone scenarios.
- 12 scenarios for the ASL geomorphic maps

The players have to create the best couples of Sakura High School.
They start at the middle of an area made of 54 cards(27 males and 27 females).
At his turn, a player can use 0, 1 or 2 actions(similar or different) among 3: 

     move one of his 2 pawns towards a card or an empty space.
     reveal a hidden card. 
     make the union of a boy and a girl with his 2 pawns on their cards.


The distance between the boy and the girl and the quality of the couple can be rewarded by any white tokens(each turn, one can be used to play 2 more actions).
Each union and each white token worth 1 victory point.
The winner is the player who has the higher number of victory points or white tokens if there is a tie.

East meets West in Club Zen, a relaxing worker placement game where the goal is to plan out your seven-day working vacation in the most Zen way possible. Manage your work and emotional stress, make new friends, attend exciting events such as hang gliding or rock climbing, collect stones for your personal Zen Garden, or take a nap! How will you spend your time at the 5-star Caribbean resort, Club Zen? The most relaxed or "zen" player at the end of their one week stay will be the winner.

Unlike traditional resource management and worker placement games, Club Zen encourages players to visit the same locations together, many times for an increased benefit for all players involved. Because of this, the game features a measure of cooperation and meaningful positive interaction, while still having only one winner. The game is a sandbox of strongly thematic and relaxing environments to explore, with each element tuned to both be strategic and evoke a real sense of immersion.

boardgame: We're Going on a Bear Hunt Board Game
description:
Roll and move game, with an end game chase between the leading player and the bear.

From the publisher:

A bright and colourful race and chase board game featuring the beautiful illustrations from the best selling book. The aim of the game is to be the first player to reach home, without being caught by the bear! Youngest player goes first and rolls one dice and move that number of spaces in the direction of the arrows on the board. If a player lands on a 'Uh-uh' space they must take a card from the top of the card pile. If the card is red 'We can't go over it' this ends their turn. If the card is green 'Oh no! We've got to go through it' they can roll the dice again and move forward.
When a player reaches the 'Run' space they must stop and take full control of the game. Throw both dice and move your character that number of spaces, then throw just one dice and move the bear that number of spaces chasing after you. Keep going like this, it's a race to try and get home before the bear catches you. If you reach the 'Home' space before the bear catches up with you, you win!

This game has strong educational components and helps children learn through social interaction as well as taking turns, all whilst having great fun. The game is for 2 - 4 players and suitable for children ages 3 years and up.

Do you have... 
A quick wit?
A creative mind?
A sense of humor?
A little acting talent?
At least three friends?

Then you're ready to take on the challenge of Act Out! You and your team will take on opposing teams in a hilarious, quick thinking game where your team improvises skits based 
on the cards you are given by your opponents!

When you play Act Out!, every game is different! Act Out! includes methods for both novice and advanced play, and is great for 
drama education, team building, or a fun night with friends!

Set the table with Snow White's help.
Snow White is moved round a table (free direction)with places for each of the 7 dwarves and herself. This move is driven by a die. Each place counts one point.
For each place there is an individual set of under plate, silver plate and chicken dish. Which have to be placed in this series while the game.
The actual player tries to find the right part from beside the plan. If the player has found the right part he is allowed to continue with the next part for the same place.
If not, the next player roles the die and continues.
If all 3 parts are found the actual player gets a diamond.
If all places have all parts the game ends and the player with the highest number of diamonds wins.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Snow White

description:
This game is about astrology. The central game board shows an astrological solar system. The board is 12 sectors for the 12 star signs. There are 7 concentric rings (wire rings in their hollows), one for each celestial body. The rings are Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter and Saturn. Each ring has a colored bead for the celestial body itself and red (favorable effect) and black (unfavorable effects).

Each player has an individual board with 2 concentric circles. They show also the 12 star sign sectors, written at the edge of the big circle. In the inner circle the sectors are numbered with 1 to 12. This numbers are called houses, which represent astrological aspects like personality, "possession and wealth", "adventure, erotic".
The inner circle has 5 concentric rings where special marker placed at the beginning on each field of the middle ring one.

At the begin of the game the inner circle is positioned by random (special dice rolling) and stays then till the end of the game.
Also the position of the rings on the central board are random turned at the beginning as described in the rules.

The player chooses a house (number) with as much red and colored beads in the equivalent sector.

Dependent on the number each player has another star sector.
Each round the sun ring is rotated by 1 (Counter clock) and for the other circles 2 dice are rolled. 1 dice shows which circle is rotated and the second die shows how much.

How many dice rolls are possible for the actual player is calculated out of the sum of red plus colored pearls minus black pearls in his star sign sector.

The player is allowed to roll the dice less than allowed.
The players look at the Pearl of the actual moved circle in the sector relevant for them. If the pearl is red or colored he moves the marker on his board 1 ring to the edge otherwise (black) 1 ring to the center.

Game Ends after a preset sun moves (e.g. 1,5 rounds). The player who has most of his marks on the outer ring wins the game.
The rules allow to read the astrological result for each player.

Description from the publisher:

Doomtown: Reloaded – The Light Shineth is a Pinebox expansion that can be added to your Doomtown: Reloaded base set to inject new mechanisms and new strategies into your decks! So get ready partner, are you ready for your showdown at high noon?

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Inquisitor's TIE Expansion Pack
description:

Personally tasked by Darth Vader to eliminate any surviving Jedi Knights, the Inquisitor had access to the Empire’s most advanced technologies, including the TIE Advanced prototype, a groundbreaking improvement over the TIE fighter designs of its time.

Thanks to Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Inquisitor's TIE Expansion Pack, this lethal starfighter appears as a carefully detailed and pre-painted miniature at 1/270 scale, along with four ship cards, including one for the Inquisitor himself. Fly into battle with the Ghost and Phantom, and recruit the talents of Ezra Bridger, Kanan Jarrus, Hera Syndulla, and the other heroes of Star Wars Rebels. description: Quiz game for children from 6 to 10 years. The Players try to answer questions in each of the 8 categories. The catagory is choosen by a simplified Trivial Pursuit game board.

description:

Despite his slovenly appearance, Dengar earned a reputation as one of the galaxy's most effective mercenaries, and his signature JumpMaster 5000, the Punishing One, was also much more than the lightly-armed, crescent-shaped scout ship it appeared to be.

With its pre-painted, large-base JumpMaster 5000 miniature and its array of ship and upgrade cards, the  Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Punishing One brings both Dengar and his surprisingly powerful ship into play, along with a maneuver dial, all requisite tokens, and the ability to fire its turret in a 360-degree arc.

The signature vessel of the bounty hunters Zuckuss and 4-LOM, the Mist Hunter from the  Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Mist Hunter is a pre-painted, small-base miniature G-1A starfighter with three attack, one agility, four hull, and four shields.

Meanwhile, the expansion's nine upgrades reflect the Scum and Villainy faction’s willingness to gain advantages with modifications of questionable legality. You'll find an illicit cloaking device and a tractor beam among the upgrades, along with four ship cards, a maneuver dial, and all requisite tokens.

description:
Passions flare and Jinteki faces a game-changing crisis in Android: Netrunner – Business First, the second Data Pack in the "Mumbad Cycle".

As the issue of clone rights rises to prominence during the Indian Union's national elections, Business First explores the tensions and conflicts within this tumultuous environment with sixty new cards (a complete playset of nineteen different cards) that leak horror stories about Jinteki's treatment of its clones, find the Corp's PR team scrambling to draw attention to its work with sustainable foods, and follow Haas-Bioroid's efforts to capitalize upon its rival's missteps.
