description: An expansion to The Great Debate Game. Covering 50 new questions and icebreakers. In addition, it contains a new voting mechanic.

Novice mages compete by completing objectives to impress their headmasters.  Plays 2-4 players in under 30 minutes.  Each turn mages will takes turns drawing mana and spells and deciding the best use of each.  By playing different spells they will complete objectives from healing damage of players to summoning the biggest earthen shield.  Or even collecting all types of mana.  Once a mystic has reached 20 objective points they win the game

Promo character card that comes packaged inside every English copy of Cthulhu Realms.

Character Ability: Before shipping goods, you may spend 1 of any good to move any one good type to the highest value position in the market, adjusting the others as necessary.

Flavor Text: Oh, you... probably don't want to deal with him. He dreams big, you see, but his business practices will drive you insane. Anyway, he spends most of his time managing his offshore holdings. - Dockmaster Schlibble

Part of Bandai's Party Joy series of lighter boardgames. (#37)

A game in search of "the world of Satan complete works" which were covered by the monster school while confronting a monster.
The game field is comprised of six boxes and boards. When somebody of the player finds "a door", those boxes are overturned and show a different aspect.

Already out of print.

The object of Calcaptum is to move all one's building blocks to the centre squares (marked "24"), one block being transferred every time a score of 24 is achieved.

The winner is either:

     the first player to complete a pyramid OR
     the player who can play on when the opponent cannot

Part of Bandai's Party Joy series of lighter boardgames. (#39)

It made an TV animation a game.
Chief character Jun is a girl of the invention enthusiast. Jun wants longed-for male Zero to come to like oneself.
Each player becomes Jun, using robot Kichinosuke, competes for sending inv. to Zero.

Each player take two pieces "Jun" and "Kichinosuke". Go ahead through the route with Jun, and prepare an invention. In addition, Kichinosuke works by going ahead through the route.
The player who let Kichinosuke arrive at the trout of Zelo wins.

Already out of print.

boardgame: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game – Secret Wars, Volume 2
description:
Description from the publisher:

As the third big box expansion since Dark City, Legendary: Secret Wars - Volume 2 allows players to play through the Secret Wars storyline within Legendary!

With sixteen all new Heroes, six new Villain groups, four new Masterminds and six new Schemes ready to challenge players like never before! Players can work both with and against other players to defeat the various Battleworlds while laying waste to planet Earth and, for the first time ever, players can now play as the Mastermind against the other players!

The game features playable characters from the Illuminati, Cabal and favorites from alternate universes including Beast, Capt Britain, Spider Gwen, Ruby Summers, Dr. Punisher The Soldier Supreme and many more!

Build your team to battle against the various universes in Battleworld including the X-Men '92, Mrs. Deadpool and the Howling Commandos, Deadpools Secret Secret Wars, and the Masters of Kung Fu!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A fascinating memory game and a valuable aid for developing language skills.

From the box:
This memory game consists of 112 different cards.  Each card has a picture side and a word side.  Played with the pictures only, the game can be enjoyed by children from 3 and by adults.  A supreme test of memory and concentration it is also an excellent aid for developing language skills.

boardgame: Russian Roulette: A Game of Luck and Betrayal
description:
Have a score to settle? Want to choose the first player for the next game on game night? Need to pick the next person to clean the bathroom? 
Russian Roulette for it!

Shuffle the deck and place it face-down in the middle of 2 to 6 players. First player to draw a card goes first. That player reads the card to all other players and does it’s action if required. Players draw one card clockwise around the table placing the cards in front of them for all to see. When a player draws a “Bang!” card, they are instantly out! They shuffle the deck, and go again. So keep your fingers crossed and hope you draw a "Click" you lucky son of a gun!

Push yer luck and pull multiple cards if you dare.


    Includes the Traitor and Gun Jam expansion cards.

Addition practice is more fun than ever—with a super-simple board game that’s “crawling” with bugs! Kids just roll the dice to move from one space to the next…collecting and losing bugs along the way. The player who collects the most bugs wins the game! 16" game board comes with everything you need for 2-4 players, including 45 plastic bugs, 2 soft dice and 4 clear storage jars with magnifying lids.

In this co-op team versus team game, you are the heroes of a buddy cop movie. The captain has assigned you as partners, but you can't stand one another. By the end of the game, with luck, you will be best friends.

The goal of the game is to get all the glory in the department by taking down the crime kingpin. You do this by collecting 10 points of evidence before the other team.

Solving Crimes requires acquiring cards through draw actions, and special locations you can shape your hand of cards to aid you in the crimes to be solved all over town.

The city map has locations like The Docks, and The International District where crimes go down. The City Hall, and Police Station are where you can kiss up to the Police Chief, and Police Commissioner. And there are even a couple spots where you and your buddy can go for some entertainment to get to know one another.

Issue game in Command Magazine Japan #124

Zhijiang Campaign, 1945 is a two-player operational game of Imperial Japanese Army's last offensive in China in April 1945.
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_West_Hunan
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Paper-and-pencil game in which two players build a playing area using the letters of the Spanish word ESQUINAS (corners) and then shade in different sectors of it, trying to "collect" more corners than the opponent without ever having the same amount.

This quiz-like game lets you choose whom your adversary will be.
Get to know your opponent's weaknesses and exploit them.

In Nivos there are 25 building blocks of different shape and colour. Each turn you place one block with simple rules: when played on the starting grid: don't put it on an empty space and don't touch a block of the same colour.

For every infraction you get a wooden cylinder. Every cylinder being a little bigger than the one before. You stack them on top of each other.

Make sure that by the end of the game you have the smallest cylinder stack.

Big changes are about to happen in the charming town of Sanset City. Reforms in mental health care, dubbed "107 City" are coming.
You live in this town and are confronted with different mental health issues. Try to find solutions by making the best use of the available services.
Will you be able to improve your quality of life?
Or will you be washed away by even more problems heading in your direction.

Each player incarnates a citizen, each with his own personal problems. For this you get a personal player board with starting stats on money, medical health, social situation. By playing cards and moving you can get to locations which improve these stats by rolling a die or get you the help of characters in the game.
You play once on each side of the board; one side representing the state of the city before reforms and the other representing the state after reforms. Make sure your citizen ends with the best stats; meaning the best quality of live!

Try your luck - will you dodge the bullet - round after round?

Play the exciting/thrill seeking game of Roulette (safely) in this easy to carry party game that everyone will get a BANG out of.

Randomly place the tokens on the revolver tile and select who is pull the trigger first!

Continue playing until someone is out of the round by pulling the BANG token.
Reload and continue...

The infirmary is full! Nurse octopus needs help to take care of the injured monsters.

In this game each player gets a personal player board depicting 6 injured monsters. On their turn a player can look through the starting pile of 36 image tiles once, to find a healed monster image that corresponds to a hurt monster on his board. The aim is to collect all six corresponding image tiles.
The first player to have helped all his injured monsters is the winner.

It is fun to play in the house, but do you recognise the dangers lurking around? Come to the aid of the children.

Find 12 sets of 3 token images. At the beginning all images are hidden and you look for a set by turning the images one at the time. Find three matching images and you can continue. Place these in order, thus identifying: danger, accident and preventive actions to take.

A mini-expansion for Leaving Earth (a pseudo-historical game about the Space Race) adding support for the planet Mercury.

It comes with location cards to allow travel to and from the planet, along with missions to complete on and around Mercury, and component cards to represent samples taken from the surface.

This expansion was offered for free to anyone who preordered Leaving Earth, and remains available for purchase separately afterwards. Later included with the base game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
This game covers the campaign around Tobruk during may - june 1942 at the regiment level.
1 turn equals two days, 1,5cm equals 3km
sold in a ziplock, with 200 colored counters, one map 40cm x 51cm and a rulebook)

Hamlet, Romeo & Juliet, Macbeth, Prospero and other mortals have strayed into the forest on Midsummer night. The Summer and Winter Courts of Faeries are hunting the mortals to feed on their passions and emotions, whilst hidden by glamour. However, the Fair Ones do not agree who gets to play with each mortal.

Midsummer is a game of hidden roles, intrigue and auctions for 4 to 9 players. It takes 30 to 60 minutes to play.

Each turn the faerie players compete to hunt a mortal from the one of Shakespeare's plays, then auction the mortal off to the other faeries for favours. At the break of day, faeries score points based on the emotions of the mortals they have claimed. The objective is for the court you are in to score the most points at the end.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In this little 48 cards game from designer Sanchuu, the girls from the Ruin series are surfing the waves on the new artifacts called Dolphin Blades, trying to perform the best maneuvers.

Players must combine the color coded cards in certain order to perform the best tricks!

From the back of the box:

Psycho is a great game for all the family for 2 to 6 players. You will need your wits about you as you travel around the Brain Drain squares before landing in the Consulting Rooms and finally entering the shrinkery.

The pitfalls you must face involve Self Analysis where you must divulge your thoughts about a particular picture, or even the Brain Wave squares where you must remember and repeat sequences backwards! It's fun, fast and very frustrating. It could really drive you nuts!

In a distant kingdom. the king's death begins a race to power among the members of the court, the coronation of the new king will be between four weeks, every member of the court is determined to win using spies and assassins to gain power and become the new king.

In the King's Death the players play a member of the king's court. Their goal is to collect more influence points of their opponents. To do this, each player has henchmen (represented by tokens) that may send to other members of the court to spy on them, bribe or kill opponents henchmen. The game play uses the secret roles and worker placement to simulate the internal struggles within the court, so bluff and intuition are fundamental.

A four player game where everybody gets 4 knights. They all start in their own castle. Players can put their knights on the board one by one. The goal is to take out all the opposing knights by bypassing them or getting in the same hex. When your knight is killed you can put anotherone on the board. There is also one sorcerers stone wich can be used to revieve knights.

Educational board game about milk. The game is produced for kindergartens.

Game board is divided to 5 areas: pasture, farm, industry, shop and home. In Her turn player draws a card with element (cow, bull, haystack, milker, cheese, sour cream etc.) and must find the same thing from the board to get the card as a point.
There are information about the elements on other side of the cards.

If the deck is exhausted the player with the most cards is the winner.

Distrify is a connection game played by two players, White and Black, on a square board. Board sizes from 9x9 to 19x19 are recommended. The game’s etymology stems from its primary mechanic, which penalizes players for the creation of 3-in-a-rows with their pieces. The result is a game with devious tactical twists and a hint of coldness.

Black moves first, placing a single stone anywhere on the grid. Players then alternate turns, placing up to two new friendly stones on empty cells. If two stones are placed, then:

a) the placed stones must not be diagonally adjacent to each other, and
b) the placed stones cannot result in the formation of a new horizontal, vertical, or diagonal three-in-a-row comprised of friendly stones.

A crosscut is a 2x2 square in which each player controls a pair of opposite corners; a player's move may never result in the formation of a crosscut.

Black wins if there exists a path of orthogonally or diagonally adjacent black stones reaching between the top and bottom rows, while White wins if there exists an analogous path stretching from the westmost to eastmost columns.
6 additional movement cards: 1 x Evasion, 1 x Nasty exchange, 2 x Backward and 2 x Joker. The rules are described in the rulebook from the base game.

Additional black car that was exclusively given to those who backend the game through Spieleschmiede. The black car Moves when a player exchanges his character. If it is on top, it takes money and gives it to the player who received the lowest money token.

In Sacramento, players draft cards from a common pool to create the strongest poker hand.

Played over multiple rounds. Each round a set number of cards are placed face up to a common pool. Each turn, a player chooses one of the cards from the pool and adds it to their hand of cards. This occurs until everyone has seven cards in hand. From those seven cards, each player builds their best five-card poker hand and is awarded points based on the strength of that poker hand. Whoever has the most points after all the rounds are played out, wins.

Sacramento is in the same family as Bonanza and Nugget.

Played with a standard deck of cards, published in Reiner Knizia's book Blazing Aces!: A Fistful of Family Card Games.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Wrath of the Righteous – "Pig From Hell" Promo Card
description:
Monster

Traits:
Animal
Demon
Mythic
Veteran

Check to Defeat:
Combat 6 THEN Combat 6 THEN Combat 6

Powers:
The difficulty of checks to defeat the Pig from Hell is increased by three times the scenario's adventure deck number.

All damage dealt by the Pig from Hell is Fire damage.

If defeated, you may draw a random non-Basic armor from the box.

"Don't go back to Munchmeat Farm."
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

description:
Trivia Party: Sports is a card game that combines a traditional deck of cards with sports trivia.  There are four different ways to play:

In Trivia Party, one player draws a card and asks a question of another player.  The answering player may choose from questions ranked from 1 (easiest) to 5 (hardest) and gains points equal to the difficulty level on a correct answer.  Incorrect answers gain no points.  After one question, the next player takes a turn.  Players play until the deck is used up or to an agreed total.

Upward Mobility is played similarly, but each player starts off being asked question one and proceeds through the questions to number five, earning the point value for each question answered correctly until he or she answers wrong.  So, for example, if a player answers questions 1,2 and 3 correctly but misses #4, the score would be 6 points.  Like Trivia Party, play continues until the deck is used up or until one player reaches an agreed point total.

In Auction Trivia, each player starts with 25 points.  One player chooses a card and reads the 5-point question.  Players then bid on the card.  The highest bidder then wins the card and tries to answer all five questions on the card, gaining points for each question answered equal to the point value of the questions (for a maximum of 15 points per card).  These points are added to his starting point balance.  The winninbg player then becomes the next auctioneer.  Players are eliminated if their balance drops to zero.  The player with the most points when all cards are used up wins.

In Trivia Poker, score is kept in the same way as Trivia Party.  However, when a player answers a question correctly, he or she keeps the card.  Players try to gain cards to build the best poker hand possible.  When a player gains a sixth card, he or she must discard any chosen card, keeping five cards.  The game ends when all cards are used or when all players hold five-card poker hands.  There are two winners: the player with the most points and the player with the best poker hand.

A turn based two player game of naval warfare where the two players try to sink each other’s hidden ships.

A divider is place between the two players so they cannot see each other’s fleet which consists of seven ships – one carrier, one battleship, two torpedo boats, and three submarines.

Each player lays out his ships (occupying between 2-5 squares) on a plastic frame that consists of two grid sections (one larger – the ocean, one smaller – the target control area) with the ships occupying the larger section. Each grid section has numbers (1-10) across the top and letters (A-L; J and K are missing) down the side.

After deciding who goes first, the first player calls a shot on his opponent’s ocean grid, say E7. His opponent must declare a hit if the grid square is occupied by a ship or a miss if it is not. The player that fired marks the grid number on his target control area with a Red bomb if it is a hit or a White bomb if it is a miss. Then the second player takes his turn, with either a hit or a miss being declared by the first player and the results recorded by the second player. Turns are taken until all the ships in one players fleet have been sunk. The five ships have a total of 21 hits. Each player keeps track of the number of hits he has made and declares himself the winner if his target control area has 21 Red bombs.

This game is very similar to Battleship, but based on the art and packaging was most likely published before Battleship, between the late 1950's and the mid-1960's.

In this space-age game of strategy, civilizations from all regions of the galaxy have clashed into an epic battle in which you must defend your planet system while attempting to capture one of your opponent’s planets.

The 6-player set includes one 24" x 24" game board with six sections, instructions, six sets of playing pieces; 18 red, 18 yellow, 18 blue, 18 purple, 18 orange and 18 green, and game piece bags.

The game board is a round checkered board where each space on the board represents a possible move for a vessel. Each player commands a fleet of 18 or 36 vessels, with six different classes; each class has a specific movement capability and range. Each player takes a turn by moving one vessel to either travel around the board to take out other vessels and get into range to capture a planet, or defend one his own planets.

Each game will play differently, based on the strategies developed by individual players.

It's Monopoly set in Branson, Missouri. Game is like the original Monopoly, with the board, cards and money done over in a Branson, Missouri theme. The 4 playing pieces are generic and colored tokens are used instead of houses. Game board measures 18" X 18". Paper instructions are included. Box does not have an insert.

This game was made in 1990, so the theatres, shows, restaurants & businesses included in the game are all from that era.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Murder at the Prancing Pony
description: The centerpiece of November’s Fellowship Event 2015 is the new expansion, Murder at the Prancing Pony. This fifty-three card scenario, produced by FFG’s in-house manufacturing, comes complete with three quest cards and fifty encounter cards.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
In the depths of space, millions of light years away from the Universal core, a habitable planet has been discovered. Orbiting a young sun and being ripe with resources it is immediately desirable for the High Races. Conquering its primitive inhabitants by force would be easy but the all-knowing, ever-present Overseers do not allow any acts of violence towards infantile races.

Each of the High Races has its own way of subduing and infiltrating the simple bipedal mammals on the planet. All aim to to build up a technologically superior and highly productive flock of humans that will rule the entire planet for all ages to come. All wish the other Races to fail in this common task.

On the planet later known as Earth a tribe of hunter-gatherers huddled together against the night-time cold and look up as an impossibly bright light opens the sky and reaches down to them.  They are not afraid, but curious…

Ancient Aliens: Creators of Civilizationsis a card driven build-up & resource management game for 2 - 4 players, ages 10 and up. Players control their own Alien Race that each has their own unique deck of cards. The aim of the game is to improve your puny humans on your continental area of influence to civilized stage.

During this process, alien technologies and powers are used to control and manipulate the humans in order to improve 3 vital abilities;  Skill, Knowledge and Loyalty. At the same time, you try to outsmart your Alien opponents to make sure your faction becomes superior! As you develop, you gain access to to higher level technologies and can build more beneficial structures and monuments for future generations to gaze upon.

As soon as any one player improves their last ability to civilized stage, it triggers the end of the game. Then Civilization points are counted (gained from built buildings, built monuments, crystals and agendas fulfilled) and the player with the most points is the winner!

A card game with otters ? That's Otter Nonsense!

In Otter Nonsense, players must during their turn place a card from their hand on one of the three stacks in the center of the table. They must follow the stack's direction, with values going either up or down. If the player cannot play, they must give points to the previous player who blocked them. Players get bonus points for emptying their whole hand, and the first player to reach seven points wins.

Double Vision is a drinking themed party board game for 4-8 adults. The contents are splash resistant so you can party with them. Players go to bars, answer drunken challenges, perform party tricks, fulfill new year’s resolutions, and talk in character to win drinks. After each talk, players can keep their name tag or trade for a new one. The first player to win 12 drinks (short game) or 21 drinks (long game) wins.

All Double Play card decks can be combined to make larger decks. Each deck includes a trivia game for 3 or more players. All cards are plastic. Cements Shoes is a game of elimination where players that collect two “cement shoes” sleep with the fishes.

All Double Play card decks can be combined to make larger decks. Each deck includes a trivia game for 3 or more players. All cards are plastic. Gangsters & Rats is a game of elimination where players collect “body armor” and “shoot” other gangsters.

All Double Play card decks can be combined to make larger decks. Each deck includes a trivia game for 3 or more players. All cards are plastic. Group Sex is a game of elimination where players collect “sex toy” cards, team up, and try to outlast other groups.

All Double Play card decks can be combined to make larger decks. Each deck includes a trivia game for 3 or more players. All cards are plastic. Dodgeball is a game of elimination where players must give enough answers to avoid getting “hit”.

2 additional roles for the best and worse players! The Champ needs to win two in a row, and the Sore Loser needs to come in last place twice. You must stop them!


A pack of three bonus cards consisting of the Champ, the Sore Loser and a rule card. Add these to your games for more variety, or swap out some cards that don't apply to your game group.

Champ
If you win two games in a row, you may take any other player's role card.

Sore Loser
If you come in last place for two games in a row, you may take any other player's role card.


Included in Pretense (AEG edition 2016).
Roll the dice and try and to get your horse to the finish line first!

An expansion for Robert Burke & Richard Launius' dueling dragon card game, Draco Magi. The expansion adds new dragons, new battlefields, new abilities, as well as drafting and deck construction rules.

Without huge rulesbook, without masses of material, without lots of fuss.
The different games in this box are small, easy to learn and you may play them everywhere.
There´s something in the box for everybody – for strategic thinkers, for optimizers, for bluffers, for memory-cracks….

Nußknacker – Nutcracker
For 3-4 players
What shows the hidden card? Ask smart and listen how the nut cracks.
Not a game for people with brains like bricks#

16 cards with the values of 1 to 8. One card is placed face down - the aim is, to identify this card) The active player asks another player about a value between 1 and 8 (a game with all 16 cards (2x8) is quite complex...). The value has to be on his hand either as single card or as sum. The others answers either with "No" (no card/s with this value on my hand) or with the value in one or more cards. The other players just see the number of exchange cards, not their values.
I.e. if one askes about a 6 and passes one card, is HAS to be the Six. If the other player passes two cards it might be 3/3, 4/2 or 5/1.
Whoever indentifies the face down card correctly, wins.

Bauab – Buildown
For 2 players
It´s a rough play amongst the building contractors. Save building sites for you, plan buildings, kick the others and force them to retreat.

Sorry, Folks, but this here is too complex, to explain it some sentences. Two players try to save construction areas for their own buildings, by placing planning-chips or reservation-chips on a variable board. Every player tries to take the last free area of his opponents chips - reminds me a bit on Go..., but of course, the game is faster and the value of the chips is important too.

Raufer – Upper
For 3-4 players
Chase the others through the staircases and take their opportunities to move. But take care that you are not sticked by yourself…

20 cards with values of 1 to 20. Quite easy card game. The active player plays a card and commends to the next "up" or "down", which means to react with a higher or lower value. If possible, the player gives the next command to his neighbour. If not, he has to take an additional card. Bad luck, if nobody can react properly - than the first player has to take additional cards. So, recall all played cards. 
Winner is the player, who played his last card.

Misd
3-8 Players
Which symbol is missing? Is it missed really? A game for fast thinkers.
Kids will blame you…

Everything planned?!
For 3-4 players
Is there a plan? Change places? Change Cards? Plan the use of your cards?
Who plans with lack of plans, wins.

Schollenschubser – Floe pushers
For 2-4 players
Be the first to cross the icy sea over swaying floes to safety. Charity begins at home. Shurely not a game for the kindhearted…

Schlauraff – Smartsnatch 
For 2-4 players
Use your dice perfectly and get the uppermost points. More than simple but never the same

Just a remark: since the producer delievered us... eh... somewhat insufficient dice (from our point of view...) we ordered better dice, and pass them with every sold box.

And another remark: authors of the games in this box are: Till Meyer, Klaus-Peter Rudolph and H.&W. Fuhr

And another one: there only one language-based game in the box, but for this and all other games we offer an english rulesbook as download. It may need some time, since Christian enjoys his holidays and we want to present you grafics too - not just plain text. :-)

A new promo card for Dominion releasing in 2015. This card was first used in the Dominion GenCon Tournament finals.

This new Event card has the text:




    
         Summon (Event - Cost 5 [coin])

    
    
        Gain an Action card costing up to 4 [coin]. Set it aside. If you do, then at the start of your next turn, play it.

    


Part of the Dominion series.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Ruins of Belegost
description:
You have entered the dark, half-sunken ruins of Belegost in search of powerful artifacts and ancient treasure.

Inside the Dwarven city, you can feel an evil presence stalking you...

The Ruins of Belegost is an exciting new quest for The Lord of the Rings LCG, first introduced at the Lord of the Rings Fellowship Event at GenCon 2015.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

boardgame: Family Guy: Stewie's Sexy Party Game
description:
Family Guy: Stewie's Sexy Party Game is a hilarious, fast-paced word game of questionable social value...not really suitable for anyone. Spin the spinner! Earn martinis! Offend your friends and relatives!

Each round, a new player is Stewie, the host of the party. Stewie spins the spinner to determine the theme of the party and draws a party card. Stewie's guests help throw the party by offering word cards to fill in the blanks of the party card. Guests score martinis by collecting sets of Family Guy characters on their word cards; Stewie scores martinis when the guests agree that the completed party card matches the theme. When the booze runs out, the party is over and the player with the most martinis wins!

From the designer:

Love Season is a card game about Little bunnies' love affairs.

In this game of chase you are a little bunny trying to go home but bunny girls find you so cute they won't let you go away.
For 3 to 6 players from 8 years old and up (works best with adults and teenagers ;) ), this game can take less than 20 min to play. It can go faster once you know it well and can end up in 5 min.

The rules :
Cards are divided in two categories. The one you can play during your turn for scoring, and the one you can play every time to any players, the bunny girls (attack) and the defensive cards to escape.

The first that reach 7 jump cards and reach a rabbit hole card win. But, while you jumping around, other player can send you bunnies that catch you and block your progression.

In Bad Habit, a player draws a card with three bad habits and chooses one. The other players ask questions and to find out what is the required bad habit. Questions must be simple. If your teammates guess your bad habit you get the card as a point. If your opposing team guess they get the point.

The team who has the most points is the winner.

In High Five, players seek five words on a given theme on the card you have drawn and write the words on a sheet of paper. The more players have written identical words found on this card, the more points you get. Player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner.

boardgame: Samrat: Rise of The Delhi Sultanate
description:
From the publisher's website:

Samrat is a board game that takes you back to 12th - 13th century medieval India. This is the time when Muhammad Ghori unravels his plans to take over India. India was ruled by Prithviraj Chauhan from Delhi and Raja Jaichand from Kannauj in the current state of Uttar Pradesh. To the west, Gujarat was under the rule of Raja Bhimdev II. While history notes the success story of how Muhammad Ghori defeats these kings, Samrat gives the players another chance to rewrite history.

Each player takes on the role of a king from medieval India and completes his objectives to earn stars. Some of these are based on true Indian history! Players build camps in their kingdoms to produce three types of resources - Gold, Stone and Wood. Use resources to raise an army and complete the identified objectives. The first player to earn five stars (2 players) or seven stars (3 or 4 players) wins the game.

Temple of the Moon (N)
Use the large tile with the Temple of the Moon on it (N) during setup to replace the large tile that is basically identical to it (E).
The Temple of the Moon space is a construction space equivalent to a Reg.

If you have a building on the Temple of the Moon, then during your Reinforcement phase, you can destroy two discs from your reserve (put them in the box) to place a solitary disc in reinforcement, wherever you like on the board, ignoring the usual reinforcement rules. That is to say that you can place this disc by itself on an empty space and/or on a space adjacent to an enemy building, and/or on a tempest space.

The Emperor cannot be sacrificed to use the Temple of the Moon.

Temple of the Sand (O)

Use the large tile with the Temple of the Sand on it (O) during setup to replace the large tile that is basically identical to it (B).
The Temple of the Sand space is a construction space equivalent to a Reg.

If you have a building on the Temple of the Sand, then one time during your turn, before your Action phase, you can destroy two discs from your reserve (put them in the box) to return an enemy disc (other than an Emperor) from the board to its owner’s reserve.

The Emperor cannot be sacrificed to use the Temple of the Sand.

The Beacon of Eon (U)
The Beacon of Eon tile (U) is intended to be used with the variants that use Tempest tiles. During the setup, add the Beacon of Eon tile wherever you like.

The Beacon of Eon tile is a Tempest tile (see base game rules, page 6).

Whoever occupies the most of the spaces around the Beacon of Eon ignores the rules for Tempest tiles (in the event of a tie, no player benefits from this effect). This player therefore treats Tempest spaces like normal spaces: She does not lose a disc by moving into or through them, and can reinforce her stacks there.

In Crazy Cat Lady players must fill the void in their life and cross the threshold into becoming a Crazy Cat Lady! While trying to acquire cats players must balance a job, renovate their home, and deal with general cat maintenance.

The entire game is represented by a deck of 36 cards. Using three smaller decks from these cards, players draw cards using a set amount of points each turn, based on their current job. By acquiring and playing these cards you can advance your career and gain more points per turn, renovate your home to house more cats, and of course acquire more cats as well. The forth deck however causes you to draw an event that adds common cat complications to your life. Plays in 5 minutes (or less).

New Print-And-Play Available Now as of May 7th, 2017 featuring art by Kyle Ferrin!

If you like the game you can purchase a professionally printed copy at the following web address: https://www.thegamecrafter.com/games/crazy-cat-lady
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: WarStuff is a free single-paged ruleset designed to be played with any miniatures or toys you have at hand. The rules are perfect as an introduction to wargaming for kids, and for conventions and pick-up games where players might have models from different lines.

Legend had it that Ocracoke Island was a haven for pirates and a most mysterious hideout for Blackbeard. To celebrate the harvest of the last plunder, he held a huge party for pirates from across all oceans. Unfortunately, while everyone was present enjoying the party, an explosion interrupted the festivities. The navy had discovered their hideout! Every guest ran for their lives, but biggest treasure was still on Blackbeard's ship, Queen Anne's Revenge. Blackbeard and his men had no choice, but to defend their ship from being captured by the navy!

In Ocracoke: Gold and Glory, if you are the navy, you fight for the glory of the country, trying to collect enough treasure that you can place Blackbeard in jail. Otherwise, you collect as many gold coins as you can, then run away from the turmoil. In game terms, you want to collect five coins before anyone else, and you do this by taking actions:


     Use the ability of one of two double-sided action cards, then flip the card to its other side.
     Declare the answer with all players.


Players continue for several rounds until one player collects their fifth coin, at which point the game ends immediately.

boardgame: Who's Boss. Animal Riots: The CARD GAME
description:
From the publisher:

Who's Boss. Animal Riots is a gang battling game for wannabe gang bosses, Rioters and control freaks who love to beat the socks off..midnight roaming shombies, terrifying sheeponators, intimidating bully-builders, a One ton crusher Hippo Butt, lamb chop LORDS who love their steak RAW ants with nanchaku skills gone nuts ,..and many many more wacky characters ready to defend their territory  no matter what!!

To win this highly strategic animal powered card game, first you’ll be forced to become a mob boss!!
A number card is placed on the table facing up. Based on the hand of cards, you must play your positively and negatively numbered cards in an increasing or decreasing sequence, kicking your rivals butt through wicked strategies and tactics, leading your blood thirsty crew to victory.

You must try to get rid of your cards by deploying ATTACK cards, COUNTER-ATTACK and REVOKE cards, cards that allow you to predict the next players move, SKIP and FORCE cards and many more!!

To win this highly strategic game, you must decide which cards to play, when to play them and which of your rival Bosses to target. 
The game is kids friendly, lots of fun and easy to play.

The game of Catan in a Big Box. Relive Catan in several new ways.

The Catan Big Box combines the base game with several expansions:

     Catan: Traders & Barbarians.
     Catan geographies: Mallorca and New York.
     A tableau with two monuments from Berlin.
     Helpers of Catan, a variant with character cards that have special abilities. 
     A set of pink playing pieces for swapping out another color.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
The prequel to Stranded No More, SNM: The Mutiny, is a solitaire game where you lead a crew through a series of missions as Captain Oscar Locke. Years of “poor treatment” has caused the rumblings of a mutiny among the crew aboard The Scream of Atlantis.  There is no agreement of who should usurp the Captain but some pirates are angrier than others. As the Captain, you’ll need to balance the anger each of your crew members has while also keeping the mutiny meter below the combustion line. If you can successfully complete all of the missions while keeping your crew happy, will avoid a mutiny and won’t get stranded.

The goal of the game is twofold: get through the chosen missions while avoiding a mutiny and to accumulate the most amount of gold possible. To do the former it’ll likely come at the detriment of the latter. Your ability to manage the levels of anger and mutiny while pressing your luck will determine your success as a Pirate Captain.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
New Dawn: Exodai is a new alien race for New Dawn.

It includes:
1x Exodai player board
8x Exodai Technology cards

Trick or Treat takes every kid's favorite part of Halloween and packs it into a fun filled, adventurous candy gathering card game.  Which is perfect for those occasional rainy Halloweens, Halloween parties or those days when your family just wants to wear costumes.

Each player takes turns rolling dice to flip cards on the corresponding houses. Their goal is to become the big winner on the block who has collected the most candy, while trying to avoid a number of obstacles that make trick or treating an adventure.

Partying Puppies is a lighthearted walk through a land of Puppyliciousness, with a dark zombie undercurrent that you are always trying to avoid. The object of the game is simple, be the last puppy standing as the other players fall to the zombie apocalypse; the last player not to be bitten by a zombie and get infected by the virus wins! It's that easy.

The twist of Partying Puppies is not only do you get to avoid getting bit by presenting an antidote card, or skip a turn, or the usual expected game mechanics but there are physical tasks to accomplish along the way. As a result, it's a combination card game, truth or dare, and charade mix. To progress a card may call on you to bark like a pup,fetch something, or force another player to do your ridiculous task.

This belongs to a series of promotional Monopoly clones featuring local businesses and trivia, published around 1975-1986*. As in Monopoly you move around a board collecting "shares" and hope for others to land on your properties, although the specifics are slightly different. Obviously the trivia elements vary from game to game within the series, and there are also some small mechanical variations, e.g. in the composition of event cards and how rent is calculated.

This belongs to a series of promotional Monopoly clones featuring local businesses and trivia, published around 1988-1992. As in Monopoly you move around a board collecting "shares" and hope for others to land on your properties, although the specifics are slightly different. Obviously the trivia elements vary from game to game within the series, and there are also some small mechanical variations, e.g. in the composition of event cards and how rent is calculated.

boardgame: Battle of Durak: Shapeshifter Promo Card
description:
This promo card is part of a special Battle of Durak promotion.

Skill Card Action:
Polymorph - Once all players have chosen their character classes, shuffle all left over skill cards and choose 3 at random. Exchange this card for those skills.

This belongs to a series of promotional Monopoly clones featuring local businesses and trivia, published around 1988-1992. As in Monopoly you move around a board collecting "shares" and hope for others to land on your properties, although the specifics are slightly different. Obviously the trivia elements vary from game to game within the series, and there are also some small mechanical variations, e.g. in the composition of event cards and how rent is calculated.

As in Monopoly you move around a board collecting "shares" and hope for others to land on your properties, although the specifics are slightly different. Obviously the trivia elements vary from game to game within the series, and there are also some small mechanical variations, e.g. in the composition of event cards and how rent is calculated.

Pathfinder Battles: Iconic Heroes Set 6 includes seven all-new miniatures featuring legendary characters from the Pathfinder role-playing universe: Seltyiel, Feiya, Quinn, Reiko, Lem, Daji and Hayato! Characters featured in this set are:


    Seltyiel, half-elf magus
    Feiya, human witch
    Quinn, human investigator
    Reiko, human ninja
    Lem, halfling bard
    Daji, animal companion
    Hayato, human samurai


Each Iconic Heroes set also includes exclusive Cards (one for each miniature) for use in the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game, with Iconic Heroes Set 6 including Boon cards.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

boardgame: Jake and the Never Land Pirates: Shipwreck Beach Treasure Hunt Game
description:
From the publisher:
Yo ho, let’s go! Captain Hook has buried pirate booty all over Shipwreck Beach. Flip over the gold doubloons and use Jake’s spyglass to decode the secret clues. When you get a match, get up and go! Snag the correct treasure with the foam sword and hurry back to score!

Promotes:
Matching
Memory
Physical Dexterity
family: Characters: Jake and the Never Land Pirates

description: Walrus is a mutant hero that can gain the vigilante special action Press.  The promo comes in every Kickstarter copy of Ninja Camp, also by Action Phase Games.

boardgame: Halo: Fleet Battles – UNSC Core Battle Group Upgrade
description: The contents of this boxed set can be used by UNSC players to either upgrade their Halo: Fleet Battles force with additional medium and small firepower or act as the foundation for a new force. At the heart of the upgrade box are four Marathon-class Heavy Cruisers and 24 Paris-class Frigates, a fantastic core addition to any UNSC fleet. Marathon Heavy Cruisers are the bulk heavy hitters for the UNSC, able to be deployed in either a standalone or supported role, and are commonly fielded in Battle Groups consisting of Paris-class Frigates in either Arrowhead or Trident Formation. Also in the box are Formation Bases and Ship Overlay Sheets for all models included.

boardgame: Halo: Fleet Battles – Covenant Core Battle Group Upgrade
description: The contents of this boxed set can be used by Covenant players to either upgrade their Halo: Fleet Battles force with additional medium and small firepower or act as the foundation for a new force. At the heart of the upgrade box are two powerful CCS-class Battlecruisers and they are joined by 12 SDV Heavy Corvettes, acting as a fantastic core addition to any Covenant fleet. CCS-class Battlecruisers are a key part of any Covenant fleet, able to lay down devastating weapons fire when needed and capable of delivering bombers or interceptors directly into the heart of a battle. Also in the box are Formation Bases and Ship Overlay Sheets for all models included.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Grey Havens
description:
The Grey Havens and its 165 new cards allow you to adventure along the westernmost edges of Middle-earth, and then travel even further west...across the uncharted waters where the proud hosts of Númenor met their doom many long generations back. Three new scenarios feature mechanics for ships and sailing, as well as a heavy emphasis on exploration. Then, even as the troubled dreams of one Gondorian nobleman spur you to undertake an epic journey to the ruins of Númenor, The Grey Havens casts you deep into turbulent waters where Corsairs, Raiders, Undead, and the seas themselves pose constant threats.

The Grey Havens, a deluxe expansion for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game, contains 165 new cards including: 3 new quests, 12 new encounter sets, 2 new heroes, as well as new allies, events, and attachments for each sphere of influence.

—description from the publisher

Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
7 Summits: A Mountaineering Challenge is a game for one to four players in which players test their mountaineering skills against the highest mountain on each continent. First player to climb the seven mountains wins the game!

Choose which mountains you want to climb at your own pace and in any order that you want. There are ten different mountains from which to choose, each with unique statistics and rewards for successfully climbing them.

Use up to four gear pieces to prevent deadly accidents represented with a roll of the dice. Upgrade your acclimatization to prevent altitude sickness. Choose which climber cards you want to upgrade as you draw them to increase your movement speed on a mountain.

There are many risks for which you must prepare in this difficult challenge of climbing the highest mountain on each of the seven continents, and there are many people who want to climb them before you do. How will you attempt this deadly challenge?

Orphanage of Fear is a social deduction game for six to seventeen players in the vein of Werewolf or Mafia. Players are either orphan children or sinister boogeymen. Hiding in plain sight the boogeymen wake each night to drag a child into their realm under the bed to fears upon their fear. During the day they hide themselves behind the masks of their previous victims.

The children have to find out who these monsters are, but they are not entirely alone. In desperation the children can take comfort with what toys the orphanage has, and may draw abilities from the toys themselves. They can shine the light of the magic projector to discover the true identity of the target, shield themselves during the night with their teddy bear, save themselves from the monster in the closet by feeding it their limberjack or hide under the blanket to shield them from all danger, but beware not all toys were placed there for the benefit of the children. The boogeymen can bring their own sackcloth doll to throw off the magic projector and make others perceive an innocent child as a boogeyman.

Unfortunately, the children can’t always rely on toys being there. The orphanage is short, even with many children now missing, and so every round the kindly, but out of his depth, caretaker sees to it that what toys are available are taken back and then given out at random.

Orphanage of Fear uses the traditional day night cycle of Werewolf. During the night the Boogeymen choose a child to drag into their realm of existence, along with the toy they are holding, to feed, and during the day the entire group debates and argues about who the impostors are before nominating a child or children. If only one child is nominated they can drag another child with them to the closet door. The player with the most votes is sent into the closet and gets eaten, along with their toy, by the monster inside. The game continues until either all the boogeymen are eaten by the closet monster at which point the children win or there are an equal amount of children and boogeymen at which point the boogeymen win.

In Hacker Dice players are hackers trying to gain control of a network of servers for their own nefarious (or virtuous!) purposes.

The game combines a Yahtzee-style mechanism of rolling dice with a memory element of remembering the defenses of the servers. Players gain control of servers by breaking through their defenses. They can increase their chances by creating malware.

Stranded is quick easy card game. It is about a group of people who crash land in a forest somewhere and need to survive until help arrives. Unfortunately everyone is out to save their own skin. Can you survive long enough to be rescued?

Game play is for 2-5 players and lasts anywhere from 10-20 minutes. Stranded is played in rounds with the simple mechanics of draw a card them play a card if able. The first player to receive 5 points or hold the highest number at the end of the deck is the winner.

Game description from the publisher:

The Shadowrun: Crossfire - Demo Box is a trial version of the game that's perfect for learning the rules. We carefully picked out a subset of the full game that shows off Crossfire's feel and mechanisms in a small, elegant package. The demo box takes you through a shorter, tighter version of the "Caught in the Crossfire" mission included in the full game — it's the quickest way for new players to learn how to play.

Catalyst Game Labs is giving the Shadowrun: Crossfire - Demo Box to select gamers at Gen Con 2013 in mid-August.


The shadows of the Sixth World have every kind of danger you can imagine: ultra-violent gangers, flesh-eating ghouls, mages that summon spirits from toxic waste, backstabbing corporate raiders, hard-nosed police officers, and even dragons. You don't have much — mainly your guts, your wits, and your friends — but maybe that's enough. Between you and your teammates, you can sling spells, hack the Matrix, talk a tiger out of his stripes, and bring down a charging ork from a hundred yards away. Will that be enough to face down the worst the mean streets can throw at you? You're about to find out.

Shadowrun: Crossfire is a cooperative deck-building card game for two to four players set in the gritty, cyberpunk fantasy world of Shadowrun. Play a shadowrunner team and take on tough jobs such as protecting a client who's marked for death, shooting your way out of downtown when a run goes sour, or facing down a dragon. In each game you'll improve your deck with a mix of strategies, while earning Karma to give your character cyber upgrades, physical augmentations, magical initiations, weapons training and Edge.

Shadowrun: Crossfire - Demo Box includes two obstacle decks, a black market deck, a crossfire event deck, a mission sheet, 4 role cards, and 2 product information cards.

Differences from Shadowrun: Crossfire

- The crossfire card deck & obstacle decks are smaller than the full game

- The Demo box contains only 1 scenario (so the only way to play this version) and it is not included in the main Shadowrun: Crossfire game

- There are only four character cards (1 of which is the troll mage card which is not found in the base set, but was reprinted in Character Expansion Pack 1)

- The Demo box does not contain any tokens, and requires the players to use something of their own (coins etc.)

- The rulebook is shorter and does not contain all the rules listed in the base game, but contains information specific to the Demo box
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
From the publisher:

Play with family and friends and recompose Jef, Walter, George, Colette and Nicolas. When it's your go, throw the dice and step by step put the body parts back together. Head, chest, legs, arms - the first one completing a whole body is the winner!

The Glitterati of Bear Valley Hills are at an awards after-party, someone says the wrong thing, then the cakes start flying! Whoever comes out the other end cleanest is the winner.

Bun Fight is a board game in which players move between tables laden with various cakes to grab and hurl at their rivals.

On their turn, a player has a number of actions enabling them to move to a table, grab a cake, push a cake in someone's face (close combat), or hurl it at someone (ranged). "Sweet Surprise" cards may be played at any time by any player, adding effects or helping players avoid/recover from trouble. Cakes (Just Desserts) cause Splat and Stun, and the player with the fewest Splat when the last cake is grabbed wins. Stun causes a player to miss actions but can be avoided by either dropping a cake or discarding Sweet Surprise cards if preferred.

On behalf of the faculty and staff of Masamune Shining Forge Academy, it is with great pleasure that we inform you of your admission to Masamune. You were selected from an incredibly competitive and capable group of applicants, and showed an intense devotion to creating weapons worthy of legend.

Masamune is a game for two to four players, and takes place over the course of an academic year at Masamune Shining Forge Academy. As a pupil at Masamune Shining Forge Academy, you and your opponents will start the game with identical 12-card decks. Over the course of the game, you will forge sentient weapons called Chibis and teach them powerful special techniques to defend the Academy from attacking monsters. Alternatively, you can use Chibis to gain more powerful cards and bolster your deck.

Just like normal schools, Masamune Shining Forge Academy uses Exams to measure its pupils’ academic growth. Every time a new card is added to your deck, there is a chance that the next card drawn from the Academy deck will be an Exam, an intense battle staged by your instructors that lures the monsters teeming in the surrounding forest to attack the Academy! After the fifth and final Exam is completed, the school year is over, and the pupil who harvested the most monsters has won the game!

The Sealed Ark is a co-operative game which can be played solo or all the way up to 5 people.

Before you roll the die, you must gather a single focus then roll the die to see where will the spirit appear i.e.
(rolls a 4, a single spirit will appear at that 4th circle.)

Players now have the choice of moving to another circle, perform an ability, banish a spirit or do nothing and keep the focus. If the player rolls a 6 on the die, a spirit will appear at the center of the ark (the void) and a spirit card will be drawn and read out loud and do what it's told.

To win, players must carefully plan out their strategies with each unique spirit of harmony and gather enough focus to seal all 5 circles before the void reaches a total of 9 spirits.

Take on the role of mighty shape shifters. By using powerful attacks and assuming the form of mighty beasts the shifters do battle to see who will reign supreme. The first player to empty their hand is the winner of the duel and can lay claim to the title of the Ultimate Shape Shifter.

Play

Select a Form - Each player selects one Form Card from their stack of face down Form Cards and places in the center of the table but in front of them.

Select a Combat Style - Each player then selects one of their combat style cards from their hand and places it face down besides their form card.

Reveal - All cards are flipped face up.

Resolution: The success of a combat style depends on the form.
If successful, players put the Combat Style Card face down in the discard pile.
If unsuccessful, players put the combat style card back in their hand.

End of turn - Players put the Form Card they just used aside, face-up and return any previously used Form Cards back to their stack.

Winning

The game ends when a player ends a turn with no Combat Style Cards in their hand.

Note

This contains all three Transmogrification Games; Transmogrification, Advance Transmogrification and Epic Transmogrification.

The Endless Land is a game with modular boards that are placed in a different way each game for a new experience each time!  Some players will choose dangerous quests and heroic battles to gain victory.  Some will set up commerce and trade routes to conquer the land.  There is magic and adventure among the dwarves, elves, and humans that live in this realm. Make sure you have plenty of food for the journey and let the quest begin!

On a turn all players will take their actions simultaneously.  They travel to a neighboring town (move their player meeple on the board) and collect the goods which have been accumulating there. The board is modular and matches up with other board parts to form a unique terrain each game.  Also, the game expands with more 8" X 10" board pieces to form a playing surface as with no knowable end.  Each player uses gold, wheat, wood, iron, and many other commodities to build farms, stores, and implements of combat.
Players may also take quests that send them into the lairs of diabolical monsters who are hoarding gold, rubies, and other valuable items.  Players can risk sneaking into the dark caverns and taking the loot, but if the monster awakens they are in trouble.
Players can build strongholds so that even when they are not in a town, that town is building up their store of goods.

Players can trade each turn to turn their wheat, sheep, or cattle into food, they can also trade other commodities for food in a town where that is in demand.

Players can travel faster buy buying a horse or boat.

Players wishing to build or buy must have food to stay in a particular place longer to transact business.

Points are gained by building structures and conquering creatures.  The player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner.

Dice-based combat with tile-based resource gathering. Spend resources to expand your armies, upgrade soldiers, or climb the tech tree.

The goal of the game is to capture 20 enemy soldiers. The first player to do this wins. However, if all of one player's soldiers have been captured (they have no more on the board or in their reserve pile), then the player who has the highest number of enemy soldiers captured wins.

The board is made up of hexagon-shaped tiles set up in a large hexagon shape. These tiles consist of six different kinds of resource tiles as well as a base tile in the middle of the board and at each of the six corners.

As players move soldiers from tile to tile, they leave behind Possession Markers to claim those tiles as part of their territory.

If you expand too far away from a base tile, another player could move in behind you, separating your territory from a base tile and capturing your soldiers.

Each turn you may collect one resource card for each soldier you have on the board - corresponding with the resource tiles they occupy.

On your turn, you can trade resource cards with other players regardless of whether or not they are your ally. Your resource cards can be spent on a wide variety of items to help you win.

Charlie's Angels (οι αγγελοι του τσαρλι) - The playing pieces travel around the map, including Hawaii, and gather evidence & points. Winner returns to start with enough evidence & points to tell Charlie the name of the criminal.

Comes with paper board, spinner, 8 generic playing pieces (2 each of yellow, red, blue & green), clue cards and instruction sheet.

Discover territories, collect resources, build, upgrade and pass through events!

COLOR: Strategy Board Game is a turn-by-turn multi-player game where players are colonizing territories, collecting resources, building connections and mines, discovering hidden lands and passing through positive or negative events.

It can be played by two, up to four players aged ten years and up. The playing time is around 30 to 60 minutes. The game can be easily played even at the very first time without being an experienced player.

Charlie's Angels Police Stories Game from Greece (ΑΣΤΥΝΟΜΙΚΕΣ ΙΣΤΟΡΙΕΣ) - Based on the TV Series. Players try to track down & capture the suspect.

Comes with 4 playing people pieces, 2 cars, colorful paper board, spinner & instructions.

A horde of terrible creatures are on the rampage in the East. The defenceless trading village of Hilt (located just to the west of Knife Edge pass) has called for heroes to help defend Hilt and the entire western lands from the oncoming horde. Choose between 1 and 3 heroes to defend the pass, defeat the oncoming horde and claim VICTORY! ... plus Hilt is super rich and has many beautiful, eligible, young men and women!

Construct the Pass using between 3 and 5 double sided cards, construct the Horde deck based upon a chosen scenario and choose between 1 and 3 heroes.  Each turn a new monster enters the pass.  You will need to upgrade your heroes and use all your skills in order to stop the advancing horde.

Beware! If a single monster makes it through the pass or all your heroes die then you lose.

User summary

The action revolves around a twenty-four card chance deck and a pair of six-sided dice, three numbers of each die are green and the other three are red.

Each player begins the game with $40,000. After the deck of cards is shuffled and placed face down on the superfluous game board, the top card is drawn and read aloud to establish the conditions of trades for that player. That player follows the direction on the card. The dice are rolled and the results are enacted based on the chart on the card.

After twenty four turns, the deck is depleted and the Power Play round begins. Each person, in turn, chooses one last trade and rolls the dice and check the card for each individual result.

The final round of play has each player roll the dice one last time to determine if each player must pay a trading fee.

Now, the game is over and the player with the greatest net worth is the winner.

Board game with questions and answers in which you have to guess a character or a place offering clues. The first to reach the final square wins. The board further has a series of boxes that hamper progress in balancing the culture of the players.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – The King's Peace
description:
Description from the publisher:

“I must have the gold cloaks. The City Watch is two thousand strong, sworn to defend the castle, the city, and the king's peace.”
   –George R.R. Martin, A Game of Thrones

Follow the events of A Game of Thrones with The King's Peace, the third Chapter Pack in the Westeros cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game. Eddard Stark has arrived at King's Landing to serve as the Hand of the King, but even as knights journey across Westeros to participate in the Tourney of the Hand, insidious characters gather in the shadows. Join battles and intrigues surrounding the tourney with memorable characters like Ser Rodrik Cassel, Ser Alliser Thorne, and Ser Gregor Clegane. Two new plots—one neutral and one loyal to House Baratheon—offer new deckbuilding opportunities and this Chapter Pack includes a brand-new agenda that’s sure to inspire entirely new types of decks.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Espião ("Spy") is a board game from the defunct publisher based in Oporto (Portugal), Karto.

It is a game that somewhat resembles the Chess and Checkers, but with extremely simple and basic rules. With a low level strategy is being accessible to all ages.

Game for 2 players where each has 16 playing pieces: One Boss, 8 agents in the front row, 7 spies in line next to the Boss.
The goal is to "hunt down" the Boss of opposing opponent, much to chess style.

Each player can move a playing piece in turn and the direction of their movement is given by the arrow(s). The only piece that can move without respecting the arrows are the Boss and can only move a single house. Spies can move up to four houses and agents can move up to two houses. The enemy piece is captured when it hits and occupies the house where the opponent's piece.

Portuguese description:
Jogo de Tabuleiro Espião da extinta editora sediada no Porto (Portugal), Karto.

É um jogo que lembra um pouco a Xadrez e Damas, mas com regras extremamente simples e básicas. A estratégia também de um nível muito baixo, sendo acessível a todas as idades.

Partida para 2 jogadores em que cada um tem 16 peças: 8 agentes na fila da frente, 7 espiões na fila ao lado do chefe e um chefe.
O objetivo é "caçar" o chefe de espionagem adversária, muito ao estilo do xadrez.

Cada jogador só pode jogar uma peça no seu turno e a direção do seu movimento é dado pela seta. A única peça que se pode mover sem respeitar as setas é o chefe e só pode mover uma única casa. Os espiões podem mover até quatro casas e os agentes podem mover-se até duas casas. As peças inimigas são capturadas quando se atinge e se ocupa uma casa onde está a peça do adversário.

When Bad Karma Strikes, Pass It On To Other Players!

You can travel down life through good deeds or bad deeds, but karma will follow you either way. In Pass The Karma, your best tactic is to collect that bad karma now and pass it on to your opponents to get the win.

This 2nd entry into the Karma Crystals game collection adds a set of elemental shards and a new way to pass on your bad karma to other players. This game also introduces a Karma Crystal scoring system to track all that bad Karma going around. Last but not least, you get 12 new monster elemental cards for your collector’s deck!

Collect up to elemental elemental shards (up to TWO per type) to represent your forward movement through your karma travels. If you get too many per type, you get Karma Crystals and get a chance to pass their reverse-move effect on your score to other players.

Roll dice to work with the various monster elemental cards - monsters are the good guys and help you win! Determine how many karma moves you score per roll and collect your elemental shards for the match, or pass on your no-match karma to another player! When you get elemental shards of FOUR types, that triggers the end of the game and scores are tallied at the end of the round.

In addition to the unique dice matching game mechanic, a unique auto player system is used to facilitate up to EIGHT total players per game. The game also provides an easy way to track multiple game wins for playoffs in case you have more than 15 to 20 minutes to play. No paper score cards and pencil needed - just Karma Crystals, crystal cubes and elemental shards to track and build your score.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Frontier Town Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

Towns on the Old West frontier are full of danger and adventure around every corner!

The Shadows of Brimstone: Frontier Town expansion allows players a deeper and richer Town experience between adventures, adding exciting new types of Towns to find (such as Plague Towns, Outlaw Towns, or Mutant Towns), six new Town Locations to visit (such as the Street Market, Sheriff's Office, Indian Trading Post, or Smuggler's Den), a new expanded Travel Chart, and a deadly new ranged Enemy – Bandits — for use both down in the Mines as well as in Town!

With this expansion, Heroes also have the opportunity to play out Adventures in the Frontier Town itself using the new Town Adventure Board! Heroes can protect the townsfolk while battling demons in the streets, have a shootout with Bandits trying to rob the local bank, or find themselves in a duel at high noon! Since the buildings are random, the camp location will now contain limited versions of any crucial buildings that were missing like doc's office, church and saloon.

Historical Battles "Verdun 1916"
It is a war game for two players set in the First World War, where we take control of German or French army to destroy our enemy.
This game has two game modes: The Front is a game system Ameritrash and Defending Line is more strategic. It also features expansions: Supremacy and Brotherhood latter with four-player mode.

This game is created by Rúnica Art Games, in 2016 in Colombia.

Thank you all for believing in the Colombian creativity.


Batallas Históricas "Verdún 1916"
Es un juego de guerra para dos jugadores ambientado en la primera guerra mundial, donde tomaremos el control del ejercito Aleman o Frances para destruir a nuestro enemigo.
Este juego tiene dos modos de juegos: The Front que es un sistema de juego Ameritrash y Defending Line que es mas estratégico. También se cuenta con las expansiones: Supremacía y Hermandad esta ultima con la modalidad para cuatro jugadores.

Este juego es creado por Rúnica Art Games, en el año 2016 en  Colombia.

Gracias a todos por creer en la creatividad Colombiana.

Le Petit Chaperon Rouge ("Little Red Riding Hood") is a cooperative memory game. The goal of the game is to find pairs of the same tiles while trying to avoid the wolf tiles. When you find a pair, The little red riding Hood advances towards Grandma's house, but if you flip a wolf tile, then the wolf advances. The players need to arrive at Grandma's house before the wolf does. There is also a Hunter tile which resets the Wolf back to its cave when revealed, but is then removed from the game.

From the publisher:
Le Petit Chaperon rouge est un jeu de mémoire où les surprises ajoutent au plaisir!

Tu dois former des paires de provision pour faire avancer le Petit Chaperon rouge sur le chemin de la maison de Mère-Grand.

Prends garde au loup! Seul le chasseur peut l'empêcher d'arriver chez Mère-Grand avant toi.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

boardgame: Sail and Steam Navies: Naval Battle System 1840 to 1880
description:
Sail and Steam Navies is a PDF set of rules designed for 1/600 scale miniatures, but has information for use with 1/1200 scale miniatures. The period covered is 1840 to 1880 when most warships were equipped with sails as well as steam power. The basic rules are 40 pages long, with charts, markers/templates, an example of play, and conversion information for 1/1200 scale. The Under Both Flags module is included and adds six scenarios, a scenario generator, and log sheets for the ships. There are over 1000 log sheets representing almost every ship involved in the American Civil War and a number of forts and shore batteries. Sets of 1/1200 scale counters in PDF format are available separately for use instead of miniatures.

Players may fight historical battles using one of the 6 scenarios provided or create their own using the scenario generator. Game length will vary depending on the number of ships used and scenario victory conditions. Most scenarios played at conventions with multiple players last 3 to 5 hours. 
 The game uses D10s for resolving combat and other actions. Usually you will only need one or two dice but more will be required for most damage checks. D6 dice are used to record momentum for each ship. A player aid and a sheet of markers/templates will need to be printed out for use. As with most miniatures based systems any flat playing surface can be used and a ruler or measuring tape is required for checking range and movement.

The turn sequence is straightforward; it starts with writing speed orders for the turn (how fast the ship will go for the turn), followed by combat (cannon fire, musket fire, boarding/melee), then movement, and finally morale checks and repairs.

From the publisher:
Welcome to the world of Two Hour Wargames!

Chain Reaction 2015 is an introduction to Two Hour Wargames. It’s a complete game – not a teaser – and is free. Chain Reaction 2015 is a set of man-to-man combat rules that can be played in a variety of periods and with any figures you may already have.

Chain Reaction 2015, like all THW games, can be played solo, cooperatively – all players on the same side – or competitively – head to head. Inside you’ll find:

     The current Reaction System, used in all Two Hour Wargames, that allows you to learn one set of mechanics to play a variety of periods.
     Quick game mechanics that yield realistic shooting and melee results.  
     Three scenarios that can be played over and over with no two games ever being the same.
     Character advancement based on your success in each game.
     An easy to use Campaign system that generates your next game based on how well you’ve done.
     Ways to generate your characters and your opponents – Police, Gangers, Militia, Guerillas, and Military.
     All this and more…


Chain Reaction 2015 lets you to learn the current version of the Reaction System used by THW without spending a dime. While our counterpart Swordplay is about hand-to-hand combat with sword and shield, Chain Reaction 2015 is about modern firearms.

Chain Reaction 2015 gives you a chance to see if you like the Reaction System, before trying one of our period specific games. Think of Chain Reaction 2015 as a preview of our other period specific games like 5150: Star Army, All Things Zombie – Final Fade Out, and NUTS!

Chain Reaction is updated every few years in line with the new editions of Two Hour Wargames other rules.  Updates so far have been published in 2018, 2020, 2023 and Chain Reaction 2026.

Charlie's Angels Throws Bullets (Lanza Balas) skill game with Farrah Fawcett, Kate Jackson and Jaclyn Smith pictured.

RULES: 
1) Tap & tilt cardboard so that bullets begin in the start position and enter maze.
2) The goal is to get each bullet in its own chamber.
3) Each bullet that enters the waste area become "loose cannonballs".
4) When 5 bullets become loose cannonballs, the game is over.
5) The player who places the most bullets in chambers in the shortest time wins.

This game originates from isolated indigenous tribes of Siberia. It was recently discovered by "mainland people" and immediately caught attention of Lame Heron Games. This superficially simple game turned out to be a marvel of an alien mathematical genius. It is unusual and elegant beyond words, it is very easy to learn but very hard to excel at.

Its unbelievably concise rules, while being simple enough for a child to learn, generate a strategy as complex as the best known "western" games.

The core of the game's mechanics is the notion of dice-perfection:
A die is perfect when the number of pips on the face of the die equals the number of dice neighboring it, regardless of those dice ownership and pips.

The drama of the game is that this perfection status (and therefore the goal) is very fragile.
The conflict of the game is that manipulating their own dice only players affect the status of the opponent's dice as well.
The trickery of the game is that the goal is impossible to achieve using only your own dice.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Wrath of the Righteous – "Valais Durant" Promo
description:
Promo Card Included in GTM Issue 187

Type: Cohort

Traits:
Outsider
Arcane
Eidolon
Veteran

Power:
If the current scenario lists cohorts, treat this cohort as if it were on that list.

If the top card of the blessings discard pile has the Corrupted trait, bury this card to add 2d4 to your combat check; if you succeed, you are dealt 2 fire damage.  If the top card of the blessings discard pile does not have the Corrupted trait, recharge this card to add 2d4 to your non-combat check; if you succeed, recharge 1 random card from your discard pile.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

description:
In Kingpins, you’re running a criminal syndicate in 1920s Temperance Town. The onset of Prohibition means it’s a race to see who will become the best bootlegger in the business! Recruit the right ne’er-do-wells to your side and undercut your rivals at the right moment to ensure that your syndicate comes out on top.

The goal of the game is to be the player with the lowest score after five rounds of play. Cards left in your hand at the end of a round will add to your score. Play smart and leverage your cards’ special abilities if you want to win!

Shuffle the deck and deal five cards to each player.
Place the remaining cards in a face down stack in the center of the playing area. This is the stockpile.Flip the top four cards of the stockpile face up and place them north, south, east, 
and west of the stockpile. These are the recruitment stacks.

GAMEPLAY
The game lasts five rounds. In each round, players take turns in clockwise order until the round end is triggered (see ROUND END).

On your turn, you may take any number of the following actions, in any order:

     Play a kingpin (12) from your hand to start a new syndicate stack in an empty space on one of the four corner positions around the stockpile (NE, SE, NW, SW). 
     Play a card from your hand to any stack. The card you play must be the next lowest in rank, but opposite in color (e.g., You can play a red 10 on a black 11).
