A giant even amongst the Sangheili, Xytan ‘Jar Wattinree is revered by his comrades for his incredible prowess in battle. Supported by the Minister of Etiology, a minor Prophet in the Covenant during the Human-Covenant war, this fleet delivers the leadership needed to harness a tireless religious zeal that will eventually lead to the Fall of Reach.

This is an ideal way to upgrade an existing Covenant force or begin building your brand new fleet. Within this boxed set you will find two ORS Heavy Cruisers, two CCS-class Battlecruisers and sixteen SDV Heavy Corvettes. Armed with a host of plasma-based weaponry, the ORS and CCS are able to launch both Bombers and Interceptors directly into the heart of battle. Supported by SDV Heavy Corvettes, watch your heavy vessels lay down overwhelming weapons fire and obliterate your enemy.

Focus On Your Karma Path And Collect Elemental Matches!

You can travel down life through good deeds or bad deeds, but karma will follow you either way. In Move Master, your best tactic is to collect that bad karma now and give it to your opponents to get the win.

This 3rd entry into the Karma Crystals game collection adds the use of elemental shards with move shards to gain movement and 12 new monster elemental cards for your collector’s deck!

Your mission is to gain as many moves forward down the karma path, collect elemental shards and be nice to other opponents so they don’t pass their Karma Crystals to you.

Roll dice to work with the various monster elemental cards - monsters are the good guys and help you win! Determine how many karma moves you score per roll and collect your karma move shards. Your move match also earns you an elemental shard, but if you get more of one type, you get to pass a Karma Crystal to another player. Your opponents also get Karma Crystals through a no-match roll, and you will get those too! When you get 11 or more karma moves, that triggers the end the game and the winner is determined at the end of that round!

In addition to the unique dice matching game mechanic, a unique auto player system is also used to facilitate up to EIGHT total players per game. The game also provides an easy way to track multiple game wins for playoffs in case you have more than 15 to 20 minutes to play. No paper score cards and pencil needed - just move and elemental shards, and of course, Karma Crystals to track and build your score!

While the base game of Operation: Dandelion is an easy to teach gateway game for families and even children, it might lack the tough choices and challenges a more serious gamer expects. Brigade Morning Sun is aimed at more experienced players who want to spice up their game a bit.

It offers 4 modules that can be combined with the base game as the players like it. Those modules offer player powers, challenging events and growing crops.

This game is actually a game collection. It hosts 4 games and 2 additional variants for one of them.

1.Yura Yura Balance Café
This is the main game the components can be played with. You have a stack of cards that represent coffee orders, several coffee “ingredients” and a playing area, which is a tablet on a dome shaped foot. The name part “balance” comes from the way it’s played. You draw a card from the deck which tells you, if you need to place a straight coffee, a coffee-to-go, a Milk Coffee or one of the 2 Macchiato versions on the tablet. There is a grid on said tablet to determine the places where you can, but on a coffee to go you can stack another coffee if you like. Whoever tips the tablet over loses. Besides the usual red cards, there are also green ones in the deck. These give you the opportunity to order somebody else to place the coffee (with a green cup), of course on their own risk.

2.Speed Order Café
This speed game needs at least 2 baristas and 1 customer. The customer shuffles the deck of cards and pulls 5 into her hand. She than reads them aloud to the baristas. After all 5 orders are read the customer gives the start sign, and all baristas start to assemble the coffees in order. Whoever is fastest stops the game. If she is correct on all coffees she wins, otherwise the win goes to the next most complete and errorless contender.

3.Black and Milk Reversi
This is just Othello played with the components of the game.

4.Coffee Cup Tower
This is a stacking game were 2 or more players (Variant A/B) are stacking Black Coffee cups and Coffee-To-Go’s as high as possible. The variants are differentiated by the footprint of the initial layer. First Player to tip the tower loses.

"A smell of blood, fire and electricity roams the dark and moist corridors. An epic battle is taking place! The mighty wizard lifts his staff, looking down on his fallen Warrior – we have a new fighter, a new hero, a new Master!"

Mastery: Become a Hero is a fantasy adventure card game that combines the dynamics of a three-dimensional board game with the strategy, speed and excitement of simultaneous battles.

In this world, you are an adventurer seeking fame and riches. In this first version of the game, you will have a warrior and a magician. Later, there will be several classes added to the game. When choosing your class, chose the one that most represents you as a character. Each class has its own completely unique combat style, that has some vital statistics which will influence the performance during battle.

Cards are the key element in Mastery: Become a Hero. Careful selection of the cards when constructing your deck will allow you to create complex and devastating strategies. Mastery cards belong to three types/categories:

1. Actions and Magic: these are the cards used to construct your deck, they characterize your characters combat style and skill.
2. Objects: treasures that you will collect through out Mastery – become a hero. They influence your combat strategies but you cannot include them in your deck: they are placed, face down, beside the game table.
3. Equipment: weapons and armor. They have special effects which influence your strategies. As with Objects, they cannot be part of your deck: they are instead placed, face up, beside the game table.

Each match round takes place through five simultaneous phases;  Turn's beginning › Card drawing › Movement  › Combat  › Turn's End. You have to choose you cards for the whole combat round in advance and your cannot change any cards after the round has started. In the world of Mastery, the deck is representing your character profile. Constructing your character profile is here summarized within these three rules:

1. The character profile must be formed by no less than 30 cards and no more than 50.
2. You can only include cards marked with the class your character belongs to (exceptions are possible on some game variations).
3. Object and Equipment cards should not be part of your deck. But you can choose and bring with you to a match 2 object cards, placed down beside the board. You can freely select Equipment cards as long as the total number of occupancy (parameter written on the Equipment card) for all the equipment chosen is equal to or less than 5.

The combat rounds continue until either of the champions has fallen and the winner has become a hero!
Ladybugs eat aphids, be the first player to roll the dice and eat 5,000 aphids for victory. Eat fast and win!

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Air Elemental Expansion Pack
description: The Air Elemental Zephrylos is the competitive prize for Month 2 of the Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing Organized Play Tournament, Elemental Evil. The prize includes one pre-painted miniature and numerous upgrade cards and tokens.
family: Game: Dungeons & Dragons – Attack Wing Miniatures Game

description:
Mysterium expansion contains 78 new cards:
42 dreams
6x2 persons
6x2 places
6x2 items
Hellweg westfalicus: Marktflecken is a postcard mini-expansion for Hellweg westfalicus.

Zooloretto Würfelspiel: Baby Boom is an expansion to Zooloretto Würfelspiel. It adds a few different new features:
- Instead of extra points, the reward for filling an enclosure first is an expansion with three more spaces for the given animal. Other players can't use these expansions.
- Heart symbols on specific enclosure spaces mark 'parent' animals, and as soon as the second heart symbol is crossed in an enclosure, a baby is born (another cross is added). Lions with expansion may even have two babies.
- For money you can still release animals from the barn OR score them for star symbols. There are star symbols on enclosure spaces as well. The more star symbols a player collects during game, the more points they can get (1 star symbol is worth only 1 point but 5 star symbols score 8...)
- Also, in the barn only one animal (of the player's choice) can be placed in infinite amount. For further animals of other species other animals' spaces have to be used (thus scoring -2 points for each).

A game with multiple choice questions where you have to guess which answer someone else would give. A handheld electronic buzzer-object will tell you if the answer is correct or not. If answered correctly, you get to draw another card, and the first person to get ten correct answers is the winner.

So each turn, you draw a card and ask the question and answers on it (for example, "what would you take out of a burning house?" A my pet, B photo album, C diary or D hairgel and facial cream). The player to your left has one electronic object, you hold the other. Choose the letter that corresponds to the answer you think that person would give and hold the two objects together. If answered correctly, there should be a buzzing sound.

You get to know each other a little better, and the best "mind reader" wins.

Seems similar to The New Electronic Perfect Match Game, but without the board.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – I.R.W. T'Met
description: This ship is the competitive prize for "The Void" episode Organized Play event.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – U.S.S. Constellation Expansion Pack
description: This ship is the competitive prize for the "The Doomsday Machine" episode Organized Play event.

boardgame: Think in a Blink Need to Know Knowledge
description:
Think in a Blink is a fun, interactive safety and ethical trivia game designed to teach children how to stay safe and to be kind.

Think in a Blink is an interactive family game where children "laugh while they learn." With humorous questions and brilliant illustration, Think in a Blink empowers children with "need to know knowledge" so they can respond safely and appropriately to life's detours.

In this edition of Think in a Blink there are four destination areas: Home, School, Recreation and Shopping. Each area is color coded and has 16 spaces for questions.  The player/team rolls the number-die and moves the number spaces indicated. When a player reaches the middle he collects the souvenir piece for that location.

Object of the game:
To answer the questions correctly and collect all 4 souvenir pieces. The player or team that collects all 4-souvenir pieces first is the winner.

Duel List is a competitive knowledge listing game in which players simultaneously list as many items in a topic as they can in 60 seconds on a piece of paper.

Gameplay: On a turn, the active player picks one of the 9 knowledge decks and rolls 2 dice.  1st die chooses category, 2nd die chooses specific topic.  Players have 60 seconds to list as many items as they can in that topic on a piece of paper.  Once the time is up, the active player starts to list out the items they wrote. Players list out their answers in clockwise order, scoring as needed. If any other player shared an answer, every player score 1 point for that answer.  Otherwise, the player receives 2 points for every valid, unique answer.

End of game: Once every player has had the chance to be the active player and choose a knowledge deck, then the game is over. Count up points, whoever has the most points wins.

Description from the publisher:

The wonderful thing about living in the glorious Age of Steam is the way that we can marry traditional randomizers, such as dice, with the new power of SCIENCE!

In addition to six d6s, Munchkin Steampunk: SCIENCE! Dice includes four new cards for Munchkin Steampunk!

boardgame: Swords and Bagpipes: Special Dagger deck
description:
The Special Dagger deck is a mini-expansion for Swords and Bagpipes board game.

It is an alternative Dagger deck for experienced players. Now the Dagger cards that you get for betrayal have additional features!

boardgame: Swords and Bagpipes: God Me Defend promo
description:
Kickstarter Promotional Bagpipe card for Swords and Bagpipes. The card's title comes from the motto of Scotland "In My Defens God Me Defend".

The card allows cancelling the special trait of the current English Arms invasion!

Two copies of the card come in the pack.

With the Terrain: Taxes expansion, players can now place villages on the map, and these villages accrue wealth as more tiles are added to the map.

Additionally, each player has a tax collector who may be sent to a village to tax the coins on it, with each coin being worth 1 victory point at game's end.

Magination is a game where you are in charge.

You can think of Magination as a deck of cards, where you can play a variety of different games. But unlike a deck of cards it’s much easier to be creative with the way you play. By experimenting with the pieces you will easily find fun and creative ways to enhance your gameplay. You can play games like magnetic golf, curling, air hockey or find new games never seen before. You can also use the magnets to build magtastic structures, either to use in games or to build new creations.

Magination will have an online community where the game can evolve. You will be able to find games, rate them and upload your own, and never run out of awesome ways to play. We know you guys will find more creative ways to use Magination than we ever could, which is why we’re handing the control to you. You vote on the best design for new pieces, making Magination completely user-driven. To make sure it grows into something personal for you, it will be possible to mix and match the pieces and colors of your choice, and easily expand the set you already own.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons Dice Masters: Battle for Faerûn – Minsc and Boo Promo Card
description:
Card Text:

While active, Minsc and Boo get an experience token (+1A and +1D) at the end of your turn. If Minsc and Boo go to the used pile, remove all of their experience tokens. Once per turn, you may remove a token from Minsc and Boo to force an opposing character to block.

Flavor Text:

"Go for the eyes, Boo!

Quibble is a quiz game, where you have to name differnt numbers of items in a specific category. You get different point for different difficulty levels and also the time is different in these levels.

Each difficulty level is represented by a pile of card, worth 2, 4, 6, 8 or 10 points and on these cards is written how many items need to be named to win the points (e.g. you have to name 8 movies that won an Oscar).

The player can offer points to other players to get help and moves foward the won points on the scoreboard, when the needed number of items was named. First player to 42 points win the game.

Cacao: Volcanoes contains three volcano tiles that replace the three gold mines. Each volcano tile has two edges that give a player 2 gold per attached worker and two edges that make a player pay 1 gold per attached worker. Thus, the placement of these volcanoes should be chosen carefully...

Additionally, the punchboard works as an inlay for the box to keep all components in place even when storing the box upright or transporting it.

In this word finding game the cards decide when two players have to duel for a minimum five letter word with the showing letters in it. Each duel a play wins is a victory point in the end and the player with the most victory points will win the game.

Dungeon Hack: Dungeon Construction is the ultimate dungeon creator!  By using a unique random generation system you can create endless dungeon levels using the Dungeon Construction set.

Dungeon Hack: Dungeon Construction uses multiple variations of Rooms, Corridors, Corridor Room, and Junctions to help create a randomly generated dungeon level every time.

Each of these components contains different obstacles such as doors, treasure locations, random encounters, etc...   You build the level and then you explore using the second system - Dungeon Hack: Dungeon Adventure.

Dungeon Hack is a solo D&D game on a micro level using 4”x4” tile cards representing dungeon rooms, corridor rooms, corridors and junctions while colored cubes represent your characters and the ever increasing monsters.  A multitude of cards depict and define the monsters, treasures, encounters, and obstacles you will face within the dungeon.

boardgame: Alpha Colony: The Galaxy Prime Expansion
description:
Build a settlement...in Galaxy Prime!

Colonial settlements have breached the infinite reaches of the universe and found their way to Galaxy Prime. Over 2 dozen of the indigenous races now vie for new territory on an unexplored world, each bringing their own unique abilities and culture to the struggle. They will have new terrain to develop and new buildings to erect, as well as new events to contend with. So whether you choose to colonize with the militant Kor or buy your way to victory with the wealth of the Thasians the fate of Galaxy Prime's Alpha Colony is in your hands!

From Technical Term Game Company.

This is a game based on the Victory Games Fleet System of tactical and operational naval warfare.

Individual named naval ships, airplane squadrons, and smaller coastal patrol combatants fight out a future 2nd Falklands War, Argentina vs Chile conflict, and other battles in the cold Southern seas.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
4th Fleet is based on the Victory Games Fleet Series system operational naval warfare featuring individual named ships, submarines, airplane squadrons, marine landing units, and smaller coastal patrol combatants.

This game covers the potential wars in The Caribbean Sea & Central America where a Nicaraguan canal has been completed.

boardgame: Vault Wars: Pitch Black Cavern Promo Card
description:
Promo card that was first available during the Kickstarter campaign for Vault Wars.

The card says:
Pitch Black Cavern
Blind-bid Auction:
After setting the opening bid, players will secretly select their bid amount, then simultaneously reveal them.

The payer with the highest bid is the winner. If tied, continue as a normal auction.

boardgame: Vault Wars: Cursed Idol Promo Card
description:
Promo card that was first available during the Kickstarter campaign for Vault Wars.

The card says:
Artifact
Before storage fees: Discard 1 non-junk item and equip Cursed Idol  to another player. That player may not use this ability this round.
After storage fees: Gain 1 [corruption icon]

boardgame: Vault Wars: Miser's Trove Promo Card
description:
Promo card that was first available during the Kickstarter campaign for Vault Wars.

Winner cannot sell items this round.

Adds 14 worker cards that can be hired each round from a new 'Worker Market'.

Each worker has a one-use ability that gives you a strategic advantage.

Rauf und Runter is a roll-and-move board game with a meerkat theme on two layers with a few twists.

The player's meerkat pawns are magnetic and move therefore simultaneously on the top and bottom side of the gaming board.
Players start in the corners and must move their meerkat to a specific location, randomly revealed, and back to a corner. Roll the die and move accordingly one to three spaces to your destination. Borders (hedges, walls) may not be crossed. Then it's the move of the next player.

Once reaching a corner, however, the board is immediately turned upside down and play commences on this side. Players that did not reached their target space get a random one-shot-bonus of either moving two extra spaces, crossing a border, or taking a second move. The first player to visit all locations, then reach a corner wins.

Be sure not to lift your meerkat from the board while moving as this will cause the corresponding figure to fall off.

Аппетитный замок (Appetizing Castle) is a push-your-luck dice game, for 2-4 players who try to build the strongest castle for princess Larissa, using dice towers.

The hungry monster Kirpicheyadny ate Princess Larissa's Castle. Players help her to build a new home using dice towers, with as many as 5 dice of different sizes. Dice should be collected from largest to smallest. If building the tower failed, Kirpicheyadny begins to move, and he will want to eat the unfinished castle!

How to draw a monster from the construction? Bake cakes: even a monster makes the right choice, what to eat, when offered a cake and a brick.

From the box:

The fun game of forward thinking and awareness!

Three characteristics... colour, symbol, size.

Place your tiles such that they match other tile(s) already on the table in at least one characteristic.  Simple?!  Sure it is.

But be extremely cautious with what tile(s) you place.  You may just be setting it all up beautifully for your opponent(s) to come in and out-think and out-score you.

Think a few steps ahead... and be aware - be very aware.

A fast paced game for 2-4 players.

This expansion comprises six new furnishing tiles which can only be played in specific locations on a player's Home board.

3 tiles which can only be played "on an underground lake":

1. Fairy Pool
2. Wishing Well
3. Hot Springs

2 tiles which can only be played "on the outside river":

4. Gold Stream
5. Harvest Spirits Shrine

1 tile which can only be played "next to an underground lake":

6. Mushroom Grotto
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Mecha 1945 is a tactical board game where you pilot big robots in a world war II alternate history.

It is a turn-based game, programming and simultaneous resolution. Each turn you can program movement, technic and combat order and resolved them on 91 hexagons board representing Manhattan.

The game is customizable with a lot of game modes, 9 board parts, around 190 cards to build your own mecha and a website with a 3D design module. The idea is that you can assemble the arms, legs, torsos, etc. so as to design the robot that suits you the most. You will have a 3D model that you can output with a 3D printer.

Depending the game mode you have choose you have to destroy foe Mecha, stay in the center of the map, capture flag, escort VIP,...

Play with you friends in teams or against them!

Argentina and Chile almost came to blows over Patagonia at the turn of the century before war was averted. What if peace negotiations had not been achieved? Who would have won the naval contest? Now, using Avalon Hill's Victory in the Pacific (VITP), you can find out for yourself.

Avalon Hill games, though dated, are still among the most fun to play. Unfortunately, they can take a long, long time to play. Even game of VITP can take a good 4-6 hours to play to completion. Patagonia War allows players to play a game with a similar system, but in a much shorter time (5 turns, 3 sea areas, 26 ships).

VAIPA Quarterly #10, Patagonia War, contains the following items:

1. The Rulebook 
2. The Map 
3. Twelve Chile ship counters. Fourteen Argentina ship counters. Six Control markers (counters). 
4. Small zip lock bags to place your counters into (Your option).

In addition, since this game is based on Avalon Hill’s Victory in the Pacific game, you will need a copy of that game. Specifically, you will need:

a. Damage markers 
 b. Disabled markers 
 c. Sunk markers 
 d. Dice 
 e. Rulebook

This expansion includes six Storm Arachn with reaper blades miniatures for the Therian army.  There are two different sculpts, five of one, one of the other, and a reference card for each.  Both sculpts have the same stats.

These units were not included in any army box but do not include new rules.

Égaru Idea to Market is a card game where you can learn the concepts of intellectual property and entrepreneurship.

The goal is to create projects starting with an Idea and ending in the Market, going through the different phases of the entrepreneurship process. To do this you must lay down cards in the correct order and possibly add booster cards to your own projects or attack cards to your opponents. A project requires between 3 and 6 phases to get to market - the more phases you get in the more points you will get.

Each player can have as many projects as he wants, but if a project is not closed with the Market card, it doesn’t count in the final score.

The player with the most points when there are no more cards to play is the winner.

Perplexity is a word game in which you attempt to collect letters to spell a 5-letter word. Letters are collected by landing on spaces containing a letter and answering a question. Questions are made up of some form of wordplay (double meanings, anagrams, sounds, etc).

Letters may be stolen or swapped with other players when landing on special locations on the board. All successful actions require answering a question.

The first player to collect 5 letters, spell a word with those letters, make it back to the center of the board and answer a question correctly wins the game.

boardgame: Monster High: Share or Scare Game
description:
From the box cover:

Grab your ghoul-friends for a fang-tastic game of truth or dare! On your turn, spin the spinner to see your fate...Spin Share and you'll reveal your spookiest secrets, monstrous crushes, and all the stuff that makes you eerily unique...try to guess each others' answers to score! Spin Scare and beware! You'll do one of a hundred challenges so fierce and funny they'll have you in stitches! It's a spook-a-rific race to get 13 points to win the game! Monster High Share or Scare-so fun, it's scary!

TXT Off (Text-Off) is a standard trivia game where players move around a board answering questions about texting language, shorthand and trivia. Categories may have all players answering or just the player whose turn it is. Players earn points by correctly answering questions. First player to 20 points wins.

ブゾク "Tribe" is a rather abstract cardgame rendering the conflict between two Mesoamerican tribes.  The game plays over four alternatingly good and bad seasons: trying to increase the size of the tribe in each good season, and attempting to avoid the perils of the bad seasons.

It's explicitly a team game.  Teams alternate play: but unlike most such card games, each team-mate plays a card secretly together, then discusses (without revealing anything about the played cards) which should take effect first.  Each card played adjusts the ownership of important resources, might grant a hunting token, or might increase (in a good season) or decrease (in a bad) the size of the tribe.  Largest tribe at the end of the game wins.

The Metagame is a card game where you and your friends share your most profound and hilarious opinions about everything. This expansion lets you ask questions like...

Which reveals the secret meaning of the universe: The X-Files or The Jetsons?

Which is more fiction than science: Cylons or the Tardis?

The Metagame Science Fiction Expansion Pack includes 54 all-new cards that add a cosmic mix of books, films, comics, games and television to your Metagame Deck. Brought to you by the future.

Add an intergalactic dose of science fiction to The Metagame!
The Science Fiction Expansion adds 54 new cards that get you talking.
40 new culture cards: Space Invaders to Starship Troopers, Metropolis to The Matrix Trilogy, Firefly to Frankenstein.
14 far-out opinion cards, such as: "Which is more alien?" and "If you were the last living human being, you would definitely want this."

The Metagame is a card game where you and your friends share your most profound and hilarious opinions about everything. This expansion lets you ask questions like...

Which is more likely to incite a revolution: Thelma & Louise or The Sound of Music?

Which is responsible for the most childhood nightmares: Fantasia or Jaws?

The Metagame Film 101 Expansion Pack includes 54 all new cards that add a glamorous mix of box office blockbusters, cult classics, animated features, and silver screen treasures to your Metagame deck. Popcorn not included.

Add a dose of cinematic magic to The Metagame!
The Film 101 Expansion adds 54 cards that get you talking.
40 new culture cards: The Godfather to Grease, The Shining to The Seven Samurai, Nosferatu to Napoleon Dynamite.
14 cinematic opinion cards such as: "Which is most glamorous?" and "Best enjoyed in the dark."

boardgame: PWNs: A Game of Strategic Mayhem
description:
PWNs is a unique game where you command your team of pawns to traverse a board filled with potentially-dangerous terrain to battle your way to victory.

Your team will use a combination of cards, abilities, and terrain to knock out enemy PWNs.

The object is to knock out just one team from the game. Then whichever team has the most remaining players with the most health points wins!

With so many possible combinations and variations randomly assigned at the beginning of the game, you'll never play the same game twice.

From the publisher:

SlapCAT is a wildly fast reflex game full of slapped cats, cats in hats, dead rats, and poo. Players race to slap the parts they need, then try to build a beautiful cat while throwing fishbones and stealing from their neighbors.

Each round players build a hand of cards by slapping from a limited selection of cat parts. The players then play from this hand of cards to build a high scoring cat in front of them. But watch out -- your neighbors may attack and take your only head! What's a cat with no head?!

A mixture of reflexes, card drafting and strategic card play, SlapCAT is feisty fun that's over so fast your friends and family will be clawing at you to play again!

Travel through the galaxy as you buy, sell and trade organizations, factions and locations as depicted in the Mass Effect video game trilogy.

Includes:

     6 Collectible Tokens: N7 Helmet, Space Hamster, Normandy, Omni Tool, Shifty Looking Cow, and Mako
     Custom Game Board featuring Elites, Special Interests, Mercs, and Crews
     Custom Chance & Community Chest Cards

This is an educational game to think and easy mechanics, wich are based on questions and answers. We must get to the place where the challenge lies for each player who randomly received a letter of challenge.This show you the objects you will need have before departure to your challenge. But to move your figure you need energy and this energy it´s won with the correct answers to the questions of the letters

Spanish description:
Un juego al estilo del trivial (Porque es de ir respondiendo preguntas), pero más atractivo al tener que buscar cierto equipo y luego llegar a un punto del mundo según la "misión" que te tocase

The Chu–Han Contention is updated and commercial edition of Chuhan War, a historical wargame of Chu–Han Contention from 206 B.C. to 202 B.C.
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chu–Han_Contention
The game includes three short scenarios and one long scenario. Each player plays the role of Xiang Yu, Liu Bang or Han Xin (only in 3-player scenario).
family: History: Warring States Period of China

description:
No moderator, no elimination, five-minute games.

Blink of Death is a fast game for 4-12 players in which everyone gets a role: One of the Shifter Demons, the Abandoned, the helpful Anchorite, or one of a dozen different characters, each with a special ability. In 30 seconds, The Demon must kill one others or lose.

Because Blink of Death is so fast, fun, and engaging, you'll want to play it again and again, and no two games are ever the same.

A great family game based on classic and recent movies.
Spend hours of fun as you try to recall details from your favourite movies. Your brain cells will be "Smokin'" as you try to beat the clock and your opponents, to be first with the correct answer.
The design of the gameboard allows you to answer questions both on your own turn or the turn of another player!
If you disagree with another player's answer you can dispute and provide another response.

boardgame: Seas of Iron: Battleship Expansion Pack
description:
From the publisher:

The Battleship expansion pack contains two additional iconic ships, German Battleship Bismarck and Japanese Battleship Yamato.

Ormen Långe is a children's game where the players roll a die with different colors on the sides, telling which square to go to. If the square has a snake piece on it, the player can collect this and add to his snake to make it longer. The player with the longest snake at the end of the game is the winner.

Het Supersnelle Kennisspel.

A quiz game for kids from the primary school. They test their knowledge about school-topics and earn victory points by answering multiple choice questions correctly. There are ways to steal points from other players too.

The objective is to gain the most number of victory points after a predetermined number of questions.

Draw and read a scenario card out loud. Players each select the cards from their hands that they think will win them the round. The person that read the scenario picks her favorite card for the scenario. The person that was picked gets a point and reads the next scenario card.

The player with the most points (White Cards) at the end of the game is the winner.

Revansch  is a real-estate game, where player starts with low capital and collects sets of properties and invests to industry. The player moves around the board trying to visit places like bank, stockmarket and salary. The winner is the only player left after the other players left the game due to bankruptcy.

Treasure Island is a roll-and-move game where players hunt for pirate treasure.  It is included in The Great Pirate Activity Book, published by Kingfisher. There are four starting points, each marked by a ship and leading to a different track.  Each player chooses a ship and places his or her starting marker on it.  More than one player may choose the same ship.  Players take turns rolling the die and moving their marker along the track, following the directions on spaces where they land.  Spaces may move players forward or back on the track or cause gain of loss of turns.  When a player reaches the pirate treasure on an exact roll, he or she then heads back the starting ship.  The first player back to his or her starting ship wins.

Often confused with Dennis the Menace and Gnasher, this is actually a very different game of set collection and movement.
Players are required to move around the board to different locations, collecting Menace and mischief cards while avoid Dad and the Slipper! The location cards all require a different number and combination of Menace cards to be completed and the first to complete a set number is the winner!

There’s a Snitch in the Mafia, and the Godfather has ordered us to take him out! Snitch is a hidden identity card game set in the world of the mafia, played with 4-6 friends in about 15 to 30 minutes. Play as the Mobster and attempt to whack the Snitch. Or play as the Snitch, and frame the mobsters so they turn on each other! Watch out for the Police, as they can be tipped off towards anyone. If we don't whack the Snitch in time, he's sure to get into protective custody!

A round consists of a hit being placed on one of the players. Whether or not they’re a Snitch is for you to decide. Use cards in your hand to attack, or protect the target. Cash can be used to influence the hit in either direction! If enough damage is dealt to a player, they can be sent to the hospital, or whacked!

Players take part in Mob Jobs. If the job is snitched on, the game gets more difficult for the Mobsters, and the Snitch gets closer to protective custody. The deck also consists of mafia associates, who's skills can be used to further your agenda.

The Mobsters win if they whack the Snitch! The Snitch wins if enough Mobsters are whacked, or if enough police are in play!

boardgame: Ascension: Cetra, Benefactor of All Promo Card
description:
Promo card from Gencon 2015

Cost: 5 Runes

Lifebound Hero

Starting with you, and then going in turn order, each player acquires a Hero without paying its cost. The Heroes acquired this way are put on top of their owner's deck

3 Honor

Promo card from Gen Con 2015

Cost: 6 Runes

Void Hero

Gain 3 Power
You may spend 3 runes as thought it was power this turn

3 Honor

Promo card from Gencon 2015

Cost: 6 Runes

Void Construct

Once per turn, gain 1 Power
When you defeat a monster in the center row, banish any monster under this and place the defeated monster under this. You may defeat the monster under this as though it were in the center row. If this leaves play, banish any monsters under this.

3 Honor

Translated from the back of the Finnish edition box:

TOM tom tom TOM tom tom ... tactful Native American drumming raises the atmosphere, the cards turn, drumming stops and the race to find the right image begins! The one who is the fastest, slaps their hand on the right card and wins the plume into his Indian headband.

The first to get five plumes into his headband will be the Great Chief, and he wins the game!

First edition:

Need someone to take the blame when you stab your buddy in the back? Look no further! The new Munchkin Vinyl Figure from Funko will keep all the orcs at bay, ruin miniatures gaming, and probably won't spontaneously combust! Huzzah! Includes a new exclusive Munchkin card!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Jeu du Cirque is a board game that is played in 15 minutes. It consists of a board, 4 pawns and 2 dice, each for a different level reached on he board, with colours and pictures on the die sides. Players roll the dice and advance their tokens along the board, like in Snakes and Ladders. Depending on the square that they end up on, they can either ride along the neck of the giraffe and win tickets, or descend on the trapeze and fall back several spaces. The winner is the player who arrives first at the circus tent, at the top of the board.

From the publisher:

The squish or be squished family game. Will you be the last player with the unsquished fish? Start the game with your own school of dough-fish. Flip cards in turn until you complete a three-in-a-row. When you do, you are awarded a "free squish" of any opponent's fish. Stay on your fins! Everyone is trying to squish your fish too! Last "fish" standing wins!

La Quête des Chevaliers (Knights' Quest) is a boardgame that is primarily a memory mechanic game.

The two players face each other, both players must remember the location of the Excalibur swords and dragons laid about the board. Then they close their eyes, moving their finger on the plateau to reach the castle of their opponent without touching a dragon. And when they find Excalibur, they win gold coins. Players must therefore memorize the course and stay well focused. The winner is the player who wins the most coins by reaching the other's castle.
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description: Devine Super Héros is a game of memory and deduction. Each game lasts 15 minutes. Among a hand of 16 cards, each player chooses a character and places it on a stand before them, hidden from their opponent. By asking each other questions, each player must guess the Super Hero chosen by the other player. The first to guess the other's superhero wins! This plays very similarly to Guess Who.

An excellent addition to the My First Play World series from HABA, the ' Animal Merry-Go-Round Motor Skill Game will be both fun and educational for your little one! Each animal has his home. The cheeky friends, however, have a lot of fun with the turning disk and love to change places! Who can return order to the jumble? To do so, little zoo friends need a good touch and some luck at turning the seesaw. Specially designed to foster motor skills and introduce the concept of games with instructions. This set includes fun game ideas to keep little ones entertained for longer. Made in Germany, the board and tiles are sturdy cardboard and the pieces are made of beech wood. The game includes 1 elephant (3.00" tall), 1 lion, 1 zebra, 1 flamingo, 1 monkey, 1 rotatable seesaw, 10 play tiles, 1 game board, set of instructions. Perfect for children ages 2 years and up.

boardgame: Raid & Trade: War on the Streets
description:
From deep within the wastelands, far from the Golden City comes a new breed of survivors. Forged in the most desolate conditions these opportunistic individuals prowl the ruins watching for passers-by – to either join or exploit.

Skilled nomads, these ‘Hitchhikers’ can twist any situation to their advantage, whether through silver-tongued and honeyed words or brute force and lethal cunning. Having been bred far from the Golden City, some Hitchhikers have only recently heard of the glittering utopia and many have made their way across hundreds of miles, braving unimaginable dangers and hardships for a chance to learn more about this potential prize.

Some consider the Hitchhikers to be parasites that latch onto and leech from others, but for them it is a way of life and the ultimate path to survival. They are wild rovers, moving from one opportunity to the next in search of the last big pay-off… But they are not the only ones who come from the shadows.. Riders & gangs, rebels & outlanders are rising in the wastelands. They tried to enter the Golden City, but they were stopped by the Forces and now they are exiled for ever in the wastelands. Anyone who is trying to reach the Golden City, has to face them and their minions!

Cha-rades? Hey, what's that? Charades is a way of describing things without using words and by using only gestures, facial expressions and body movements. The aim of the game is to guess as many of the things acted out during the game as fast as possible. Classic fun for all ages! Includes blank cards so you can act out whatever you like. Portable Version in a tin by HABA!

Explore lands where no other explorer has yet set foot. Is this cold, brutal land inhabited? And if so, by what?

This expansion contains 96 additional cards that will increase the size of the Continent and offer you a new curse to unravel!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A Bible trivia game translated from the Dutch game Kwistet. 396 questions about people, places, and events of the Bible.  
Answering questions correctly allows players to move their pieces around a Pachisi-like board; the winner is the one to get both playing pieces 'home' first.

Two decks of cards provide different difficulty levels; a third deck (for children) is available separately.

Final Boss is the long awaited mini expansion for the base game:

It adds 3 new fighters, an entire new mode (co op) 3 new "boss cards" and new type of cards to be used in the new mode.

The package is small but it adds a lot to the base game

Card filler on the mechanics of "rock-paper-scissors."

"The old dungeon of the castle of King Cybertania full of weapons, stones, and ancient scrolls. However, the magician Algorimus created evil robots-Golems, who attack anyone who dared to walk through the dungeon. You - a brave knight, whom the king had locked in the dungeon, along with your rivals. Your task - to survive in the dungeon, to preserve and increase your team Knights and collect the most Trophies and destroy more SteamBots"

Players in the game turn-by-turn move tokens of his knights on the game field, consisting of Terrain cards and take the card out of the deck, indicating findings on that place.

It may be Trophies, robots-SteamBots or pieces of another Territory. If two or three players meet on one Terrain card, they fight among themselves for the findings by the "rock-paper-scissors" card.

The winner is the player that kept more Knights in the team, destroy more STEAMBOTs and earned the most Trophies. For all of this, The King get Gold. More Gold - and you are best (and famous) Knight.

Final English Rules: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1Emk6UhyrtiV8kl0vL0e5B0rizKIRUKjt

boardgame: Big Box Office "The Movie Business Game!"
description:
Big Box Office "The Movie Business Game!" lets you become the Producer, Director, Writer and Agent of a Big Box Office Hollywood movie!

When you arrive in Hollywood, you first go to work.  After you make $10,000 you enter the main playing board.  As you move your token around the board, you accumulate "Industry Title Rings," such as Black for Producer, Red for Director, Blue for Writer and Green for Agent.

As you go around the board, you accumulate "Movie Titles" when you enter the theatre where the movie is playing.  You enter the theatre and multiply your profits depending on how many "Industry Title Rings" you have on your token.

Other squares have "Pick Director (or other industry title) Card."  These cards direct you to other areas of the board, which can be good or bad, depending on which "Movie Titles" you have.

You can also land on a "Spin the Wheel" square.  This wheel will either give or take away your industry title.

The first person to make $1,000,000 wins the game.

Orléans: Invasion, the first large expansion for the award-winning game "Orléans", contains six scenarios designed by Reiner Stockhausen and Inka & Markus Brand. It also includes a new set of Place Tiles that can be used with or without any of the scenarios.

The new scenarios are:

    Prosperity: An extensive scenario introducing new Events and a Carpenter that all players may use to build Structures for victory points, designed by Inka and Markus Brand.
    Invasion: A cooperative scenario in which players have to work together to fend off an invasion while fulfilling personal objectives, designed by Inka and Markus Brand.
    The Duel: A two-player scenario in which both players race to be the first to complete a series of objectives, designed by Reiner Stockhausen.


In addition, Invasion contains three single-player challenges by Reiner Stockhausen.

    The Dignitary: You are an important dignitary trying to gather as many Citizens as possible.
    Capital Vierzon: Develop the city of Vierzon to become the capital of its region.
    Traveling Salesman: Travel through the country and make important deliveries to faraway cities.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Space Yoghurt is a competitive game for two to four space adventurers. By rolling dice and making use of the moon's strategic places, the player maneuvers his or her crew into the spaceship's cockpit and strives to win the cosmic vacation. Will you be the one who receives the Golden Ticket from the Galactic Empress Yolibolibana III?

Each player has a crew of three spaceters and takes turn to roll the three crew dice to move along the moon track. If you end up on a Bouncy Crater you can make haste and bounce all the way to the cockpit, soaring past the other spaceters. Ending up on a Yoghurt Pool gives you special powers, but the Space Yoghurt is wacky strong and must be handled with care. It might win you the game, sending other players' spaceters home or be so bitter that you must go home and rest for a while. Easy rules, fast play and big wins! See you out there, brave space adventurers!

Far in the future, humanity makes contact with a distant, dying alien race, the Sectridians, whose survival depends upon a constant influx of a rare substance known as primordium, found only in a remote section of an asteroid field close to the Sectridian home world. Unfortunately the Sectridians lose the ability to acquire the primordium for themselves, because the asteroids become infested with another hostile alien species, the monstrous Raptezoids and their horrific mother, the Hive Queen.

Encounters with the Raptezoids prove deadly to the feeble Sectridians. Thus, they are forced to abandon their primordium rich asteroids and, consequently, begin to die off. In a final desperate act, the Sectridians send a distress signal throughout the galaxy, hoping another benevolent alien race might come to their rescue. When their demise seems all but certain, a few rag-tag human factions receive the Sectridian cry for help, and, greedily sensing an opportunity to cash in on their plight, agree, for a handsome fee, to mine the Primordium for the Sectridians, battling the Raptezoid scourge in the process. These human factions also learn that, by gathering and selling specimens of the Raptezoids to a few curious members of an enigmatic scientific community sprinkled among them, they can earn a small, yet not insignificant, additional profit.

It is not long before the lust for wealth sends these mining factions into conflict with one another, as they each seek to claim more and more asteroids for themselves. Ordinary space miners, most often equipped with nothing more than their trusty pick-axes, have to quickly learn to become soldiers as they are sent headlong into bloody battle against the armies of other mining factions. These conflicts become known as the Primoridum Wars, and rage for several years before the Sectridians suddenly, and mysteriously, disappear.

You will play the leader of one these human factions. You will expand your primordium mining operations throughout the asteroid field, wage war against your opponents, fight the terrible Raptezoids, and gather Raptezoid specimens. At the end of five rounds, the player with the most wealth wins the game.

The Primordium Wars is a quasi 4x, tile placement, resource gathering, and worker displacement game where players place tiles representing various structures onto their asteroids in order to increase their primordium mining operations. It is also a game in which combat plays a significant, though secondary role, where players attack each other in order to claim more resource producing asteroids, or to disrupt their opponents' operations. The game takes place on a modular board where, in each round, players slowly shift the asteroids around on the game board, subtly, or often drastically, changing the strategic situation.

In each round, players will first acquire, in a bidding phase, tiles that represent the items needed to expand their mining empires such as: Various asteroid modules to build on the asteroids (mining facilities, orbital turrets, pulse cannons, science laboratories, etc), various starship upgrades (warp drives, sniping lasers, stealth devices, etc), miner and mercenary reinforcements, build points for building new starships, and grav technology for tethering asteroids together into clusters (a core mechanism that greatly increases mining production, and expands the functionality of most modules built on the asteroids).
Players will then deploy their newly acquired items onto their asteroids and starships, take turns moving their pieces deeper into the asteroid field in order to expand their mining empire, and battle other players, Raptezoids, and the dreaded Hive Queen.
At the end of each round players will collect their income from their mining operations and Raptezoid specimens, and a new round will begin.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 2 – Reggiane Re.2001 Falco II
description:
Publisher Blurb:

The Reggiane Re.2001 Falco II was an Italian fighter aircraft. With its flexible design, it proved to be able to undertake a number of roles, from convoy escort to night fighter. Thanks to its maneuverability, it could dogfight with more powerful opponents like the British Supermarine Spitfire. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW2 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW2 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards. This is not a complete game. You must own a WW2 Wings of Glory - Starter Set to play.

From the publisher:

GrowthBreaker. It’s here. The world’s second tabletop novel.

This is a card game for anyone to play alone. It contains whimsical items, enjoyable places, and helpful friends. Your heroes will be challenged in their quest to reach the goals of each season, which are broken up into several serialized decks known as episodes.

The game is also a story. The major plot of GrowthBreaker is always the same, but what you do during the game, and if your survive, is up to you. It can be played an endless amount of times, without ever experiencing the same game twice.

The object of the game is for the player to move a token around the PLACE PILE, acquiring cards out of the DECK as you travel. The more cards you acquire, the stronger your heroes will grow, allowing more powerful cards to be played.

But be warned, the more powerful your heroes become, the more powerful the enemies that will find them.

A game of GrowthBreaker ends when Starting HERO card(s) of the episode is/are Cycled.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 2 – Yakovlev Yak-1
description:
From the publisher:

The Yakovlev Yak-1 was a Soviet fighter aircraft. It was extremely maneuverable, fast and well armed, and, just as importantly, it was easy to maintain and reliable. Some Soviet pilots considered it to be, in its tactical and technical characteristics, equal to the Messerschmitt Bf 109G. The Yak-1 was also employed by the elite all-female unit including the most famous fighter pilot woman of all times, Lydia Litvyak. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW2 Wings of Glory Airplane Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW2 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards. This is not a complete game. You must own a WW2 Wings of Glory - Starter Set to play.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 2 – Avro Lancaster B Mk.III
description:
From the publisher:

The Avro Lancaster is a British four-engine World War II heavy bomber, designed and built by Avro for the Royal Air Force (RAF). It first saw active service with RAF Bomber Command in 1942 and, as the strategic bombing offensive over Europe gathered momentum, it became the main heavy bomber used by the RAF. The "Lanc", as it was affectionately known, became the most famous and most successful of the Second World War night bombers, delivering 608,612 long tons of bombs in 156,000 sorties. Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW2 Wings of Glory Special Pack is a ready-to-play large size model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW2 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards, as well as specific rules and components necessary to use the airplane. Bonus contents in this pack include one double-sided industrial complex card and special rules. This is not a complete game. To play, you must own a WW2 Rules and Accessories Pack.

From the box front:

The 3D Balancing Animal Game. Easy to learn, exciting and quick to play and Educational too! To beat the clock, pile on the pressure, don't tip the balance. Hundreds of ways to win.

From the box rear: The first player turns over top card and the sand timer. The player then attempts to Score by performing a successful Balazoo before the timer runs out. A successful Balazoo occurs when all 5 animals are arranged in a pattern very similar to that shown on the configuration card. The player must take their hands away and the animals must freely stand.

A chess-style game using a hexagonal grid (with seven cells across each edge) and 25 custom pieces per player. Pieces may move in straight lines in up to 12 directions, either through the sides or corners of the cells, capturing pieces by landing on them. A player loses when their "God" is captured.

The game supports either two or three players, with each player starting with control of a "universe" that is the region of cells formed within the triangle of three consecutive corners of the board. In a two-player game, the universes are opposite. In a three-player game, they form a complete ring around the edge of the board.

Players may place their pieces anywhere within their universe at the start of the game (with no fixed set-up), even holding some pieces back to be placed on the board (in their universe) during play. The exceptions are the three corners, which must be empty before play begins (the central corner is termed the "throne" and the two flanking corners are the "palace gates").

The types of playing piece (with number of pieces per player listed in brackets) move as follows:

     God (1): one step in any direction.
     Ruler (2): unlimited steps in any direction.
     Ambassador (4): unlimited steps through sides only.
     Warrior (4): unlimited steps through corners only.
     Priest (2): one step through sides only.
     Spy (2): one step through corners only.
     Minion (10): one step in any direction.


Only a God or Ruler can occupy the throne. A Minion can be substituted with a different captured piece if it reaches an opponent's palace gates (to any piece except a Ruler) or an opponent's throne (where it can be substituted for a captured Ruler).

An educational game that uses Abstracts of  Sciences to assist students  learning the major oxidative pathways, glycolysis and the citric acid cycle. The multicolored gameboard shows these pathways  with each metabolic intermediate identified by name, structural formula and number of carbon atoms. The names of the enzymes are printed on arrows that symbolize the chemical reactions of the pathways.

The players move tokens representing carbon atoms along the pathways. A molecule of a carbon compound is symbolized by a stack of the proper number of tokens. Thus glucose would have six tokens, and 3-phosphoglyceraldehyde, three.
 A deck of 70 “enzyme cards” is used to regulate the play. Each card shows a reaction present on the board with the name of the enzyme. To move a molecule from one step in a pathway to the next, a player must have in hand the appropriate enzyme card. There are three enzyme cards in the deck for each reaction on the board as well as four “wild enzyme” cards which may be used to catalyze any reaction. The game makes use of ATP  “currency” of three denominations: one, three and ten ATP units. The ATP is gained or lost by a player depending on the reactions being  carried out as the game proceeds. The object of the game is to gain more ATP than the other players. Oxidative phosphorylation is simulated by the use of tokens representing NADH and FADH, and special cards representing oxygen. Oxygen debt is simulated  whenever a player  with reducing power lacks oxygen cards.

Designed by Hiroyuki Okinoi, this game is published in a Japanese / English edition.

The Empress of Time inadvertently triggers a malfunction that causes different time intervals and parallel words to intersect. You are an adventurer who is unwittingly sucked into this time spiral. Your objective is collecting a form of energy called mana and bringing the world back to order, allowing you to return to your own time.

The objective of play is to travel the game board and collect mana, a strange form of energy, before the other players. Players advance the number of squares according to the number rolled on the die. Cast Cards can be set along points of the board, allowing a player to collect payment from others. “Cast” refers to the residents of the alien world. Once a player collects 100 mana, he wins by returning to Start. This means that even if another player has more mana than you, if you reach the goal with 100 mana in hand, you are the winner. To decide runners-up, count the number of mana each player has at the end of the game.

description:
"Fight monsters to impress the King and become a Knighted Royal Mage of Ebenor!"

Brief feel of gameplay
Players use Mana cards and optional character abilities to fight a shared field of monsters in a way requiring light, but engaging strategy and careful observation of their opponents situation.

Mechanical overview
The structure of the game is for players to balance the value of cards they place down below monsters, in order to draw new cards and gain either their cards or the monsters themselves as 'prestige' (points) over multiple rounds until there are no monsters left or any player has run out of cards.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Duel Decks Anthology
description:
From the official website: Never before have so many gathered in one place. These will be fights to remember.

These eight, ready-to-play, 60-card Magic decks showcase the first four dueling match-ups. Rejoin the fray as the game's most famous Planeswalkers go head to head. Relive the Divine clashing with the Demonic, and Elves confronting Goblins. Replay the original duels, or mix and match the combatants to create new battles across the Multiverse.

Duel Decks: Anthology contains all eight decks from the first four Duel Decks products:


     Duel Decks: Elves vs. Goblins
     Duel Decks: Jace vs. Chandra
     Duel Decks: Divine vs. Demonic
     Duel Decks: Garruk vs. Liliana


Note: Does not expand the base game. Not collectible. Completely stand alone 1v1 duel decks.

Bluff-Yatzy is a variation of Yatzy, where the player can hide dice with the help of 3 stackable cups. The player may when be bluffing about the result. The winner is the player with the best dice-combinations and/or best bluffing skills.

Välkommen till samhället is a board game there the players starts as a new born and walks thru lifes different events. The player roll dice and moves around board, coming in contact with agencies, feelings, standards, institutes and different problems. The aim is to try to stay alive as long a possible.

As the leader of the team, you should make the best use of the heros at your disposal.
The more wars, armors, and territories you claim, the closer you are to victory.

Powerful prophets and sacred animals will help you reverse the situation.
But watch out for other players, maybe they are figuring the same thing.

Hero cards form the player's hand cards.
Players use them to claim Legend cards, attack other players, and defend oneself.

Legend cards include Wars, Armors, Territories, and Sacred Animals.
They provide VP in different ways and are contested by players.

Players score VP by claiming Legend cards, the player with the most VP wins the game.

Summary for Game Flow

Turn flow:
Turn over 2 Legend cards, and the active player performs 3 actions.

Actions:
(A) Claim a card with your Hero.
(B) Claim a card with your Prophet.
(C) Snatch a card from another player.
(D) Recall Heros from your Hero deck.

Game End:
When the rotated card is revealed, the final round begins.
The game is played for 10/7/5 rounds in 2/3/4 -player games.

boardgame: Star Wars: Box Busters – Battle of Naboo & Battle of Hoth
description:
From the packaging:

"Two Players face off in an epic battle of planetary conquest in the Star Wars universe. Roll the dice, blast through your opponent's defenses and capture their command center to win"

Each player needs their own Battle Cube, which is a small diorama. Each has a unique combination of abilities.

This set has Star Wars: Box Busters – Battle of Hoth, Battle of Naboo is exclusive to this set.

Corporations begin setting up special zoos across the world, but the cost for security spiraled out of control and the zoos began to keep the dinosaurs tranquilized. Demand quickly waned and underground fight clubs of excess dinosaurs became popular and began producing huge dividends. Larger and larger dinosaurs were pitted against each other until today we have Ultimate Dinosaur Fighting. With millions of dollars involved, special training schools appeared, and soon Dinosaur Pit Fighting was the most popular sport in the world.

Ultimate Dinosaur Fighting is a Tactical Battle board game. Players win by wounding other dinosaurs to attain victory points and staying alive. When the first dinosaur in the pit dies the game ends on the following turn.

Players choose between 8 different dinosaurs to fight with and place their miniature on the board. Each dinosaur has a different number of life points and different custom abilities and tactics needed to win. Each turn is a combination of movement based on speed and simultaneous action declarations.

You can play one-off fights or use the campaign rules to control a pen of dinosaurs and give them Training, Mutations and Tech upgrades to improve your dinosaurs and make them unique in the world of Ultimate Dinosaur Fighting.

Brought to you by Dark Cult Games
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A promotional card for Dead of Winter, from Filosofia Editions Online Store.

This promo introduces a new Crossroads Card that you can use in the game.

Part of the Dead of Winter series.

From the publisher's website:

Momentum Football is truly unique in that it allows you to quickly play a full game in 15-20 minutes while yielding not only team stats, but also individual player stats. With Momentum Football you'll be able to track the following team and individual stats:Passing Attempts, Passing Completions, Total Passing yardage, Interceptions, Fumbles Lost, Sacks, Rushing Attempts, Rushing Yardage, and all scoring.

Momentum Football uses a drive-by-drive approach to resolve games. But, don’t let the simplicity fool you. Over 254 variables are used to create each team’s chart making them highly accurate for both team performance and individual player performance. And best of all, we’ve designed the game so that there is little to no set-up time involved. As you become comfortable with the rules, you’ll be able to grab two team sheets, a score sheet and four dice and begin play immediately.

Games are resolved drive-by-drive, rather than play-by-play. But, again, don’t let that fool you into thinking that individual contributions aren’t important. You’ll quickly see the impact of great rushers, passers and receivers on offense and pass rushers, cover men and big hitters on defense. So, while the game is resolved by drives, individual player contributions are the difference between winning and losing.
Don’t fret; special teams, penalties, turnovers, home field advantage, and a team’s momentum are all captured within the game. You’ll be amazed as you watch everything unfold on your tabletop just as it does in real life.

As the name of the game implies, capturing the momentum as it swings from one team to the other is a big part of the game. Our system is unique in that it doesn’t overly dominate the game mechanics nor is it over stated. However, just as in real life, a team’s momentum can mean all of the difference between winning and losing.

Citrus: Neue Landschaftsplättchen (Citrus: New Landscape Tiles) is a mini-expansion for Citrus that consists of three new Landscape tile types (with three tiles of each type). They are:

• Plow: You can take over a neutral region that is one or two Plantation tiles in size. Place one of your Workers onto the region to show that it now belongs to you.

• Storehouse: You can store one Plantation tile that you just bought from the market to build on a later turn. You may build the Plantation only when you choose the action "Build" and build at least one other Plantation from the market. (With more than one Storehouse, you can store more than one Plantation, with one tile per Storehouse). You may, in the same turn, first build a Plantation tile from your Storehouse and additionally store a new tile from the market.

• Large Well: When you build a Plantation tile adjacent to the Large Well, this region is immediately scored. Count the points for this region and add two more points for the Large Well. Place your Worker back onto your tableau and immediately earn income for it. Then remove the Large Well tile from the game.

The expansion, like the game, is language-independent.

Shattered Earth is a tabletop war game set on Earth many decades into the future, after the world has been ravaged by an unprecedented number of natural disasters. The geology of the Earth has been forever changed, now cracked and broken, existing landmasses drowned in vast oceans whilst others have formed anew, created by the seismic movements of tectonic plates.

Each cataclysmic shockwave formed by extinction-level natural disasters pounded against the door of another world – a mirror image of our own – until eventually that door gave way. The resulting breach flooded the Earth with an esoteric power known as Ether, a volatile energy that is the very foundation of the Immateria, the world of dreams. And in return, the material world bled into the breach, corrupting and choking everything it touched.

Mankind now struggles to comprehend this new power, seeing in it both ruin and salvation.  Scientists attempt to harness its energy to create wondrous new technology, whilst others practice dark rituals and worship strange new powers, using Ether to alter their surroundings and themselves, bending reality to their whim.

This is 30 A.E. This is Shattered Earth.

boardgame: The Good Dinosaur: Roarin' River Game
description:
Race the rapids to get home!

Work together as a team to hep Arlo and his friends outrun the flooding river! Travel through Dangerous Canyons, Mountain Forests, and Sweeping Plains!

A dice rolling, platforming, race to the finish game.  
Use a myriad of items to alter your bros turn, or boost your rolls, use your cheat code to level the playing field or get ahead of the competition.
Be the first to get to the flag pole to claim your victory and ultimate bragging rights.

Mech Defense  is a cooperative, tower defense inspired, strategy game where a group of 4 players pilot 20ft Mechs and rain down destruction on their foes.  Your squad fends off wave after wave of incoming enemies over 12 missions and permanently alter your battlefield with every decision and explosive attack.  The more you destroy, the more you get paid, but the stronger your foes become.

As a Legacy game, with over 20 sticker sheets included in the box, players will permanently upgrade enemies and modify the map with every mission you play.

The game also includes a native application for Windows, iOS, Android, and Windows Phone to help manage progress between missions.  For people who prefer not to have the digital component, players can use a printable sheet to track progress.

Promotional card special Essen 2013 available on the stand of ludimaniac.

description:
How do crocodile babies come into the world? If a hamster awake at night? Where is the hedgehog in winter ? With knowledge, a little luck and a good memory you collect the most chips. By the way you will learn interesting facts about the animal world.

To play the game, 30 image tokens need to be assign to the correct one of the 5 theme tables, with each player revealing two tokens on his turn and chosing one. If you manage to place your tokens on the marked spots, you earn victory points.

boardgame: Wieso? Weshalb? Warum? Unsere Welt: Das pfiffige Wissensspiel

To play the game, 30 image tokens need to be assign to the correct one of the 5 theme tables, with each player revealing two tokens on his turn and chosing one. If you manage to place your tokens on the marked spots, you earn victory points.

description:
This Wyldfolk Of Annyr Starter Box contains 8 highly detailed metal miniatures for gamers and collectors.

The enclosed miniatures represent a fully playable warband of 160 points, and in addition you get a printed version of the Quick-Start Rules.

With this Wyldfolk Of Annyr warband and the Quick-Start Rules you are able to immediately start playing Godslayer – the fantasy miniatures skirmish game set in a bronze age world of sword and sorcery.

Get your starter box and start discovering the world of Godslayer!

Battles for the Galactic Empire is a two-player game. Players attempt to outwit and outplay their opponent to achieve victory by scoring Chaos points. Chaos points are scored through control of the six regions in the sector. Control of the regions is determined by skillful deployment of space fleets, play of action chits, and tactical choices during combat. Players build their fleets choosing among six types with varying capabilities including military, morphogenetic (ability to convert opposing forces to the friendly side), and deployment/movement options. They pick chits randomly from 5 categories to enhance movement, tactical and build abilities, recruit special units, and disrupt opponent's plans.

—description from the rulebook
