If you have first edition of Dice Brewing and you don't want to buy second edition of this game, but still you are interested in changes and new components, we have a special set of elements for you.
We have decided to prepare a Dice Brewing: Upgrade kit, where you can find 4 new player boards (bigger ones and with new rule about getting the blue die) and 3 sets of tokens. All new materials are high quality, well made (with exacly the same materials as basic, second edition of Dice Brewing) and of course they are an effect of players comments for the first edition of Dice Brewing.

Drink-a-palooza is a party game that takes many common popular drinking games and puts them in a structure of a roll-and-move board game. The game is played on a Monopoly-style board, with player pieces as small six-packs. Players roll a die to move around the edge, and the space that the player lands on directs the player what to do. Depending on where you land, you will either play a group game such as high/low (card game that involves everyone playing) OR there are twelve bottle spaces on the map. The bottle spaces cause the players to play a mini-games of beer pong (throw a ping pong ball into a cup), quarters (bounce a quarter into a cup), or flip cup (flip a cup from right side up to upside down). If you win the mini-game, you receive a small plastic bottle to go in your six-pack. First player to six bottles wins the game.

From the publisher:

Drink-a-palooza is the ultimate drinking board game that mixes everyone’s favorite college drinking games & packs them into one dynamic competition. Whether you prefer beer, spirits, soda or water; you will love playing these games! This game eliminates the need for a large Ping-Pong table & makes for the perfect housewarming gift. "Pre-game" with drinkapalooza or take it to your buddy’s keg party. Get the bachelor & bachelorette party started & throw a killer “after party”. Drink-a-palooza board game takes your favorite games and makes them a thousand times tastier.

At the zoo are lots of animals to see, but after we see them once, will we find them again? Do we know where all the animals are hiding?

In Vier im Zoo, first the cards are laid out face up, then after all players have had a look at the cards, they are turned face down. Starting clockwise, each player takes a turn shaking the wooden disc inside the cover, then trying to find the corresponding animal card. Whoever is first to find four animals wins!

Space is a hard place and making a bit of scratch out here is even harder. Racing against fellow scavengers you must reach derelict ships and earn enough cash to buy your way out of this long, harsh and tedious life!

In Space Junkers, 2-4 players compete to grab fuel cells and spend them to reach derelict ships. Players roll dice and place them on fuel cell cards in order to outbid or block their opponents. Valuable fuel cells are used to buy derelict ships, earning victory points. The first to 12 points has collected enough from the derelict ships to move on to a better life. May the best salvage company win!

The game of impossible choices and hilarious results.

3 famous names; 3 hard choices.

Who to Snog. Who to Marry. Who to Shove.

Drew Barrymore, Lucy Liu, Cameron Diaz, Enrique Iglesias, Antonio Banderas, Ricky Martin.

Decisions, decisions!
It's hard enough trying to decide what you'd do, let alone guess what everyone else is thinking!

Let's get a little personal, shall we?
There are hot seat question cards for you to make up your own choices - family, friends, work mates, ex-boyfriends, ex-girlfriends, or, if you're really daring, your fellow players!

Watch out for those Same Sex spaces. There's only ONE agonising decision to make... who to snog?

The winner is the first to get to the finish (and is still talking to their partner!)

—description from the back of the box

Sheep Happens is a look into a ridiculous world where incredibly dumb sheep will have to figure out how to save themselves from the angriest, craziest, and most bizarre goats you've ever seen. A card game where you need to steal and imprison enemy players sheep, using powerful and dumb goats to attack them, while you defend yours. Defend from your opponents attacks placing fences in front of your sheep, using traps or scrolls from your hand and If you have any goat cards in your hand, attack your opponents in order to capture as many sheep as you can. Each captured sheep is worth one point, and the first player to reach seven, five or three points, wins the game.

The game consists of 2 items: poles and balls.The balls’ holes are the connection point of the items. Each of the 2 players picks a colour with its 44 items: 12 balls and 32 poles.

RULES FOR 2 PLAYERS

The game starts with the player who selects the white items. The player can choose to start with a pole or a ball. Each player plays their turn using only one item at a time: a pole or a ball.
Every piece played must be linked to the pieces played beforehand. As the poles are inserted in the ball holes a three dimensional figure will begin to form. Once an item is placed, no changes or modification can be made.

AIM OF THE GAME
A player wins the game if during their turn they manage to:
• Insert 3 poles more than their opponent in any played ball
or
• place in a straight line 4 balls of the same colour.

Given that each ball has 6 holes and each player wins by placing 3 poles of the same colour more than their opponent the winning possibilities are 3-0 or 4-1. The players build up a three-dimensional network where there are different diagonals (2D or 3D) that allow placing 4 balls of the same colour in line. The game is considered a draw when both players use up their pieces and no player can place 3 poles more than their opponent into a ball or 4 balls of the same colour in line. Positioning and placing the pieces of the game in a clever way is part of the winning strategy.

In order to create a solid structure and to minimise the risk of the structure breaking during the game, we suggest placing the poles firmly in the ball holes.

RULES FOR 4 PLAYERS
The same rules for 2 players apply. Pairs of 2 players start the game with 44 elements each, playing on alternate turns and interposing one player of each couple.

3 Monkeys is a party game in which player must make static mime of various emotions - fear, hunger, ambition, greed, despair... - with their face. Every round, a player must mime three emotions - one with hand over ears, one with hand over eyes, and the last one with hands over mouth. It's not always that easy...

A new, official, Ancient One card, given to attendees of the Arkham Nights event held on 9-10 October 2015.

Consists of Ubbo-Sathla, a new Ancient One.
family: Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Eclipse: Black Hole is a mini-expansion for Eclipse that consists of two black hole hexes and a flux wormhole discovery tile. During set-up, shuffle one of the black hole hexes with the other Outer Sector hexes. When a ship lands on the black hole, roll a die to determine whether the ship returns to play immediately (and takes one damage) or returns to play after 1-2 rounds. When the ship returns to play, it comes back on any Inner Sector hex or any hex with at least one wormhole pointing to empty space, with the effect dependent on which black hole hex is in the game.

With the flux wormhole discovery tile, you can place it on the edge between any two hexes to connect them with a full wormhole, thereby making each of them worth one extra VP at game's end.

Rocket Shogi is a stand-alone small board (5x7 grid, with 8 pieces per player) Shogi variant. Unusually, it uses all standard Shogi piece types except for pawns.

It can be played using standard Shogi pieces.

It is also included as a second independent game (using its own board and pieces) with Simple Shogi in a combined package sold under the title "Simple Shogi".
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Tricky Pirates is a trick-taking, area control game.  Players play cards in tricks that allow them to move their own pirate loaded ships, stab opposing pirates, or sink opposing ships. Pirates in ships fight for control over treasure kept on five islands. But watch out, pirate ships work on wind power and the wind will not always blow your way.

At the beginning of the game, the treasure on the islands is bonuses and play effects. But very quickly it becomes doubloons. The player that controls three islands worth of doubloons at the end of a trick wins.

A group of men and women with special talents have been summoned in Mafia Casino: Henchmen Extension, and they are ready to do whatever it takes to help the Godfather in his race for wealth.

This expansion includes a new Mission, a new game of chance, additional cards and poker chips, and many optional rule variants that will challenge you and your entire family to discover who will best navigate the world of Mafia Casino!

Space-themed, dexterity luck, skill, strategy game using dice, marbles and discs. Its bocci ball, bowling, shuffle board, craps, pool, and a little bit of monopoly.

Players send out their ships by flicking and rolling from cornered docks. The objective is to land on the positive spaces, ring bearers or the black hole with your disks. Watch out for negatives and the black hole with your marble and dice.

Defense is also allowed if you can hit your opponents ships with yours. Setting a round maximum, the player with highest points at the end of the set round wins. You can actually win by losing in this game. Simple to learn and competitive to play.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: The Elite Missions
description:
September 22nd, 1935: “We’ve been successfully completing expeditions to the Tomb for generations, however in all of my years of experience, I’ve never witnessed challenges like these!. I’ve been to the Tomb more than anyone else, and I’ve survived more than you could imagine; from supernatural otherworldly beings of awe-striking power, to power hungry fascists, to beasts of myth and stories of old. I’ve held in my hands priceless artifacts, as well as the heads of my dying brothers and sisters. We were given a mission, one that we felt confident we could achieve. But when we got to the Tomb ... Nothing could have prepared us for what happened next...”

Mission Briefing: The previous quote was taken from a
veteran adventurer that attempted an elite mission...
Proceed with caution!

Secrets of the Lost Tomb: The Elite Missions, adds a whole new level of depth and challenge to the already Epic experience. Fun to play and simple to add, players are able to use one or more Elite Mission cards to overlay any existing Secrets of the Lost Tomb scenario. Contained within this special expansion are 12 Elite Missions to choose from, each with a diverse storyline and deadly consequence. Many will attempt these missions, few will survive... will you?
family: Game: Secrets of the Lost Tomb

description:
Coloretto: +3-Karte is a promotional expansion for Coloretto consisting of 2 cards, given away for free at Stadt-Land-Spielt 2015.

The card adds 3 points to the final standings.

boardgame: With All Due Respect: The Party Game
description:
With All Due Respect is a new light-hearted game about what you and your friends really think of each other!

With All Due Respect - The Party Game is an adult party game for 2+ people. Gameplay is incredibly simple and unique.

Before the game starts decide who is lucky enough to be on your VIP LIST. When a player is answering a "With All Due Respect" playing card they choose their answer from this list.

You can add other people who are not playing the game to the VIP LIST. Adding these other people will give you more names to choose from when answering questions.

On your turn: 
1.) Draw a playing card, choose any question from the card and read it out loud starting "With All Due Respect".
2.) You then secretly write down on your answer pad a name from the VIP LIST that you think is the best answer. 
3.) Now everyone else will use their 2 Poker Chips to guess which answer you selected.

As fun and ridiculous as it may seem, it gets even more hilarious. The questions alone will sprout hours of laughter and debate. But unlike any other party game their are Drawing Cards and Wild Cards to play.

You will have the opportunity to draw a person from the VIP LIST and everyone else will use their 2 Poker Chips to guess your drawing.

Playing With All Due Respect - The Party Game is like taking your basic adult party game and injecting some steroids into it!

The player with the most points after 12 rounds wins the game.

“Welcome to Rome” the eunich said as you enter the official's ante chamber, “We have been expecting you and have prepared the Senate for your arrival.” Lucius Quintellius will be here within the hour with your requested documents...

Patron is a card game where each player takes the role of a freedman, a class without oblique power, but very energetic and ambitious. You will seek to control the correct mix of Senators from various classes, affluent locations with vast prestige and resources and the laws of Rome itself.

Setup

Shuffle all the cards of Patron deck together and choose a starting player. Draw cards appropriate to the number of players, and choose one to play and discard the rest. In a 2 player game, draw three cards. In a 3 player game draw two cards. In a 4 or 5 player game draw two, but players also have the option of playing the top card of the discard pile instead of drawing.

Playing the Game

When playing a card, you must do that action stated on the card, but only if can: i.e. if a card requires you to sacrifice a Patrician and you don't have one, you may ignore the effect. In a 4 or 5 player game, do not mix sacrificed cards with the discard pile. A sacrificed personality is removed from the game.

Each player draws and plays in a clockwise fashion until there are no cards left to draw and the game is over. If there is only one card left the last player must play this card as their final card. Then each player tabulates their total.

Determining a Winner

The player with the most points at the very end of the game wins. Many cards are worth variable points based on the number of symbols, locations, agendas or personalities that player has.

boardgame: Jenga: Transformers – Rise of the Fallen
description:
From Publisher:

Are you ready for a new twist on Jenga? Bring home the classic game of falling bricks coupled with elements from the blockbuster Transformers: Revenge of the Fallen movie. For ages 6 and up, this family game for two or more players allows you to take control of Cybertron-- or family game night-- with your robot-like brick-removal skill. The tiles feature Autobot or Decepticon faction logos and are made from a tasteful, black material. Build and battle with the Transformers Revenge of the Fallen Jenga Battle Game!

How to Play:

Divide up into two teams Autobots and Decepticons. The Decepticons go first. Teams alternate turns, rotating so that each player gets chance to play.

on you turn, remove one block. Then see if the block shows a symbol:

    if it does not show your TEAM´s symbol, stack the block on top of the tower
    if it shows your TEAM´s symbol, pass it to a player of your choice on the other team. this player must stack the block before the team takes its turn.


Winning:

If the tower falls over on your team´s turn, your TEAM LOSES. The other TEAM WIN!.

APEXWAR: The Armageddon
(The Height of Warfare)

A Game for Big Minds!
In the Era of a highly Militarized New World, Play Chess like a M.A.D. man and start Armageddon !!!

It's 2030 A.D. :
Advances in the developments of the FORD class and SHTORM class Aircraft Carriers and with the advent of the 6th Generation Multi-role Fighters, leads to a new balance of power between the adversarial spheres of influence.
Some are not happy with this new balance, whilst some are over-zealous with it.
Tension, Twilight, Point of no Return, Mutually-Assured-Destruction, and World War 3

You are Commander-in-Chief of a Super-Power Military Force about to clash with another.
- 1 Globe
- 2 Hemispheres
- 2 Commanders-in-chief
- 2 Super-Powers (Each spread across both hemispheres)
- 4 Navies (2 each)
- 4 Armies (2 each)
- 4 Air-Forces (2 each)
- Special-Forces Marines 
- The Secret-Service (including Double Agents) 
- Military Top-Brass; Generals, Admirals and Air-Marshals
- Strategic ICBM’s and Nuclear Deterrents
- Missile defense systems, Satellites & Radar
- Bunkers, Cities, Logistical & Economic Infrastructure including the 'Global Monetary Fund', bank rolling it all.
This is epic war on all fronts.
It's Armageddon!

ApexWar is a truly epic game in scope and in depth. A true union between strategic and tactical gaming.
An expansion on the ApexWar army and navy themes including other forces and a commander-in-chief, you control a super-power spanning two hemispheres.

In this epic struggle for supremacy, your primary mission is to protect the commander-in-chief piece from being captured at all costs to avoid losing the war and to win the war, you must capture the opposing commander-in-chief.
Your secondary mission is to protect your flag bearers and strategic facilities namely your admirals or capital ships, generals, air-marshals, satellites and radar from capture or destruction as they control your unit forces, whilst trying to capture and destroy the opposing commanders and facilities to significantly weaken their forces.
Everything else is canon-fodder, dedicated to upholding and achieving your primary and secondary missions.

The army and navy pieces all move correspondent to their representative standard chess character. Most of the other pieces also all move similar to standard chess characters but some of them incorporate modifications to their movements, capturing and abilities. Then their are the static pieces that do not move as the represent infrastructure, so they have to be defended.
Finally, the whole epic plays out within an economics frame, where you have to acquire control and protect resources like oil, steel and gold to boost your war efforts and also to protect your cities to keep up morale.

It's not easy being the President, Good Luck.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Cat and Medieval and Contemporary Household Themed. The cats are divided into royalty designations/strength ranking and are attempting to exert control over locations in a Kingdom i.e. a bedroom for the purposes of napping.

The gameplay consists of a drawing card then taking 1 or 2 actions of the following: placing cats in territories, placing Battle Cards of various types from the player's 4 card hand to give cats battle strength and scoring potential, battling opponent cats for control, and restoring cats after a battle. Drawing territories out of the deck and certain event cards add variety to gameplay/strategy.

The goal of the game is to have the most victory points at the end of the 3rd draw deck. the victory points are gained by dominating territory based on the cat's size, faction, and royal stack relative to opponents. A second way to earn victory points is to have a valid quirk or bonus VP stack by the game's end. There are penalties to VP for holding certain cards and for cats being defeated in battle.

In Re-Extinction, dinosaurs have been resurrected for battle. Armor your dinosaurs to defend them from environmental hazards like stale grandma smell and boiling rivers of taco meat. Make every other player's dino re-extinct and be the last surviving dinosaur!

boardgame: The Three Pillars: Extreme Hauntings Expansion Set
description:
READY FOR MORE?

The stakes are about to be raised - the cases have gotten more dangerous as have the things that await you at each one. All-new card types challenge your team to the extreme! Entities appear when least expected to try and do more than just scare off your Pillars. Access the new Extreme Deck to search for Objects to help in the battle but there is a grave risk in doing so...the potential to be cursed!  Perhaps your only hope lies in the new Clergy Pillar.

THE THREE PILLARS™: EXTREME HAUNTINGS expansion requires the Premiere Set to play. With 68 all new cards, you can add to your existing decks or freshen them up completely!

Postcard mini-expansion (available for download from the designer's website in German or English).

Dortmund is a new merchandise card.

Every time the owner of the card sells in Dortmund an item of merchandise of the types displayed, he received one extra Thaler (in combination with similar cards accumulating).

Rogue Squad is a cooperative science fiction board game that combines fast-paced combat action with exciting strategic challenges.

In this compelling futuristic adventure, 1 to 4 players step into the boots of a misfit band of mercenaries who must battle their way through ever-increasing swarms of monstrous aliens.

Rogue Squad is an exciting turn-based game with easy-to-learn rules. Depending on the mission’s level of difficulty, one game lasts from 1 to 3 hours. A fully developed universe, intriguing characters, and story-based missions allow you to focus as much (or as little) on roleplaying as you like.

Rogue Squad at a Glance

• fast-paced combat action
• fully cooperative
• one-of-a-kind alien spawning mechanism
• unique combat system using custom dice
• no game master necessary – aliens are controlled by the game itself
• rich in variety: versatile elements can be combined in many ways (troopers, sidekicks, co-op robots, aliens, boss, non-player characters)
• add-ons change up gameplay even more
• mission-specific rules and special features make every gaming session fresh and fun
• troopers’ main weapons and skills can be upgraded
• large selection of weapons and combat equipment
• search, event, alien and alien overlord decks
• easy-to-learn rules
• can be played either as stand-alone scenarios or multi-game plot arc
• maverick (= skirmish) and expert modes offer truly grueling combat for especially ferocious fighters
• free mobile app for even more innovative gameplay options

The Luzon Campaign, 1945 is a wargame, purpose-designed for solitaire play, which covers the fighting across the main Philippine island of Luzon early in 1945. You, as the single player, actively commands the invading US forces, while the rules direct you in the deployment of the defending Japanese. You win or lose via the seizure of (or failure to seize) key areas on the map. You are cast in the role of US Sixth Army commander Lt. Gen. Walter Krueger. In turn, you’re under considerable time pressure from theater commander Gen. Douglas MacArthur (portrayed passive-aggressively by the victory conditions and six-turn limit) to be finished with the major portion of the campaign no later than the end of March. That’s necessary to allow the redeployment of major aero-naval assets for the Iwo Jima and Okinawa battles.

World at War #59

boardgame: Eaten by Zombies!: Burn it down!
description:
The long awaited expansion to the hit Zombie deck builder Eaten by Zombies! The Zombies aren't the only thing returning from the grave!

This new expansion builds upon the Fuel mechanic that was introduced in the Weapons of Mass Destruction mini set. With a new Starting card (Fuel Can), now you always have fuel for your Chainsaw. With the Oil Lamp, Fire Starter you can burn your way through the Zombie Horde. Take a quick swig of the Old Jack Rye #1 for a pick-me up or just smash one of the those undead zombies in the face! You can't pass up a good hiding place even if it is under a car, or just push a zombie down there they don't climb very well. Or get the rest of the survivors to help you out and give them the order to "Fire!" everything they've got at the horde!

With the changing of the guard there are 2 new zombie sets. -The first zombies hate the world of man and they not only try to eat your face, but they will destroy any equipment they come across dwindling the already scarce Swag Piles. And the new fearsome zombies will scare the Stuff right out of your backpack! Forcing every player to discard cards from their hands and redraw, bringing even more zombies into your hand.

now on Kickstarter: https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/gingeralegames/eaten-by-zombies-burn-it-down

A rather rare football simulation from Wilson Sports. Play happens through use of cards, dice, a gridiron board, and a wheel for offense and one for defense upon which players select their chosen plays and then compare results.

User note: I contacted Wilson Sports Equipment, and they at first told me that Wilson never made sports games, then, when I insisted that I had one in front of me, said that they had no knowledge of any such products.

Terra Incognita is a scenario based solitaire game about organizing an expedition and explore unknown lands in the heart of Africa at the end of the 19th century. Your goal is the accomplish the chosen sceanrio by organizing and leading your expedition. You have to prepare your expedition with crew members and items to survive in the wild territories of Africa and then face events and decide how to continue your journey to reach your goal and return to the civilization to acquire fame and glory!

The game use a tile reveal mechanism in a point-to-point system. You discover new tiles as you progress on the map. On each tile you will find mandatory or possible actions what you have to resolve with the help of your crew members, items and the use of six sided dices. Time has a fundamental role in the game as you has to track the time spent on expedition and provide supply for your memebers from time to time. Random events also occures what can effect your scenario and you have to react them.

From the publisher:

Pass the Grass is an interactive board game that borrows elements ranging from the innocent world of Milton Bradley to the psychedelic vibe of the Grateful Dead. Rolling both dice and joints, players make their way around the board in a quest for the perfect buzz while completing hilarious challenges and satisfying their lust for “munchies.” Pass the Grass can be played with 2 or more players but they must be 21 or over. Clearly, this is a game for adults (cough).

Take turns drawing cards (and puffs) and complete challenges to win. Includes: 1 game board, 2 dice, 6 game "tokens," 2 decks of cards, and a rule book.

GAME PLAY: Everybody puts $1 in the jackpot. There are two sets of cards, you roll dice and there are places on the board that tell you to do different things. You take a card, and each card will tell you to do something different. There are credit and pass cards. A credit card means you do what the card says, which could be anything from taking a hit off a joint, to eating some munchies, to telling an embarrassing story. If you take a pass card, you pass it to anybody and then they have to do it. If they choose not to, they’re out. If you land on the jackpot, you get all the money and everyone replenishes it with $1 each, but it can be more if players want. Players who choose not to ante up are out. Last player still playing wins the pot. (Everyone else wins a nice buzz.) For a shorter game, play ends when someone lands on the jackpot.

Players may substitute a shot of booze for a puff of pot.

This is a game on political conflict in the last years of Japanese Edo period (1856 to 1867). The basic game engine is based on Twilight Struggle.

Players control either pro-Tokugawa Shogunate or anti Shogunate (imperial) faction. Each turn players play historical event or common political operation cards. This mostly helps them to place their own influence points or remove the opponent's influence points. The difference of Influence points determines the control of hans (domains), and it also affects control of areas.
The winner is determined by victory points, mostly gained by area control.


Issue game of Game Journal #56
family: History: Boshin War & Meiji Era Rebellions

description:
Bone Head: the spooky little card game for 2-9 players.

The Bone Heads are on the hunt for roses!

Players take turns to add their Bone Heads to a forming chain by linking hands and avoiding other Bone Heads with the same colour top hat. If a player is unable to play a Bone Head they must collect another from the graveyard.

The first player to play all their Bone Heads successfully collects a rose from the graveyard or steals one from another Bone Head.

Domino+ mechanic with a spooky twist.

Based  upon a popular youth fiction book.

Be part of the story, work alone or co-operate with your team and build the time machine!
At the start of the game, every player gets a secret role card, which determines the team the player is part of. During the game, the players have to deduce who their partners and who their opponents are. The goal is to find as many parts of the time machine as possible. It's not enough to find the parts though, the teams have to defend their findings as well, because it's possible to steal items from other players.

During the game, players use their action cards to move the characters on the board. These cards have different possible actions based upon the location of the moved character on the board. These characters can find and pick up parts of the time machine, and the player who moved the given character gets the part, which is essential for winning the game.

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Nightmare Expansion Pack
description: The Nightmare Hordinox is the competitive prize for the Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing Monthly Organized Play Tournament, Month 1: Saddle of the Cavalier. The prize pack includes one pre-painted miniature and numerous upgrade cards and tokens.
family: Game: Dungeons & Dragons – Attack Wing Miniatures Game

description:
Micropolis is a family city-building game with a modular board.

On your turn, roll two 10-sided dice, then select actions to perform using the numbers you rolled.

     Acquire Property — Place a roof token on a city block bearing the rolled number. (Once you have two roof tokens on a city block, no one else can build on it any longer.) You can use 0 as a wild to place on any number, and doubles grant you a free bonus action of placing a single roof on any city block where you already have a roof.
     Construct Buildings — Remove roof tokens from the footprint of the building specified on the contract card (you must have sole or shared control of each city block in question), and then place the appropriate building pieces as depicted on the contract card. Bonuses and penalties specified on the card for positioning relative to other features apply.
     Build Monorail Line — Start the monorail line between city blocks, or extend the existing line, if it has already been started.
     Acquire a City Tile from the deck.
     Place a City Tile adjacent to existing tiles.
     Reroll
     Exchange Contract Cards


Once both Contract decks are depleted, the endgame is triggered, giving each player one more turn. The player who has accumulated the most points wins.

How Good Is Your Karma? ...You'll know after playing this!!
  
Unpredictable and designed to be smoker-friendly, it's a great way to spend a stretchable hour or so with like minded friends. Not sneaky, not dishonest but likely to provoke profanity!
  
Chill with friends and visit Morrocco, Marrakesh and Amsterdam to try and score. Then, if you manage that, run the gauntlet of customs, police and your own Karma to get rid of it. The winner leaves loaded and holding, the losers just have some fun.

Originally created by stoner students in a UK university in the 1970's, the legendary Big Dope Deal board game is now available for anyone to play! Travel to London, Amsterdam, Marrakesh, and Kabul and score weed along the way, with all the board game fun of dice, fake paper money and full-color cards. Big Dope Deal is ideal for 2 to 6 adult players and an average game takes about one high-stakes, hilarious hour. The 15 x 12.8 inch board is flexible and rolls up easily, and it's laminated so don't worry if you spill some beer on it.

Frogs are very hungry, and a whole menu of insects is within reach. With Frog Menu, you should eat as many insects as possible, and to do this you must jump from insect to insect, eating non-stop. Be sure not to get isolated while doing so!

To start, shuffle 2-4 starting tiles with a bunch of insect tiles, then lay out the tiles face up in a 6x6, 7x7, or 8x8 square (based on the number of players). Each player starts with two random power tiles (fish, egg, tongue), with each type of tile having a different special action. Each player starts on the board wherever their starting tile turns up.

On a turn, the active player jumps their frog based on the possible move shown on the tile where that frog is currently located; the player can rotate the tile to use the move in one of four directions, claiming this tile and removing it from the board. As tiles are removed, the board gets holes in it, and players can jump their frogs over these holes. Once a frog is isolated, that player takes no further turns. Once every frog is isolated, players claim the tile under their frog, then tally their points.

Players score based on the types and quantities of insects they've collected, with the game including seven types of insects. A player's starting tile counts as an insect of their choice. The scores for the different groupings of insects are:


    5 insects of the same species, 50.
    5 different insects, 40.
    4 insects of the same species, 30.
    3 insects of the same species + 2 insects of the same species, 20.
    3 insects of the same species, 10.
    2 insects of the same species, 5.


Whoever has the most points wins, with ties being broken by whoever has the combination of insects with the most value.

Alexander Cornelius V, the king of Dayrm is dead and has left no heir.  The nobles houses are each sending envoys to Alexandria to vie for the crown and be the new ruler of the land.  Each of the other houses are also hiring brigands to try and capture the noble lineage and stop them from making their journey to the capital city.  Can you and your trusted knights and envoys navigate to the capital so you will have the political clout to become the new ruling house of the realm?

Each player has the same 9 cards consisting of capturing pieces and pieces that give extra points if not captured.  Each turn players will play their cards representing envoys, guards, and kidnappers in 3 different paths to Alexandria.  Play continues until all cards are played and points are calculated.  Whoever has the most points wins the round and gains a token.  First one to 3 tokens is now the new ruling kingdom of Darym.

Micro Rome: Aegyptus adds the Roman province of Aegyptus (Egypt) to the game.

This 7 card expansion allows you to add the Nile River, the Pyramids of Giza, the Sphinx and much more to your Roman holdings. However, all of these additions come with an increased winning score and their own decisions to make concerning your path to victory.

Micro dexterity game featuring Princess Fantastic. And drinking. Lots of drinking.

The basic rules are as follows:

1. Set this card in the middle of the table
2. Flick, bounce, or whatever, bottle caps at my face
3. Land on my face 3pts | Land on the card 1pt
4. First to 11 or whatever wins!

There are also a growing number of variant and advanced rules available.

Freight is a train game combining network building and pick up and delivery. During the game, players build tracks between cities and use these tracks to ship goods.

In a turn, a player builds track (but can also buy more freight wagons and build new towns). After that she picks up goods produced in one city/town and delivers them in another, making use of track built by herself or by other players. After building and moving, she has to pay interest to the bank on all her loans (because players start the game without any money).

To win the game, a player must have a large network, be debt-free (i.e. without loans), and have made several very profitable deliveries.

B-I-N-G-O! An assigning game with animals for 1-6 children, and perfect for ages 2 and up. The game will grow with your child as it includes variation for older players.

Each player receives a picture card which shows four animals. Players now take turns drawing an animal tile. If the tile matches an animal on one of the player's cards, that player takes the tile and places it on the card. The first player to collect all four tiles on his card wins the game.

Blood & Fortune is a tense, competitive card game of negotiation, deduction, and betrayal. You must offer influence in order to gain influence while trying to deduce who is winning and avoid helping them further. Giving too much influence too early, or withholding it too long, might cost you the game. More importantly, only the strongest and most recent alliances will triumph.

Optional role cards and special actions provide variety, depth, and a touch of misfortune. Now, bring your friends close and your enemies closer to see who will prevail through Blood & Fortune!

User Summary:
Cat's Party is a card-drafting game where each player holds their own party and tries to invite cats. If you gather many cats of the same color, your party warms up and you gain high reputation. You may simply play a cat card or two special cards, two cats card or a fish card. Fish card can lure other cats. Basically the goal is simple: gather as many cats of the same color as possible. But to do so, you should consider several factors while drafting, such as what color opponents are gathering, or whether you should play special cards now or not. That makes this party game so strategic that everyone from children and adults can enjoy together.

Publisher's summary:
『Cat’s Party』は、ドラフトタイプのカードゲームです。各プレイヤーは、自分のパーティー会場にねこを集めていきますが、なるべく同じグループ(同じ色)のねこを多く集めたほうが、パーティーが盛り上がります(得点が高くなります)。「さかなカード」(別の会場に行こうとしているねこをおびき寄せるカード)をうまく使えば、より多くのねこを集めることができます。基本は同じ色のねこを集めるだけ、という簡単なルールながら、「2ねこカード」や「さかなカード」などの特殊カードを使うタイミングや、他のプレイヤーの会場のねこの色を確認しながら取るカードと渡すカードを考えるドラフトゲームの戦略性がエッセンスとして含まれ、小さなお子様から大人まで一緒に楽しく遊べるファミリーゲームです。

boardgame: ASL Action Pack #12: ASL Oktoberfest XXX
description:
From the publisher's website:

Action Pack #12: ASL Oktoberfest XXX is a celebration of the 30th running of ASL Oktoberfest in Greater Cleveland, Ohio. The pack spans the globe and nearly 13 years of small unit actions surrounding the World War II era, with scenarios brought to you by four Ohio designers who have been integral cogs in the ASLOK machine over the years.

The pack contains one new board (73, designed by Bill Sisler) and 10 new scenarios (designed by Sisler, Pete Shelling, "Wild Bill" Hayward, and Bret Hildebran). These scenarios are:

AP111 The Katanas Come Out At Night – 6½ turns, 31 July 1938, Changkufeng Hill, near Lake Khasan, Russia
AP112 First Ally – 7 turns, 5 September 1939, Zabrzez, Poland
AP113 Maintaining the Box – 6 turns, 15 February 1944, Sinzweya, Burma
AP114 A Lion In The Field – 6½ turns, 29 June 1944, Gavrus, France
AP115 Bats Outta Hell – 7 turns, 15 September 1944, Peleliu, Palau Islands
AP116 Mook Point – 6½ turns, 25 September 1944, Mook, Holland
AP117 Second City – 7 turns, 11 October 1944, Debrecen, Hungary
AP118 Wise’s War – 6½ turns, 13 October 1944, Aachen, Germany
AP119 Konev Cross – 6½ turns, 27 April 1945, Anhalter, Berlin, Germany
AP120 Kingston of the Hill – 9 turns, 22 April 1951, Hill 902, near Hwachon Reservoir, North Korea
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: The starting set-up is always the same in Trideo, but once you start playing — trying to find the fastest way to get one of your own colors into a winning position — things start getting difficult, especially since your opponent will keep placing their pegs in your way time and time again.

Description from the publisher:

One player has the role of the wishing machine, and as such selects one out of nine wishes. All others play the agents who try to find out what that secret wish is. If the agents are ready, the wishing machine spits out four wishing cards and states whether or not they show the preferred item. Another set of four wishing cards and another statement follows. Who will be the first agent to guess the secret wish of the machine?

With each row of cards and statements made in Wunschmaschine, you gain new information about the secret wish of the machine. Sooner or later it will become clear what the wishing machine really wants. Depending on the distribution of the cards, you can solve the problem after just a few rows, or it may take you longer. Haste and pressure from your opponents ensure that mistakes will happen along the way – much to the other players' delight as they profit from your mishaps. To increase the level of difficulty, look for the experienced gamers variant enclosed at the end of the basic rules.

Bite Nite is a new tabletop game about competition and supremacy among vampires. Each player is a vampire and during his turn he must bite a victim in the city. This is not always an easy job. Fortunately there are some powerful magic items to help during their midnight snack. In his turn the player draws a power card that he can use to bite his victims. Each victim has a different value due to difficulty to be bitten.

The blood-lust may force the players to deal with Dracula, they could try to steal his victim during the Dracula's Masquerade Ball. To complicate their life another dangers hides in the city: a wandering werewolf is killing the poor victims during the night.

The night ends when there are no more victims in the city to bite while the game ends after three nights. The winner is the vampire who collected more victim points.

Bluff is a traditional card game which could be called a card version of Liar's Dice. Players are dealt hands of only one card each and bid for poker hands that are present in all players hands' combined.

During a turn the player can raise the bid or challenge. If challenge succeded, then the bidder gets plus one card at the start of each consecutive turn. If challenge failed, then the challenger gets plus one card at the start of each consecutive turn.

The game ends when one player gets her 5th-10th card (depending on the number of players). Whoever has the fewest cards is the winner.

Zobrist cubes are similar to the classic "Soma Cube" puzzle, except that there are many more 3d shapes having sizes up to 5 small cubes.  There are enough pieces in the set to simultaneously construct 5 3×3×3 cubes.

The set comes with a code book containing thousands of codes, each of which specifies a set of pieces from which a 3×3×3 cube can be constructed.

The competitive version is to race another player to construct a cube with similar difficulty, but different pieces.

boardgame: Invasion 1944: The Americans in Normandy – Introductory Game to Panzer Grenadier
description:
From the publisher's website:

Panzer Grenadier covers dozens of topics with thousands of scenarios. Invasion 1944 is your gateway to that world.

Based on the battles waged by American troops in the days following the landings in Normandy, Invasion 1944 is specifically designed to introduce new players to the Panzer Grenadier system. Each player commands the troops, tanks and artillery of the U.S Army and the German Wehrmacht, using leaders to activate and move forces.

There are twenty-one scenarios (separate battle situations) depicting the fighting, presented in graduated fashion – you only need to learn some of the rules to play the first few scenarios, then some more rules for some more involved scenarios, and so on. Panzer Grenadier is already a pretty easy game system to learn how to play, and with Invasion 1944 it’s even easier.

There’s plenty here for veteran Panzer Grenadier players, too: Two new maps by Guy Riessen, depicting the bocage and farmlands of Normandy. 176 new playing pieces (including units, leaders and markers). Twenty-one new scenarios by ace designer Mike Perryman, most of them based on actions of the U.S. Army’s 29th “Blue and Gray” Infantry Division.

boardgame: The Kokoda Campaign: Jungle Battles in New Guinea – A Panzer Grenadier Game
description:
From the publisher's website:

Japanese troops landed in northeastern New Guinea in July 1942, and quickly began a march over the forbidding Owen Stanley Mountains toward the vital anchorage and airfield at Port Moresby on the southern coast. They ran into Australian infantrymen of the 39th Militia Battalion, starting a three-month campaign waged in some of the world's most forbidding terrain.

An Australian militia brigade went up the trail to face the Japanese, who poured in reinforcements of their own. Next the veteran Australian 7th Infantry Division, the heroes of Tobruk, plunged into the jungles and finally stopped the Japanese attacks. While Australian soldiers fought on despite rampant malaria, Japanese troops hauled artillery shells over the mountains by hand and resorted to eating Australian prisoners of war. Finally in September 1942 the Japanese withdrew from one of the war's most brutal campaigns.

The Kokoda Campaign is a complete boxed game in the Panzer Grenadier series. Based on these battles, it includes the two maps that appeared in our old Kokoda Trail book and 242 playing pieces that originally appeared in our out-of-print Guadalcanal and Afrika Korps games. It has 30 scenarios originally designed by Dave Cheever and re-worked by Matt Ward and Daniel Rouleau, plus a campaign game.

Skirmish Wars: Colonize is a standalone adaptation of Skirmish Wars: Advance Tactics. Skirmish Wars is a tactical print-and-play wargame that uses printable, customizable maps for endless variety and simple mechanics to streamline gameplay. The game is based on Advance Wars, a videogame by Intelligent Systems.

The Award Winning Game plunges you into the hellish underbelly of the advertising industry… where clients have horns, deadlines are always an hour away and showing a little cleavage wins you accounts.

From humble beginnings, you and your friends will run your own ad agencies, working (and sabotaging) your way up to hiring the world’s most pretentious creatives, and winning accounts from famous brands… only to throw them away for the shot at winning awards.

Because, y’know, who needs clients when you’ve got prestige?

Word Topper is a great way to build your vocabulary while having fun with your friends and family! Use as much word building strategy as possible to try and score the most points on every turn.

GAME PLAY

Word Topper is quick to set up and easy to play. The more times you play, the more words and combinations you will learn to make.

Start by choosing a dealer. The dealer deals out six cards to every player. After the deal is over, the dealer puts the rest of the deck face down to create a draw pile. He then draws 7 cards and places them face up in the middle of the table creating the community cards for everyone to play off of.

On their turn, each player uses the cards in their hands to make the best scoring word possible by laying them on top of the cards placed in the middle. Scores their turn, then draws from the draw pile to make up for the cards they just played. The next player then plays their cards on top of the cards the previous player just played creating a new word and also new community cards. Each time a player plays the community cards and the players cards change, creating a constant change to the game every turn. Game play continues until all the cards are gone from the draw pile. The highest scorer wins.

Bonus cards include double word points, skips, wilds, and switches.

boardgame: Screw: A Sexual Game of Who Done It?
description:
A Sexual Game of Who done it?

As an adult, how often do you actually have to piece together the details of a murder? In this revamped version of the classic game Clue, instead of solving a mysterious murder, players must piece together the details of the previous night's drunken activities. Did you screw Miss Scarlett with a condom on in the utility closet? Perhaps, after he called you a failure, you hit Professor Plum with a desk lamp in the library. Maybe you pleasured Miss Peacock with your finger in her ass. Have fun with your friends trying to sort out the twisted details because, chances are, you'll really have to solve these kinds of mysteries soon, you big party animal, you.

Munchkin Edycja Rozszerzona which means Munchkin Extended Edition is a Polish version that contains Munchkin and Fairy Dust expansion. Other than that nothing was changed.

Go down in the dungeon. Kill everything you meet. Backstab your friends and steal their stuff. Grab the treasure and run.

Admit it. You love it.

This award-winning card game, designed by Steve Jackson, captures the essence of the dungeon experience... with none of that stupid roleplaying stuff. You and your friends compete to kill monsters and grab magic items. And what magic items! Don the Horny Helmet and the Boots of Butt-Kicking. Wield the Staff of Napalm... or maybe the Chainsaw of Bloody Dismemberment. Start by slaughtering the Potted Plant and the Drooling Slime, and work your way up to the Plutonium Dragon...

And it's illustrated by John Kovalic! Fast-playing and silly, Munchkin can reduce any roleplaying group to hysteria. And, while they're laughing, you can steal their stuff.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: In Suisse Mania you are ballooning over Switzerland and its 26 cantons. During your travel you learn a lot of interesting but also quaint things.

boardgame: Small City: Character Meeple Expansion 1 – The Political Activist
description:
This is the part#1 of the stretch goals unlocked thanks to the KS Campaign. It contains :

     One new character meeple called The Political Activist 22mm with one sheet of rules
     2 Official Variants :
         The peaceful mayor : He can collect votes instead of blocking the building.
         The parks : The parks are now eligible to be upgraded. Parks level 2 and 3 can not be built anymore though.
    
     One Standardization chart numerated and signed by the designer Alban Viard that explains the relation between the tiles, the money, the Vote points, the pollution and the mini-meeples.

boardgame: Small City: Player boards Expansion #1 – The Beach
description:
This is the part #2 of the Stretch goals unlocked thanks to the KS campaign. 
It contains :

     4 New player boards called the Beach  printed both side (Easy and Expert). The Beach now counts as a giant Cultural Building and helps the development of the Residences.
     One sheet of rules

A Decktet game in which you are a trader in the land of Aucteraden taking goods from market to the different clans within the province. Your object is to acquire goods at a fair price to create trade routes. You must be frugal and plan your routes well to amass your wealth and expand your influence.

In this solitaire game you take cards from a draw, or market area at the top of the playing board.  Chips can be spent to draw certain cards to place in a tableau.  Scoring consists of ascending/descending sequences of cards (1, 2, 4, 5).  At the end of the game each suit's longest sequence is tallied for a final score.  If this score is greater than or equal to 40 then you have won the game.

Playing as researchers on a quest for exotic specimens, Dart through the Amazon leads you through the world's largest rain forest to collect colorful dart frogs for scientific study. Racing against your fellow researchers, this expedition is no simple task, for amidst the Amazon, danger lurks around every bend.

As a 2-4 player dart frog collecting game, Dart through the Amazon  features two modes of play designed for both children and adults to enjoy!

The base mode which is played without special rules and abilities offers younger children the opportunity to play the game. We also recommend this mode for beginners who are first learning the game.

The advanced mode of play features Researcher Cards that have their own special abilities adding new twists throughout the game. This mode is enabled by selecting one of our five Researchers before the game begins: Duke, Koral, Samuel, Tobias & Domingo.

In Fianco, which is Italian for "side" or "flank", you want to be the first player to move one of your pieces to the opponent's back line.

On a turn, you move or jump with one of your pieces either forward (whether orthogonally or diagonally) or sideways; backwards movement is prohibited. You must take an opponent's piece, if possible, but only one at a turn. Control over the sidelines is important since pieces there cannot be jumped as easily.
Roll the die, search for the correct key. Use your memory to remember where it was. Unlock the chest and don't disturb the pirate. First to six coins wins.

Sealed Despatches, an extension to the Admiral's Order versions Edition 74 or All Hands!, pays even more attention to enabling a historically correct simulation. In particular the historically accurate differences in the armament of the ships are covered very precisely. While still keeping the rules as simple as possible, it simulates the effect of these differences in a way that requires the player consider them for each individual ship. This should result in a more authentic set of tactics, which are chosen when commanding a fleet.

While Admiral’s Order with its original rules is an interesting tactical and relatively realistic game, Sealed Despatches now provides an even more accurate simulation of the weapon technology of the time.

1867 is an 18xx game that is an expansion to 1861: Railways of the Russian Empire, set in Canada. It is essentially "1861 on a new map." The map represents the central part of Canada, consisting of the southern part of the provinces of Ontario and Quebec.

The key new features are:
a) There are no fixed positions for the minor companies, you  choose a start position when you put up a minor for auction.
b) The State Railway doesn't run its trains and save up for another, but rather just takes the next new train each round. This speeds up the game a little in comparison to 1861, and also accelerates the trains somewhat.

In 2017, this expansion was released as a stand-alone game: 1867: The Railways of Canada.

In The Art of Bidding, you are a gallery owner trying to increase the Prestige of your business. Buy and sell paintings by Jackson Pollock, Vincent Van Gogh, Johannes Vermeer, Eugene Delacroix, and Andrei Rublev and become the most Prestigious gallery owner.

In game play, players own three paintings by one of the greatest painters of all time. Each player will auction their own paintings (one of which is a fake) and try to acquire paintings of other masters throughout gameplay. During bidding, players may bid with all assets at their disposal and the player auctioning a painting may accept the best offer and does not have to simply accept the highest monetary offer.  Players also can draw Gallery cards that may give investment dividends or may be used as end game multipliers of Prestige points.

There are 37 holes and ecah player has 18 pieces. Everybody places their pieces on the board so that only one hole is free.

Each player tries to take out more pieces than his opponent.

To take out one of his pieces he has to jump over one of his pieces provided there is an open hole on the other side. He can jump over an opponents piece but without taking it out.

At the end of the game the player with less pieces wins!

Qwixx: Big Points is an expansion for Qwixx that consists of two 80-page scorepads that provide a new way to score big points.

Each scoresheet adds two new rows to the four rows on the original scoresheet: a red/yellow row between the red and yellow rows and a green/blue row between the green and blue rows. While playing the game, if you've crossed out, say, the red 5 and you're presented with a red 5 later in the game (whether through your roll or an opponent's roll), you can cross out the red/yellow 5.

As in the original game, you can't cross out a number to the left of a crossed-out number, so once you've crossed out the red/yellow 5, you can't cross out the red/yellow 2, 3 or 4.

The game still ends when a second row has been completed or when one player has failed to cross off anything for the fourth time. Players then score their four single-colored rows, with the Xs in the dual-colored rows adding to the total number of Xs in both of those colored rows. Whoever has the highest score wins!

Build your own town! Complete your very own town with houses, trees, bicycle, recycle bin and rain water tank. The town on each playing board can be joined together in many different ways for more creative play! The perfect dice game for the little ones to learn to save our environment.

Setting up:
1. Each Player takes a board
2. Put all the playing pieces in the center

To Play:
1. Roll the dice
2. Pick up the item that is the same color as the dice and place it on the spot on the gameboard with the corresponding color.
3. Next Player roll the dice
4. If the player rolls a same color as he previously got, he has to place the item at the next player's gameboard.

Winning:
The winner is the first player with all pieces in correct colors and positions, thus completed building an EcoTown!

The Alex Morgan Board Game! appears inside the back cover of the book Alex Morgan by Illugi Jökulsson. It follows the life and career of American women's soccer star Alex Morgan. It is a simple roll-and-move game where players roll a die to progress along a track. Spaces along the way refer to setbacks and successes in Morgan's life and career, corresponding to moving forward or back on the track or gaining or losing turns. The first player to reach the end of the track by an exact roll wins.

When shape shifters do battle they do with displays of their skills and strength. The shifter who best adapts to the tactics of their opponents is the victor.

Set Up
Each play gets one set of the four Form Cards. Unused Form Cards are set aside.

The Combat Style Cards are shuffled and dealt out until each player has an equal number. Un-dealt cards are set aside face down.

Objective
Be the first player to empty your hand of Combat Style Cards.

Play
Select a Form - Each player selects one Form Card from their stack of face down Form Cards and places in the center of the table but in front of them.

Select a Combat Style - Each player then selects one of their combat style cards from their hand and places it face down besides their form card.

Reveal - All cards are flipped face up.

Resolution: The success of a combat style depends on the form.
If successful, players put the Combat Style Card face down in the discard pile.
If unsuccessful, players put the combat style card back in their hand.

End of turn - Players put the Form Card they just used aside, face-up and return any previously used Form Cards back to their stack.

Winning
The game ends when a player ends a turn with no Combat Style Cards in their hand.

boardgame: ATS Spanish Blue Division: Eastern Front 1941
description:
Using the Advanced Tobruk System, this installment depicts the efforts of the 250th Infantry "Blue" Division, made up of volunteer Spaniards supporting the German invasion of the Soviet Union between 1941 and 1944. Specifically, the scenarios here deal with small-unit actions around the area of the Volkhov River, near Leningrad, in northwest Russia.

From the website... Spanish Blue Division is an Eastern Front-themed historical module for ATS that completly re-vamps that which came before. Maps, new, counters, new, changes, yes, color 3-hole rules, yes Nothing held over from before, and uses WINTERIZED MAPS!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
From the publisher's website:

As the morning of 27th May dawned the 8th Panzer Regiment under Lieutenant Teege led Rommel’s wide sweep around the British southern flank at Bir Hacheim. With one hundred and eighty tanks, the regiment was arrayed in spearhead formation with the first section moving forward on a wide front of one and one half miles by about a mile deep. At about 0715 hours, the call rang out in the headsets, “Enemy armor, twelve o’clock!” A British lieutenant of the 8th Hussars also dryly noted enemy armor to his regimental HQ by radio. “It looks as if Jerry’s come with a Panzer brigade,” only to correct himself moments later. “There’s more than a brigade, its the whole bloody Afrika Korps. Alert! Alert!”

And thus began the historic Battle of Gazala. It was May of 1942, Hitler’s armies were still the masters of Europe, with more conquests to come. And yet YOU, tactical-level gamer and ATS devotee ... has only experienced this battle in ‘penny packets’, a tank here, two tanks there. Until NOW. Because the ATS GAZALA MONSTER is here to rescue you from never being able to cross off, ‘big, real Western Desert warfare experience’ ... from your bucket list. Imagine the old Tobruk orders-of-battle ... with all of their one-to-one representations ... then knife forward with your formations of 10, no 15, wait add in Troop Leaders and panzer leaders ... and make that 17 AFVs ... in a full-blown presentation that gives you ALL of the tanks, guns and men (i.e., all combat counters; just bring your ATS rulebook and markers) ... and the playing area to duke it out in.

The set also debuts the first collection of 11” × 16” folding DESERT GEO boards and a selection of overlays to go with them.

It’s Nothing Personal is your basic race-to-the finish game – assuming your definition of “basic” involves harpooning the other racers in the legs so you can gain the lead. Friends become enemies and enemies become bigger enemies as you race along your own individual tracks from the starting space to the finish line.

Here’s how it breaks down:

1. Roll the die and move forward that number of spaces
2. Decision time!  You could:
     a. Move yourself forward a few more spaces.
     b. Move another player back a few spaces.
     c. Place an obstacle on an opponent’s track
     d. Place a benefit on your own track
     e. Draw one of 60 mildly zany chance cards and let fate be the boss of you.
3. After each round of turns, there’s a non-secret vote to determine who should move back several spaces.  Now’s your chance to convince the others to gang up on the player who’s winning, the player who wronged you last turn, or the player who just doesn't share your opinion about the 1973 NBA All-Star Game. 
4. Repeat.  Exact revenge as needed.

Empires of Tal is a set of miniatures rules for 10mm miniatures set in a fantasy setting but can easily support historic battles in the same scale. Empires of Tal has an extensive library of in game spells, monsters, equipment and special units for use.

Description from the publisher:

State the Picture encourages children (and adults) to learn about the fifty U.S. states through a family-friendly drawing charades game. Players move across the board by correctly guessing the names of states based on their fellow players' corresponding, hand-drawn clues.

boardgame: ATS Maxim Gorki II: The Siege of Sevastopol 1942
description:
ATS MAXIM GORKI is from prolific designer Mark 'Tank' Porterfield. Mark brought you the fruits of his massive military library and penchant for research with Panther Line + Berlin 1 and Berlin 2 (not to mention the Monster Berlin versions), all set on World War II's Eastern Front. Before that, almost 20 years ago Tank brought you Pointe du Hoc, with a healthy on-site assist from Steve and Rob Pleva, plus map work (and on site research too) by Phil Nobo. The TANK is back and this time he is modeling another uniiqe Festung, the Sevastopol bastion of Stalin's Soviet Union and this is a MONSTER MODULE to do his work justice.

Some of the interesting details you'll receive: Russian Naval Infantry, German demolition AFVs including Kettenkrad and Bren Carrier Demo vehicles; massive Naval Gun Turrets; Concrete Trenches; German Engineers, Fortress rules; and so much more. Plus a large map with healthy-sized hexes, more terrain variants, all using official ATS nomenclature for the SSRs while avoiding 5 point type and eye-watering verbiage bloat in what will be the largest Engineer-operation ever gamed at this scale.

From the publisher's website:

Legendary designer Ian Daglish (dec.) left behind his take on the Great War for tactical-level gamers and the baton has crossed the finish line in the form of ATS GREAT WAR I: Tankschreken!
The module includes all the combat counters needed to play plus 10 scenarios, all featuring British vs. German actions ... and of course some of them debut tanks ... the name deriving from an attempt to keep this new technology secret by making the enemy think ‘water tanks’ were being constructed. You also receive—as a SPECIAL BONUS—ALL OF THE BOARDS NEEDED TO PLAY in 11” × 16” folding format. You also receive a Play Aid to augment standard system play aid cards.

You receive all the combat counters you need to play. Special rules in color, 3-hole format. And of course, a collection of color format scenarios (8 in all) that all feature German/British combat.

boardgame: ATS Great War: Blasted Woods – French vs. German Scenarios!
description:
From the publisher's website:

Prior to his unexpected passing designer and author Ian Daglish (dec.) sent in his GWASL module materials, joining the work of other CH designers in the effort to bring another era of warfare to the collection. Losing top men is sad, and they can never, ever be replaced.

But their contributions, like authors of yore, can speak to us, and we are grateful to have this chance to make the Great War return for an encore ... on your gaming table in the form of ATS GREAT WAR MODULE II. It is all very straightforward, and the Special Rules give you the handbook you need to maneuver the men and machines, some representing combat systems never seen before, but prominent since. The Chauchet LMG, new French soldiers, Attack a' Outrance rules and couinters, French tanks and Guns, and more!

You receive all the combat counters you need to play. Special rules in color, 3-hole format. And of course, a collection of color format scenarios (8 in all) that all feature German/French combat.


     Schneiders at Soissons – Soissons, France, July 18th 1918
     Window of Opportunity – Bois Des Caures, France, February 22nd 1916
     Blasted Woods – Bois Des Caures, Verdun, France, February 21st 1916
     Dead Man Hill - Bois Des Caures, Verdun, France, March 8th 1916
     Flag Signal: Advance! – St Pierre Aigle, France, May 31st 1918
     Pilckem Ridge – Pilckem, Belgium, April 22nd 1915
     Baptism – The hamlet of La Ville Aux Bois, France, April 16th 1917
     The French Spirit – The Alsace border town of Altkirch, France, August 7th 1914

This little set includes everything you need to play an introductory Panzer Grenadier scenario: the rules and charts (in downloadable form), a scenario card, a set of 40 superb laser-cut, mounted real playing pieces, and an 11x17-inch real full-color map on heavy cardstock.

The pieces, map and scenario are drawn from Panzer Grenadier: Burning Tigers.

Comes packaged in 9"x12" ziplock bag.

In A Cha Co, you become a pyrotechnician wanting to be the first to construct 10 fireworks to win the game!

Roll the dice (number is dependent upon the amount of players, n+1 formula). You choose the colour of highest quantity rolled. Then, everyone hides a gem of the colour of firework they wish to produce, with a simultaneous reveal afterward. Those who have chosen a singular colour (that no one else has selected) each receive a ring of that colour, representing one firework.

The point value system is the following:

White = 1 point
Blue = 2 points
Red = 5 points

The steps are repeated until a player reaches 10 points first and wins the game!

boardgame: Was ist Was TV: Das Quizspiel zur TV/-Serie
description:
Google translation:

Together we are unbeatable! We know everything! Yup! You do not believe that? You want to try it really, to beat us? Well, then you sit down times!

The trivia game for the TV series is based on the facts of the popular WAW-IST WAS books. Theo, Tess and Quentin present interesting facts and exciting from six different topic areas.

The choices are; Ancient Rome, whales & dolphins, volcanoes, Planet & Space, Cars and Ritter. On the basis of 198 questions per topic, players can prove that they are attentive and well rested to go through the world. And while playing you can also learn a lot.

In the game it comes to answering questions correctly and thereby collect chips. Anyone who has ever Theo, Tess and Quentin completely, it can be exchanged for a three WAS IST WAS / point. And who has the most points at the end wins the game.

Content:
6 card sets of 32 sheet
3 fixtures printed on both sides
1 tableau cards with WAS IST WAS chips
1 character
1 set of instructions


From the Backside (German) 
Zusammen sind wir unschlagbar! Wir wissen alles! Jawohl! Ihr glaubt das nicht? Ihr wollt es wirklich probieren, uns zu schlagen? Also gut, dann setzt euch mal hin!

Das Quiz-Spiel zur TV-Serie basiert auf den Fakten aus den beliebten WAW-IST-WAS-Büchern. Theo, Tess und Quentin präsentieren Interessantes, Wissenswertes und Spannendes aus sechs verschiedenen Themengebieten.

Zur Auswahl stehen; Das alte Rom, Wale & Delphine, Vulkane, Planeten & Raumfahrt, Autos und Ritter. Anhand von 198 Fragen pro Themengebiet können die Spieler beweisen, dass sie aufmerksam und ausgeschlafen durch die Welt gehen. Und beim Spielen kann man natürlich auch viel dazulernen.

Im Spiel geht es darum, Fragen richtig zu beantworten und dadurch Chips zu sammeln. Wer einmal Theo, Tess und Quentin komplett hat, kann diese drei gegen einen WAS-IST-WAS/Punkt eintauschen. Und wer am Schluß die meisten Punkte gesammelt hat, gewinnt das Spiel.

boardgame: Lock 'n Load Tactical: Days of Villainy
description:
In March of 2011, Libyans in opposition to Colonel Muammar Gaddafi and his government took up arms. For eight months, the opposition, fighting under an interim governing body, the National Transitional Council (NTC), fought Colonel Gaddafi’s Loyalist forces all across the country. In October, during a fierce battle in the coastal city of Sirte, Gaddafi was captured and killed.

Days of Villainy is an expansion that can be played with ANY core/base games of the Lock 'n Load Tactical series.  Days of Villainy depicts four of the fierce battles that occurred during the Libyan Civil War, including Colonel Gaddafi’s final epic confrontation. Both sides are loaded for bear, armed with RPG-7s and FN MAG and PKM machine guns. The Loyalists have the armored support of BTR-60s and T-55s, but the NTC rebels have plenty of well-armed Technicals to drive into battle, as well as KPV heavy machine guns and the aid of seasoned fighters from the Katiba Tiger and Al-Saiqa commando brigades.

Scenarios Include:

•    Hold or Die: An armored column of Loyalist forces attempted to retake the vital oil hub of Marsa al-Brega, on the road to Benghazi. A tough fight was ahead of them, and the sun was setting.

•    On the Road to Sirte: A convoy of NTC rebels and Al-Saiqa commandos tasked with delivering ammunition to Harawa was ambushed by a small force of Loyalists. Despite the initial blasts of roadside mines, the remnants of the convoy had to push through.

•    Assault on Bab Al-Aziziya: In late August, NTC rebels entered Tripoli. Many neighborhoods were easily taken, but the Bab Al Aziziya compound, symbol of Gaddafi’s contemptible regime, was still intact. The compound had become a fortress that had to be taken.

•    Game Over: On October 20th, 2011, an American Predator drone identified a Loyalist convoy leaving the city of the pro-Gaddafi stronghold of Sirte. The drone and French aircraft in the area attacked the convoy, and NTC rebels in the sector closed in on the commotion. Little did they know that Gaddafi was a passenger in the convoy. The stage was set for a vicious, climactic battle.

boardgame: Lock 'n Load Tactical: Bear and the Jackal
description:
For 10 years, from 1979 to 1989, the Soviet Union fought a bloody and, ultimately, unwinnable war in Afghanistan against the Mujahideen. It was a long, difficult war, and lessons were learned the hard way. The terrain and conditions were brutal. There were few, if any, stand-up fights. Ambushes, helicopter assaults, raids, and cordon-and-sweep were the necessary missions, the types of squad-level missions that are ideally suited to the Lock ’n Load Tactical Series.

The Bear & The Jackal (TB&TJ) contains nine fierce firefights from the Soviet-Afghan War. It expands Heroes of the Gap, and utilizes new counters of Mujahideen and Soviet forces, including Spetsnaz special-forces units; and it’s played on two plus-sized (approximately 9.5” x 16.5”) geomorphic maps. Grab an RPG-7 and find a rocky place to hide behind: there’s a Soviet convoy coming down the road!
