Investor is a game about owning and investing in companies. Each player starts of with a private fortune, a company and a company fortune.

The turn order is decided by the company you start with and every time you pass start you will be able to buy more companies - and thereby get more turns each round. Passing start also adds more money to your fortune and allows you to pay out dividend from your company if you have qualified.

Movement is decided by rolling either one or two dice - the player decides - but if you roll 2 and get two of the same you will receive a bonus. Where you land you will have to pay rent but that will also allow you to place your company logo on the square. From then on all players that land on the square will have to pay you rent as well.

If you get your logo on 5 different types of squares or a lot of players land on the same squares this makes the companies on them worth more.

In the game you can pay to get helpers in the form of lawyers and accountants that makes it possible to buy and sell more companies and negate consequences of special squares and event cards such as tax and a visit from the Fraud Squad.

Before the game starts it is decided when to end the game: At a certain time, at a certain fortune, at a certain number of companies owned or so on. The game should have a length of minimum an hour though. When the game ends your fortune is tallied up and the one with the largest one wins.

Lost something? No worries! In Lost'n'Found you have to be quick and careful: memorize the images, flip the card, then name the item that was lost but now appeared before your opponent does it. Be the first one to name five "found" items to win the game. Thanks to the various difficulty levels, parents and kids will both find Lost'n'Found engaging.

The Annual Alchemy Competition is taking place today! Gather the ingredient elements within the time limit, throw them into a magical pot, and create the minions written in the recipes. The one who has created many and powerful minions wins the competition!

The Minion Recipes takes place over five rounds, and in each round players draft both element cards and pot cards. When a player has a pot and the necessary elements to complete one of the recipes on display, they can stop the round in order to create the minion, possibly taking advantage of its special ability at the same time. Players can drop out of the round in the middle of a draft, throwing away the remaining cards instead of passing them, but they forfeit the ability to create a minion. Players can carry over three cards into the next round, giving them a head start toward a recipe, but everyone can see which recipes are available, so you'll still be racing to complete something before anyone else.

This is a game played with 18 cards: each with a different value.

You play with only 2 cards at a time in your hand and manipulate other players to gather the best cards for yourself.

The object is to gain the most points.

In Quiz Granny o Cywilizacji: część 1 (Granna's Quiz about Civilization: part 1) players move on the board with 18 fields arranged into circle and try correctly answer question from card.

Player rolls dice and move. If move ends on field with other player's pawn, he try answer question and if it's correct he gains point. Player with 3 points is the winner.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Terror and plague reign in the great kingdoms of the East. You are a ruler of one of the great City-States and you must war against the other kings to secure resources and ensure the survival of your kingdom.
 Woe is a drafting card game for 2-5 players where your goal is to gather resources and deprive your opponents in order to become the strongest kingdom. The first player to accumulate 5 of two of the resource types wins.

Players draft a hand a 5 cards and then play the hand, attempting to get singles, pairs, triples, etc. Resource cards in-hand will typically gain you 1 resource per card. Warrior and Priest cards allow you to manipulate the resources and cards of other players.

Once a hand is complete then a new hand is dealt, drafted and played. This happens until one player has 5 of two different resources.

Trivia game with 1000 unexpected, funny and surprising questions.
Be fastest some times only one can answer. Speak Finnish, ask questions, tell the scores and comment about game-play. Includes 3 AA battery.

This is a soccer game. The player who scores the most goals in a prearranged time wins the game. Each team has 11 player figures. Movement is one space per turn for an attacking player figure and any spaces in a straight line along friendly player figures or empty spaces for the ball. The defender may move one player figure two spaces or two player figures one space.

This pack consists of the following five promo cards that were previously released separately.

4 Wu-Feng cards: B-Rice Lee, Chuck No-Rice, Jean-Claude Van Rice and Steven Qi-Gal.
1 Ghost card: Crazy Shaman

These cards were originally released as downloadable pdfs and later obtainable at certain game conventions.

All cards can be used with the base game and any of the expansions, with the exception of the Steven Qi-Gal card, which requires the White Moon expansion.

In Rise of Man: Stone Age, you are a tribal leader, and you’ll have to gather actual resources from the Stone Age period to triumph. You’ll fight saber tooth tigers, hunt woolly mammoths, gather food and herbs, build shelters, weather the environment, and overcome any obstacle that the Stone Age will throw at you.

Rise of Man: Stone Age is a strategic card drafting board game. Players will choose Resources specific to the Stone Age to overcome Obstacles and Hazards that they will face in each of the 5 Rounds of play.

As tribal leaders, players must overcome a new Obstacle (or Hazard) in each of the 5 rounds of play by choosing and accumulating suitable Resource cards. At the end of each Round, players earn points for overcoming Obstacles, but lose points by failing to overcome their Obstacles. Resource cards accumulate each round so that players can also play “Special Events” to earn additional points. Special Events are played by using different combinations of specific Resource Cards. Special Events include Chieftain, Feast, Shaman, and many others.

You’ll build a life for yourself and your tribe to really create something worth fighting for. Here, it’s not victory or defeat, but life or death, and it’s all up to the choices that you make.

boardgame: Epic Card Game: Kickstarter Promo Cards
description:
Expansion for the Kickstarter edition of Epic. Released in a shrink-wrapped cards-only package (no box), outside the main box.

Contents 36 cards:

- 12 Year one promos
- 8 Kickstarter exclusive promos
- 8 Alternate art tokens
- 4 Demigod cards
- 4 God cards
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Innovation: Deluxe is a complete set of Innovation (Third Edition) and its four expansions, all in a new upgraded art style.

The included expansions are:
 Innovation: Echoes of the Past 
 Innovation: Figures in the Sand
 Innovation: Cities of Destiny
 Innovation: Artifacts of History

Innovation is a civilization-building card game that focuses on the ideas and inventions across the ages. There are no maps or battles to fight, and your success is determined by clever use of the special powers granted by the innovations you choose to play. Each of the 105 cards has a unique effect, which can vary from drawing new cards from higher ages, to scoring points, to even demanding other civilizations surrender their innovations to you!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: A social deduction game in which players are an orchestra full of players working at cross purposes to try to make something approaching music (or "an orchestra"). Each player receives a role and must decide how to use its power, whether to trade the role with another and what they will say to help the manager reach their final decision regarding who to eject from the orchestra. When the manager makes their choice the game ends and everyone wins or loses based on their role - the manager wants to catch the artiste, the artiste wants to escape, most musicians want the manager to sort things out but a few have their own agendas.

Players start out as Banner, get angry and change into the Hulk, smashing everything in their path as they try to get to the Abomination.

The objective of the game is to knock the Abomination cube into your color's win area.

After moving Banner to a transformation square you take your turn by rolling two dice that can allow you to move, attack or revert back to Banner.

You are working against the other Hulk players to defeat the abomination yourself.

Gilded Devils is an asymmetrical game of negotiation and economic conflict for 2-8 players. Set in America's Guilded Age, half the players take on the role of Industrialists, building railroads and factories in an attempt to make more money than their fellow industrialists, while the other half of the players take on the role of politicians, selling political favors to the Industrialists, and using those profits to fund their political campaigns, attempting to garner the needed votes before the competing politicians.

The game consists of a deck of 53 cards. On each card is a different cat breed, belonging to one of 17 different subgroups. Each player gets five cards from the start. Each round you get to ask one player for a certain cards that you need to complete a subgroup. If the other player has the card, you get it and can ask for another card. If the other player doesn't have the requested card, you have to take a card from the draw pile and it's the next player's turn. When you have a complete subgroup, you put it on the table. When the deck runs out, you count the score.

An extension of Machi Koro that allows up to 6 players.

The recommended rule for this expansion is as follows:

Green : Nothing changes
Blue : Only receive money from the bank during your turn and those of your two neighbors
Red : Only receive money during your two neighbors turn
Purple : Nothing changes
It is highly encouraged to use the Marina card setup and to use 12 piles instead of 10.

Includes MGA Game Center" (Gaming Mega Store) and Fabrique de jouets du Père Noël.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This expansion introduces new band abilities which can be used in both standard and basic variants. Before the start of the game each player randomly takes one of the disc with a special ability and places it by his or hers band board. There is an ability depicted on the disc.

This (mini-)expansion is included in the Kickstarter edition of Thrash 'n' Roll.

boardgame: Oath of the Brotherhood: "Sneaky" John Promo Card
description:
"Sneaky" John is a promo card for Oath of the Brotherhood.

It consists of 1 Follower card with the following ability: 
Gain 3 VP when you invite "Sneaky" John.

boardgame: Victory by Any Means Campaign Guide (Second Edition): With a Purpose
description:
With a Purpose is the next supplement book for the modular Victory By Any Means campaign system with a focus on objectives and goals. Campaigns have a tendency to focus on goals and exclude the story element. This expansion has a more granular scope, focusing on a commodore instead of a supreme commander. Instead of an open ended campaign, players are working to achieve victory points.

This is done by defining the major objectives given to the players from their central government. Additionally, players will receive high priority missions that need to be completed. Campaign Moderators will have more freedom to explore the motivations of an empire, becoming a true 5x game (eXpand, eXploit, eXterminate, eXplore, and eXplain). With A Purpose supports a number of players and team play for those players who wish to try cooperative campaign games.

Since players represent low level commanders than a typical Victory by Any Means campaign, the scope of the degree is altered. Campaign turns represent shorter periods of time and each ship and ground unit is all the more precious to pull off the work given from their central command. The area will simply have more territory to explore as these campaigns include more minor systems that may exist between all the important campaign worlds in a normal game. Players must be ever vigilant of their actions and careful to control the amount of tension in the region.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Pirates of the 7 Seas: Black Friday
description:
Pirates of the 7 Seas: Black Friday is a unique promo card specially made for the Pirates of the 7 Seas release at Spiel 2015. Its uniqueness, apart from its limited quantity, is the combination of two cards (Curse card and Port card).

Pirates, who brought rich booty to sell, are in despair. The time for Black Friday has come and all the goods cost just 1 chest of gold for each no matter its quantity. It is somebody’s bad intent, for sure! It’s probably a trick of those whose ships are carrying just air and rats into their holds.

boardgame: Star Wars: Box Busters – Tusken Raider Attack & The Battle of Yavin
description:
From the packaging:

"Two Players face off in an epic battle of planetary conquest in the Star Wars universe. Roll the dice, blast through your opponent's defenses and capture their command center to win"

Each player needs their own Battle Cube, which is a small diorama. Each has a unique combination of abilities.

This set has the Star Wars: Box Busters – Battle of Yavin, also available as a single set. Tusken Raider Attack is exclusive to this 2-Pack.

boardgame: Ticket to Ride: Europe – Orient Express
description:
Ticket to Ride: Orient Express is a mini-expansion for Ticket to Ride: Europe that consists of seven new destination tickets, with these tickets set along the line of this legendary railroad. To use them, simply shuffle them into your deck of destination tickets.

The terminals are Athena, Budapest, Constantinople, London, Munchen, Paris and Venezia.

The routes and their scores are:

     Budapest to Athena               (7)
     Budapest to Constantinople       (7)
     London to Constantinople        (18)
     Munchen to Budapest              (4)
     Paris to Munchen                 (5)
     Paris to Venezia                 (5)
     Venezia to Constantinople       (10)


Part of Ticket to Ride series.
Yunnan: Dian Mu Temple is a postcard expansion for Yunnan that features a new building on one side and the rules for use of the building on the reverse side.

An expansion for Greenland, Greenland: Sea Sámi adds one tribe to the game — the Sea Sámi — along with the possibility of playing the game with four players.

The Sea Sámi were talented boat builders, praised even by the vikings, and research shows that they hunted whale very early, probably several thousand years ago. It's likely that they visited Greenland regularly, and as a snow-adapted people (probably being the inventors of skiing) the Sámi may also have had settlements on Greenland. As a Sea Sámi, you have access to daughters who use skiing and magic drums.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Watch the skeleton bones appear as you race against the ghost to uncover hidden treasures.  A haunting game of suspense for all ages. The object of the game is to collect as many treasure items as possible before the skeleton is completed in the centre of the board.

Players roll two dice (one red and one white) and move around the house collecting cards from each cupboard in the different rooms. The face down cards can be treasures of varying values or special power cards or one of the eight parts of the skeleton.

In addition to the moving their own pawn (according to the number on the red die) players also move the ghost (according to the white die).  With the ghost they can attempt to steal cards from the other players!

The winner is the player with the most treasure points when all eight skeleton pieces have been fitted together.

A promo item card for use in all [redacted] missions.

Setup
Shuffle up to two copies of Smoke Bomb into the items deck before drawing starting items, in any [redacted] mission.
If playing with 2 or more types of promo items (more coming in the future), shuffle all copies of all such items together and randomly (and secretly) add four of these cards to the items deck.

Rules
A passive player may use a Smoke Bomb to avoid an interaction.
When the active player enters a room, the passive player may play a Smoke Bomb before an interaction can happen.
These players may not have an interaction this turn, and the active player continues playing.
Note: The Smoke Bomb can only be played in the dark if the passive player also played Night Vision Goggles.

The Tinker scorns traditional wisdom, determined to form his own understanding rather than rely on the teachings of others. Through constant experimentation, he gradually develops and refines his own tools, every one the first of its kind. Due to its unique origins, the Tinker's magic is not quite like any other in the world...

This package includes the Tinker, along with his power cards, plus a new event and an artifact. A must-have for all Darkest Night fans!

Adventurer's Kit is a culture exploration and genuine artifact excavation game, bringing kids from ages 3-12 a true adventure experience.

For the game preparation, players first setup the world map by randomly placing landscape boards with the landscapes icon side facing up, and the adventure/treasure side facing down. Each player then draws a vehicle, which corresponds to a particular starting point on the map and a holds a special ability. After getting prepared, the adventure through the ancient four continents begins!

Each turn, a player rolls a die to determine how many steps the player can move. Upon moving on to a landscape game board, the player may flip it over to find out whether it is an adventure event or treasure. Event game boards effect players located at different continents (such as Bigfoot effects Americas and Mummies effect Africa), while treasure game boards from different locations brings treasure cards from different continents. Collect the artifacts on the way and beware of the events that may occur. At the end of the game, the winner would be the first to dig out the ultimate treasure and become the great Adventurer!

Features:

► Adventure
Tornados, UFOs, Bigfoot, Kappa, and more. Overcome all the adventure obstacles through decision making, and collect more than 50 treasures on your journey to become the true adventurer!

► Culture
From the lost empires of the four continents, comes more than 30 ancient civilization that once ruled the world, including Egypt, Greek, Maya, China, Persia, and lots more.

► Artifacts
Through a series of research and testing, we are finally ready to remake the greatest legacy of civilizations out of the EXACT material, such as Terracotta Army with 100% pottery.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The single print run Kickstarter gameplay exclusive expansion, Hound of Tindalos, was offered in Richard Luong's art book Kickstarter as an add-on.

"Sandy Petersen has been gracious enough to allow me to offer an Exclusive Hound of Tindalos Expansion for Cthulhu Wars! Each 80 mm miniature will come with a loyalty card designed by Sandy. Jo Brumby of Fenris Games has collaborated with me to design an amazing Sculpt showing the Hound emerging from a 90 degree angle. This will be an extremely limited run and will be a highly sought-after collectors item... I hope. :)" Richard Luong

Gameplay Features:

To Acquire: Pay 2 Doom and 2 Power to acquire the monster loyalty card. Then place the Hound at one of your controlled gates.
Cost: 4 power to summon

Combat: 4

Movement: Move one unit at its normal movement cost, then teleport the Hound for a cost of "0" to any gate of his choice.

Special: Cannot be killed unless it is pained into an area without a gate or the gate in its current area is removed by a faction ability and/or spell book.

Two to four players card game for Naval Arms Race prior World War Two. Mostly card represent hull, gun, engine or other equipment based on used or planed battleships in World War Two.

The game divided to two stages. In the first stage, players design new battleships by combine of parts cards. In the second stage, the winner of design competition is fight against imagined enemy's battleship.

Winner is determined by battleship design competition and combat result.

The first edition named "Senkan Kyosokyoku"(is means Battleship Roaring Songs) had a focus only on battleship design. It was self-published as one of Yuichi Kazama's Competition series. And then, combat rule were added by re-develop.

boardgame: The Big Book of Madness: Mime Promo Card
description: A promo monster given away at the game's release ; just shuffle it with the other Grimoire cards and draw 5 random Monsters at the beginning of the game, as usual.

description:
Blink! is a Doctor Who miniatures game, written as a pre-sequel to the Doctor Who story, “Blink!”  The background to the story is as follows; The Doctor and Martha Jones have been investigating the strange disappearance of a number of people which have brought them into the garden of an old abandoned house.  They are then ambushed by alien monsters called “Weeping Angels”, which they must escape from.

The game is involves one player taking the role of the Doctor and Martha, the other takes control of the four Weeping Angels.  The aim of the game is that the Doctor and Martha must escape from the garden to win before the Weeping Angels capture them.

The game was first published in the fanzine Ragnarok, issue 60, with instructions on miniatures and scenery required.

boardgame: Professor Nod and His Turtle Race
description:
"You are racing your turtle to be the first in the Winner's Circle. Howeve, along the way, you must get special instructions from Professor Nod. He can help you win - or lose - so try to please him!"

This is a fairly basic spin-and-move game with the addition of a plastic "Professor Nod" figure which is used to allow or disallow certain actions. If a player wishes to take a shortcut, ends a turn on an opponent's space, or lands on a "Spin Again" space, that player must push a "Question Card" into the base of the Professor Nod figure. If he nods, the move is acceptable, if he shakes his head to the side the move is denied.

boardgame: Star Wars: Box Busters – Endor Attack
description:
From the packaging:

"Two Players face off in an epic battle of planetary conquest in the Star Wars universe. Roll the dice, blast through your opponent's defenses and capture their command center to win"

Each player needs their own Battle Cube, which is a small diorama. Each has a unique combination of abilities.

boardgame: Star Wars: Box Busters – Battle of Hoth
description:
From the packaging:

"Two Players face off in an epic battle of planetary conquest in the Star Wars universe. Roll the dice, blast through your opponent's defenses and capture their command center to win"

Each player needs their own Battle Cube, which is a small diorama. Each has a unique combination of abilities.

boardgame: Star Wars: Box Busters – Battle of Yavin
description:
From the packaging:

"Two Players face off in an epic battle of planetary conquest in the Star Wars universe. Roll the dice, blast through your opponent's defenses and capture their command center to win"

Each player needs their own Battle Cube, which is a small diorama. Each has a unique combination of abilities.

The letter that begins with the famous expression "Dear Boss" was received on September 1888, Thursday the 27th, by the press agency Central News Agency of London, which delivered it to Scotland Yard two days later. It was written in red and contained many macabre details that they could not be ignored. It was signed: Jack The Ripper. The horror of Whitechapel had finally a name...a name that would have badgered the district forever.

Letters from Whitechapel: Dear Boss, the first expansion for Letters from Whitechapel, will help you to enter the sordid depths atmosphere of Whitechapel: You will better know the main characters of the events of that fall and face, both with Jack and policemen, new and intriguing challenges.

description: The players start at their harbor at the edge of the game board and have to reach their second harbor on the island in he center of the game board. Movement is determined by a die. When they reach one of the white cicles, they have to go around before advancing to the next circle. If a player gets on an occupied space the gets thrown back according to the game rules, if he gets on a mine he has to skip a turn and if he gets on a burning ship he has to go back to his harbor and start anew.

boardgame: Santa's Little Helpers and the Ice Cube Jam
description:
Santa needs your help!

Help Santa collect enough of the colorful cube-shaped fruits that magically appear on the snow and ice around his Workshop to make his famous “Ice Cube Jam!” Change the map, slide on the ice, pick up cubes and be the quickest elf to drop them off to get the biggest helping of the delicious jam!

Each player is a North Pole Elf, venturing out onto the snow and ice to collect sets of colored cubes and deliver them back at the Workshop in the center of the map. The map is a randomized grid made up of terrain cards. On a player's turn, he/she can modify the map by playing a new terrain card onto it and then move their elf pawn twice. Movement can either be a single step into the adjoining square, or a long slide across open ice, collecting ice cubes along the way.

Collect a set of 3 cubes -- 1 of each color -- and deliver it to  Santa's Workshop to score. Be the first to score 2 or 3 times (depending on the number of players) to win!

boardgame: Naruto Shippuden Deck-building Game: Mecha-Naruto Promo
description:
Oversized hero card for the Naruto Shippuden Deck-building game from Cryptozoic.

Card text:
"+1 Chakra for each filled Chakra Point.
Chakra (3): +2 Power"

Features 1 "Equipment", 1 "Starter", and 2 "Enemy" Chakra Points.

boardgame: NHL Power Play Team-Building Card Game: Henrik Sedin Promo
description:
Additional oversized Captain card for use with Cryptozoic's NHL Power Play Team-building Card Game, Henrik Sedin.

Card text: 
"During each of your Shots on Goal, if you play one or more Attackers, you may destroy a card in your hand or discard pile."

A Star Wars themed collectible disc game. Players use character discs and their Star Wars knowledge to battle for points.

Each disc has four abilities (Attack, Honor, Deceit, Intelligence) and an ability level from 0 to 5. Players can connect their disc to another on the board if their disc's ability level is higher. The player earns points equal to the ability level marks on both sides of the connection.

When playing with the Star Wars Story Connexions rules, additional points are awarded depending on the rim color of the new disc.

The player with the most points wins.

A drinking-style game that parodies the Pokemon CCG.  Each player starts with one monster in play and 4 more in hand.  On your turn, you take 2 actions -- attack or play a new monster.  The attacks have humorous flavor text but mechanically just do a fixed amount of damage.  Better attacks can only be used after you have 'powered up' a certain number of times -- you power up once each turn by doing some out-of-game task agreed upon ahead of time (e.g. drinking or smoking).  The last player with creatures still in play wins.

Description from the designer:

The trading card game that's a little bit hazy.

Smoke 'em Mon is a multiplayer battle game where the objective is to kill all your friends smokies (monsters) and become the Grand Champion Master Sensei. To "Power Up" each player is subjected to smoking, drinking or whatever other tempting tortures your friends can dream up.

Smoke 'em Mon the trading card game features the first 101 smokies known to man and can be played with 2 to 99 players. Thanks to the special "Anything Goes" game rule battles can go in any direction at any time!

We Built This City is a fight between builders to develop the most valuable properties possible. Place your factories in their backyards and hope they don't build a trash dump in the middle of your developments. Remember to connect your buildings to city hall or all of your plans will go to waste!

Players place cards, developing the city block by block. Players will develop houses, offices, and factories but the neighborhood they are building in determines how valuable these are. The player with the most valuable properties, and thus the most points, at the end of the game is the winner.

Pandemic: Promo Roles is a two-card promo featuring for use with Pandemic. Each card is double-sided, meaning there are four roles included, two of which you can use in any given game.

Card 1: Epidemiologist and Operations Expert
Card 2: Generalist and Medic

The key difference with these promo characters is they lose health when cubes are added to the city they are in, or if the city they are in outbreaks. If all four health of a promo character is ever exhausted, the players lose the game. This increases the difficulty of the game.

boardgame: Game of the Visit of Santa Claus
description:
Typical and simple spin and move race game. The first player, who cross all the road first, wins the game. There is "gift" cards, which players collected as they played the game.

Based on the famous poem "A Visit From St. Nicholas," first published anonymously in 1823, which inspired countless illustrations and spin-off goods for sale.

There are four figures and five cups. Hide the figures under the cups. Your opponent wil draw a card. You will need to point out the cup under whitch the figure on the card is located. If you're right you win the card. If you are wrong the card goes away. First to five cards wins.

Bumba is a popular Belgian children's TV-show.

From the back of the box:

Lace up your skates and prepare to face off for the NHL Edition of SCRABBLE. Assist yourself and score high with an exclusive NHL dictionary as you power play your way through the most action-packed SCRABBLE game ever!

In Scrabble, players draw letter-tiles to form words on the gameboard. Each word earns points based on points of the tiles, and any bonuses. A word can only be played if it uses at least one already-played tile or adds to an already-played word.

In Scrabble NHL Edition, the board looks like a hockey rink with many NHL symbols on it and a grid pattern. The double letter spaces have the NHL logo, the triple letter spaces have the Western logo, the double word spaces have the Eastern logo on it and the triple word spaces have another NHL logo.

There are also Bonus Words that are allowed that are hockey related (NHL, Wild, Pads, Net, Save, etc.) that are worth an additional 5 points.

There are also terminology and team names that are allowed as bonus words for 10 points (Kings, Senators, Hockey, Coyotes, Faceoff).

Finally there is a section of special rules : Pre-Season, Trades, Trade Deadlines, Delay In Game, Too Many Men On The Ice, Pulling the Goalie and Offsides, designed to make the game feel more like hockey.

boardgame: Imperium Chronicles: Fleets at War – Theater of Operations: The Talion Front
description: Theater of Operations: The Talion Front (ToO:TF) is the first expansion for the Theater of Operations strategic wargame. It includes a completely new strategic map that can be used along with the original ToO map or alone. The expansion also includes a new faction (allied with the Magna), the Talion Republic, and a set of enemies called Marauders that attack the other main factions. Each faction has new ships including space installations (space stations and star bases).

Race to be the first one to get all your frogs into the bucket! Kids can use the bucket as a target to flip their frogs into or they can just have the frogs race each other as they let their fingers do the flipping! Game comes with 12 frogs in four colors and a reusable bucket for easy cleanup and storage.

Typical and simple spin and move race game. The first player, who cross all the road first, wins the game. 
The box has fabulous graphics with nice patina.  The box artwork is stellar with super detailed artworks of: elephant, lion conductor, hippo, leopard, rhino, ostrich, monkeys, dog and others. 
The street car is marked “MONKEYVILLE and ZEBRA STREET”, “No. O 694”.

The year is 2112 and the machines being produced now run on quintessence, a fully renewable energy source held in Spirits.  In 2040, Dr. Russel VanOrman Quine discovered that Spirits contain energy that can never be destroyed.  Even when used to power heavy machinery, this energy source (quintessence) dissipates into the ether, returning to its unknown source.  All we need to do is collect it.

You have just been hired by B.E.E.net to collect Bound Ethereal Energy Spirits (B.E.E.S.).  Seems simple?  Just find B.E.E.S. wandering around in any haunted manor and grab them with your VanOrman Machine aka “H.I.V.E.” (Held Intensity Vitality Extractor).  The main issue is a H.I.V.E. needs to be exactly Arrayed to grab a Spirit by joining Tubes to match it.   Of course there may be some side effects.   So... BEE careful.

Holding Spirits makes a H.I.V.E. rise in Pressure and Tubes may leak.   A failed Tube can lead to the unbinding of the Spirit and the Tubes must be trashed.  The Spirit is released back into the unknown perhaps to be rebound another day.

Also keep in mind that although Spirit collectors work together, you must all fill your quota each day or you may lose your jobs!  Alternatively, an especially good harvest may get your pictures on the wall at headquarters.

B.E.E.S. can be played cooperatively or competitively, and the cooperative game can be played with one player.  Your goal in the cooperative game is to collect a quota of Spirits and Quintessence, and your goal in the competitive game is to have the most Quintessence when the game ends.  To collect a Spirit and acquire its Quintessence, you must build an Array with Tube cards from your hand and pass both an Array test and a Pressure test.  When you collect a Spirit, it goes into your H.I.V.E. and affects you Pressure.  Players must manage their hand of Tubes and decide how to build their Array while balancing the board state when deciding which Spirit to target.  Gameplay is relatively simple, but the nuances in your decisions determine your level of risk vs. reward and can also greatly affect the other players.

Stratego Dice Game is a two-player dice game, with each player having five dice, one red and the other blue. Each die is different, and on each turn each player tries to choose the right die; they then roll off to see who wins and who loses (or whether the battle is a draw). The used dice are placed on the side, then players start a new turn. Whoever first wins five battles wins the game!
family: Versions & Editions: Dice Versions of Non-Dice Games

description:
ASL Compatible expansion from Lone Canuck Publishing covers the actions of September 22nd and 23rd around the Dutch town of Mariaheide.

Product includes pre-cut double-sided counters, map, historical background, scenarios, and mini campaign game.

Trivia San Diego is a clone of Monopoly set in the city of San Diego, California. The game board locations are businesses and attractions from the city.

From the Publisher: Trivia San Diego is designed to bring hours of exciting challenges for all participants while teaching them interesting facts about their community. This game is for 4-16 players (2 to 4 teams of 2 to 4).

Test your knowledge on 100 players, coaches and builders from the most prestgious team in hockey history.

Learn more on these illustrious characters who gave made this team the envy of all sports organizations on the planet.

3 clues on the card. 3 points for a correct answer on the first clue, 2 on the second and 1 on the third. 0 for an incorrect answer. After 3 periods the player with the most points wins the game.

Zirkus Leo brings Leo the Lion and his friends into the ring where players will experience many turbulent events. In this speedy card game, players try to play the cards from their hand as quickly as possible, and to carry out all actions quickly and correctly, so as not to receive any more cards.

Players must concentrate, because all actions shown on the cards are carried out at the same time. As a general rule, whoever is the last to perform the action receives all the cards in the ring.

Pay attention, as the illustrations on the cards provide assistance. However, since there are a total of 14 different action cards, it is not always easy to perform the correct action immediately. Whoever has the fewest cards at the end wins the game.

boardgame: Wales (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial Fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.


This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop. You can use stations if you wish.

There is an extra set of cards for the "cities", each with a value to be earned by visiting that "city".  These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules. Some of the cards give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks, except the special express route.

The main new rule is the option to discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn.  This is very useful if you are desperate for engines or a particular colour card. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Midmoon: The City of Guilds is an economic strategy game with the elements of RPG. The action takes place in Midmoon, city of the Dreamscape carnival universe. Players take part in settlement development that gradually evolves from small town to a big megapolis. Players take roles of businessmen planning to capture the authority in future city.

It's possible to play in a legal way by building a trade empire that leans on the powerful guards or to rule from the shadows by hiring thieves and killers. A skillful player can combine both ways.
Lucky Cake is a children's game that features a cake.

You have a good eye? Learn to recognize the places, monuments, characters, landscapes, buildings and the most famous works of art.

Roll the color dice for the categorie of the card, take a card, look at the pictures, identify the named one and earn that card.

The first player to win three cards in three different categories or six cards of the same categorie wins.

Puki Duki is a charming matching and observation game for children ages 3 and up. Two best friends—a kitten and a puppy—have favorite toys they adore. But can you figure out what connects them?

Players arrange cards in groups of four, making sure to link the kitten and the puppy with an object they both like. With 36 beautifully illustrated cards by Laima Zulonė, the game encourages kids to sharpen their observation skills and notice subtle similarities in the world around them.

Simple to learn and delightful to play, Puki Duki is perfect for 1–4 players and helps develop focus, pattern recognition, and logical thinking.
The Path of Repentance is the first add on pack for the 7th Continent and contains 30 cards that put you up against an adversary that not even the most proficient adventurer can escape: Time itself.

description:
Ti-Joe Connaissant is a quiz game inspired directly by "Joe connaissant". Designed for children aged 7 years and older, it offers to young smart two ways to play. The reversible game board shows a cooperative version and a competitive version of the game. The puzzles cover a range of topics tailored to the interests and level of knowledge of the youngest.

Version Cooperative
Players must solve all puzzles in order to accumulate 20 points. To encourage the players have six supporters that should not be disappointed by wrong answers.

Competitive Version
Three supporters of two teams clash. The team that will solve most puzzles with the least indices and has the fewest disappointed supporters will win the game.

Arnhem: Defient Stand and Oosterbeek Perimeter combined in one master set.The Battle of Arnhem in super detail, with a building by building map with big hexes (you can put a bunch of counters in each one).

10 mostly small scenarios
Two sheets of die cut counters with new art - big 3/4" AFV counters including lots of unusual German AFVs
Giant-hex two sheet map depicting downtown Arnhem in September 1944
Super-sized deluxe AFV cards for all AFV types
Sturdy box
Two ten-sided dice

Oosterbeek Perimeter is an stand-alone game that links with Arnhem: Defiant Stand that provides two additional maps and 16 more scenarios to expand your Battle of Arnhem action to include the entire British Airborne perimeter!

The new street-level maps at 50-yards scale provide the battlefield where King Tigers smashed toward the Hartenstein Hotel and numerous Victorica Crosses were won for bravery.

In keeping with the Advanced Tobruk approach you will receive a stack of small, fast-playing scenarios to get you on the maps right away, as well as monster-sized action across the entire map!

Features:
Two new historical game maps on heavyweight stock;
16 new Scenarios printed on separate heavyweight cards;
A snazzy ziploc bag to hold it all.
Both games in one package.

boardgame: City of Iron: Clockwork Fortress and Cave Market Promo
description:
Clockwork Fortress and Cave Market are a set of two promotional cards for the 1st edition of City of Iron.
These cards are added to the "E" buildings; that is, they are available to build from the start of the game.  
The Cave Market adds one coin to your income for each different type of good you own.  
The Clockwork Fortress allows you to change the order of your citizen and military decks each time you refresh that deck.

These cards may also be used with the 2nd edition of City of Iron, where they are placed above the usual row of buildings and are not removed until they are purchased.

boardgame: No King of England: A Solitaire Game of the Hundred Years War (1337-1453)
description:
BACKGROUND: NO KING OF ENGLAND is a semi-historical simulation of the Hundred Years War. The Game begins shortly after Charles IV of France dies in 1328. The Queen of England, Isabella of France, is unable to claim the French throne for herself, and has claimed it for her son, Edward III. France rejects the claim, maintaining that Isabella could not transmit a right that she did not possess. The Player represents the House of Plantagenet with the ambition of attaining control of France. As the Commander of English Forces, you must control a significant number of French Cities and Towns before the last Campaign Card is played.

GAME OBJECTIVE: Player takes command of the English Forces. The Player’s Objective is to attempt to control at least 10 French Cities and 12 French Towns before the last Campaign Card is Played. If the French control London and Portsmouth at the end of their Turn, the French win. If the last Campaign Card was played and Player does not control 10 Cities and 12 Towns, France Wins.

GAME FEATURES: NO KING OF ENGLAND features lots of colorful game counters of various Combat Units including Nobles, Knights, Archers, Handgunners and Cannon. English Nobles will gather their Forces and move on the Map in a strategic manner along rout lines of key settlements (and Naval Lanes). Combat is resolved in a tactical manner that includes individual combat between Champion Knights and Archers. The game, also, includes an eventual appearance by Joan of Arc.

NO KING OF ENGLAND can be found at the following URL:
http://www.berserkergames.net

How To Kill A Spider is an easy to learn, fast paced, trick-taking style card game with a dark and hilarious twist.

We were inspired to make a game we could play with our friends and family, having easy-to-learn familiar mechanics, but with a twist! How To Kill A Spider fits in the traditional genre of games such as Spades and other trick-taking games, with a bit of inspired "Take That!" flavor from games like Fluxx and Munchkin.

In "How To Kill A Spider" players take on the role of roommates, trying to clear their house of those horrible home invaders by using an assortment of completely practical household weaponry! The roommate with the most spider cards in their Squash Pile at the end of 3 rounds is our winner! But be careful, if the house is infested with more spiders than the winner's Squash Pile, all players lose!

The base game comes with 130 cards, including 10 "SWAT" expansion pack cards that modify the game further for additional variance and mechanics.

You are a FRAUD!

No, really, you are. But you're one of the best in London. You could convince a legless man to buy a pair of shoes. You could spit in a jar and sell it for medicine. You could sell a widow a charm to increase her husband's fertility. You rush forth where honest men fear to tread. Where other people see the love and joy of God, you see a chance for a profit.

Using your unscrupulous skills - and a copy of the Legenda Aurea - you work your way through the streets of medieval London, taking trash and turning it into treasure. You're just trying to help, of course, and if they're happy, who are you to let a little thing like honesty interfere with that? Why, you're doing a public service!

Your ultimate goal is to give up the life of deceit on the streets, and instead, become a lay clerk at St. Paul's Cathedral. Fortunately, you know where you can purchase, ahem, volunteer for the job. For the price of a proper bribe, why, it's a steal! Sadly, there are other citizens who share your desires, and they plan to take the job first. The first one to acquire the cash gets the job.

The object of the game is to roll dice to travel around the board, trying to collect Relic cards from the corner squares, and then sell the Relic cards at the markets.  Gameplay is based on rolling dice for movement, and drawing an Action card at most squares.  Action cards will affect the value of relics, the gain and loss of shillings (money), and the possibility of getting arrested.  The winner is the first player to acquire thirteen shillings and return to the corner square, St. Paul's Cathedral.

62 Exclusive Cards:

2x Kickstarbear (KS)
2x Highlandbear (KS)
2x Firefighterbear (KS)
2x Monsterhuntbear (KS)
2x Poltbeargeist (KS)
1x King Koala
1x Killer Koala
1x Kid Koala

3x Cheerleadbear

Aeric is a very peculiar knight. He was raised by pandas, and so believes to be a panda himself. He fights with a panda's true courage and cuddliness.

Aeric is a single-character expansion to Arcadia Quest with the special ability: "Rolls +2 dice on defense rolls against Ranged attacks."

Farfalla is a half-elf maiden posessed of supernatural empathy. Daughter to an important elf-lord, she traveled to Arcadia in a diplomatic mission and must now choose whether to stand among the courageous humans to help them or return to her noble kindred as duty requires.

Farfalla is a single-character expansion to Arcadia Quest with the special ability: "When using one of her Attack cards, she may exhaust any Close Hero's Attack card instead."

A set of 22 Heroica character cards for use with the Heroica game system - all printed in German.

The cards include:


     6 Good characters (Barbarian, Wizard, Ranger, Druid, Rogue and Knight)
     4 Potion Cards (each card lists 4 Potions)
     4 Monster Cards (each card lists 10 Monsters, back and front)
     4 Weapon Cards (each card lists 6 Weapons, back and front)


Cards provided in a foil-style bag.

     4 Treasure Cards (Scepter, Helmet, Crystal and Chalice)


The cards come in a foil pack.

The cards can be used to:
1 - Randomize hero selection at the start of the game.
2 - Provide players with handy reference cards for Potions, Weapons, Treasures and Monster strength values.
3 - Face down treasure cards may be used as an alternative end game goal instead of using the assigned treasure in a game.  May also be used for random treasures in fan made dungeons.

This surprise personality card is made especially for the 10th anniversary of Quined Games. On the cards’ art you can see Arno, the founder and owner of Quined Games. During the day he is a lawyer and during the evening he is an editor. Enjoy playing with this character, here are the rules on how to use him.

The Lawyer may immediately buy a parcel not owned by any player. He pays the normal price. In addition, nobody can contest the lawyer from buying a parcel. In case of a duel with the Lawyer
for buying a parcel, the challenging cowboy goes back to the personal reserve of this player, before the duel is resolved. For all other actions, duels against the lawyer are resolved as
usual. Cash limit : $60.

The Editor publishes a story inspired by your success. At the end of the income phase (round 1/2/3/4), you win a victory point for every $5 / $10 / $15 / $20 of income from all your buildings, rounded up. You do not earn points on income from the attacks of other players buildings or on the money other players would have robbed you. Example: in round 2, you get a total income of 33$ from your buildings, so you earn 4 victory points. Cash limit: $40.

---

Note: As these are included in the Big Box of Carson City, this small expansion is meant for owners of the base game.
The Lawyer/Editor was also a giveaway together with the Teacher at Essen but without the Townhall.

From the back of the box (translated):

GTP Compet brings the entire family together.

Suitable for all ages 6 to adult.  It helps everyone learn the initial concepts of solid geometry while having fun and develops logic, dexterity, manual agility and a sense of observation. Educational and of exceptional content with numerous variants.

For two players, you must be the fastest to achieve the challenges imposed by the puzzle or 3D maps.

Solo, you need to build cubes of different sizes and difficulties from thousands of possible combinations.

Artifacts of History is the fourth expansion to Innovation. Artifacts of History features unique artifacts from the ages, such as the Declaration of Independence. These cards feature very powerful dogma effects, but are difficult to wield.

Artifacts of History is simpler than the other expansions released to date, Unlike the other expansions, there are no major new mechanics on the cards. Instead, the new artifact cards are difficult to acquire but have very powerful effects. The artifacts are especially good for players who are behind.

There are a few minor new mechanics. One is the compel effect. This effect is similar to the demand effect of the base game, except that it affects players who have more icons of a given type rather than fewer.

description:
From the introduction:

The Heroes are trapped in the monster infested, ever changing, dungeon of the Lich King! They must find the exit and escape before the Lich King summons an undefeatable Dragon to kill them all!

User comments:

Bulk of document consists of print-and-play components, primarily paper maps to construct the dungeon and paper counters representing the players and various monsters.  Game play consists of placing character tokens on a starting map, surviving combats and collecting treasure, and then moving into new areas looking for the dungeon exit.  Game provides four character "types" (Wizard, Archer, Barbarian, Knight) and three monster "types" (Orc, Goblin, Dragon).

EcoChains is a multiplayer card game about climate change -- life and the struggle for survival in a rapidly warming Arctic.  Players build food chains as they learn about Arctic ecosystems and how humans impact the environment (e.g. how carbon pollution and diminishing sea ice can impact Arctic species). The game was produced by Jogolabs and designed by a team of environmental scientists, educators and game designers.

Gameplay is simple: Sea Ice cards form the base of each player's food web.  Each player builds food chains by connecting various Arctic species cards including polar bear, walrus, ringed seal and narwhal.  Event cards such as carbon pollution occur, which melt sea ice and cause some animals to migrate or die.  Players can also play Action cards in order to help protect their food chains.  The player who keeps the most animals alive wins.  Goal cards also provide additional Victory points.

http://www.ecochainsgame.com

Inconceivable: Kingdom at War is an intense microgame in which you must strategically read your opponent(s) and respond accordingly. Each player controls a part of the remains of a broken kingdom, fighting to restore stability to the fallen empire. With four battle cards in hand, you must choose: Should you play your Knight? Your Archer? Do you call in your Sorcerer, or save it for a future round? It all depends on the action of your foes, so do not be deceived by their trickery.

Inconceivable is a 2-3 player game that lasts about 10-20 minutes on average. Each player has 4 warrior cards, which can be seen below. There are 18 loot cards that are mixed up and placed face-down, creating the playing field. On each turn, there can be anywhere from one to three rounds to win these loot cards. The progression of each turn is a mystery; so be strategic!

City of Ideologies is a strategic board game in a futuristic setting for two up to four players.

Humanity had to leave Earth centuries ago and travels in search of a new home in a huge city by the vastness of space. Players assume the role of leaders who are trying to reshape the city after their vision in an erupting revolution against the old system. For this they must legislate, kindle demonstration, gain influence over the districts of the city and always keep an eye on the mood of the people and the changing game board.

Both the game board, as well as the laws are constantly changing, it is important to keep a cool head, gain advantage through agreements with other players or through own concepts or intrigue to weaken the position of the other players.

City of Ideologies combines different game mechanics: You move protesters across the board, which turns around three times each round, role dice and push your luck, gain influence in districts, use different cards to perform unique actions and at the end of each round, one person is chosen by election of all players who is allowed to change a paragraph / rule mechanism.

Each action may be carried out in various ways. In most cases, they are paid with influence points, which vary from faction to faction depending on their main focus. If a player can not muster enough points for an action, then he can trade with his fellow players. Or he activates a district, which lets him perform the action or pulls a card that allows him to perform the action or uses a person of his faction. Or he changes a paragraph, so that the action costs less points.

The player who collects the most victory-points will win the game after five rounds. The way you collect victory points depends on the one hand on your ideology, which you choose at the beginning of the game. So every player gains victory points in different ways. For example, one player gets a victory point every time he prevents other players actions. Another player collects victory points if the other players give him voluntarily cards or influence points. On the other hand players can gain victory points on certain districts, by cards and paragraphs.

At the end the player will be victorious, who is both the most accurate follower of his ideology, as has also been successful in competitive areas.

The objective is to capture the opposition’s sacred book, release one’s captive ancestors and figuratively destroy the captured book at one’s altar.

Mechanically, the game is a roll and move format. Four players are required and the game is played in two teams--The People of Light versus The People of Darkness. Each player takes on an identity.

Game components include, a game board, deck of draw cards for each team, two sets of dice and other parts related to identity, movement and possession of goods.

The game board has an innovation where the end game expands the board by including a middle insert where the end game is played.

Welcome to the city of Machi Koro. You've just been elected Mayor. Congrats! Unfortunately the citizens have some pretty big demands: jobs, a theme park, a couple of cheese factories and maybe even a radio tower. A tough proposition since the city currently consists of a wheat field, a bakery and a single die.

Armed only with your trusty die and a dream, you must grow Machi Koro into the largest city in the region. You will need to collect income from developments, build public works, and steal from your neighbors' coffers. Just make sure they aren't doing the same to you!

Machi Koro is a fast-paced game for 2-4 players. Each player wants to develop the city on his own terms in order to complete all of the landmarks under construction faster than his rivals. On his turn, each player rolls one or two dice. If the sum of the dice rolled matches the number of a building that a player owns, he gets the effect of that building; in some cases opponents will also benefit from your die (just as you can benefit from theirs). Then, with money in hand a player can build a landmark or a new building, ideally adding to the wealth of his city on future turns. The first player to construct all of his landmarks wins!

The Machi Koro: Harbor Expansion injects further excitement into the game that has everyone talking! Want even more fun in your box?! Perhaps ten new establishments, one new starting establishment and two new landmarks will help?

This expansion adds historical battles related to the Persian wars (499 – 449 BC), to the Persians vs Greeks wars and to the Peloponnesus war (1st period: 431 – 421 BC) between Athens and Sparta.

There will be 90 unit cards (24 different) belonging to Athens, Sparta, Persia and other Greek poleis and mercenaries: 
- Persia: Melophores guard, Sparabaras archers, Takabara mercenaries, Archers with shields, Sicklers, Usubari Dromedaries, Asabari Cavalry, Pasti Infantry and Persian War Chariots (stretch goal). 
- Sparta: Hippeis Cavalry (stretch goal), Spartan Hoplites, Helots infantry, Helots slingers and Spartan Cavalry.
- Athens: Hoplites, hoplites attendants, Scythian Archers, Athenian Cavalry and Peltasts (stretch goal).
- Greek mercenaries or allies: Peripolos, Toxotes Archers, Thorakitai, Theban sacred battalion (stretch goal) and Boeotian Hoplites.

With this expansion you will be able to play with scenery and its new set of rules for sieging, incendiary projectiles, and more…

It contains different types of terrain like woods, hills, rivers (depicted in tiles with several different drawings) and landmarks like towns, bridges, rams, wood/stone fortress sections, barricades, fire counters, assault towers, and more!

boardgame: The Task of Ezrok: Deluxe Edition
description:
The Chest of Seth has found its way into the heartland kingdoms. Fortunately, King Morem has ordered the destruction of this evil relic. Your aim is to oversee Ezrok and his companions as they transport the chest far away from civilization. This task will take you through the Swamps of the Black Dragon, the Hills of Vorkin, and deep into an icy wasteland where the chest must be destroyed in the Eternal Chasm.

This game uses a tile laying system to move Ezrok and his companions across the different regions toward their goal. It is after this point that Ezrok can arrive at the Eternal Chasm to destroy the chest and begin the second challenge- the return trip.

This compilation includes the base game, a new deck, and favored expansion components to create the ultimate solitaire tile-laying adventure. The edition includes:


     Tile Cards
     Character Roster Cards
     Quest Cards
     Cosmic Boost Deck (new)
     Wandering Threats Deck
     Wild Cards Deck
     Power Cards Deck
     Enhanced Reference Material
     Rulebook and Bonus Booklet
     Printed Box


___________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

The game can be played in two different modes. The "Training Operation" mode is a free-for-all where players try to prove they are the most qualified hacker for the secret agent position. The "Special Operation" mode is a team mode (all vs. one) where one player takes on the role of a rogue agent trying to protect secret information. The other players try to recover all of the critical data before security shuts the whole system down.

In both modes gameplay proceeds in turns. During a player's turn he will move his pawn from one tile to another (which represents accessing different computer systems) across connection points, revealing facedown tiles in the process. Each revealed tile will indicate how many data tokens are to be placed on the tile facedown. A player on that tile can choose to take the data tokens which each indicate a value on their face which is kept hidden from the other players. Collected data tokens can be saved by a player by returning to his start tile. A player can also leave firewall and virus tokens on a tile which will slow other players down. In addition to finding data on revealed tiles, players can also find antivirus tokens and firewall access tokens which will allow them to move to a tile containing a virus or firewall. If two players' pawns are on the same tile one of them may initiate a hack attack, which involves rolling dice. The victor may steal a data token from the loser.

The game continues for 11-15 rounds after which the player who has saved the most data is the winner (in "Training Operation" mode).

In "Special Operation" mode the rogue agent must prevent the other agents from retrieving all of the data tokens before time runs out.
Orleans: Neue Ortskarten N°3 ("Place Tiles N°3") is a mini expansion for Orléans that consists of three new place tiles: Port, Quarry, and Workshop (Hafen, Steinbruch, und Werkstatt).

A small expansion for Peloponnes, where every player starts with an extra token. This token is worth points at the end of the game, or can be used once during the game to acquire a tile that would otherwise be discarded.

This DIY kit was handed out at Essen Spiel 2015.  The bagged kit consists of a German/English/French rules sheet and dual-sided printed card that contains the 8 Victoria Chits that must be cut out by the owner.

Each player owns six pieces of heights 1, 2 or 3. The objective of the game is to move the pieces thus the pieces of same height are in orthogonal adjacency. A single piece may move exactly as many steps in any direction as it is high. As soon as a piece is adjacent to an other piece it loses its own movement but may only move as far as one of the neighbours induces by its height.

boardgame: World of Tanks: Rush 3 – Last Stand
description:
Last Stand is the second expansion for the popular card game World of Tanks: Rush, based on the online-hit World of Tanks.

Tank battles take a new turn — Last Stand adds Japanese vehicles, new achievements and special rules, special universal tanks, destruction cards to weaken the decks, and much more.

Adapt to new standards of war to defeat your opponents!

Russian description:
«Последний бой» — второе дополнение к популярной настольной карточной
игре World of Tanks: Rush, разработанной по мотивам онлайн-хита World
of Tanks.

Танковые сражения принимают новый оборот — «Последний бой» добавляет в игру бронетехнику Японии, новые достижения и варианты правил, особенные универсальные танки, карты разрушений, которые ослабляют колоды, и многое другое.

Адаптируйтесь к новым стандартам ведения войны, чтобы одержать победу над соперниками!

One part hot potato, one part math — Math Dice Chase tests your ability to quickly do mental math. Game players must sit in a circle. Two players each roll a pair of dice, then multiply the two numbers shown on the dice. Hurry to roll the dice, call out your answer, and pass the dice before you get caught with a second pair in front of you! Once you get caught with two pairs of dice, you are eliminated. The final two players enter a modified round of play, and the person who can multiply the quickest wins.

Stratos Spheres is a the classic four-in-a-row game without the restrictions of the 2D board. In fact, there's no board at all because you build the game as you go. Using three-dimensional spheres, each player tries to connect four of their spheres in a row while stopping their competitor from doing the same. The first to connect four spheres horizontally, vertically, or diagonally wins.

The players move from earth to the moon and back. The first player back on earth wins the game. Movement is determined by 3 dice. The player may chose the result of one of the 3 dice to move his piece. After each players turn, the turntable is rotated one step, changing the available flight paths. The players may block each others flight manoevres by placing stop-counters on the flight control panel of their opponents.

A traditional style quiz game that features a Canadian theme and optional added elements of risk and wagering.

In the 'basic' game, players earn points by either answering the trivia questions posed by their opponents, or by their own questions being incorrectly answered twice.

In the advanced version, players are allowed to wager on whether opponents will correctly or incorrectly answer the question being asked.

A 3rd 'classroom' variation allows for group play by eliminating all advanced elements and simply posing questions to each team, with incorrect answers allowing the other team to 'steal' the point.

Play continues over as many ~5 minute rounds as players pre-determine, with the player or team with the most points being the victor.

Interestingly, this game was self-published sometime between 1980-1984 (based on question content) and includes no copyright date, publisher, or author information, other than a post-office box in Ottawa.  A unique piece of Canadiana.

From the box: A quiz game exclusively for Canadians.
Thousands of Questions. Over 100 Categories.

Encompassing an "all-Canadian" theme, the questions cover a wide variety of topics. From PRIME MINISTERS to LAKES, from HOCKEY to EARLY DAYS, from INVENTIONS to MOVIES, there are 101 topics touching on all aspects of Canadian life. The questions are presented at three levels of difficulty with a corresponding point system. Players will find some questions easy, some "memory-testers", others educational.

Other Features of CANADA QUEST include:
-three variations
-second chance on each question
-time control
-wagering
-classroom use.

For 3-6 players / teams, Ages 12-adult. English.

The Classic Video Arcade Owners Game.

The classic video arcade in the golden age of gaming! Experience the lights, sounds & fun of the greatest arcade games on show and own them all! With players playing, buying & repairing cabinets the action heats up fast! Look out for power cuts, free plays, breakdowns and remember to empty your cabinets of all those shiny coins!

Overview:
Arcade-opoly is a 1(solo) to 4 player game where players dash around a classic video game arcade.

The object of the game is to buy and setup arcade games and begin to earn quarters from them. Your opponents will have to insert coins to play your arcade games should they stop in front them. Popular games will earn you double but don’t let your games break as “Out Of Order” games will earn you nothing.

Players move around the arcade clockwise by first throwing both a black and a white die. You may only move with either dice, choose wisely which of the dice to move with!

Gameplay cards collected by landing on the Gameplay spaces can help or hinder you. Some cards will offer free games, free plays or free repairs. Other cards though will trigger power cuts, force you to play an opponents game, move with only with one die or even send you back to Level 1.

To win the game you must be the first to collect enough coins from your arcade games coin box and reach the target money amount of 70 quarters. Be careful not to play or buy too many games because other costs may arise such as repair bills, loose change, power cuts and game crashes. Should a player lose all their startup money of 30 quarters the game ends. All the remaining players can then total up any coins left in their games with the highest being declared the winner.

Unique features:
Classic video game arcade theme.
2 dice choice mechanic
Money track scoring
16 colour wooden arcade cabinets
50 plastic coin Quarters to play & collect from the machines
36 gameplay cards
Power cuts, Free Plays, Free Repair and “Out Of Order” events
“Top Games” that earn double their price.
45 minute playtime.
3 lives per player for extended play.
Solo play variant as you compete against three CPU players.

boardgame: Tesla vs. Edison: War of Currents – Bonus Luminaries
description: Set of two bonus cards for Tesla vs. Edison, Ada Lovelace and Michael Faraday. These cards are used in Phase 1. Ada Lovelace has 2 Inventions, 1 Manufacturing, and 1 Propaganda. Michael Faraday has 3 Inventions and 1 Propaganda.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Wrath of the Righteous – "Improvised Dinosaur" Promo Card
description:

Dinosaur
Bludgeoning
2-Handed
Mythic

Check to Acquire:
Strength / Survival 18

Powers:
For your combat check, bury this card to use your Strength skill + 1d20. You may additionally expend any number of mythic charges; for each charge expended, add 1d20. Discard your hand and end your turn.

After playing this card, you may succeed at a Strength or Survival 20 check to recharge this card instead of burying it.

"Throwing a dinosaur is one way to stop a fight-or to start one!"
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

description:
Human beings are at the edge of existence — but they don't know it yet! Mother nature is about to strike back and reduce mankind to save the planet from being destroyed by the species that calls itself "homo sapiens".

In Viroid, players cooperate in taking the role of a virus and trying to infect people all over the world. They use events like the Olympic Games to spread their deadly effects. To be a step ahead of the human scientists, the players develop special features like infection by blood, infection via animals, or resistance against medicine, heat or cold.

The game is played in clockwise order. On your turn, draw an event card, which forces you to take back one or more of your tokens from the board (healing a city). If it is the last one in this city, the "healing token" on the specific track moves forward and you lose if it meets your marker on that track. The event card also gives you some additional "DNS points", which are akin to money. In the second step of your turn, you spread your virus depending only the methods for infection you have already developed. You gain additional DNS-points for each infection, such as placing new tokens onto the board in the cities. Third, you develop (buy for DNS) more features for your virus like infection via animal, resistance against cold or medicine, or your ability to kill people. Finally, you throw dice according to your skill for killing, and depending on the result, you replace your tokens on the board with dead tokens.

The players win if they have placed all dead tokens in the cities. They lose if the draw pile runs out or the human beings have made enough healing steps on the specific track.

Will you be able to save the planet and reduce the population of the greatest danger for the world — human beings?
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Pot 'O' Gold is themed after playing a slot machine, but is a dexterity game where players take turns spinning gold coloured plastic chips onto the game board. On the game board, there are cherries, oranges, plums, bells, and 7's, separated by pots of gold. If the chip lands on a pot, then the chip is added to the "jackpot". If the chip lands on one of the other symbols, players mark off that symbol on their score pad.  After marking off any of the symbols for the fifth time, a player wins the current jackpot, and then that symbol will no longer win a jackpot for any player.  The game ends when a jackpot is won for each of the five symbols, with the player with the most chips being the winner.

There is a unique game board design in Pot'O'Gold, where the board is actually 5 giant jigsaw pieces.  This does not effect game play, is simply an alternative to having a game board that folds for storage.

In the 1990's, Video Lottery Terminals, or VLTs, became legal in the Canadian province of Alberta, and most bars and lounges had them installed.  Different games could be played on the VLTs, including a five wheeled slot machine where you won by matching up cherries, oranges, plums, bells and 7s on a line.  Pot'O'Gold is themed after the slot machines on those VLTs.

Two players battle it out in the attempt to knock out their opponent. If you decrease your opponent's health to zero, you win.

Players manage a dojo of skills using Experience Points to build a deck of attack and defense cards that can adapt to an opponent’s evolving strategy.
 The linear mat component allows players to control distance to avoid damage and get in range to strike.

Stuns and takedowns break an opponent’s rhythm leaving him/her without enough stamina to pull off brutal combos.

Holds can create an opening for attack and often work in combination with other moves creating another level of strategy.

'You Don't Say' is a word game played over multiple rounds, where players attempt to guess a specific word based on the verbal clues provided. 
Players are first divided into 2-4 teams of at least 2 people, who are either the 'Clue Givers' or 'Guessers', which may change between turns.

As the 'guessers' attempt to determine the mystery word, the clue givers are restricted by 3 simple rules:
-Cannot spell all or part of the word
-Cannot use the word itself, in any form
-Cannot use 'rhymes with' or 'sounds like' clues.

Each turn lasts the length of the included timer (about 1 minute) and so multiple word clues may be attempted if teams talk and answer fast.
Teams progress around the board based on the number of correctly guessed words each turn, plus a roll of the random 'bonus die'.

The first team to reach the 'FINISH' space on the gameboard is the winner!

From the Box:
The Talk FAST, guess FASTER game.

Talk about it. Act it out...But DON'T SAY IT!

You Don't Say is the hilarious, high-energy guessing game where fast talkers team with fast guessers to win!

Get your teammates to guess words based on your clues, without saying the words. The more they answer correctly, the father you move on the board.

With six world-encompassing categories and 3000 simple-to-challenging words, YOU DON'T SAY is fast-talking, quick-moving fun for everyone! 500 Cards! 3000 Words!

boardgame: Pocket Dungeon Quest: Don't Go Alone Expansion
description:
Don't Go Alone, the first expansion for Pocket Dungeon Quest, adds new concepts to the core gameplay that gives additional strategic choices to the Heroes. It also introduces campaigns and equipment upgrades that carry over through many sessions of play.

Gear is the highlight of the Don't Go Alone expansion. Gear in PDQ is pieces of equipment that the Hero pulls out of the Treasure Pile as a reward for Leveling Up. There are many different types of Gear which add loads of new skills and abilities to the Heroes as they adventure through the dungeon.

Gear is earned by killing Monsters and Leveling Up and finding keys on the Dungeon Floor. Hardcore players can randomize which pieces of Gear they equip. Otherwise you get to choose which combination of Gear best fits with your style of play. Mix and match the Gear for interesting effects and abilities, and most of all, HAVE FUN!

boardgame: Empires: Age of Discovery – Denmark Player Board and White Figures
description:
Optional player board for Denmark with special national power and white player figures.

Denmark
Fur Trade: Starts the game with one Fur and one Fish Trade Good. At any time, the Danish player may secretly look at the Discovery counters located in New France and New England.

Ottoman Empire
Holy Warriors: When sending Soldiers out using the Colonist Dock, they may be treated like Missionaries (i.e., +1 Colonist in region).

Prussia
Professional Military: Once per Age, when resolving one Battle during the Warfare event, each attacking Prussian Soldier in the selected region eliminates 2 enemy units (maximum 1 enemy Soldier may be eliminated per friendly Soldier).

This Next War supplement will contain the following items:

•Cyber Warfare Capabilities
•Alternate Advanced Air Game
•Submarines
