            SPECIAL***
        
    


This entire supplement will be available for free download via the Next War Series Support Site, including PDF files for the required counters (which will be DIY).

Daggers is an asymmetrical abstract board game played on an 8x8 board.

As described by João Pedro Neto on The World of Abstract Board Games:

UPHILL & DOWNHILL - "Downhill" refers to the direction towards the "Low player", and "Uphill" refers to the direction towards the "High player".
DAGGER - Daggers slide along the column or diagonals downhill as far as they like, but only one cell uphill or diagonally uphill. There is no side movement. Daggers can capture any piece along the diagonals (going either uphill or downhill) and can capture Crowns (but not Daggers) along the column.
CROWNS - A Crown can move to any (orthogonal or diagonal) adjacent empty cell. A Crown can also capture any enemy piece on any (orthogonal or diagonal) adjacent cell. 
TURN - On each turn, each player moves one of his pieces. The High player moves first.
GOAL - A player wins by capturing an enemy Crown or by moving one of his own Crowns into the opponent's first row.

PitRush is an action-packed game of skill in which players try to capture the pits of the game board with their disks in order to earn the most points. Players take shots by flicking their disk with their finger. Opponent disks can be struck off, and pits can be taken over by stopping on top of the disk in the pit. Players score points based on the distance of the hole they occupy (or cover) from their shooting location on the edge of the board.

A strategy card game about well intended people in bitter competition over exactly how to save the planet from climate change...and how to serve their own special interests while doing so.

Take on the role of a special interest group trying to save the world from a changing climate. However, while all the interest groups want to maximize positive impact, they all have different priorities for how to go about it. Are you advocating clean energy, habitat protection, or island nations that might get flooded? Will you pass laws to create subsidies, institute a carbon tax, or maybe attempt an ambitious geo-engineering project? Every method has its own benefits and drawbacks, and you will need strategic planning and political cunning to lead your organization to success.

Mechanics:
Players attempt to pass bills into law, both to have an impact in general and to further their own special interest's goals. However, all players will need to pursue a mixed strategy to succeed -- your special interest does not dictate your strategy. For example, a player might be rewarded for seeing enough geo-engineer projects succeed, regardless of who plays those cards -- so they might play those cards themselves or negotiate for someone else to play them.

Carbon Copy Politics uses a novel "clock" mechanic to track the progress of activities over time. Players play cards onto the clock, which ticks down each turn. Cards that reach the end of the clock resolve, building variable delays into the play of every card. The delay requires players to plan ahead, allow them to set up combos, and creates opportunities for disruption by opponents. For example, bill cards are typically delayed by 3 turns before they are evaluated, so you might play it one turn, play an action to boost public will-for-change the next turn, play a modifier on it the next turn, and then discard cards as the bill resolves to pay the rest of the cost.

Bill cards are rated by their difficulty (cost) and impact (score). Different types of bills offer different ways to pay the cost. Carbon Tax bills get easier to pass as more taxes are passes, while Cap and Trade bills are easier if several are played at the same time by different players.

boardgame: Monopoly: Copa Mundial de la Fifa Brasil 2014
description:
Esta nueva versión del clásico Monopoly incluye dentro de su tablero a 22 de las 32 selecciones que participaron en la Copa del Mundo: Brasil, Colombia, España, Argentina, Alemania, Italia, Holanda, Portugal, Uruguay, Francia, Chile, Inglaterra, Estados Unidos, Rusia, Nigeria, Japón, México, Ecuador, Camerún, Corea del Sur, Costa Rica, y Honduras.
Monopoly Copa Mundial de la FIFA Brasil 2014 incluye: Tablero con las 22 selecciones, 6 fichas con diseños alusivos al mundial, 28 tarjetas de título de propiedad, 16 tarjetas fuleco y 16 tarjetas trofeo, 1 paquete de dinero, 32 casas, 12 hoteles y 2 dados.



Version of Monopoly themed on the 2014 Fifa World Cup, including competing nations in place of the traditional coloured property sets, stadia in place of the stations and half time/full time cards instead of the chance/community chest cards.

Alchemy of Askrias is a casual board game for 1 to 3 players (1 to 5 players with the added 4 to 5 player expansion). The game uses 'Askrias Cubes' which allow the players to socket game tiles, each of which have different abilities on them; which then allows the players to either create the victory points for the win or powerful spells to interfere with your opposing Alchemist's!

The game is focused mainly on actual dice building - each turn players will have the chance to add tiles to a 'Askrias Cube' depending on the formula that they choose which will then alter the way the game is played.

With extra cubes, the game can also be expanded up to 5 players.

boardgame: Colt Express: The Time Travel Car
description:
The Time Travel Car can be used with or without the first expansion: Horses & Stagecoach.
The Time Travel Car is placed in front of the Locomotive.
The Time Travel Car is considered as adjacent to the roof and the inside of the Locomotive. So your Bandit can enter it thanks to a Move action or a Horse action if you play using the Horses and Stagecoach expansion.

Several Bandits can go in the Time Travel Car at the same time, but in order to trigger the action, your Bandit has to be alone in it at the end of a round (after applying the event, if the current round has one).
If this condition is met, all of the other players close their eyes. In the meantime, you must take your Bandit pawn and hide it below the Locomotive; below one of the Cars; or below the Stagecoach (if you are playing with the expansion). Then, the next Schemin’ phase is played and none of the other players know where your Bandit is. The Bandit’s location is disclosed at the beginning of the Stealin’ phase and the pawn is placed inside the Car where it was hiding. Then all of the actions are resolved.
At the end of the game, after the event is resolved, if one Bandit is alone inside the Time Travel Car, the player receives $500 and all of the loot that may be contained inside.

boardgame: The Twinkles Game: His Trip to the Star Factory
description:
The Twinkles Game: His Trip To The Star Factory. Based on the General Mills Twinkles The Star Shaped Cereal with the Twinkles the Elephant mascot.

The players choose to be one of four characters: Fulton the Camel, Wilbur the Monkey, Leopold the Lion or Sanford the Parrot. The players begin on the Start square, and the Twinkles playing piece starts several squares ahead. Players draw cards that have colors on them and move to the next square with that color on the board. Players must draw a card for Twinkles and move him on his turn, as well. Players cannot pass Twinkles, even if that means they cannot move. Certain squares award the players stars, and others take stars away (if they have any). When Twinkles finally makes it to the Star Factory, the game is over. The player who has the most stars, wins.

The cruel seamonster has taken a baby dino captive on the small island, guarding the waters around the island voraciously. The dinosaurs walk around the board, collecting the watersteps needed to get out to the island and rescue baby dino. On the way you can meet both the sea monster that steals watersteps and the pterodactyl that can fly you to a location of your choice. If you meet another dinosaur you can challenge it for watersteps. The winner is the player that reaches the island first and and saves babydino.

You start with placing the tiles on the board backside up, then take turns moving along the lines collecting waterstep tiles needed to save the baby dino, stone tiles are useless, landsteps are needed to cross certain sections.

boardgame: Isle of Skye: From Chieftain to King – Brettspiel Adventskalender 2015 Promo Tiles
description:
Contained in Brettspiel Adventskalender 2015 for Day 22.

Comprises two new scoring window tiles: 3 points per horizontal line of at least 3 contiguous landscape tiles and score any one scroll in your territory (if the scroll is in a completed landscape area, score double points for it as normal).

The box description (in Polish):
Giełda, makler, akcje i obligacje... Te i inne ekonomiczne terminy weszły już na trwałe do naszego języka i życia. Proponujemy grę, która wprowadzi nas w świat giełdy, oswoi z prawami rządzącymi rynkiem i finansami. Grę dla wszystkich, bo wszyscy odczuwamy skutki prawdziwej gry giełdowej.

Monopoly-like game about stock exchange. Buying and selling stocks, obligations, etc. to earn more money than other players.

The tracks on the board are two circles, touching on one edge.

boardgame: 7 Wonders Duel: The Messe Promo Card
description:
The Messe Essen Convention Center is a Wonder for the 7 Wonders: Duel game. The completed Messe is worth 2 points at the end of the game.
It also allows the player, in the moment when it's built, to take one card from the topmost row of the drafting structure (the line lying directly on the table), even if the card is still covered, and build it in their own city for free - only paying the price of the wonder.

Ophidian Wars Hallowed Arenas is a FREE print-to-play Expansion Set for Ophidian Wars Tabletop Game, but this release also features a bonus of two brand new Gladiator cards for Ophidian 2350 CCG/Ophidian 2360 card games. Meaning that above you will find:

9 cards (2 Gladiators + 4 Maneuvers + 3 Arsenal Items), fully compatible with Small Cave Games’ Ophidian Wars and Dark Rooks.
2 cards, fully compatible with Ophidian 2350 CCG and Hack and Slash Games’ Ophidian 2360.

Now you also can get your printed cards, as starting today they are available to buy. And as a bonus, you will get exclusive to the printed version 2 game-counters cards to track your respective "Adrenaline" and "Cheer".

boardgame: The Big Bang Theory: Fact or Fiction Card Game
description:
This game is a small card game with 180 trivia cards in a little tin. It is small enough to fit in a small bag.

Roll the die to get a Fact or Fiction card. Answer a card correctly and you win that card. Be the first person to collect 5 cards and you win the game.
family: TV Shows: The Big Bang Theory

boardgame: Generalship: From the 4th Century B.C. to the 19th Century
description:
Generalship is a compact strategy game and the players can experience the history of Europe.

You will compete using 49 highly famed generals in Europe from the 4th century B.C. to the 19th century. You choose a nation to rule: Austria, Byzantium, England, or France, and then appoint a general from the 49 ones available as your commander. Using this, you will run your nation internally by accumulating political power, increasing production and population, and developing technologies. You will handle your external politics by raising armies and managing alliance relationships.
 
While shaping history with other players, based on your efficient use of actions, the abilities of your commander, and a little bit of luck, you will be able to win by earning more victory points than your competitors. Specifically, players can earn victory points by winning battles or developing new technologies.

The Others can be played by 2 to 5 players. One player must pick the role of the Sins Player, while the rest are Hero Players. The Hero Players all cooperate with each other, with the Sins Player playing against them all.

The Apocalypse expansion offers players a whole new play mode, only for the very brave (or very crazy), including the huge and frighteningly detailed figures of War, Pestilence, Famine, Death, and Apocalypse itself.

This expansion introduces a brand new Apocalypse Track to the game. On this track, instead of drawing new Apocalypse cards as the track advances, new Avatars of the Apocalypse are  brought onto the board, eventually culminating with the appearance of Apocalypse itself.

boardgame: The Others: 7 Sins – Gamma Team Expansion
description:
The Gamma Team expansion consists of 5 heroes, including a Leader, a Bruiser, a Sniper, and two Fixers.

Gamma Team brings 12 more Upgrade cards to shuffle into your inventory deck, adding more variety and different strategies for the heroes. There are 6 different cards, with 2 copies each, featuring things like Inner Peace training which can be sacrificed to reset your Corruption, and a Bionic Arm that grants +1 dice to both Fight and Skill rolls.

This expansion also gives heroes 3 extra Hero Dice for their powered-up rolls.

It includes as well 5 new map tiles.

boardgame: The Others: 7 Sins – Delta Team Expansion
description:
The Delta Team expansion consists of 5 heroes, including a Leader, a Bruiser, a Sniper, and two Fixers.

Delta Team brings another 12 Upgrade cards to add into your inventory deck, giving your heroes new options to face the Others. There are 6 different cards, with 2 copies each, featuring equipment such as Wengsi Wong's amazing Laz Blades which crave for the bigger baddies, IR Goggles that are perfect to make sure your Ranged attacks hit their target, and some disposable Liquid Courage that can heal all your wounds.

This expansion also gives players 3 extra Hero Dice.

The Delta Team expansion includes 5 brand new tiles set in the Old Town part of Haven, adding even more variety to the map setups you can use in your games. The box will come with a leaflet featuring maps for all 7 core box stories using these new tiles.

boardgame: The Others: 7 Sins – Sons of Ragnarok Expansion
description:
The Sons of Ragnarok are a notorious outlaw biker gang, that could very nearly have become Acolytes of Sin themselves. However, their total rejection for following orders made any approach to try and conscript them to aid the sins result in a very dead servant of sin. This biker gang is obsessed with Viking mythology, with members assuming nicknames in reference to the Norse Gods. The realization that the Apocalypse (or Ragnarok, as they prefer to call it) was upon them stirred the gang into a battle frenzy! However, their contempt for authority also means they would never agree to join FAITH. Though that doesn't stop them from carrying on the fight against the Others on their own terms!

The Sons of Ragnarok Expansion box contains a full team of 7 heroes, including a Leader, 2 Fixers, 2 Snipers, and 2 Bruisers. These rebels tend to operate outside of FAITH, but they can be mixed and matched with any FAITH team as your group may choose. This team follows the same rules as any FAITH group, though they were designed with special synergy among themselves.

description: The Men of FAITH box gives you 5 new Heroes to use in your FAITH team, including their miniatures and Hero dashboards. This set contains a Leader, a Fixer, a Sniper, and two Bruisers!

The Gluttony Expansion box comes with Avatar, Controller, 6 Abominations, Sin board and 12 sin cards.

The Others: 7 Sins is a horror board game for 2 to 5 players. One player controls one of the 7 Deadly Sins, employing monstrous incarnations of these corruptive forces in an attempt to consume the world and bring about the apocalypse. The rest of the players cooperate as heroes of the F.A.I.T.H. organization who will attempt to fight back the invasion!

The Sins player, on the other hand, has access to the different monsters of each Sin, including Abominations, a Controller, and the terrifying Avatar of Sin! These monsters can attempt to destroy the heroes, or simply hinder their progress on their missions.

Each Sin provides the Sins Player with a different deck of Sins cards that can be played at different times to surprise players with different effects, often tied to the strategy of each Sin. Yes, because each Sin taints the game with an overarching mechanic that is always in effect.

description:
Help your cat to undress so that he can go to sleep.

Deal five pajama tops and bottoms to each player. Stack your pajamas on your cat. Place BJ the Cat on the game board. Roll the die and move BJ the Cat. If your pajamas match in color or pattern, you can take them off. The winner is the first person to get all of the pajamas off of their cat.

Not the same game as Cat's Pajamas:


    The IDEAlogists game is a card game, where you collect sets of sleeping kitties. 
    The Haywire/Tactic game does not have any cards, and you don't collect sleeping kitties but you help your cat to undress so that he can go to sleep.

Each player has a set of 3 pawns that start on the starting spaces of his color. The goal is to move the pawns around the board along the spiral to the "home" section. Movement is controlled by a die. All players move around the same board, so they may capture each other's pawns. Captured pawns are returned to their player's starting place and must start their journey over. The winner is the first player to move all pawns "home".

On the back of the game board is the game Nine Men's Morris (Mühle).

Squish is a fun, family friendly, dexterity game for 1 to 6 players in which you drop player order cards (also referred to as boot cards) in an attempt to squish bug cards that are on the ground. You try to be the player with 10+ points at the end of a round. The bug cards are thrown into the air at the beginning of the game and fall where they may. After, you are ready to get Squishing!  You go in number order according to your player order cards. You drop your player order cards from shoulder height, number side up, in an attempt to completely cover a bug. Bugs range in size and value. The bigger the bug, the more points it is worth. Beware though, because if your player order card lands boot side up someone can cover your number and take one of your squished bugs from you. You squish the bug and your friend steps on your toes and takes it from you. This could turn a win into a loss or a loss into a win.  Getting 10 points is easy, keeping them is tough.  Some dropping techniques can make it so that your card is boot side down and keeps you safe from anyone's nefarious plans of stepping on your toes.

A variant for younger crowds makes the game a bit easier by the requirement to cover a bug goes from, covering the entire bug, to covering just the head or body.

Squish: The Dexterity Card Game of Bug Squishery is a fun and easy game for kids of all ages and plays in only 30 minutes.

User Summary:
This is a competitive Sugoroku (Japanese type of Parcheesi) game in which players move 4 of their own pieces from their side to their opponent's side. JumPico is characterized by the fact that players advance their pieces while blocking the movement of their opponent's pieces, as in Backgammon. The first player to move 4 of his pieces to his opponent's side wins the game. Passing is prohibited, so a player with no legal moves automatically loses. Blank pieces can always be used with no risk as they always move one space and are thus key to creating a winning strategy.

Publisher's summary:
４個の持ち駒を自陣から敵陣まで移動させる対戦型すごろくゲームです。
バックギャモンのように自分の駒で敵の移動を妨害しながら交差して駒を進
めるのが特徴です。駒の数字によって移動できるマス数が決まっています。
早く４個の駒を相手陣地まで移動させれば勝ちですが、パスは禁止なので合
法手が無ければ負けになります。
無地駒は必ず１マス進めるので、これを安全パイに使いながら移動の戦術を
組み立てます。

boardgame: The Others: 7 Sins – Beta Team Expansion
The Others: 7 Sins – Omega Team Expansion
description:
The Omega Team Expansion box includes the Kickstarter Exclusive Stretch Goals:

- Nicolas (Monster)
- The Mayor (Monster): a monster in the service of Sin, and also 3 new Apocalypse cards to bring him into the game.
- Calavera (Leader)
- Adrian (Fixer Hero)
- Baldur (Fixer Hero)
- Rosario (Fixer Hero)
- Nichole (Sniper Hero)
- PHD (Sniper Hero)
- 6 New City Tiles
- Faith Dice Bag
- 7 Sins Dice Bag

The Ultimate Football Strategy Game! The game is a dice and card, turn based game. The object of the game is to score more goals than your opponent in a set amount of time, or to be the first to score a pre-determind number of goals.

Players roll the dice, and move their playing pieces around the board, trying to get close enough to their opponents goal to take a shot. When a player lands with the ball on one of the Super 11 squares in their opponents half, they play an attacking card. These bonus cards can give them an advantage over their opponent through a shot at goal, yellow or red card, or a chance to roll again.

The game ends either after an agreed period of time, or once a player has scored the pre-agreed number of goals.

This is an expansion for the Heroclix miniatures game. These objects cost nothing to use.

Contents:

1 Double-Sided Map: convention center and construction site
4 Object Figures: light post, crate, dumpster and mailbox
3 Double-Sided Scenario Cards
1 Rules Sheet

This is an expansion for the Heroclix miniatures game.

Contents:

1 Double-Sided Map: factory and mansion
4 Object Figures: computer, bookcase, soda machine, and desk
3 Double-Sided Scenario Cards
1 Rules Sheet

The first Heroclix figure with the ability to capture foes, the Sentinel. It comes equipped with a set of closed fists and a second set of hands that holds captured figures.

You need Marvel Heroclix Starter Set to use the Sentinel in a game.

Contents:

-Big Sentinel figure (17cm tall)
-One set of extra hands for capturing foes
-Instruction sheet

description:
The object of the game is to become a successful Pop Group Manager, by gaining control of as many Groups as possible, and obtaining the cards for as many Cities marked on the board as you can. The ultimate success is to become a "Millionaire".

Imagine you are the manager of your favourite "Pop" group and that they are giving performances in the various cities where your counter lands.

Money is made from Group performance fees and 'Hire of Hall' charges, received when a player has the exclusive performance rights for a particular city. Fees are doubled if the rights for all four cities in a country are held.

Based on The New Adventures of Huckleberry Finn TV show produced by Hanna-Barbera and based on the classic Mark Twain characters. It featured live-action heroes in an animated world.

Comes with 60 paint strips and Injun Joe spinner. Players take turns spinning and collecting paint strips. Players lose their turn if they pick Injun Joe or a messy paint strip. The first player to "paint" his fence with 15 correct paint strips wins.

TINY GARDEN GROWERS

Game Summary
Tiny Garden Growers is a game about growing vegetable plants and harvesting the vegetables.
This is a dice rolling and placement game that also uses dexterity and counting skills.

Number of players: 2-3
Components: 1 game board and 50 dice (30 green or white, 10 red, 10 yellow) and rules!
Playing time: 10 to 15 minutes

Tiny Token Toss

Tiny Token Toss is an 18-card - 1 or 2-player Dexterity chit tossing game. "I may be starting a trend with these games!"

It uses the same chit tossing mechanic from castle under siege but in a nice and simple point scoring game.

Tiny Token Toss uses 6 cards in front of each player "a seventh card can be added to the back row" with each card having a circle with point values in it. Each player takes turns tossing chits at the cards to try and obtain the most points or trying to knock their opponents chits off the cards.

The rules are simple and the game is a light filler style game! I calling this new innovative style of game "The chit toss game"! LOL

14 cards make up the scoring cards.
2 cards represent each player's toss card.
2 cards represent the bean bags.

If you think of the beach bean bag tossing game, that's this game in a nut shell!

CASTLE UNDER SIEGE

Castle under Siege is an 18-card - 1 or 2-player Dexterity Medieval Castle Siege game.

Player-1 controls the Catapult and tries to loft "boulder, fireball, and disease" towards a castle. You use one of the cards as an actual catapult; tossing ammunition at the castle.

Player-2 maintains the castle and must use his forces to put out fires, repair boulder damage, and take card of the injured soldiers. To do this you must move around the castle trying to reach and resolve each situation before it gets out of control. All the while trying to stay alive because if a boulder lands in an area where your forces are they are either killed or injured.

You have a limited supply of ammunition and once you run out the game is over. You tally your score to see if you breached the castle wall or tower. Or see how many forces you were able to kill!

The player of the castle wins by preventing a wall or tower breach and trying to save as many soldiers as possible.

Help Mr. Twiddle...Find The Runaway!

Players spin & move around the edge of the game, landing on question marks with different colors and numbers on them. Using a number wheel, players get a clue to the color, character or activity of the runaway. The winner is the first player to identify the runaway.

Die Portale von Molthar: Sonderkarten contains two language independent playing cards: Joker-Perlenkarte and Tischlein deck dich, and 1 German rules card.

Included in the Spielbox Essen Spiel special edition 2015.

This beautiful tableau shows the town Carcassonne and offers a lot of possibilities to put on your tiles at the beginning of your game.

The picture is known from "Der Graf", where it was included as single tiles. (Carcassonne: The Count of Carcassonne)

Join Elefun's newest friend, the trouble-loving Chasin' Cheeky monkey in this ring-tossin' Chasin' Cheeky game. In this up-and-active game, Chasin' Cheeky will race around the room as you try to get your colored rings on his tail. Once you get all of your rings onto his tail, be the first to grab his banana and shake it with Chasin' Cheeky to celebrate your win.

Play again by putting the banana back in Cheeky's mouth.

description:
The Game on Fire is a mini-expansion for The Game: Spiel... so lange du kannst! that consists of six cards (22, 33, 44, 55, 66, 77). When one of these fire cards is played onto a pile, that card must be covered by the end of the next player's turn or else players have lost the game.

This expansion is contained in new editions of the game (Look for a blue note on the box cover). See The Game + The Game on Fire.

In Russia, everyone is responsible to the Motherland to ensure every citizens is a good comrade.

In Names of the Disloyal, you must turn in the names of those who are striving to disrupt the harmony among the people. Roll dice to gather the letters needed to discover the dissidents, use the help of your comrade workers, and find those rumored to be disloyal.

On your turn, you will roll dice, claim cards to your hand and then make names with those cards. The first player to a set number of names triggers the end game.

Game Contents:
162 Letter Cards (Letters of the Revolution)
36 Name Cards (Find the Disloyal)
8 Multi Letter Cards (Workers Unite)
7 Dice each in 4 different colors
12 Scoring Tiles
1 Scorepad

Unitar is a simultanuous-choice 8 round 3 person strategy poker and, hopefully, the Arabic game.

Goal
You win Unitar by dominating the field with your color, in other words the game color with the highest number of frames on the playing field wins.

Strategy
Each turn, every player selects one block of stones that will be moved or rotated. By doing so, blocks may start to overlap. When blocks overlap, actions can be selected to eliminate the overlapping pieces, or to unify the two blocks, which means that the squares they occupy are divided amongst the two colors.

Poker 
You have to guess your opponent's intended moves, because your own intended move will only be performed if it shares two characteristics with the other player's moves.

The first 140-card expansion for the Ophidian 2350 CCG, Rise of Champions continues the storyline for Ophidian and introduces new gladiators, additional level cards for some gladiators from the original set, new effects, new card types, and more! This series is offered in 30-count booster displays.

Unfortunately a printed expansion never got green light as the publisher Fleer/Skybox International has announced that '...based on current market conditions,' it is canceling its Rise of Champions booster for the Ophidian 2350 CCG.

In making the announcement, Fleer Senior VP Sales and Marketing Lloyd Pawlak noted Fleer's inability to get retailer support as the determining factor in ending new release support for the game. 'The strong core support of our players and volunteers and the only 5 star gameplay rating for CCGs in Scrye this year... was not enough to convince more retailers to carry and support the game,' he said.

Fleer Director of Gaming Initiatives David Chase told ICv2 that not all support for the game is ending.'We are still soliciting sales for Ophidian 2350,' he said.'Ophidian still runs Organized Play along with supporting various conventions with product and tournament support.'

And due to the Fan support, Ophidian Inc, the creators/designers of the game, managed to release the expansion as a print and play version.

Ophidian 2350 CCG X-Mas (2004) was the second print and play expansion set for Ophidian 2350 CCG and the first fully created using fan submissions.

The expansion consists in 9 Ophidian Christmas based cards:

1 Level 1-2 Brand New Gladiator Card
1 Level 3-4 Brand New Gladiator Card
5 Brand new Minions Cards
1 Quick-Hit Card
1 Pump Card

boardgame: Wave of Fictions (fan expansion for Ophidian 2350)
description: Wave of Fictions is a 140-card expansion set plus 3 brand new promo cards.The Wave of Fictions set was the first and last Ophidian 2350 CCG set to be made with a mix of official Ophidian 2350 CCG cards with Fan Submitted card making a unique set that expanded the Ophidian 2350 CCG Universe in a way never made before in the card game industry.

boardgame: The OutWorlds Revelations (fan expansion to Ophidian 2350)
description:
The OutWorld Revelations is a 140-card expansion set and has as his main theme some characters from the OutWorlds. The set is based in the Fallen from Grace Fan Fiction, the Grakkan's Union & Grakkan’s Union Revised Fan Fiction and various official Storylines that took place in the OutWorlds region. The set also brings more Ophidian League Gladiators that weren’t part of any Fiction, but are showcased on the Ophidian League Bios on the Main site.

The OutWorld Revelations also features 2 New Card Types:

Managers
 This card type works like the Reinforcements, but they will have 0VP, and may be played at the beginning of the first wave. The Managers have Life, Rage & ADMG values, but they mostly do not interfere in the Wave. Their special abilities may change the nº of cards in the hand, the nº of CP, and other skills, like giving some additional abilities for the Team of the manager (for Teams see above).

Sponsors Cards

You may play 1 Sponsor at the start of the game, after playing your Gladiators. This card is not part of your deck, and cannot be discarded, nor placed out of play. Some sponsors will override the standard rules of CP, VP, Cheer, Hand Sizes, Bonus Cards, and Victory Conditions.

And a brand new Mechanism:

Team affiliation

Some Gladiators cards will include a new keyword that will indicate that they belong to a Gladiator Team. Example:  The Gladiators Freakshow, Striking Dragon, Ryla & PK have the keyword indicating that they belong to the Team Machine, as seem in The Saga showcased in the Main site.

The Ophidian Legends is a 25-card expansion special mini-set and has as the main theme the Legendary Ophidian League Gladiators. And there’s more, much more as we also showcase in a 5 cards subset focusing the most brutal war that happened in the Ophidian Universe, the Demon Wars!!!

And as if that is not enough the set also brings back the Reindeer's Minions that were seen last year in the Holiday 2004 set, after all we’re on Christmas Season, and although we don’t have the return of Arkon Klaus, this set also includes a 5 cards subset with many cards for him (or any other Gladiator) to use.

The expansion also features two brand new mechanisms:

Legendary Cards

This Set include Gladiators, Minions and Pumps with a  keyword that indicate that they are Legendary Characters, this keyword is currently seen in two Gladiators cards Arkon Klaus (from Holiday 2004 set) & Pestilence (from WOF set). The Legendary Characters are Ophidian League Gladiators, they may be included in any Team, and they act like any regular Gladiator. When a Legendary Gladiator is destroyed he is placed in the Funeral Pyre, and your opponent’s gains the double of the VP of the Legendary Gladiator. The Legendary cards are also unique cards, and even though they don’t have the  keyword written on them, they automatically count as unique cards.

Minions that act like Gladiator Cards

This Set include Minions with the following text “CARD NAME counts as a XVP {DISCIPLINE}{DISCIPLINE} Gladiator”. Currently only one Minion cards has this Mechanism, Doombringer (from The OutWorlds Revelations set). The Minion can use Pumps that apply for the stated Disciplines and VP, but for all the purposes the card is still a Minion card. It also mean that when the Minion is destroyed he also is Placed in the Funeral Pyre alongside the Gladiators cards, and as that the
opponent will gain the VP mentioned on the Minion card. If the Minion card has also the Legend  keyword, the VP that the opponent will gain when the minion is destroyed is determinate by the Legendary Cards Mechanism.

Posted with permission of John Yianni, designer of Hive.


Hive Colony is a collection of unofficial expansions for Hive designed by Stephen Taylor. It includes Hive: Original, Venom, Swarm, Predators, Guardians and Honeycombo.

Hive: Venom – Includes all the original pieces in addition to some new creatures on the reverse sides. You must decide which face up to place the pieces depending on the current state of the board and your future plans. You can flip the pieces but this will cost you a tempo. The new pieces are designed not to over power the Originals be rather to compliment them and present the player with more strategic choices.

Hive: Swarm – A mode of play which adds in a little bit of randomness to Hive and challenges the player to make the best use of the hand they are dealt. It can be played with Hive Original or any of the expansions and even allows you to place your opponent's pieces!

Hive: Predators – Introduces some powerful pieces with killer moves which can permanently remove an opponent's creatures from the Hive. It also introduces the alternative victory condition of removing your opponent's Queen Bee which encourages even more interesting ways to play and the greater possibility to come back from a losing position.

Hive: Guardians – Dominate the top of the Hive with these high flying creatures. You now have to fully surround the Queen Bee so even the top must be covered to win. They can also be very useful in neutralising some of the new expansions when playing with all the expansions together.

Hive: Honeycombo – This piece is shared by both players which allows you sacrifice your current turn in order to then play two moves in your next turn. This opens up many new possibilities such as being able to place and move a piece without it getting pinned, or moving two pieces in to surround the Queen without your opponent being able to move one away with the Pillbug.

Please see attached files for the rules and print and play stickers.

boardgame: The Little Big Game of Lexis Hold'em
description:
The Little Big Game of Lexis Hold'em transforms Texan Hold'em into a word game. Instead of forming straights, flushes or pairs, players spell words. The game ends when only one player has chips remaining. That person is the winner.

Each player receives an equal number of betting chips. The dealer deals 2 "hole" cards to each player and 5 "community" cards face down. Players must place a chip into the pot to play in the hand.

The Pre-Flop (Round One): After looking at their own cards, the players can check or bet. The following players can call (equal the last bet placed in the pot), raise (increase the last bet placed in the pot), or fold (sit out the hand and lose all bets placed so far).

The Flop (Round Two): The dealer turns over the first 3  "community" cards.

The Turn Card (Round Three): The dealer turns over the 4th "community" card.

The River (Round Four): The dealer turns over the last "community" card.

After each round players use their own cards and the community cards to form a word and a round of betting occurs. After the last round players secretly write down their words before betting.

The Showdown: After all betting is done, players who have not folded show their cards. The player that forms the word worth the most points takes all the chips in the pot.

description: This thrilling Pulp Alley expansion introduces two new character types (Gangs and Associates), ten new perks, over two dozen new abilities, and rules for Bystanders. Vice Alley also includes a thrilling multi-chapter campaign designed to play just like the old pulp magazine and serial adventures.

As well as introducing some new rules, our Pulp Leagues book is also a compilation of all our Pulp Alley character types, perks, abilities, and resources previously published in our rulebook and expansions. In addition, Pulp Leagues includes tons of fun optional rules for playing all kinds of pulp genres — including sci-fi, fantasy, old west, horror, swashbuckling and more!

Biff Baff is the crazy Drumming-Game which will unleash your inner musician. With 13 fun and yet challenging gaming options each player can test his or her sense of rhythm. Just let loose and beat the drums along with up to three other players while discovering your own musical potential. You don’t even require a conductor; the integrated rotary disk will lead you through the different rhythm patterns which you can choose for yourself. Different symbols for the disk ensure diversified rhythms and suspense for your games. The winner of Biff Baff will be the one who can keep the beat going the longest and is thus crowned the new drumming king. The whole game is made of wood which ensures stability and persistency. Playing the game will improve your sense of rhythm and helps building social skills for children starting at the age of three.

Game Explanation.
The gaming board is divided into four colored sections which gives up to four players the opportunity to drum at the same time. Every section is imprinted with one blue and two red hand symbols which mark the two sounds (bass, snare). Additionally there is a white arrow which points towards the middle to the rotary disk. This disk fulfills the position of the conductor during the game. Up to six magnets can be placed on the disk which symbolize different activities like playing the bass drum or snapping one’s fingers. To start the game one spins the disk once it stops the arrow   points to the action which has to be performed.

Biff Baff: Assisting development without anxiety but lots of fun! The game offers over ten types playing options depending on the age of the child which improve the following aspects:

- The importance of community and the role of the individual
- A sense of rhythm and reaction time
- Sharpening the senses: Auditory and visual perception
- eye-hand coordination, emotional sensibility, body-awareness
- Elementary mathematics: calculus, cardinal numbers, comprehension of quantity
- Speech development

"The Eng Chiang" is a board game, that combines a Taiwanese retro game with a common snack food, roasted sausage.It is a very interesting and effective ice-breaker for events and gatherings.
Welcome to the sausage world of Taiwan!

The players put together a sausage with "The Eng Chiang Tao" Card x2 "The Eng Chiang" Card x8.

Starting with the Dealer, each player takes turn clockwise in rolling the dices.

The player who rolls the largest number takes sausage of the player who rolls the smallest number.

When a player could only put together a "mini sausage" (with only eng chiang tao card),the player with the longest sausage wins a Garlic,and the game resets to the next round.

Goal
a. A player who obtains three Garlics wins.
b. A player who obtains the most Garlics within 30 mins wins.

7 promo cards (from the Kickstarter) offered in Essen 2015 with the main game:

User summary:
In the game of マトリンゴ (Matringo), players control three sisters of Matryoshka (Russian dolls) to gather colourful fruits from a tree.

You roll a die three times, first to move the youngest sister, second for the middle, and third for the eldest. The younger two sisters drop fruits from the tree, and the eldest picks them up and put them into current player's basket.
When a player fills their basket, a round ends and players collect victory points according to their fruits gathered. The more fruits of the same colour you gather, the more victory points you gain.

All components are hand-made with a collaboration with a pyrography (wood burning) artist.

Publisher's summary:
マトリョーシカの三姉妹がカラフルな実を集めるゲームです。
ダイスを振ってマトリョーシカを動かします。
木の上の二人が実を落とし、木の下にいる娘が実を拾います。
カゴに実を集めていっぱいになったら全員同時に点数計算をします。
同じ色を多く集めた方が点数が高くなります。
箱を含め、内容物はすべて手作りで、ウッドバーニング作家とのコラボレーション作品です。

From  the publisher:

Angry Birds Action Game is full of familiar fun, in a more compact form! With more wooden blocks, birds and pigs you can let your imagination run wild. The rules are easy: build a structure, place the pigs in/on it and see how far you can fling them with a couple of Angry Birds.


Exciting Angry Birds launching Pig Action that's designed to play on any tabletop surface! Includes a sturdy gameboard, 10 wooden structures, various cardboard platforms, 2 pigs and 3 abs & gold stars to track who wins each round. Player who wins 3 rounds, wins the game!

boardgame: Transylvania (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial Fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.


This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game with a vampire theme and the option to visit Dracula's castle. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes (detailed in coffins instead of rail spaces)and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop). You can use stations if you wish.

There is an extra set of cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules. Some of the cards give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks, though there are limited selective options for the latter. Engines are wild cards but only for the central red and black routes.

The main new rule is the option to discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn. This is very useful if you are desperate for engines or a particular colour card. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Tick ​​Tack - the clock is ticking - Let it roll! Quickly Choose the right track in this ball tower before time runs out! Roll your ball into the right goal - or goes it in the tray of your opponent? If the ball lands with you - well done - then go to the points you!

In this game you drop a ball in a ball tower.  As the ball drops you only have six seconds to get it in the right tray.  So you will need to find the quickest way down.  Shift tracks and get the best way for your ball to roll.

The Disneyland (60th anniversary commemorative) Pin Trading Board Game is a simple spin-and-move game (some spaces have variable effects as listed on the back of the box) along the Pixie Dust Path celebrating the attractions, films and magic of the Disneyland Resort. First player to the spinner in the center of the board wins.

The game includes 3 hinged standee token pins (Mickey Mouse, Aurora and the Genie) and a jumbo spinner pin. More player tokens (pins) and "completer pins" can be added with the separately-sold additional 3-pin token set (Minnie Mouse, Donald Duck, and Mr. Toad) and the 4 completer pins representing board locations / park attractions (Grizzly River Run, It's a Small World, Sleeping Beauty Castle, Space Mountain).
family: Versions & Editions: Disney Theme Park Editions

boardgame: The Rivals for Catan: Participation in the Big Game
description:
At the Internationale Spieletage (International Game Fair) in Essen, Germany, which took place from October 8 to 11, 2015, during his signing session at the Kosmos booth Klaus Teuber gave away the Special Cards 2015 for the game "The Rivals for Catan."

Because of the Catan - Big Game Event on Gen Con and on the games fair in Essen this year an additional special card was produced. The German title for this card is "Teilnahme am Big Game".

Card text, in English: "If you have registered for a Big Game or would like to participate in the next Big Game, in addition to the resources you receive as a result of the production roll you also receive the same resources your opponent obtained."

A solo campaign for the 2014 game "Orléans" - you have to travel to Tours and deliver three Citizen Tiles and three tiles of every good.

A Dutch version of this expansion is contained in Orléans (Dutch first edition). An English version is contained in Orléans: 5th Player Box.

Not to be confused with bezzerwizzer kompakt. This version contains no board.

BEZZERWIZZER Kompakt is a new way to play BEZZERWIZZER that makes it easier to play on the go, at the beach or anywhere else for that sake.

The game contains 150 cards with questions in the same 20 categories as the original game, which means that they can be used as new challenges in the original game as well.

To start with the players are divided into two to six teams. Each team can consist of one or more players. Each team then gets a bezzerwizzer token that can be used once per round (see below).

Now the game starts: Each team picks a token at random out of the included bag. There are 20 tokens - one for each category in the game. The token must be kept secret from the other players. The first team to go then choose either to answer a question in the category on their token or to swap with one of the other teams, without knowing what they will get. If they answer the question that they end up with correctly, they will receive 3 points - a wrong answer does nothing. Scoring is kept on the included pad. This continues till all teams have answered a question. The last team will not have the opportunity to swap, as all other teams have used up their categories.

Each turn, each of the teams have one opportunity to bezzerwizze one of the other teams question. This means that if the team does not answer the question correctly, the team that has bezzerwizze'd has a chance to answer it as well. If the first team answers correctly however, the other team will have wasted their chance to bezzerwizze this round. The number of points you get for bezzerwizzing and answering correct depends on when you choose to do it; if you choose to bezzerwizze after the category is revealed, but before the question is read aloud you will get 2 points for a correct answer. If you do it after the question has been read aloud, you will only get 1 point for a correct answer. If the teams that bezzerwizzet can not answer correctly either, the original team will receive the points.

The turn order then switches one place and the process starts again. If there are fewer category tokens in the bag than the number of teams when a new turn is started, all of the tokens are returned to the bag.

The game ends when one of the teams has 20 points (continuing the round to completion to allow other teams to reach 20 as well). Alternately, teams can choose to only play until the bag is empty.

CS&C is a unique design of miniature gaming for land warfare during the Napoleonic Era.  Unique, in that unit movement and fire/combat is regulated by the use of a grid square marked playing area.  No tape measuring or movement rulers are used.  Unit movement and other actions are limited to the cost of a unit’s available Actions Points during each turn phase, as determined during a turn order's sequence of play.

The basic command level is the brigade (consisting of several infantry battalions, cavalry squadrons and/or artillery batteries).  At the end of a turn, one or more Brigade Morale roll(s) are made to determine that brigade's willingness to continue the fight (number of rolls is based on the number of units destroyed or currently in "bad order").  The roll(s) determine if that Commander retreats 1 or more squares.  The command radius (in squares) is always determined from the respective brigade commander's position or unit objective square (if orders achieved). If a unit starts a turn within "Command Radius," it allows the unit to be issued an order cube, otherwise it must use all of its movement for the turn to try and get back in command.  An experienced player can control a division or corps with little difficulty after only playing the game a couple of times.

Players provide their own miniatures and terrain, using any size scale and basing of miniatures (generally 6mm to 28mm range).  The general rule figure scale is based upon a 60:1 scale for infantry/Cavalry.  The gaming area can be marked by various methods to show grid square designated areas.  An optional grided gaming mat can be used as the terrain base.

The initial release of the rules covers the campaign conflict between France and Austria in 1809 (but other nation units from different theaters of operation and time frames fought can easily be adapted for play).  There's also a special section for set up of tournament style battles.

description:
Es un juego de mesa para dos jugadores, donde las pirámides, momias, faraones y flechas deberán ser colocados de forma estratégica para sacar la diferencia necesaria para ganar los sets.
El objetivo del juego es ganar dos sets.
Cada set se gana obteniendo una diferencia de 7 puntos sobre el rival.
Los puntos se obtienen al conectar las pirámides con las flechas.
También se obtienen al colocar las momias y los faraones sobre las pirámides.
Además, al finalizar el juego, momias y faraones generarán puntos según la influencia que tendrán sobre cada uno de los territorios.

Si tratta di un gioco da tavolo per due giocatori, in cui le piramidi, mummie, faraoni e le frecce devono essere posizionati strategicamente per fare la differenza necessaria in set vincente.
L'obiettivo è quello di vincere due set.
Ogni set si vince, ottenendo una differenza di 7 punti sul rivale.
I punti sono guadagnati durante la connessione piramidi con le frecce.
Essi sono anche ottenuti mettendo le mummie e le piramidi dei faraoni.
Inoltre, dopo la partita, mummie e faraoni accumulare punti secondo l'influenza che avranno su ciascuno dei territori.

It is a board game for two players, where the pyramids, mummies, pharaohs and arrows must be strategically placed to make the necessary difference in sets winning.
The goal is to win two sets.
Each set is won, resulting in a difference of 7 points over his rival.
Points are earned when connecting pyramids with arrows.
They are obtained by placing the mummies and the pyramids of the pharaohs.
In addition, after the game, mummies and pharaohs accumulate points according to the influence they have on each of the territories.
A race to the finish game with a beautifully lithographed board featuring a land of monsters, wild beasts and fairy tale creatures. Players draw cards to move.

1863 Kampania na Kurpiach ("1863 Kurpie Campain") is a historial board game showing the hardships of brutal insurrection life during the anti- Russian Empire uprising of 1863 in Poland. The goal is to survive and finish as many misions as possible. The players get a random mission and travel the board. Players move according to their movement characteristic. Each movement results in a random adventure card. Additionally Tsarist forces randomly move around the board patroling in larger numbers each turn. Each mission has a turn limit after which the game ends. Players collect victory points for each adventure and succesful mission.

boardgame: Felix The Cat's Dandy Candy Game
description:
Players choose one of the Cowboy/Cowgirl character disks that have been punched out of the insert. Game also includes 12 orange slice disks and a punch out spinner with an inner and outer ring.

Players begin on the Starting House and move forward by the number of spaces on the outer ring of the spinner. When a player lands on an orange slice space, they receive an orange slice piece and spin again, this time using the inner ring of the spinner. Players continue until someone reaches the Candy House. The first player in the Candy House gets 2 orange slices. The player who has the most orange slices at this point, wins.

On the other side of the board is a checkerboard, and black and red checkers can be punched out of the insert, as well.

The card throwing game of fantasy siegecraft.

Each player chooses an army which they can choose to customise if they wish, then takes turn throwing cards across the table at each other's castle. If the castle (enemy deck) is hit, it loses cards based on the accuracy of the hit and type of card thrown. Dead Mammoths deal more damage then severed heads. When a player's castle runs out of cards, it is considered to have been razed to the ground and they lose.

You can use sneaky tactics cards to foil your foes plans and fight ground troops using rock, paper, scissors (each unit getting advantages and disadvantages which decide draws.)

It's a fast paced game which lasts around five minutes per bout, but rest assured, as soon as you're done, you'll want another chance to hone your throwing technique and grind your enemy's castle into dust.

This is a promo item for Werwölfe Vollmondnacht (One Night Ultimate Werewolf) containing the Prince character given out for free in Essen 2015.

The Prince is a villager with the handy ability of not being able to be killed.

The Prince also is part of the promo pack 1 for One Night Ultimate Werewolf.

boardgame: Play Me: Alice in Wonderdice – Tea Time
description: Expansion for the dice game Play Me: Alice in Wonderdice, so you can play the original game with up to nine players.

A starship travelling through the farthest reaches of space has flown into a vast field of orbital mines. Its defense systems are more than capable of destroying the mines, yet the small devices are equipped with an A.I. module - now they are trying to surround and annihilate the starship. Which side shall prevail in this deadly duel?

Players will move tiles (starship for one and mines for the other) on a space map and they will try to compete in order to achieve their result: pass the orbital mines fields without any relevant damage or destroy the starship.

This is a promotional Game: Black Stories given out for free at the publisher's booth at SPIEL Essen 2015. It will be contained in the upcoming Black Stories Scary Music Edition.

Black Stories are tricky, morbid, sinister riddle stories. The players try to reconstruct the particulars of each incident, piece by piece, by asking yes-or-no-questions.

The promo story can be played on its own, without any actual Black Stories edition.

Hawai'i-opoly allows you to collect your favorite Hawaiian properties and increase the rent by adding Waves and trading them in for Islands. We're talking beaches, canyons, and even Loco Moco. It's all fun and games... until someone gives you the Stink Eye and you're out of the game for three turns!
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Three promo items for Mysterium, containing an additional character, location and object psychic card as well as the equivalent ghost cards.

They are numbered P1 (character), P2 (location) and P3 (object).

You are a cunning alchemist, a wielder of one or several of the Six Disciplines. Create fantastical servants, potent spellbombs, transmutations, and nexuses within your laboratory—then use them to reduce your rival's lab to rubble!

Construct a deck of cards representing your laboratory, each card a room that can contain an alchemical recipe, reagents, or workspace: every card in this game can be played in any of three different modes. Combined with our flexible resource system, this means no dead turns. There's always a use for what's in your hand... but finding the optimal use will be necessary if you want to excel at competitive alchemy.

Master Combustion, Metallurgy, Reanimation, Astrology, Naturalism, Toxicology, or ANY combination thereof, and use their power to attack or otherwise damage your opponent's lab. Your goal? Empty their deck, destroying every room in their laboratory. Alternate turns gathering reagents, playing recipes, and attacking with your servants.

Laboratory Mayhem is a game of complex strategy. Experienced players are constantly discovering new strategies and tactics. For new players, there are entry-level duel decks designed to let you jump in immediately and learn as you go. This game is designed for 2 players to go head to head but is playable with any number, and our team and fans are always thinking up new formats. Lab Mayhem can be bought in sets, so, while you can trade with other players, you can also get all the cards you want up front for a reasonable price.

This is kind of a soccer training simulation. One goal keeper (red) plays against up to 3 field players (yellow, black, green). Movement is determined by a die. The goal keeper has 9 meeples and each field player has 3 meeples. The goal keeper tries to catch the field players and vice versa by getting on the same space. If the goal keeper has lost all his meeples the first field player to reach the square goal space wins the game. The goal keeper wins if the catches all field players.

Nemo, The Lost is a kickstarter exclusive, single character expansion that came with the optional purchase of the Mazu's Dreadful Curse expansion.

Included is a single red hero miniature, a deck cannon miniature and a stat card. He comes in a small brown box unto himself, without retail
packaging art, along with his card and cannon.

Madeline Tyler is a kickstarter exclusive, single character expansion that came with the optional purchase of the La Brise Sanguine expansion.

Included is a single green hero miniature, 2 deck cannon miniatures and a stat card, packaged in her own separate brown box without
retail style art.

The city is full of fine foods.  You love to go out with your friends, wandering around and eating tasty things.  But you might have overdone it a bit.  You're all feeling a bit plump lately.  One of you speaks up: "Let's all dieting contest!  Whoever loses the most weight wins!"

And so it begins.  A pleasant game of watching what you eat while you stroll the streets and dine together.  If you feel an item has too many calories, pass it off on a friend.  Try to be the first to reach your goal.

The players collect food cards, trying to keep the cards lowest in fat while passing off the unhealthy cards to their friends.  Whoever empties their hands first scores zero, while the other players count the amount of weight gained in their remaining cards.  After everyone has been start player once, whoever has gained the least weight wins.

boardgame: The Foreign King: Railroad / Catholic Against Liberals
description:
A small package which contains 2 mini expansions:
- The Railroad Expansion
- the Catholic against liberals Expansion

The rulebook also contains rules for a 3th mini expansion: Riots, but since this was promised to be a Kickstarter Exclusive, the tokens are missing.

Railroad Expansion

Who will rule the Belgian Railroads?
Adds the opportunity to build railroads at a price. During the end game scoring, the longest railroad generates points.

Catholic Against Liberals Expansion

Which Faction will get the most Political influence?
During the game there are opportunities to convert Provinces to your faction (Catholic, Liberal, Neutral). During end game scoring, the faction which controls more Provinces gains Points. This adds an interesting twist to the 4 player game, where 2 players will be working together!

What could be more fun then Trading good in the Mediterranean? It happens when you combine LCR components to create YADE (Yet Another Dry Euro). Move you ship from port to port and discover the shipping needs. Do the best job of matching the needs to win with the most Victory Points!

Objective: To have your sea ports end the game with the most total value in goods Shipped to it, and insuring that your home port has more value then your sister ports.

Game-Play: Players will take turns Useing dice to Launch/Land from port to port. While there they peak at the requested good and attempt to marry the right goods card in following turns. After the first chip is revealed take ownership of the port and begin to fill orders. Look out for other players that chose to mess with your effort at the port. They can improve the value of a sister port or try and ruin your home port value.

from the developer's website: Grand Central Station is a dice game set in the early days of steam when railroads first became big business. You will be in competition with several other leading magnates of the 19th century that are all vying to build a controlling influence in the industrial cities of the American Northeast.

Your dice represent the rolling stock of your railroad that you roll and place each turn in various enterprises around the edge of the board. This is the business engine you must use efficiently to get resources and other benefits needed for your expansion. Obtaining more dice is essential and will generate further opportunities for growth.

With enough growth and the strategic placement of your locomotives, you can control several major city stations for additional advantages over your competition. Each station has a unique ability - so chose your path of expansion carefully; but beware, someone may try to take your place. Perhaps it would be wiser to first invest in some R&D to expand your technological edge. Once the dice are cast you must decide...

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Stewards of the Secret
description:
Dark forces seek to recover the secrets of an evil sorcerer. Only a few noble heroes can help the order of the Stewards protect their knowledge. Take up arms with Seer Kel and her fellow heroes or reveal forbidden knowledge to call the ferrox to your side in Stewards of the Secret.


Stewards of the Secret is a Hero and Monster Collection for Descent: Journeys in the Dark.

It contains

     four heroes
     three blood apes
     three nagas
     four ferrox
     two new quests


Seer Kel has an extraordinarily powerful hero ability: figures and obstacles never block her line of sight. With Seer Kel, you can always find the spaces between bodies to ensure your attacks strike home. Even as you use your unnatural sight to look past those who would stand in your way, you may gaze into the future and see what plans the overlord player has in store for the heroes. As your heroic feat with Seer Kel, you can look at the top five cards of the Overlord deck, giving you unparalleled insight into the traps and punishment that will shortly arrive! What’s more, you can choose one of these cards and place it on the bottom of the Overlord deck before you shuffle the remaining cards and replace them on top of the deck. In this way, you can mitigate the most dangerous possible threat before it ever becomes reality. Ultimately, you’ll need magical tricks like this to achieve victory in Stewards of the Secret, especially as the overlord player calls upon horrific monsters spawned by ancient sorcery.

The ferrox can sap the power of their enemies and use it to repair their own bodies. Sustained by their victim’s strength, a ferrox is a worthy match for any hero. When you call upon the ferrox as the overlord player, you’ll quickly see the attributes that made them such useful servants for Devis. A ferrox can hit dangerously hard in melee combat, and it can spend its surges to pierce even the thickest plate armor and spread disease to hinder the heroes turn after turn. The master ferrox boasts an even more dangerous ability. By spending an action, a master ferrox can use Extract to choose an adjacent hero. That hero must test its might—if he fails, he suffers two fatigue, and the ferrox recovers two damage! Not only does Extract give your ferrox unexpected staying power, dealing fatigue can restrict the heroes’ movement and their use of Class cards. If you wish to recover the dangerous secrets of the mad mage Devis, you can do no better than to call upon the ferrox for your own dark army.
family: Game: Descent – Journeys in the Dark

description:
A Game: Black Stories edition with stories from the Bible.

Black Stories are tricky, morbid, sinister riddle stories. The players try to reconstruct the particulars of each incident, piece by piece, by asking yes-or-no-questions.

Take your quest for Slack on the road – the geeks are going on Spring Break! In addition to 40 new cards that celebrate the ups and downs of travel, like Inexplicable Blizzard and Beach Nookie, you also get to change the rules with 16 new, giant Event cards! Go on a trip to Baja or attend SkiCon – no matter where you go, Slack is there for the taking!

Multinational 2 is a boardgame where the players have to grow their multinational company. This is done by buying stock in ten multinational corporations, and then influence stock prices by buying factories, buying countries and playing cards. All this in order to sell the stock at a better price.

A successor of Multinational with more innovative features and new game modes.

This game recreate the first democratic elections in spain in 1977 after Franco period. Each player choose a political party and start with money for the political campaign. Roll the die and move your counter on the map. If you finish the movement on a city square, you remove and take a square counter wich represents votes, if not some event happens, . The game finish when all votes have been removed from the board. The player with more votes and secondary money wins the game.

This coaster was given as Promo in Brazil.

Variant - Inquisition
Place the coaster on the table with the Inquisitor side facing up.
The coaster slightly changes the Duke gameplay:
 If it shows the two Dukes the game plays with the normal rules.

While the coaster shows the Inquisitor, whenever a player influences a Duke to get 3 coins, another player may claim to influence the Inquisitor to receive 1 of the 3 coins leaving the Duke player with only 2 coins from the treasury. Players might challenge the Inquisitor as usual. In case of more than one player claiming to influence the Inquisitor, whoever has less money has preference and if they have the same amount, the Duke decides whom to give the money.

Action - Subvert
Pay one coin to the central treasury to flip the coaster.

boardgame: Battle at Lone Jack: A Missouri Street Fight
description:
Both sides saw the other as "invaders," so the fight quickly escalated to one of extreme violence. The fight seesawed up and down the main street of Lone Jack, and when the rebels fired the Cave Hotel, a Yankee stronghold, their advantage appeared decisive. Foster now wheeled his only two artillery pieces forward, blasting the gray attack with double-shotted canister. 

The Confederates owned the field as well as the dead. They quickly gathered the remains of the fallen, already spoiling under the torrid sun. Friend and foe alike they buried beneath the arching shade of the lone jack oak.

About the Rebel Yell! system

Rebel Yell! is a simulation of American Civil War small-unit tactics. The playing pieces in the game represent companies of infantry and cavalry or sections of artillery Each infantry and cavalry unit has one strength point and is equivalent to between 45 and 100 men, depending upon the scenario. Each artillery unit has two strength points and equals two guns. The players of Rebel Yell assume the roles of divisional commanders.

Each game turn is broken down into segments. During an action segment, the units of a particular player may conduct a single action. In the beginning modules these actions are: Move, Fire, Assault, and Rally. During any Small Arms segment, the units of a particular player may only fire.

There are no movement phases, fire phases, etc., as there are in other Civil War games. This absence, in addition to the fact that player segments are structured reciprocally, produces a feel of simultaneity uncommon to other designs.

One of the most difficult problems faced by the Civil War tactician was the "problem of approach," in other words, how he should maneuver his unit to some objective while keeping his casualties and disorganization at a minimum. A commander who did not pay heed to the destructiveness of "modern" weaponry would find his formation damaged up to three hundred yards from the enemy. Rebel Yell addresses this problem. Within restrictions, defending units may fire at the enemy as he advances. Like a real-life commander, a player can no longer be certain that when he initiates an advance, his units will reach their objective. Players may find that even one or two companies Standing to Fire will be difficult to dislodge by frontal assault.

Although Rebel Yell is a complex design, it is not out of the reach of the novice gamer. The rules are programmed, allowing small portions to be digested first and getting players into the game quickly. Provisions are made for such factors as infantry line formations (Standing to Maneuver, Standing to Fire, Skirmishers, and Cover), volley effects, leader quality, morale, and regimental flags. The rules for cavalry and artillery are also extensive.

Although an excellent multi-player game, Rebel Yell accommodates the solitaire player, Likewise it can be played in a physically limited area. Rebel Yell is big only in the sense that it is detailed.
Diametric is an abstract co-operative card game. The game consists of only 18 cards and uses hidden information to provide a challenge for 2 or 3 players.

In a fight that largely determined the fate of eastern Kentucky, two small armies clashed along the swollen tributary of Middle Creek. An officer of some promise, Humphrey Marshall, generaled the CSA. A relative unknown commanded the USA by the name of James A. Garfield.

At the end of the day, Marshall had no choice but to withdrawal. His supplies were thin and he was fearful of his army deserting. The battle unmade his reputation and he was barely heard from again. The Federals, conversely, celebrated a victory. Colonel Garfield earned a brigadiers star, and took the first step along the road that eventually led to the White House.

Contents:
1 28-Page Rules Book
1 4-Page Exclusive Rules Book
1 12-page Examples of Play and Glossary/Index
140 half-inch full-color, die-cut counters
1 22x17 Map/Playing Surface
Charts and Tables

boardgame: Star Trek: Five-Year Mission – Janice Rand / Wesley Crusher Promo
description:
A promo card for Star Trek: Five-Year Mission.

The card is labeled #8, and it is two sided like all other character cards, with one side for the Classic series and the other for the Next Generation.

The Classic side features Yeoman Janice Rand, who allows you to swap values on any 2 different-colored dice. The Next Generation side shows Acting Ensign Wesley Crusher, who allows you to exchange 1 die with 1 die from the pool.

Abstract strategy game for two players, played on a 6x6 square board.

Each player has a pawn. Initially, the pawns are placed on the c6 and d1 squares.

In each turn, players move own pawn to adjacent square, and then place a cube in any square. The cube can stacked up to three levels.

Pawn can move vertically, horizontally and diagonally. In addition, pawn can climb to the cube on one level. However, pawn can not go down the level, climb two levels at once or climb on top of the three levels of the cube.

A player win if the opponent's pawn can not move.

From the rules sheet:

The organizers of the race feel annoyed by the strange habit of the Camels to form stacks and carry each other along. To prevent this from now on, they introduce the Referee Camel.

The game is played as usual. At the beginning of each Leg, the player to the right of the player owning the Leg Starting Player marker places the Referee Camel onto any free space of the Race track.

When a Camel unit ends its movement on the space of the Referee Camel, all Camels of that unit are asked to "unstack" and to line up next to each other on that space. The Camels are "unstacked" from top to bottom and are lined up in the same order from left to right. (Therefore, the Camel on top is placed on the very left at the edge of the board and continues to be the leading camel of the former unit.)

Once all Camels of the unit have been "unstacked" (or a single Camel has landed on the space), the Referee Camel is removed from the Race track and placed next to the Game board. It enters the game again at the beginning of the next Leg (see above).

This small expansion can be added to the Camel Up base game as well as the expansion Supercup.

TOKAIDO is a game for 2 to 5 players, with unique atmosphere and aesthetics, in which the players are pilgrims on a journey along the legendary East Sea Road of Japan. Along the way, you will try to collect the most beautiful souvenirs, discover glorious vistas, enjoy enriching encounters, soak in soothing hot springs, sample sumptuous cuisine, and much more!

The Deluxe Edition is the post-Kickstarter public release of parts of the Tokaido: Collector's Edition. It includes the first expansion Tokaido: Crossroads. It does not have all the character tiles and miniatures available in the Collectors Edition, nor are the character miniatures painted. The Tokaido: Matsuri expansion is also not included in this edition.

In TOKAIDO DELUXE you will find exclusive material. This edition immerses you even more in the aesthetic and relaxing universe of Tokaido with:

- the bigger box of the Collectors Edition
- the giant game board of the Collectors Edition
- all cards for Tokaido
- all cards for Crossroads expansion
- 16 character tiles (Tokaido + Crossroads)
- 16 unpainted Traveler miniatures (Tokaido + Crossroads)
- 50 metal coins
- 5 colored Furoshiki markers
- 5 color bases for Traveler miniatures
- 1 wooden die (Crossroads)
- 1 fabric bag
- soundtrack CD with 22 songs
- Tokaido + Crossroads rulebooks
