description:
Throw the die and move your pawn between the shops. Take a card with a letter, you have to say a word in 20 seconds, which begins with the letter taken and you can find in the shop you finished the movement. If you win, you earn money; if you lose, you leave your money.

Whoever earns the most money wins.

Fear the Devourers is the second gameplay add-on for The 7th Continent.

Beware of the insatiable Devourers!
Once they have your scent, these terrifying beasts will hunt you to the ends of the continent, erupting out of the ground at any moment!

Kickstarter Optional Buy. 7 Rock Worm figures, 30 cards.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
User summary: 
かいちん (Kaichin) is the name of Japanese sweet made by Ishikawaya-Honpo. This is a noted confectioner in the Ishikawa prefecture in Japan, and the shapes and designs of their sweets are special. The game designer was given permission to use the Kaichin name (it means marble chip) and designs in this game. The game comes with Japanese and English rules.

The game itself is a simple abstract game. Players are secretly dealt cards corresponding to two of the 12 sweets in the game, and another card for one of the 6 colours. You win by moving a specific Kaichin chip that you have the card for across the board to its destination.

Otherwise, the game ends when there are no more available spaces on the board (some empty spaces might be blocked). In that case, the player holding the colour card with the most colour chips on the board wins. The tie breaker is the player furthest clockwise from the start player.

On your turn, you may either move any Kaichin chip, or make an attack. Kaichin chips can move one space (not occupied by another Kaichin chip) and then you place a matching colour chip under it. A Kaichin chip can leapfrog a single adjacent Kaichin chip, but cannot move onto or over any Damage chips. A Kaichin chip can move two spaces, if the first space has a colour chip of its colour. If a Kaichin chip ends on a colour chip of the wrong colour, that gets swapped out.

Damage chips are placed when an Attack occurs. If they hold the matching Kaichin card, a player simply declares a Kaichin chip is the attacker, and damage is assessed in 1 of the 6 directions around the attacker. Only the first Kaichin chip on a line that is vulnerable suffers damage. A Kaichin chip under attack is flipped to show a black border and a black damage chip is swapped under it. If a Kaichin chip suffers two damages in the game, it is eliminated. Kaichin chips not in play yet are immune but cannot attack. Damage chips are never removed.

During the game, nobody declares ownership of any Kaichin chip. During an attack, the player making the attack simply damages the target Kaichin. Only when the game ends can you discover who owned which Kaichin.

Publisher's summary:
「かいちん」とは、金沢の昔の方言で「おはじき」という意味だそうです。
ゲームタイトルは、金沢「石川屋本舗」様のお菓子の名前です。
「石川屋本舗」様に許可を頂き、タイトル・チップデザインに砂糖菓子「かいちん」のデザインを取り入れたゲームを作りました。
ゲームは「陣取り＋旗取り＋正体隠匿」のハードな造りですが、きらびやかにお菓子を並べて、その美しさもお楽しみ下さい。

プレイヤーはあらかじめ「かいちん」カード（2枚）と、「色」カード（1枚）を配られます。
このカードの内容はゲーム終了まで他プレイヤーに公開しません。

プレイヤーは以下のいずれかで勝利を狙います。
・自分のカードの「かいちん」チップをゴール（スタート地点の対面）まで移動する
・全てのマスが埋まった際に、自分の「色」チップが最大面積となる

プレイヤーは「かいちん」を「移動」あるいは「攻撃」できます。
「かいちん」は移動するごとに、足跡のように「色」チップを置いていくので、これで盤を埋めていきます。
他プレイヤーに気付かれずに「かいちん」を移動するか、盤を埋め尽くすか。
シンプルながら駆け引きの悩ましいゲームです。
The Army Book: Lahnars is a supplement for Confrontation: The Age of the Rag’narok. It contains everything you need to play with or battle against the Lahnars (Lions).

boardgame: The World of Smog: On Her Majesty's Service – Gentleman Pack
description:
A kickstarter-exclusive expansion to The World of Smog: On Her Majesty's Service, consisting of two additional Gentlemen - Suzanne Taylor and Colonel Steel - and four additional Agents - The Puppet, The Mad Scientist, The Comedian and The Hybrid - each with their own card and effect.

Also includes agent cards for all six gentlemen, and all cards in a foil version.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
User summary:
In the game カードの迷宮 (The Labyrinth of Cards), players explore a six-floor deep labyrinth. In each floor, there is treasure whose value is hidden until someone explores that floor. You collect that treasure if you commit most effort to exploring that floor.

The game is played like blind auction, but six floors are auctioned simultaneously. You may play an exploration card, which indicates how much effort you commit, face-down on your current floor or on a deeper floor, but once you explore a deeper floor, you may not go back to an upper floor.

At the end of the round, count up the treasure values players have collected. The player with higher total wins the round. You have to win two rounds to win the game.

With four players, the game is team battle (2 players vs. 2 players).

Publisher's summary:
カードの迷宮は【後戻りができない】地下迷宮を探検すると言うテーマのゲームです。探検がテーマですがやることは競りゲームです。探検の労力をより多く割けば地下迷宮から財宝が得られるでしょう。財宝をより多く獲得すればラウンドで勝利となります。２つのラウンドで勝てればゲームの勝利になります。４人プレイの場合はチーム戦になります。
publishers: ガーデンゲームズ (The Garden Games)

boardgame: Catan Scenarios: Big Game Big Honor
description:
The exclusive mini-expansion for participants of the Catan Big Game.

If they pay 1 wool or 1 grain, players can send "settlers" to participate in the Big Game represented by a game table with slots for each of the ten production numbers. The first player to place three settlers on the table receives a bonus victory point that (similar to Greatest Army or Longest Road) has to be passed on if another player surpasses him.

If the Big Game table is full, new settlers may displace settlers already on the table - the production dice are rolled to determine where the new settler is placed.

Whenever the production number of a settler at the Big Game table is rolled, the player receives a laurel token. Laurel tokens can be exchanged for game benefits:

One laurel token will send the robber back to the desert.
Two laurel tokens let the player steal a card from the hand of another player.
Three laurel tokens let you choose a resource card from the bank.
Four laurel tokens let you build a road.
Five laurel tokens let you take a development card.

Five new wonders to use in the game in the the new (big) format:

Colosseum: You have an extra building space.

Brandenburg Gate: Immediate -> Take a Glory tile from the stock and put it on this wonder. Count it at the end of the game as usual.

Amsterdam Stock Exchange: Activate -> You may spend up to 3 VPs in order to achieve 3 Gold for each spent VP.

Monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial: Activate -> Place a cube from your reserve on any number of your cities. Get the gold as normal for placing cubes. Cities composed by 3+ cubes cannot be attacked.

Mount Rushmore: Immediate -> Take back one of your discarded civilization cards and play it in addition to your current civilization card; apply immediately its effects.

THE CARD TOWER

Players: 2 or 4
Game time: 15 to 25 min
Age group: 10+

Synopsis
The Card Tower is a Dice/Card game where 2 to 4 construction companies are competing against each other to win a lucrative contract to build the next luxury tower in downtown Manhattan.  During each turn the companies are trying to build each of the four construction zones of the building floor plan faster than their opponent.  During the construction phase each company has a saboteur who is trying to stop the other companies from completing their job.  As the tower rises, by placing a new tower card on top of the previous card with dice, it’s up to you to decide what’s more important; building a zone of the floor plan or sabotaging your opponent.  If you build the most high quality sections of the tower, you will win the contact and the honor of putting your name of the tower.

As you play you are actually building a tower of cards with dice becoming the supporting structure.

Components
18 – Tower cards
18 – Sabotage cards
36 – d6 dice “9 dice each of four colors”

description:
The game takes 13 rounds. Each round the number of Cards will be lifted or lowered. Players have to say, how many tricks they will take this round. Who was correct, gets points. Who was wrong looses points. The winner is the one with the most points at the end.

Material: 52 cards, 50 matches, 1 ruleset

Das Spiel geht über 13 Runden. Pro Runde wird die Anzahl der Karten um eine erhöht bzw. wieder verringert. Die Spieler müssen pro Runde vorhersagen, wie viele Stiche sie machen werden. Wer richtig vorhergesagt hat, bekommt Punkte. Wer falsch lag, verliert Punkte. Wer am Ende die meisten Punkte hat, gewinnt.

Bestandteile: 52 Karten, 50 Streichhölzer, 1 Spielanleitung.

Jack Attack is a trick-taking game with an augmented deck, described by the publisher as "combining the best elements of Spades, Hearts, and Bridge." The deck contains the standard 52 cards, plus 4 suited "1"s, 2 "Big" Jokers, and 2 "Little" Jokers.

The object is to avoid  taking Jacks, although a player may collect all four for a "Grand Slam", similar to shooting the moon in Hearts.

The players are dealt a number of cards dependent on the number of players. The dealer determines trump/no trump, and makes the opening lead. Suit must be followed, with the exception of the Jokers, which may be played on any trick. Trick is won (in order of precedence) by the first "Big" Joker, highest trump, or highest card of the suit led. ("Little" Jokers cannot win tricks.)

Play continues until one person has taken 10 Jacks, or 10 hands have been played. Low score wins. ("Grand Slams" alter this somewhat.)

Runa Negra:
Es un original juego de cartas cooperativo, con ambientación medieval fantástica y una estética muy trabajada. 
En el, cada jugador asumirá el rol de un campeón que combatirá junto a un grupo de compañeros a las hordas de enemigos corruptos por el poder de la Runa Negra.

El juego consta con un sistema de generación de runas y enemigos innovador que hace que cada partida sea diferente. E incorpora varios niveles de dificultad adaptables, para que pueda ser jugado tanto por jugadores poco experimentados en juegos de cartas como para los habituados a ellos.

Runa Negra es un juego de cartas cooperativo en el que un grupo de entre 2 y 5 jugadores, que representan a unos campeones, se enfrentarán a hordas de enemigos que tratarán de sobrepasarlos. Los campeones deberán luchar hasta derrotar al jefe final, portador de la Runa Negra.
Tendréis que colaborar entre todos para combatir a la Runa Negra aprovechando el poder de las diferentes runas. Y dispondréis de varias clases de personajes para elegir con atributos y características únicos.


Black Rune:
Is an original cooperative card game, with fantastic medieval atmosphere and a very elaborate aesthetics.
In it, each player will assume the role of a champion who will fight with a group of peers the hordes of enemies corrupted by the power of the Black Rune.

The game has a system of runes and innovative generation of enemies that makes every game different. Also incorporates several levels of adaptable difficulty, so it can be played by inexperienced in card games and also for those who are accustomed to them.

Black Rune is a cooperative card game in which a group of 2 to 5 players, representing some champions, will face hordes of enemies that try to overcome them. The champions will fight to defeat the final boss, The Black Runa carrier.
You will have to cooperate with each other to fight the Black Rune harnessing the power of the different runes. You have several character to choose, with unique attributes and characteristics.

Bowling Dice Game is a simple "push your luck" game based on Bowling.

Roll 1 normal dice and count the pips. 
If you roll a 6 then it's an instant Strike or spare regardless of previous counted pips.

You can stop rerolling whenever you want and enter the score in the scoresheet.

After a Strike or spare you get bounspoints from the next rounds

Highest score wins.

Schinderhannes is a historical character similar to Robin Hood. He lived around 1800 in the region of Hunsrück, Taunus and Eifel (center of Western Germany).
Easy dice game. Players roll and move on a board of a network of spaces. One player is the "Schinderhannes" and tries to take as much loot as possible by landing on the appropriate space, the other players try to catch him. Who has the most loot after a couple of games wins.

Tripoley: A standard deck playing card game played with a special layout (or board) and poker chips. It is a modern version of the game of Poch. Each hand has three phases: "Hearts", "Poker", and "Michigan Rummy". All cards are dealt out, including one extra hand which remains unseen. Players place chips in the spaces for the special combinations on the playing mat/board, the "Poker" pot, and the "Kitty". The first phase pays off for holding certain cards or combinations (that match the combinations on the playing layout/board). The second phase is a hand of poker; each player selecting five cards from his hand to play. A hand of Poker betting takes place, with bets added to the "Poker" pot, and the winner wins the "Poker" pot. In the third phase, players play a slight variation of the game of Michigan (similar to Fan Tan), and the first to go out wins the chips in the "Kitty".

Backgammon (Acey Ducey): a classic abstract strategy game where each player has a set of 15 "men" that must be moved from their starting positions, around, and then off the board. Dice are thrown each turn, and each player must decide which of his men to move based on the outcome of the roll. Players can capture each other's men, forcing the captured men to restart their journey around the board. The winner is the first player to get all 15 men off the board.

Number Chek

Horse Race

Checkers: is an abstract strategy game where players move disc-shaped pieces across an 8 by 8 cross-hatched ("checker") board. Pieces only move diagonally, and only one space at a time. If a player can move one of his pieces so that it jumps over an adjacent piece of their opponent and into an empty space, that player captures the opponent's disc. Jumping moves must be taken when possible, thereby creating a strategy game where players offer up jumps in exchange for setting up the board so that they jump even more pieces on their turn. A player wins by removing all of his opponent's pieces from the board or by blocking the opponent so that he has no more moves.

Délirolinguo is as the Tower of Babel! This party game invites you to discover 30 common phrases in 12 languages: English, Spanish, Italian, German, Arabic, Japanese, Portuguese, Mandarin Chinese, Hindi, Russian, Turkish and French.

Rules of the Game: Unmask the language that opponents have!

The game starts with a card showing expressions in all 12 languages. To win, you just have to read this term to your opponents in the language that they have. Each opponent checks the answer on his card in his language and answers "yes" in case of right answer and "no" on error.

If you're right, you choose a Language card of one of your opponents to start your collection.

The winner of the game is the one that has the largest collection of languages. He will become a "polyglot genius".

DéliroLinguo, the languages game ​​which makes you totally crazy!

Material: 24 Language cards, 30 Expressions cards, 16 Special cards, 1 hourglass, 1 pencil, 1 notebook

---

Délirolinguo, c’est un peu l’auberge espagnole ! Ce jeu d'ambiance vous propose de découvrir 30 expressions usuelles dans 12 langues : l’anglais, l’espagnol, l’italien, l’allemand, l’arabe, le japonais, le portugais, le chinois mandarin, l’hindi, le russe, le turc et le français.
La règle du jeu ? Démasquer les langues que les adversaires ont piochées !
La partie commence avec une carte présentant une expression dans chacune des 12 langues. Pour gagner, il suffit de lire ce terme à ses adversaires dans la langue que ces derniers auront pioché. Chaque adversaire vérifie la réponse sur sa carte et répond dans sa langue : « oui » en cas de réponse juste et « non » en cas d’erreur.
S’il vise juste, il choisit une des cartes « langues » d’un de ses adversaires pour commencer sa collection.
Le gagnant de la partie est celui qui aura la plus importante collection de langues. Il deviendra ainsi un « polyglotte de génie ».

DéliroLinguo, le jeu de langues qui rend complètement fou !

Matériel : 24 cartes Langue, 30 cartes Expressions, 16 cartes Joker, 1 sablier, 1 crayon, 1 carnet

Every player has ten tiles in his bag. One with four cats, two with three cats, three with two cats and four with one cat. On your turn you draw a tile from your bag and place it on the table with the goal being having ten of your cats in a row.

Each player is dealt a cone card. The reverse side of it tells you what you should be adding to your ice cream cone: chocolate, strawberry or vanilla ice cream, almonds, cherries or wafer sticks. On your turn you draw a card and place it on your cone or replace the topmost card on your cone with the new card or you can place the card on someone else's cone. The game continues until everyone has five scoops on their cones. Then you score every card. If the card shows components that were required by your cone card you score positive points for the card and vice versa.

Climbing game using wooden tiles numbered from one to nine in four suits in descending order of strength: Suns, Moons, Stars and Clouds. There are also four Jokers. Tiles are divided evenly between players and everyone gets a Joker. The player with One of Clouds starts. You're playing singles, pairs, three of a kinds or straights of two or three. The first player to get rid of his tiles wins.
The Tribute promo card for Uruk II is yet another of the rare black cards, making it ever-so-slightly easier to play other black cards.
family: Series: DDD Verlag Small Box Games

description:
Going Ashore is a solitaire deck building game where you play the part of the Marines carrying out a series of battles as you hop from island to island. You must defeat all of the enemies on each island before you can move to the next. Clear the enemy from five islands to claim victory, any other result will re-write history as a loss for the allied forces!

- Publisher's blurb
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Two additional artisan cards featuring the game designer Koota Yamada and UTSUROI co-founder Nakagawa Kozi. These cards were not in the box, but given as a free promos with every copy of IKI at SPIEL 2015.

boardgame: Assault on Doomrock: Doompocalypse – Kickstarter Promo Pack
description:
This deck contains a bunch of fun cards of different types, which adds more variety to the existing decks. This pack of 25 cards is the Kickstarter reward for the people who ordered the Assault on Doomrock: Doompocalypse expansion.

Ability cards

        Aura of doom
        Light nova
        Lob shot


Common items

        Axewand of the Fairy
        Cheat Codes of Doom
        Dartdaggers
        Impact Warhammer
        Old Helmet
        That's a Trout
        Weakness Analyzer X-3000


Epic items

        Amulet of Blind Rage
        Fan of Misery and Doom
        Fifty Shades of Great Axe
        Gatling Gun
        Impaling Pauldrons of Doom
        Jar of Bees
        Lifetap Branch


Mountain Events

        Adventuring expo
        Angry mob
        Dragon moths
        Zombie walk


Wilderness Events

        Catpocalypse
        Dire beavers
        Fwuffy bunny
        Golden fish, granting wish

User summary: 
御朱印あつめ (Goshuin Atsume) means Goshuin Collection in English. This game was designed by a priest working in a Zen temple in Tokyo, Japan to promote the work of the temple and the experience for visitors.

Goshuin is originally a stamp given as a seal of submitting hand-written copied sutras to temples. Presently, Goshuin is a popular souvenir among people visiting temples.

In this game, the players visit temples and offer copied sutras to collect Goshuin. You may choose to form connection with many Bodhisattva deities or deepen the connection with certain deities. The player who manages to achieve the highest score through such connection wins the game!

Goshuin Collection is a bidding and hand management game for 3 to 4 players.
The box with words written in Indian ink contains components, such as Goshuin cards, Sutra chips, and Goshuin Notebook boards. 29 Goshuin cards and the Sutra chips are in a Buddhist monk’s own handwriting. The original Goshuin Notebook boards were made to order by Aribundou, a long-established bookbinder which has been in business since the Meiji period.

After drawing a Goshuin card from the draw pile, players offer their sutras. Once all the players have taken their turn, the player who has offered the longest sutra receives the Goshuin. Place the Goshuin you've received in lines next to your Goshuin Notebook board. You can also score substantially by collecting the Goshuin of certain deities!

But watch out for non-Goshuin cards which have slipped in! Well, let's just pray for the better...

Experience the fun of forming your original Goshuin notebook!

Publisher's summary:
テーマ：お寺、競り、役

御朱印とは、写経したお経を寺院に納めた証のこと。平成の世においては、参拝した記念の証として親しまれています。
プレイヤーはお寺の参拝者となり、写経を納め、御朱印をあつめていきます。たくさんの仏さまとご縁を結んでもよし、同じ仏さまとご縁を深めるもよし。仏さまとのご縁をいただき、もっとも得点を獲得したプレイヤーが勝利者となります！

『御朱印あつめ』は３人～４人で遊ぶことができる「競り」と「役」のカードゲーム。
墨字が目立つ箱の中には、御朱印カードと、お経チップ、御朱印帳ボードなどがおさまっています。
２９枚の御朱印カード、お経チップはお坊さん直筆。専用の御朱印帳ボードは、明治から続く製本屋「有文堂」様にお願いしました。

"
山札から御朱印カードを一枚めくり、プレイヤーはお経を出しあいます。一巡した時点でもっとも長いお経を納めたプレイヤーが御朱印を拝受。獲得した御朱印は、御朱印帳ボードの横に並べましょう。特定の仏さまがそろうと得点が大幅アップします！
でもちょっと待って！御朱印のなかにハズレがまぎれこんでいるかもしれません・・・合掌。

さあ、あなただけの御朱印帳を完成させましょう！

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Queenie 10: Totem Chamber
description:
Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Totem Chamber is an expansion for the Escape base game that includes single game board on which players must activate gems by stacking their dice in the middle of the chamber.

A die can be stacked only if it shows either a golden or black mask. If they stack 5 dice, they activate 1 gem; if they stack 10 dice, they activate 2 gems.

User summary:
Cats~魔女と泥棒~ (Cats Witch & Thief)
Each player acts as a witch with her two black cats, and sneaks into the mansion of a corrupt merchant to steal gold coins. The black cats' duty is to steal the gold coins. Use magic to support your cats.

You can steal gold coins from your rivals by jumping over them.
While stealing gold coins from each other and dropping and picking up gold coins, head for the goal, the room with the safe.

Publisher's summary:
プレイヤーは魔女となり、２匹のクロネコと共に悪徳商人の屋敷に忍び込み、金貨を盗みます。
 金貨を盗むのはクロネコたちの役目。
プレイヤーはクロネコたちを魔法でサポートします。
 部屋ごと回転させたり、ライバルを転ばせたり、ときには金貨の袋に穴が開くといったアクシデントも。
ライバルを飛び越すことで金貨を奪います。
 金貨を奪ったり奪われたり落としたり拾ったりしながらゴール地点の金庫部屋を目指します。

User summary: 
ぼくのひな祭り (Boku no Hinamatsuri) means My Dolls' Festival in English.

The players score points by placing their cards to form their original tiered stands of traditional Japanese Hina dolls.

Starting with the dolls of Five Court Musicians, the display is followed by Three Court Ladies, the Empress, and the Emperor. The players simultaneously complete their original tiered stands of Hina dolls by each using 10 cards.

Choose the 10 cards as you like. You may safely choose high-score cards, collect items to aim at a rally at the end, or bet on your luck by collecting dice.

It's a game designed to provide the warmth of Japanese tradition.

Publisher's summary:
カードを並べて自分だけのひな壇を作りつつ得点を競います。
五人囃子からスタートし、三人官女、女雛、男雛と続き、10枚のカードで各々のひな壇が同時に完成します。
どんな10枚を選ぶかはあなた次第。
無難に点数の高いカードを選ぶも良し、
最後の巻き返しを狙ってアイテムを集めるも良し、
サイコロを集めて運に賭けるも良し。
和のぬくもりを感じられる一作です。

Mr. Maksimov, the town's wealthy recluse, has passed away and many old rumors are swirling about. Some say that he was descended from royalty, others say that he was an adventurer or a smuggler, and there are even those who claim he was a wizard.  Whatever they believe, the towns people are wasting no time rushing to his estate sale after his death seeking answers to the mystery around him... and to get their hands on as many of his possessions as they can.  Some of Mr. Maksimov's possessions could be worth a fortune but others might end up being worth nothing.

Mr. Maksimov's Manor is played in four rounds.  In each of these rounds players will bid on Maksimov's possessions during his estate sale. Item cards that players can gather will either build on each other for more points or cause the player to lose points. When bidding, players need to balance how much value can be gained from the available items with what cards could hurt them. Also, if Mr. Maksimov's will turns up, it could change everything!

Mr. Maksimov's Manor is a casual game that both non-gamers and strategy gamers can appreciate.  Casual gamers will enjoy the game's easy to learn rules and the feelings of excitement and anticipation that the game play yields. What will keep strategy gamers coming back is the ability to manipulate the game's odds, constant risk and reward decisions, and subtle elements of social deduction.

One day the Humans had fiddled with the Earth until it could take no more, and the Humans ended. Then the Voles created their civilization and fiddled with the smaller things of the Earth until the Voles ended as well. Now the tiny creatures of the forest compete to find the artifacts and knowledge left by the fallen empires so that they too can mess it all up in their turn.

Each game of Vasty Wilds begins with a randomly placed network of Root cards. The players add cards to the board to expand the forest. Each card placement includes movement and other beneficial effects (Progress) for the player and detrimental effects (Misfortune) for her opponents. A player's opponents are in charge of placing her objectives in inconvenient places on the board. The player who collects all of her objectives first wins.

While Vasty Wilds is easy to learn for younger players or beginners, successive plays will reveal more complex strategies for card placement and effect combinations.

Lead Rome to greatness and become its first Emperor!

In Triumph, you want to turn the Roman Republic from a small city into a vast empire as the leader of a great noble family. Vie for dominance on every front of the Republic's life, and outmaneuver your rivals as you compete for power and prestige. Vanquish all external enemies and secure new provinces for the glory of Rome. Will you rise to the challenge and lead your family to ultimate triumph?

With a deck of cards representing your family's most illustrious figures, you'll bid to secure the offices of consuls, praetors, and censors. Those will, in turn, provide you with clients, gold, and legions to govern provinces, lead successful military campaigns, and control key sites within Rome itself – all to gain enough prestige to proclaim yourself the first Emperor of Rome!

You are a cluck operator competing in the Cluckbot Fight League International, a brutal realm of mechanical combat as popular as it is illegal. Pick one of 10 cluck models, gather your arsenal, and take them into the arena!

Cluckbot is a board game for 2-player combat dueling. Each player controls a robotic cluck, one of 10 models, each with its own “cluck sheet” defining their attacks, gadgets, grapples and rush combos. You have 8 turns in the pit to eliminate your opponent either through raw damage or forcing them off the stage.

Each turn, players set their cluck’s movement (direction and facing) and action (step or leap) while the opponent does the same, double-blind. After revealing the commands, players resolve actions while spending "charge" to activate weapons and gadgets as they go. The right commands can foil your foe’s assault. Will you lure them under your claws? Outflank? Surprise attack? Strategic planning, anticipating your foe, and a bit of bold improvisation are key to victory!

boardgame: Operation Martlet: The 49th Infantry Division Face 12 SS Hitlerjugend West of Caen – A Pint Sized Campaign for Chain of Command
description:
Operation Martlet is the fourth of Pint-Sized campaigns for Chain of Command, designed to be played using the campaign handbook At the Sharp End.

Twenty eight pages long, Operation Martlet follows the established Pint-Sized Campaign format, with an overview of the forces involved on both sides, their deployment shown on period maps and the course of the campaign described in detail before going on to present a mini-campaign covering this combined arms operation launched by the British 49th Division immediately prior to Operation Epsom to seize the Rauray Spur from the defenders from 12 SS Hitlerjugend.

The campaign is a total of six game tables with the duration running between six and eleven games.  Briefings are provided for both sides, along with measurable objectives, period maps, force and support option listings and everything you need to play this campaign through to its conclusion.

Like all of previous Pint-Sized campaigns, it is available for the price of a pint in the local pub of the publisher.
family: Series: Pint Sized Campaigns for Chain of Command

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Chada & Thorn – Stinner: Die Solo Erweiterung
description: This set of three hero and three equipment cards allows you to play any legend as the single hero Stinner (from Legends of Andor: Journey to the North), rather than as the two usual heroes Chada and Thorn.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Queenie 9: Exchange Chamber and Teleporter Chamber
description:
Introduction from Queenie 9 by the Publisher Queen Games:

"Works only if played with module 1 and 2 of the basic game. After placing 2 chambers next to the starting chamber, shuffle the teleporter and exchange chambers with the rest of the chambers before adding the exit tile. Place the teleporter token within easy reach next to the chamber stack."

This Queenie features two new Chambers, and a Teleporter Token. The Exchange Chamber allow players to exchange Curses for Treasures. The first player to the Teleport Chamber gains the Teleporter Token. This token can later be dropped in another Chamber, and now allows instant access from the Chamber with the Teleporter Token to Teleporter Chamber, and vice versa.

Welcome to the second generation of space refugies! The little Flouks were born on Earth, but some of them are Zybrides, who inherited their traits from the two peoples their parents belong to.

10 cards P'tits Flouks
5 cards P'tits chefs
14 action cards
1 card décret

This 55 card deck includes TWO entirely new suits of pirate to add to your game, allowing it to be played with up to 6 players (when added to the base game).

The first suit is UNDEAD pirates, allowing you to add a horde of shambling zombie pirates to your game.

The second suit is SNEAKY pirates! These shadowy pirates let you flip some of your crew face-down, to keep the other captains guessing!

A pack of 18 exclusive Kickstarter Bonus cards that can be added to the base and expanded 5/6 player game.

Cards include:
6 Loot cards (Albatross, Captain's Musket, Mighty Cannon, Powder Bomb, Monkey's paw & Giant Clam)
6 Pirate Crew Horde cards (1 for each of the 6 suits)
6 Pirate Crew Navigator Cards (1 for each of the 6 suits)

This is a Jenga clone with a die. It consists of plastic bricks in three colors which are stacked up in rows of three, each row containing all three colors.

The active player rolls a die. According to the result s/he now must: 
- pull out one brick in a specific color and stack it onto the top of the tower
- pull out a brick of any color and stack it on top of the tower
- pull out two bricks of any color and stack them on top of the tower
- Lose a turn.

When the tower falls over, the last player to have placed a brick before who didn't topple it is the winner.

You were aboard the yacht "Northern Pride", which a small group of friends had chartered for a party that night off the shores of New England. The group asked the Captain to take the boat way out to sea, away from any lights so they could party under the stars and maybe see the Northern Lights put on a show. When the boat set out, it was a clear, pleasant evening and the sea was calm. The Captain did mention something about a possible storm front that was due to move in early the next morning.

For hours, the party aboard the boat had enjoyed themselves. Then someone noticed the stars had all disappeared, and the stillness of the sea was quickly turning into gale force winds. The storm front had moved in faster than anyone could have expected. A sudden microburst tore the boat to pieces, plunging you all into the sea.

Now you all find yourselves sitting atop a piece of the boat, bobbing in the water, somewhere in the Atlantic Ocean. Scattered in the water all around you, are pieces and debris from the boat. As it all happened so fast, the Captain had no chance to radio for help. You realize it may be some time before you are rescued, and you will need to survive until then.

To survive (and win the game) you must find a flotation device, a signaling device, a water source, and a food source. To find your survival tools, you will have to swim around searching the sea for floating parts. You might even have to might have to steal parts from others, or withhold parts from them to save yourself.

Survive being awash at sea by using deduction, hand management and memory.

This game uses cards as boat pieces, and floating debris. It comes with 6 player tokens to move from card (boat piece or swim card) to card. Each turn you have 2 strokes to move. As the game progresses it takes more strokes to move as the pieces are drifting further apart.

boardgame: Run, Fight, or Die!: Jive Cat Boss Expansion
description:
This expansion adds components and rules players can use to replace the Base Game Mutant Boss.

It includes the following components:

1 Jive Cat Mutant Boss miniature
1 Location Card (The Jive Cat Lounge)
16 Jive Cat Mutant Boss Cards
1 Rules Sheet

Publisher's Description:

"The Solo Campaign consists of 4 mini games in which the player must find resources and Followers in order to defeat the Zombie Mutant in Phase 4.  You start the campaign just as the apocalypse has begun, and you also start the game with limitations that could possibly remains in play the entire game."

The Solo Campaign includes the following components:

15 Trait Cards used for variable player limitations
15 action cards that replace the base game action cards
3 upgrade cards
1 courthouse player mat

boardgame: Character Quest: Heroes The Card Game – Game Pack #3: Haunted Oak
description:
The rumors are true!  The Crypt King has shown up on the edge of civilization with a legion of undead.  On this border, an old Tavern named Haunted Oak has re-lit it's fires.  The Elf Champion and his many allies are starting to fill the tavern and getting ready to face this new threat.

In Character Quest: Heroes The Card Game – Game Pack #3 , up to four players take turns collecting heroes and sending them out to gain victory points for your tavern. Each player will try to create a powerful combo of heroes to defeat even the toughest locations.

Only one deck is needed for up to four players; extra decks can be used separately by each player or shuffled together for everyone to share. Game Pack #3 can be played separately or with Game Pack #1 and #2. TCG is for 'the card game'

Heroes of Normandie: Gazette #4 is the fourth issue of a quarterly gazette that contains new scenarios, rules, and punchboards with new units, surprises, and other items. Gazette #4 has four pages of text with special rules for parachuters and two scenarios as well as one punchboard that introduces new components.

Contents :

– 1 punchboard

– Historical informations about Fallschirmjägers and Skorzeny commando

– Scenario “Operation Eiche” – Italian (US) (210 pts) against Germans (310 pts)

– Scenario “A mess in the Ardennes” – Germans (700 pts) against US (680 pts)

– New rules about new special abilities : Charm, Double fire, Brandenburger, Perfect shot.

Scenarios require the Carentan expansion and the Battleground terrain set.
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: Guardians' Chronicles Expansion Pack
description:
Heroes of Normandy opened the liberation paths of Europe. This has been made possible with strong and powerful icons, going beyond what the simple man could do.

These men and women have become something greater. They have become superheroes!

Fast, agile, endowed with extraordinary faculties, with mythical equipment or advanced technology.

Send Captain Justice, the Super Yankee, at the front. He will stand in the front line of German armies, equipped with its only strength and patriotism. Meanwhile, Lady Britain will illuminate the dark sky with mighty lightnings.

The Allies were not the only ones to have their superheroes! The Reich has his own figurehead facing the opposing titans. Animal rage accompanies her and bloodlust is her reason for living. Her opponents are her next meal. A dark she-wolf gets in the way of these superheroes. She will leave them no chance, and if they ever manage to escape, her pack of werewolves will track them … Evil Anja is the mother of the pack.

Superheroes will have access to a new type of recruitment option: the superpowers! Instead of suffering hits, they can decide to lose superpower.
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

boardgame: Shadows over Normandie: Cthulhu Mythos Call #1
description:
Some thought that with the destruction of the Deep Ones village, peace had returned to Normandy and the Madness shadows had dispersed.

In the shadows, new pawns move and come to threaten this apparent “peace”. The war was continuing elsewhere in a more “conventional”.

From misty recesses of the Normandy campaign, the Sons of Cthulhu begin to chant their dark prayers to their god that is just as.

In villages deserted by war, an insane scientist tries terrible experiences on combat deaths. A new life seems to rise, accompanied by the roar of those who should not walk.

To combat these horrors, a new investigator brings his tomes laden by spells, incantations, each more disturbing than each other. Beware to they do not fall into the wrong hands, or “Cthulhu” alone knows which horrors may cross the mystical barriers of our world … why not a Shoggoth?

Expand Shadows over Normandie with cultists, new characters, the Necromancer, books & creatures, and more. This blister pack is filled with creatures from the deep and new ways to battle them.
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

boardgame: Our Royal Bones: The Battle of the Bouvines
description:
Our Royal Bones recreates the 1214 Battle of the Bouvines using the simple and playable Shields & Swords system. France's King Phillip II Augustus was up against a coalition of German, English, Flemish, and more. Phillip nimbly avoided battle until he could find ideal ground for his cavalry. The result was a humiliating defeat for the coalition that led to the overthrow of Otto IV, and the signing of the Magna Carta by John of England.

This battle isn't just important historically, but makes for an exciting gaming situation. The French have a weak left flank that must be protected at all costs to avoid automatic defeat. The Coalition's center wing has a clear superiority in Infantry, both in terms of quantity and quality, that might just prove decisive.

There are plenty of tactical decisions to be made, and one crucial strategic one: when the French Player declares initiative for the first time, it brings his powerful cavalry reserve-- and that of his opponent-- into play. Do it too soon and the German horse might help turn that vital flank. Wait too long, and they won't turn the tide. Timely deployment is key, and the "right" timing is highly contextual, based on what the players did before and what they do after-- resulting in an engaging and replayable game.
family: Series: Shields and Swords (Tiny Battle Publishing)

boardgame: The Prince: The Struggle of House Borgia – Condottiere
description: 4 Condottiere (Power 4, Mercenary Fee: 5) cards. Each of the 4 is in a different language. One of the appropriate language should be added to the deck.

A pack of 12 cards containing 5 Jester cards, 1 reference card, and 6 base cards.

The Jester is a promo role for Coup which is used as a replacement for the Ambassador.

A player claiming the Jester role may cause Disorder, taking a card from the Court deck and a card from the Target (another player of their choice). The player may secretly change one of their cards for one of the cards received. The player then returns a card to the Court deck and a card to their Target.

The Target may also claim the Jester role to avoid giving (and thus receiving) a role card.

The Target of the Captain may claim the Jester in order to block Stealing.

This promo was available as part of the KickStarter edition of Coup: Rebellion G54.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Surrender Unto Caesar: The Siege of Alesia, 52 BCE
description:
Surrender Unto Caesar is the 17th game in the Pocket Battles series of games from LPS, Inc.

Surrender Unto Caesar is a complete mini-game with map and rules (on the back of the postcard) and counters which need to be cut out. Also needed for play is a standard deck of playing cards and 1D6.

The game goes for 4 turns, divided into multiple rounds. Players each draw a card to determine who goes first that round. A turn ends when both Jokers have been drawn from the deck.

The map is divided into Siege Wall Areas (SWA), and each player takes one action (sometimes two actions, occasionally none) in any one SWA. Actions cause combat losses or reduce Roman/Gallic Resistance Levels (RRL/GRL). A player loses once their RL reaches 0. The Gallic player also wins if at the end of any turn Vercingetorix is in a SWA free of Roman infantry units. If the Gallic player hasn't won by the end of turn 4, the Roman player wins.

A competitive board game about being a Viking captain, sailing an uncharted ocean, trying to collect 5 Rune Stones. You can collect Rune Stones either by discovering them in the sea, or by attacking and stealing them from players who have already found them.

Reveal the board as you play by randomly drawing tiles each turn, creating a different sea every game. Aside from Rune Stones, the sea is also filled with islands that can help or hurt (i.e. some islands will kill some of your crew, some islands will provide food for you to feed your crew).

Aside from other players, you also have to contend with the sea itself. You never know when a storm will blow in, knocking your ship off course, or if the mysterious and deadly Ghost Ship will materialize.

From the game box:
"Today is your neighborhood swap meet! Dozens of folks have hauled their unwanted stuff into their driveways. Swap your old stuff you don’t want for… well, more old stuff, but old stuff you’d rather have. Be warned, there are other bargain hunters out there too! Go too slow and you lose the deal. Go too fast and you might miss out on the ultimate swap!"

This game was created as an educational tool to teach students about unit conversion. Players start with certain goods and play cards to convert (swap) those goods into other goods, for example Comic Books to DVDs. Players are competing to complete a Deal by chaining swaps to a shared Goal and collect points based on the number of cards used in the Deal. Play continues for a number of rounds and the player with the most points win.

The Speculator is a promo role for the game Coup which is used as a replacement for the Duke.

A player claiming the Speculator role may Gamble, taking coins from the Treasury equal to the amount the player currently has, up to a maximum of 5.  If the Speculator is successfully challenged, any coins received are returned to the Treasury and the first challenger takes the active player's remaining coins.

As a Counteraction, may block Foreign Aid.

If playing with Allegiances from Coup: Reformation, the Embezzle action excludes the Speculator instead of the Duke.

This promo item was only available in the KickStarter edition of One Night Revolution, and included:
- 5 Speculator cards
- A full set of 6 playing cards for Coup
- Rules/reference card.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In Popularity Contest, 4 to 6 players play cards on their turn to try and be the most popular. Being popular is good but beware, sometimes if you are too popular, your friends will turn against you. Play cards to add or subtract from your or an opponents popularity. If you are the least popular you will be eliminated. Don't get too secure in your popularity, if you get too popular someone may give you a zero card and you may also get eliminated.
If you are eliminated, you are not out. Eliminated players use their remaining cards to help determine the winner. Be the last person standing (the most popular) and win the game.

The elite SSU Special Forces Spetsnaz have arrived in the Middle East! Hidden in the deep deserts, they strike at the Allies and the Axis, leaving no survivor and back in the shadows before anyone can find them!

Content of the box:
- one hero: Roza Shanina
- one squad of five Spetsnaz, the Heavies
- one Natasha (Tsh) walker with pilot
- one rule booklet with the introductions rules to Dust Tactics
- twelve Dust dice
- one tank trap
- one ammo crate
- one gaming mat
- six terrain tile
- one catalog
- 3 unit cards
- and one leaflet

The Marines have landed in the Middle East! With the mission of protecting a region crucial for the Allied war effort, the USMC now fights the Axis and the SSU in this part of the world.

Contents of the box:
- one hero: Tom "Diver" Derrick
- one squad of five Marines, the Firestarters
- one Bushmaster light antitank walker
- one booklet with the introduction rules to Dust Tactics
- twelve Dust dice
- one tank trap
- one ammo crate
- one gaming mat
- six terrain tiles
- one catalog
- 3 unit cards
- and one introduction leaflet

A nice simple and fun game for kids of all ages. Each player spins the wooden wheel to collect robot parts. The first player to assemble his small robot wins. The box contains 1 wooden roller tray, 24 wooden magnetic pieces (4 robots), 1 set of rules.

boardgame: Quest: Zeit der Helden – Umhang der ewigen Wache
description:
This item Card gives you +1 Charisma

it was given away at Essen Spiel

In a harbour of an island of the Antilles, 4 ships are to be loaded with goods. Each player wants to attribute his porters to the ships in order to get money when the ship leaves the harbour. In your turn, you must play one of your action cards. The card tells you how many porters you are allowed to attribute to a ship or if you may change their places. If there are as many porters in a ship as the ship needs, it leaves the harbour. All porters earn money. The player who triggered the departure must pay a commission according to the number on his played action card. But if he is the starting player of the round, he may denominate any other player with at least one porter on that ship to pay the commission.

Promo Card # 18
If played you can play two cards each turn and all other Special Karnickels are eliminated.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Mix and match dragon, vampire, and werewolf body parts to create a fearsome gladiator that you will pit against your opponents' creations. Fight to the death against the other monsters in a limb rending battle that only ends when one heart remains beating.

The game has two distinct phases, first you draft all of the body parts between all players, and build your monster with the parts you've drafted. Then players take turns attacking each other using an innovative system where the limb you use to attack dictates which area (upper, middle, lower) of your monster will be vulnerable to the next attack aimed at you.

Combat continues as limbs take damage and eventually fall off. If an attack targets a space that is missing a limb it goes through to the torso, once your torso is destroyed you are out of the game.

Each body part has a thematic offensive or defensive battle effect which adds to replay value as you explore all of the possible monster combinations.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Zamki szalonego króla Ludwika: Polskie zamki
description:
Zamki szalonego króla Ludwika: Polskie zamki (Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Polish Castles) is a set of ten promo tiles representing famous rooms in Polish castles. All rules from basic game apply to this expansion.

This expansion is an add-on made available to people who joined the crowdfunding campaign on the Polish platform wspieram.to.
family: Game: Castles of Mad King Ludwig

description:
Promo of SPIEL 2015 for the game Dungeon Fighter.
Stinkerjell uses marbles instead of dice and is related to the game Potion Explosion.

Card text:

To throw marbles, follow the same rules as throwing dice with the exception that landing in the Target holes counts as a hit (0 damage).

Light Touch: Throw a marble. If you Hit, add 4 gold coins on the chest.

Healing Peck: Throw a marble. If you Hit, all Heroes heal 2 hit points.

Fairy Sting: Throw a marble. If you Hit, deal +4 damage to the monster.
4 Promo cards: Cerberus, Giant, Kraken, Pegasus, available as pre-order bonus or at Essen Spiel 2015.
Ashur Kersam promo card for Bastion, available at Essen Spiel 2015. Language independent. Value of 8, archmage, legendary enemy. When in play, no alchemy spells can be used.

boardgame: The Voyages of Marco Polo: The New Characters
description:
As a thank-you for winning the Deutsche Spielepreis, this small mini-expansion was commissioned.

This expansion contains:


    4 New Characters
    4 New Contracts
    1 Small Town Bonus Tile
    15 Gift Markers
family: Game: The Voyages of Marco Polo

description:
Given out for pre-ordering the game at Spiel '15. Part of these were also stretch goals in the Kickstarter campaign;

The set contains the following cards:
- P01 - Li'l Kleist
- P05 - Strategic Planning (x8)
- P08 - Sabotage
- P09 - Air Support
- P10 - Strategic River Crossing

Description
Ideal for acquiring a good foundation, History in primary school is for young adults, but also looking for a family game or a cultural product for easily dive back into history.
Chronicards, a whole collection to mix !
Each card is unique!

Medal at the Lépine contest.

Contents
60 tarot cards formatted with instructions in French.

How to play ?

Chronicards is a card game played using 60 cards. 
Each card depicts a historical event on one side with explanation and the year in which that event occurred on the other side. 
Players take turns placing a card from their hand in a row on the table. After placing the card, the player reveals the date on it. If the card was placed correctly with the date in chronological order with all other cards on the table, the card stays in place; otherwise the card is put in the right place and the player takes another card from the deck.

The first player to get rid of all his cards by placing them correctly wins. If multiple players go out in the same round, then everyone else is eliminated from play and each of those players are dealt one more card for another round of play. If only one player has no cards after a bonus round, he wins; otherwise play continues until a single player goes out.


Description
Conçu en collaboration avec des professeurs des écoles pour une utilisation parascolaire, ce titre s’adresse à un large public à partir de 10 ans.

De Lucy l’Australopithèque (vers -3.000.000) à la mise en circulation de l’Euro (2002), les 60 dates traitent de la Préhistoire et de l’Histoire de France.

Idéal pour acquérir de bonnes bases, L’Histoire à l’école primaire s’adresse aux plus jeunes mais également aux adultes à la recherche d’un jeu familial ou d’un produit culturelermettant de se replonger facilement dans l’Histoire.

Chronicards, toute une collection à mélanger !
Mélangez les thèmes pour enrichir votre chronologie. Chaque carte est unique !

Médaillé au concours Lépine.

Contenu
60 cartes au format tarot avec notice en français.

---

Comment jouer ?

-Preparation de la partie -
Distribuer 5 cartes côté texte visible devant chaque joueur sans retourner les cartes. Les joueurs laissent les cartes posées sur la table. Déposer la pioche côté texte visible et retourner la première carte du paquet côté date sur la table. C'est le point de départ de la chronologie.

-Déroulement-
À tour de rôle, chaque joueur choisit une carte dans son jeu en énonçant à voix haute le titre de l'événement. Il la positionne ensuite chronologiquement par rapport à celle(s) déjà déposée(s). Il retourne alors la carte côté date pour vérifier s'il a raison. Si oui, il ne pioche pas de carte. Si non, la carte est repositionnée au bon endroit dans la frise chronologique et le joueur pioche une carte. C'est ensuite au joueur suivant. Si un joueur ne se souvient plus de l'événement correspondant à une carte déjà posée, il peut la retourner pour la consulter.

-Fin-
Le premier à se débarrasser de toutes ses cartes l'emporte. Si des joueurs terminent dans le même tour, ils continuent à jouer en piochant une nouvelle carte et sont éliminés en cas d'erreur. Le dernier encore en jeu gagne.

Publisher Blurb:

Baseball Highlights: 2045 is like watching TV highlights of early 21st-century baseball games, with the gameplay being full of theme with no outs or innings and without bogging down in a play-by-play baseball simulation. In this quick and interactive game, two players build their teams as they play, combining both strategy (building your team) and tactics (playing the game) without any of the downtime. Game includes regular head to head play as well as solitaire and 3 player variants and the means to play 4 player tournaments. The game also supports combining boxes for even larger tournaments. 1-4 players 45 minute play time. The Super Deluxe Edition comes with seven mini expansions: Naturals Magna Glove, Cyborg Pitchers, Robot Hitters, Rally Cap, Coaches, Big Fly!, and Errors!.

Bezzerwizzer Musik uses the same rules as Bezzerwizzer Kompakt but contains only questions regarding music.

The game contains 100 cards with questions in 12 music categories and therefor only 1.200 questions - less than previous versions. Both question cards and category tokens are kept in an orange color to make them distinct from the original BEZZERWIZZER game and BEZZERWIZZER Kompakt. This means that you can mix tokens from all of the games together and create your own game with precisely the categories you like.

The rules below are an exact copy of the ones from Bezzerwizzer Kompakt:

To start with the players are divided into two to six teams. Each team can consist of one or more players. Each team then gets a bezzerwizzer token that can be used once per round (see below).

Now the game starts: Each team picks a token at random out of the included bag. There are 20 tokens - one for each category in the game. The token must be kept secret for the other players. The first team to go then choose either to answer a question in the category on their token or to swap with one of the other teams without knowing what they will get. If they answer the question that they end up with correctly, they will receive 3 points - a wrong answer does nothing. Scoring is kept on the included pad. This continues till all teams have answered a question. The last team will not have the possibility to swap as all other teams have used up their categories.

Each turn each of the teams have one opportunity to bezzerwizze one of the other teams question. This means that if the team does not answer the question correctly the team that has bezzerwizzet has a chance to answer as well. If the first team does answer correctly however the others team will have wasted their chance to bezzerwizze. The number of points you get for bezzerwizzing and answering correct is dependent on at what time you choose to do it. If you choose to bezzerwizze after the category is revealed but before the question is read aloud you will get 2 points for a correct answer. If you do it after the question has been read aloud you will only get 1 point. If the teams that bezzerwizzet can not answer correctly either the original team will receive the points.

The turn order then switches one place and the process starts again. If there are fewer category tokens in the bag than the number of teams when a new turn is started all of the tokens are returned to the bag.

The game ends when one of the teams have 20 points (the round is finished to allow other teams to reach 20 as well). Alternately you can choose to only play until the bag is empty.

Smarten-Up is a game based on health and nutrition.  The game consists of 6 packs of question and answer cards with 48 cards in each deck, 1 die, 2 score pads, 1 pen, and one rule/reference sheet. Each deck has a different colour back and a different point value.

The players roll the die to determine which deck to draw from. The starting player asks the question on the card to the player on the left. If the player answers the question correctly, they earn the full point value of the card. If the player fails to answer correctly, the asking player may score half of the point value themselves, or ask the question of the player on the first player's left. If that player answers the question correctly, they score full points. If the player fails to answer correctly, the asking player scores full points (except for true and false questions).

Some cards are bonus cards. The asking player reads the cards and gives the points to the player on the left. These two players then exchange places. This eliminates any possible preferred table positions. The game ends at a predetermined time or when a predetermined point value is reached. The player with the highest score is the winner.

Casper's cousin Spooky, the tuff little ghost, haunts this simple marble maze game. The Maze is made of cardboard with cardboard tabs that are pressed down to form bumpers for the marbles to dodge. The top of the box is placed under one end of the box bottom so the maze is angled down. Game comes with five marbles.

In turn, players take and play ALL of the marbles by dropping them into one of the holes at the top of the maze. The marbles must be held one inch above the hole when dropped. The player receives points as the marble lands in one of the slots at the bottom of the maze. If a player gets a marble in each of the slots at the bottom of the maze on one turn, they get a 10 point bonus. If a player gets all the marbles stacked into one slot at the bottom of the maze, they get a 10 point bonus. First player to 100 points wins.

Somewhere outside your safe nest there is a tasty cheese. The catch is that the stupid humans have not only placed it in a maze but also that the maze keeps changing. Nevertheless, you want that cheese, even if it means that you have to face dangerous traps. Beware, there are other mice in the maze as well and they may trick you to leave the cheese in their nests instead.

For each cheese that you take to your private nest, you score 1 point. For each cheese that you take to a shared nest, the players of the adjacent private nests score 1 point each. The player with the most points when all cheeses have been taken or no more actions are possible wins.

The game starts with 7x7 maze tiles, of which only the central one (the Cheese Chamber) is turned face up. The players start with 1 maze tile each. Each turn, a player does the following:

1. Move your mouse as far as you can along the path. In a nest, you may choose not to move, and in the Cheese Chamber, you may choose through which exit you move.
2. Move marbles as far as they can along the path. Marbles are traps that return mice to their nests and cause them to lose 1 turn.
3. Replace a maze tile anywhere in the maze by placing the one on your hand face up. If the maze tile you replace is turned face up, the next maze tile you replace must be turned face down.

When you leave the Cheese Chamber, you take a cheese, and when you enter a nest, you leave the cheese there, even if it is not a nest of your own. If you are hit by a marble, the cheese is left in the maze, and if you pass another mouse, you exchange whatever you carry. If you move out of the maze, you return to your nest and loses one turn while the cheese is returned to the Cheese Chamber. If the path is a loop, neither mice or marbles move.

The combination of free placement of maze tiles and forced movement through the maze creates a strong take that mechanism. Not only can you prevent other mice from finding the cheese but you can even force them to take it to your own nest. When you place a maze tile, each other player gets to place a maze tile so you must be very careful when moving in the maze!

Once upon a time there was someone who loved telling stories... well if you know that someone, then they'll be a winner at playing The Story Telling Game! Whether they tell tall, short, fairy or scary stories, this fast-paced game will be a best-seller for children aged six upwards who have a vivid imagination, a competitive streak and are quick thinking. The Story Telling Game is a great birthday and Christmas gift and it not only encourages creativity, but it will keep children entertained on rainy days and school holidays. Created for budding Jackanorys, The Story Telling Game sees each player start with five words on their cards and when the clock starts, the first players starts telling a story using one of their words. Then it's the turn of the next player who has to incorporate one of their words into the former player's story... and so it goes on with each player adding to the story with one of their words at a time until the buzzer declares that it's 'The End' for the player who's talking when the buzzer sounds. When this happens, the player has to pick up all the cards used by the other players and begin a new story, this time with the opening 'Twice upon a time'. The story doesn't have to win awards or be a work of literary genius - it can be about anything but has to include one of the words on each of their cards. The Smiley Trump card adds a twist to the game as it can be used as any word and if a player is caught out by the buzzer, the Smiley Trump means they get off scot-free and the next player has to collect all the cards.

User summary:
おてんきカエル (Otenki Kaeru) means Weather Frog in English. This game is designed to be played by a family who is new to card games.

Players are apprentices of the god of the sky who controls the weather. Their goal is to fulfill one wish, which is either children looking forward to a sunny picnic tomorrow or a frog waiting for rain.

They take turns playing one or more weather cards (sunny or rainy), while controlling their hands so that there is exactly one wish card in their hand at the end of round.

When the fourth sunny or rainy card is played, tomorrow's weather is determined and the round ends. They reveal their hands. If a player's sole wish card matches to tomorrow's weather, that player has fulfilled the wish and gains victory points printed in the wish card.

After several rounds, the player who has earned four victory points wins the game.

Publisher's summary:
親子や夫婦で初めて遊ぶためのカードゲームとして製作されました。
プレイヤーは天気を司る空の神様の見習いです。
他のプレイヤーとともに天気カードを出して投票し、明日の天気が晴れか雨か決めます。
ピクニックに行きたい二人の小学生と、雨を待つカエルの願いは、点数カードとして手元に届きます。
決まった天気に合った得点カードを持っているプレイヤーのうち、一人だけがその点数を得られます。
最も早く決められた点数を集めたプレイヤーが勝利します。
publishers: トーホクウィステリア (Tohoku Wisteria)

description:
User summary:
ジュエルデュエル (Jewel Duel) is a 2-player game which is derived from Japanese traditional dice game チンチロリン(Chinchirorin) with magical flavour.

As a graduation exam from wizards' school, you have to gather jewels and forge miraculous rings from the jewels before your opponent does.

To collect a jewel, you roll three dice to determine the magic power and beat your opponents. A special effect is added if you roll a triple or a sequence.

Then you may transform three jewels of the same colour into a ring, or any three jewels into one jewel of your choice. If you have equal or fewer rings than your opponent, you may spend one jewel to conjure an elemental for a random effect. It may change the game drastically in your favour or your opponent's favour.

You win the game if you forge three rings first.

Publisher's summary:
日本古来のダイスゲーム「チンチロリン」をスタイリッシュにアレンジした２人対戦専用ゲームです。
魔法使いの卒業試験は、宝石を集めて奇跡を起こす指輪を作ること。しかし、同僚よりも早く指輪を作らなければなりません。
宝石を集めるために、プレイヤーは魔法を唱えます。何も難しいことはありません。魔法が込められた３つのダイスを振るだけです。ただし相手プレイヤーよりも強力な魔法、つまりは強い目の組み合わせを出さなければなりません。
相手よりも不利な状況にある場合、宝石を消費することで精霊を召喚することができますが、その結果もダイスの目次第。大逆転を呼ぶこともあれば、さらに窮地に追い込まれることもあります。
先に指輪を３つ作ったプレイヤーが勝利します。
publishers: トーホクウィステリア (Tohoku Wisteria)

description: The world's most beloved beagle is back! A legend in his own mind. Snoopy takes off as the Flying Ace in constant pursuit of his nemesis; The Red Baron. It's high-flying fast-paced fun around the Eiffel Tower as Woodstock and his team of mechanics lend support from the ground. Pick up the most tiles to win!.

boardgame: Chronicards: Les grandes inventions et découvertes scientifiques
description:
Description
Ideal for acquiring a good foundation, The great scientific inventions and discoveries is for young adults, but also looking for a family game or a cultural product for easily dive back into discoveries.

Each card is unique!

Medal at the Lépine contest.

How to play ?

Chronicards is a card game played using 60 cards. 
Each card depicts a great discovery or invention on one side with explanation and the year in which that event occurred on the other side. 
Players take turns placing a card from their hand in a row on the table. After placing the card, the player reveals the date on it. If the card was placed correctly with the date in chronological order with all other cards on the table, the card stays in place; otherwise the card is put in the right place and the player takes another card from the deck.

If multiple players go out in the same round, then everyone else is eliminated from play and each of those players are dealt one more card for another round of play. If only one player has no cards after a bonus round, he wins; otherwise play continues until a single player goes out.

Idéal pour acquérir de bonnes bases, Les grandes inventions et découvertes scientifiques s’adresse aux plus jeunes mais également aux adultes à la recherche d’un jeu familial ou d’un produit culturel permettant de se replonger facilement dans les découvertes.

In Halloween, players play the role of dark lords recruiting minions across a graveyard. Whoever recruits the best army of undead will win the game.

MATERIAL

- 80 counters (headstones) in 4 colours (20 each of red, blue, yellow, green).
- 4 dark lords (red, blue, yellow, green)
- A deck of 36 mini-cards with the following structure:

-- The back of the cards shows a closed grave. There are 4 colours of graves (9 each of white, gray, black, brown).

-- For each grave colour, there are these fronts: 2 zombies (4 points each), 2 skeletons (3 points each), 2 ghosts (2 points each), 3 will-o'-the-wisp (1 point each).

GAMEPLAY OVERVIEW

Shuffle the whole deck and place the 36 cards facing down forming a 6x6 grid. This is the graveyard.

Players take turns moving their Dark Lord and claiming graves. When claiming a grave, the card is turned facing up. Players can also use their will-o'-the-wisps to inspect unclaimed graves.

The game ends when all cards have been revealed. Each player then adds up the value of his claimed cards. The highest score wins.

Friendly Fire Pack 10 contains eight scenarios featured in the Friendly Fire 2015 Advanced Squad Leader tournament.

The scenarios are:

FrF77 - Ghostbusters by Mattias Ronnblom
Germans vs French, 7 June 1940
German assault with VP survival as the VCs

FrF78 - No Glory in War by Mattias Ronnblom
Germans vs Russians, 7 September 1941
German Combined Arms attack against Russian fixed defense.

FrF79 - Saving the Center by Mattias Ronnblom
Germans vs. Russians, 27 January 1942
Snowy meeting engagement.

FrF80 - Breaking Bad by Peter Struijf and Chris Mazzei
Japanese vs Dutch, 14 March 1942
Dutch attack in the Dutch East Indies with variable victory conditions based on prisoner count.

FrF81 - Across the Rio Grande by Martin Svard
Russians vs. Swedes, 16 May 1942
Swedish assault on a prepared Russian position.

FrF82 - Riders on the Storm by Peter Struijf and Chris Mazzei
Germans vs. Allied Partisans & Americans, 26 August 1944
German attack on Partisan positions with American counterattack.

FrF83 - Phantom Army by Mattias Ronnblom
Germans vs. Russians, 16 April 1945
Combined arms Russian attack featuring heavy armor.

FrF84 - Bazooka Town by Michael Koch
Germans vs. Americans, 30 March 1945
Exit VP VCs for the Americans vs. CVPs for the Germans

One day, a safe suddenly appears in the castle ruins - but what is the code to open it? Luckily, the four amateur detectives keep on finding cards with pictures on them on their way through the city, which lead them to the code numbers. How is it done? Think of a word that describes the card and break it down into syllables in order to move the figures. Crack the code with a sharp mind and a feel for language!

Contains board with 2 sides for 2 different games to play "with" your Charmin' Chatty Cathy doll: Chatty At The Fair and Chatty Skate 'N Slide. Includes a record to insert into the Charmin' Chatty doll so she talks during the game. (Child can play games alone & doll can be the second player, or up to 4 players can play)

The first game is Chatty Skate 'N Slide: Play this game with the skates attached to the playing pieces and use side 1 of the record. Begin on the Start space, Chatty moves first. Pull the Chatty-Ring all the way out and let go. Chatty will say "Race to Orange", "Move ahead to Yellow", etc. Player will go to the next space of the color indicated. If Chatty says "Race to Orange and Slide", move to the next space of that color and use the slide if there is one. You cannot use the slide if Chatty does not say so. If Chatty says "You cannot move!", you cannot move on that turn. The winner is the first player to get to the winning "End" circle. Chatty must call one of the colors in the circle: red, orange, yellow or green, whether she says slide or not.

The second game is Chatty At The Fair: Use the playing pieces without the skates and use side 2 of the record. Child starts on My Start space and Chatty starts on Chatty's Start space. (When 3 or 4 play, 2 start on My Start and the rest start on Chatty's Start) Pull the Chatty-Ring all the way out. She will say HOW TO TRAVEL to the World's Fair. ("Why not ride the Pony?", "Go take the Whirlybird", "Go by Choo Choo Train", "Get in the Car and go", "Take the red Ferryboat", or "Take the Jet Plane - zoom!") Move to the next picture on the players path that matches what Chatty says. If Chatty says "Stop to see the sights", player does not move. If Chatty says "Green Light, go ahead 3" player moves ahead 3 picture spaces. Winner is the first player to get to their winner space (Whirlybird for My Start path and Choo Choo train for Chatty's Start path) (If player is 1 to 3 spaces from the winner space and Chatty says "Green Light, go ahead 3" player moves to the winner space and wins.)

This Kickstarter-exclusive edition contains, in addition to all the contents of the base game, the mini expansion "Tiny Epic Galaxies: Satellites & Super Weapons Mini Expansion".

From the description of Tiny Epic Galaxies:

A thirty-minute game of galactic conquest, Tiny Epic Galaxies is driven by an exciting dice-rolling mechanism that rewards thoughtful programming of the results. Players control a home galaxy and a fleet of space ships. As players upgrade their galaxies, they gain access to more ships and more dice.

Each turn, a player rolls a set of dice; how many dice are rolled is determined by the level of that player's galaxy. Each side of the six-sided die represents a different type of action: Movement, Colony Action, Harvest from Culture Planets, Harvest from Energy Planets, Improve an Economic Influence, and Improve a Diplomatic Influence.

After the roll, the player sorts the results of the roll (one selective re-roll is allowed) and organizes the dice in a desired activation order. Each die, in order, is then resolved and the results are immediate, which allows the player to pull-off unseen combos and surprise other players. Other players have the option to copy other player's actions...at a cost!

As players expand their galaxy by colonizing other planets through economic and diplomatic influence, they gain victory points AND the special powers brought in by those planets! In addition to galaxy upgrades, effective resource management provides luck-mitigating options that can sway the game in a calculated player's favor.

Whoever achieves the most points from acquiring planets and upgrading their personal galaxy wins!
